title: A Bachelor's Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 oz. of grated bread
- 4 oz. of currants
- 4 oz. of apples
- 2 oz. of sugar
- 3 eggs
- a few drops of essence of lemon
- a little grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Pare, core, and mince the apples very finely, sufficient, when minced, to make 4 oz.
2. Add to these the currants, which should be well washed, the grated bread, and sugar
3. Whisk the eggs, beat these up with the remaining ingredients
4. When all is thoroughly mixed, put the pudding into a buttered basin
5. Tie it down with a cloth and boil for 3 hours

title: A Cheap Gravy For Hashes, &c.
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Bones and trimmings of the cooked joint intended for hashing
- 1/4 teaspoonful of salt
- 1/4 teaspoonful of whole pepper
- 1/4 teaspoonful of whole allspice
- a small faggot of savoury herbs
- 1/2 head of celery
- 1 onion
- 1 oz. of butter
- thickening
- sufficient boiling water to cover the bones

## Directions

1. Chop the bones in small pieces, and put them in a stewpan, with the trimmings, salt, pepper, spice, herbs, and celery
2. Cover with boiling water, and let the whole simmer gently for 1-1/2 or 2 hours
3. Slice and fry the onion in the butter till it is of a pale brown, and mix it gradually with the gravy made from the bones
4. Boil for 1/4 hour, and strain into a basin
5. Put it back into the stewpan
6. Flavour with walnut pickle or ketchup, pickled-onion liquor, or any store sauce that may be preferred
7. Thicken with a little butter and flour, kneaded together on a plate
8. The gravy should just boil, to take off the rawness of the flour

title: A French Mode of Cooking Artichokes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 or 6 artichokes
- To each 1/2 gallon of water allow 1 heaped tablespoonful of salt
- 1/2 teaspoonful of pepper
- 1 bunch of savoury herbs
- 2 oz. of butter
- a large piece of cinder in a muslin bag
- plain melted butter

## Directions

1. Cut the ends of the leaves, as also the stems.
2. Put the artichokes into boiling water, with the above proportion of salt, pepper, herbs, and butter.
3. Let them boil quickly until tender, keeping the lid of the saucepan off.
4. When the leaves come out easily, they are cooked enough.
5. To keep them a beautiful green, put a large piece of cinder into a muslin bag, and let it boil with them.
6. Serve with plain melted butter.

title: A German Method of Cooking Potatoes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 to 10 middling-sized potatoes
- 3 oz. of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 1/2 pint of broth
- 2 tablespoonfuls of vinegar
- a laurel-leaf

## Directions

1. Put the butter and flour into a stewpan; stir over the fire until the butter is of a nice brown colour, and add the broth and vinegar
2. Peel and cut the potatoes into long thin slices, lay them in the gravy, and let them simmer gently until tender, which will be in from 10 to 15 minutes
3. Serve very hot

title: A Good Beef Gravy for Poultry, Game, &c.
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of lean beef
- 1/2 pint of cold water
- 1 shalot or small onion
- 1/2 a teaspoonful of salt
- a little pepper
- 1 tablespoonful of Harvey's sauce or mushroom ketchup
- 1/2 a teaspoonful of arrowroot

## Directions

1. Cut up the beef into small pieces, and put it, with the water, into a stewpan.
2. Add the shalot and seasoning, and simmer gently for 3 hours, taking care that it does not boil fast.
3. A short time before it is required, take the arrowroot, and having mixed it with a little cold water, pour it into the gravy, which keep stirring, adding the Harvey's sauce, and just letting it boil.
4. Strain off the gravy in a tureen, and serve very hot.

title: A Good Family Soup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Remains of a cold tongue
- 2 lbs. of shin of beef
- any cold pieces of meat or beef-bones
- 2 turnips
- 2 carrots
- 2 onions
- 1 parsnip
- 1 head of celery
- 4 quarts of water
- 1/2 teacupful of rice
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Put all the ingredients in a stewpan, and simmer gently for 4 hours, or until all the goodness is drawn from the meat.
2. Strain off the soup, and let it stand to get cold.
3. Save the kernels and soft parts of the tongue.
4. When the soup is wanted for use, skim off all the fat, put in the kernels and soft parts of the tongue, slice in a small quantity of fresh carrot, turnip, and onion; stew till the vegetables are tender, and serve with toasted bread.

title: A Good Mutton Soup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- A neck of mutton about 5 or 6 lbs.
- 3 carrots
- 3 turnips
- 2 onions
- a large bunch of sweet herbs, including parsley
- salt and pepper to taste
- a little sherry, if liked
- 3 quarts of water

## Directions

1. Lay the ingredients in a covered pan before the fire, and let them remain there the whole day, stirring occasionally.
2. The next day put the whole into a stewpan, and place it on a brisk fire.
3. When it commences to boil, take the pan off the fire, and put it on one side to simmer until the meat is done.
4. When ready for use, take out the meat, dish it up with carrots and turnips, and send it to table; strain the soup, let it cool, skim off all the fat, season and thicken it with a tablespoonful, or rather more, of arrowroot.
5. Flavour with a little sherry, simmer for 5 minutes, and serve.

title: A Good Sauce for Steaks
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 oz. of whole black pepper
- 1/2 oz. of allspice
- 1 oz. of salt
- 1/2 oz. grated horseradish
- 1/2 oz. of pickled shalots
- 1 pint of mushroom ketchup or walnut pickle

## Directions

1. Pound all the ingredients finely in a mortar, and put them into the ketchup or walnut-liquor
2. Let them stand for a fortnight, when strain off the liquor and bottle for use
3. Either pour a little of the sauce over the steaks or mix it in the gravy

title: A Good Sauce for Various Boiled Puddings
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of butter
- 1/4 lb. of pounded sugar
- a wineglassful of brandy or rum

## Directions

1. Beat the butter to a cream, until no lumps remain
2. add the pounded sugar, and brandy or rum
3. stir once or twice until the whole is thoroughly mixed, and serve

title: A Good Wash for the Hair
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pennyworth of borax
- 1/2 pint of olive-oil
- 1 pint of boiling water

## Directions

1. Pour the boiling water over the borax and oil
2. Let it cool
3. Put the mixture into a bottle
4. Shake it before using, and apply it with a flannel

title: A More Simple Flanc
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- paste
- white of egg
- raw fruit
- sugar
- apricot jam (optional)

## Directions

1. Roll out the paste and cut the bottom of a round or oval shape
2. Cut a narrow strip for the sides
3. Stick the sides to the bottom piece with white of egg
4. Fill the flanc with raw fruit and sufficient sugar to sweeten it nicely
5. Bake until the crust is of equal thickness throughout and perfectly joined so juice does not escape
6. Serve hot or cold, garnished with candied citron or melted apricot jam poured over

title: A Nice Plain Cake
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 1 teaspoonful of Borwick's baking-powder
- 1/4 lb. of good dripping
- 1 teacupful of moist sugar
- 3 eggs
- 1 breakfast-cupful of milk
- 1 oz. of caraway seeds
- 1/2 lb. of currants

## Directions

1. Put the flour and baking-powder into a basin; stir those together; then rub in the dripping, add the sugar, caraway seeds, and currants; whisk the eggs with the milk, and beat all together very thoroughly until the ingredients are well mixed.
2. Butter a tin, put in the cake, and bake it from 11/2 to 2 hours.

title: A Nice Plain Cake For Children
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quartern of dough
- 1/4 lb. of moist sugar
- 1/4 lb. of butter or good beef dripping
- 1/4 pint of warm milk
- 1/2 grated nutmeg or 1/2 oz. of caraway seeds

## Directions

1. If you are not in the habit of making bread at home, procure the dough from the baker's, and, as soon as it comes in, put it into a basin near the fire; cover the basin with a thick cloth, and let the dough remain a little while to rise.
2. In the mean time, beat the butter to a cream, and make the milk warm; and when the dough has risen, mix with it thoroughly all the above ingredients, and knead the cake well for a few minutes.
3. Butter some cake-tins, half fill them, and stand them in a warm place, to allow the dough to rise again.
4. When the tins are three parts full, put the cakes into a good oven, and bake them from 13/4 to 2 hours.

title: A Nice Plum Cake
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 1/4 lb. of butter
- 1/2 lb. of sugar
- 1/2 lb. of currants
- 2 oz. of candied lemon-peel
- 1/2 pint of milk
- 1 teaspoonful of ammonia or carbonate of soda

## Directions

1. Put the flour into a basin with the sugar, currants, and sliced candied peel
2. Beat the butter to a cream, and mix all these ingredients together with the milk
3. Stir the ammonia into 2 tablespoonfuls of milk and add it to the dough
4. Beat the whole well, until everything is thoroughly mixed
5. Put the dough into a buttered tin, and bake the cake from 1-1/2 to 2 hours

title: A Nice Useful Cake
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of butter
- 6 oz. of currants
- 1/4 lb. of sugar
- 1 lb. of dried flour
- 2 teaspoonfuls of baking-powder
- 3 eggs
- 1 teacupful of milk
- 2 oz. of sweet almonds
- 1 oz. of candied peel

## Directions

1. Beat the butter to a cream
2. Wash, pick, and dry the currants
3. Whisk the eggs
4. Blanch and chop the almonds
5. Cut the peel into neat slices
6. Mix the dry ingredients together
7. Add the butter, milk, and eggs
8. Beat the mixture well for a few minutes
9. Put the cake into a buttered mould or tin
10. Bake it for rather more than 1-1/2 hour

title: A Nice Yeast-Cake
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 lb. of flour
- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 1/2 pint of milk
- 1-1/2 tablespoonful of good yeast
- 3 eggs
- 3/4 lb. of currants
- 1/2 lb. of white moist sugar
- 2 oz. of candied peel

## Directions

1. Put the milk and butter into a saucepan, and shake it round over a fire until the butter is melted, but do not allow the milk to get very hot
2. Put the flour into a basin, stir to it the milk and butter, the yeast, and eggs, which should be well beaten
3. Form the whole into a smooth dough
4. Let it stand in a warm place, covered with a cloth, to rise
5. When sufficiently risen, add the currants, sugar, and candied peel cut into thin slices
6. When all the ingredients are thoroughly mixed, line 2 moderate-sized cake-tins with buttered paper, which should be about six inches higher than the tin
7. Pour in the mixture, let it stand to rise again for another 1/2 hour
8. Bake the cakes in a brisk oven for about 1-1/2 hour
9. If the tops of them become too brown, cover them with paper until they are done through

title: A Pavini Cake
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of flour
- 1/2 lb. of ground rice
- 1/2 lb. of raisins stoned and cut into small pieces
- 1/4 lb. of currants
- 1/4 lb. of butter
- 2 oz. of sweet almonds
- 1/4 lb. of sifted loaf sugar
- 1/2 nutmeg grated
- 1 pint of milk
- 1 teaspoonful of carbonate of soda

## Directions

1. Stone and cut the raisins into small pieces
2. Wash, pick, and dry the currants
3. Melt the butter to a cream, but without oiling it
4. Blanch and chop the almonds, and grate the nutmeg
5. Mix all these ingredients well together
6. Make the milk warm, stir in the soda, and with this liquid make the whole into a paste
7. Butter a mould, rather more than half fill it with the dough
8. Bake the cake in a moderate oven from 1-1/2 to 2 hours, or less time should it be made into 2 cakes

title: A Pickle For Tongues Or Beef (Newmarket Recipe)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 gallon of soft water
- 3 lbs. of coarse salt
- 6 oz. of coarse brown sugar
- 1/2 oz. of saltpetre

## Directions

1. Put all the ingredients into a saucepan, and let them boil for 1/2 hour
2. Clear off the scum as it rises
3. When done pour the pickle into a pickling-pan
4. Let it get cold, then put in the meat, and allow it to remain in the pickle from 8 to 14 days, according to the size
5. It will keep good for 6 months if well boiled once a fortnight
6. Tongues will take 1 month or 6 weeks to be properly cured
7. In salting meat, beef and tongues should always be put in separate vessels
8. A moderate-sized tongue should remain in the pickle about a month, and be turned every day

title: A Plain Cabinet or Boiled Bread-and-Butter Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 oz. of raisins
- a few thin slices of bread and butter
- 3 eggs
- 1 pint of milk
- sugar to taste
- 1/4 nutmeg

## Directions

1. Butter a pudding-basin, and line the inside with a layer of raisins that have been previously stoned
2. Nearly fill the basin with slices of bread and butter with the crust cut off
3. In another basin, beat the eggs; add to them the milk, sugar, and grated nutmeg; mix all well together
4. Pour the whole on to the bread and butter; let it stand 1/2 hour
5. Tie a floured cloth over it; boil for 1 hour, and serve with sweet sauce
6. Care must be taken that the basin is quite full before the cloth is tied over

title: A Plain Christmas Pudding for Children
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 1 lb. of bread crumbs
- 3/4 lb. of stoned raisins
- 3/4 lb. of currants
- 3/4 lb. of suet
- 3 or 4 eggs
- milk
- 2 oz. of candied peel
- 1 teaspoonful of powdered allspice
- 1/2 saltspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Let the suet be finely chopped, the raisins stoned, and the currants well washed, picked, and dried.
2. Mix these with the other dry ingredients, and stir all well together.
3. Beat and strain the eggs to the pudding, stir these in, and add just sufficient milk to make it mix properly.
4. Tie it up in a well-floured cloth, put it into boiling water, and boil for at least 5 hours.
5. Serve with a sprig of holly placed in the middle of the pudding, and a little pounded sugar sprinkled over it.

title: A Pleasant Drink for Warm Weather
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 pint of good ale
- 1 bottle of ginger beer

## Directions

1. Ensure the ginger beer is in an effervescing state, and the beer is not in the least turned or sour
2. Mix them together
3. Drink immediately

title: A Pound Plum-Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of suet
- 1 lb. of currants
- 1 lb. of stoned raisins
- 8 eggs
- 1/2 grated nutmeg
- 2 oz. of sliced candied peel
- 1 teaspoonful of ground ginger
- 1/2 lb. of bread crumbs
- 1/2 lb. of flour
- 1/2 pint of milk

## Directions

1. Chop the suet finely; mix with it the dry ingredients; stir these well together, and add the well-beaten eggs and milk to moisten with.
2. Beat up the mixture well, and should the above proportion of milk not be found sufficient to make it of the proper consistency, a little more should be added.
3. Press the pudding into a mould, tie it in a floured cloth, and boil for 5 hours, or rather longer, and serve with brandy-sauce.

title: A Pretty Dish of Apples and Rice
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 oz. of rice
- 1 quart of milk
- the rind of 1/2 lemon
- sugar to taste
- 1/2 saltspoonful of salt
- 8 apples
- 1/4 lb. of sugar
- 1/4 pint of water
- 1/2 pint of boiled custard

## Directions

1. Flavour the milk with lemon-rind, by boiling them together for a few minutes
2. Take out the peel, and put in the rice, with sufficient sugar to sweeten it nicely, and boil gently until the rice is quite soft
3. Let it cool
4. In the mean time pare, quarter, and core the apples, and boil them until tender in a syrup made with sugar and water in the above proportion
5. When soft, lift them out on a sieve to drain
6. Put a middling-sized gallipot in the centre of a dish
7. Lay the rice all round till the top of the gallipot is reached
8. Smooth the rice with the back of a spoon, and stick the apples into it in rows, one row sloping to the right and the next to the left
9. Set it in the oven to colour the apples
10. When required for table, remove the gallipot, garnish the rice with preserved fruits, and pour in the middle sufficient custard to be level with the top of the rice, and serve hot

title: A Pretty Dish of Oranges
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 large oranges
- 1/2 lb. of loaf sugar
- 1/4 pint of water
- 1/2 pint of cream
- 2 tablespoonfuls of any kind of liqueur
- sugar to taste
- salad-oil

## Directions

1. Put the sugar and water into a saucepan and boil them until the sugar becomes brittle
2. Ascertain the sugar is sufficiently boiled by taking up a small quantity in a spoon and dipping it in cold water
3. Peel the oranges and remove as much of the white pith as possible
4. Divide them into nice-sized slices, without breaking the thin white skin which surrounds the juicy pulp
5. Place the pieces of orange on small skewers
6. Dip them into the hot sugar
7. Arrange them in layers round a plain mould, which should be well oiled with the purest salad-oil
8. Line the sides of the mould only with the oranges and leave the centre open for the cream
9. Let the sugar become firm by cooling
10. Turn the oranges carefully out on a dish
11. Fill the centre with whipped cream flavoured with any kind of liqueur and sweetened with pounded sugar

title: A Quickly-Made Gravy
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of shin of beef
- 1/2 onion
- 1/4 carrot
- 2 or 3 sprigs of parsley and savoury herbs
- a piece of butter about the size of a walnut
- cayenne and mace to taste
- 3/4 pint of water

## Directions

1. Cut up the meat into very small pieces, slice the onion and carrot, and put them into a small saucepan with the butter.
2. Keep stirring over a sharp fire until they have taken a little colour, when add the water and the remaining ingredients.
3. Simmer for 1/2 hour, skim well, strain, and flavour, when it will be ready for use.

title: A Substitute for Caper Sauce
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of melted butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of cut parsley
- 1/2 teaspoonful of salt
- 1 tablespoonful of vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil the parsley slowly to let it become a bad colour; cut, but do not chop it fine.
2. Add it to 1/2 pint of smoothly-made melted butter, with salt and vinegar in the above proportions.
3. Boil up and serve.

title: A Sweet Dish of Macaroni
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of macaroni
- 1-1/2 pint of milk
- the rind of 1/2 lemon
- 3 oz. of lump sugar
- 3/4 pint of custard

## Directions

1. Put the milk into a saucepan, with the lemon-peel and sugar.
2. Bring it to the boiling-point, drop in the macaroni, and let it gradually swell over a gentle fire, but do not allow the pipes to break.
3. The form should be entirely preserved; and, though tender, should be firm, and not soft, with no part beginning to melt.
4. Should the milk dry away before the macaroni is sufficiently swelled, add a little more.
5. Make a custard by recipe No. 1423.
6. Place the macaroni on a dish, and pour the custard over the hot macaroni.
7. Grate over it a little nutmeg, and, when cold, garnish the dish with slices of candied citron.

title: A Very Good Seed-Cake
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of butter
- 6 eggs
- 3/4 lb. of sifted sugar
- pounded mace and grated nutmeg to taste
- 1 lb. of flour
- 3/4 oz. of caraway seeds
- 1 wineglassful of brandy

## Directions

1. Beat the butter to a cream
2. Dredge in the flour
3. Add the sugar, mace, nutmeg, and caraway seeds, and mix these ingredients well together
4. Whisk the eggs, stir to them the brandy, and beat the cake again for 10 minutes
5. Put it into a tin lined with buttered paper
6. Bake it from 1-1/2 to 2 hours

title: A Very Rich and Good Mushroom Sauce, to Serve with Fowls or Rabbits
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of mushroom-buttons
- salt
- grated nutmeg
- 1 blade of pounded mace
- 1 pint of cream
- 2 oz. of butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Rub the buttons with a piece of flannel and salt, to take off the skin.
2. Cut off the stalks, and put them in a stewpan with the above ingredients, previously kneading together the butter and flour.
3. Boil the whole for about ten minutes, stirring all the time.
4. Pour some of the sauce over the fowls, and the remainder serve in a tureen.

title: A Very Simple and Easy Method of Making a Very Superior Orange Wine
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 90 Seville oranges
- 32 lbs. of lump sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Break up the sugar into small pieces, and put it into a dry, sweet 9-gallon cask, placed in a cellar or other storehouse
2. Pare the peel of the oranges quite thin and put into one large pan or wooden keeler
3. Put the pulp after the juice has been squeezed from it into another large pan or wooden keeler
4. Strain the juice through a piece of double muslin, and put it into the cask with the sugar
5. Pour about 1-1/2 gallon of cold spring water on both the peels and pulp; let it stand for 24 hours, and then strain it into the cask
6. Add more water to the peels and pulp when this is done, and repeat the same process every day for a week
7. Apportion the quantity as nearly as possible to the seven days, and stir the contents of the cask each day
8. On the third day after the cask is full, that is, the tenth day after the commencement of making, the cask may be securely bunged down
9. Bottle it in 8 or 9 months, and it will be fit for use in a twelve month after the time of making

title: A Very Simple Apple Charlotte
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 slices of bread and butter
- about 6 good-sized apples
- 1 tablespoonful of minced lemon-peel
- 2 tablespoonfuls of juice
- moist sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Butter a pie-dish.
2. Place a layer of bread and butter, without the crust, at the bottom; then a layer of apples, pared, cored, and cut into thin slices.
3. Sprinkle over these a portion of the lemon-peel and juice, and sweeten with moist sugar.
4. Place another layer of bread and butter, and then one of apples, proceeding in this manner until the dish is full.
5. Cover it up with the peel of the apples, to preserve the top from browning or burning.
6. Bake in a brisk oven for rather more than 3/4 hour.
7. Turn the charlotte on a dish, sprinkle sifted sugar over, and serve.

title: A Very Simple Method of Making Coffee
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 oz. or 1 tablespoonful of coffee per person
- 1 pint of water per oz. of coffee

## Directions

1. Have a small iron ring made to fit the top of the coffee-pot inside
2. To this ring sew a small muslin bag
3. Fit the bag into the pot and pour some boiling water in it
4. When the pot is well warmed, put the ground coffee into the bag
5. Pour over as much boiling water as is required and close the lid
6. When all the water has filtered through, remove the bag and send the coffee to table
7. Pour the water on the coffee gradually so that the infusion may be stronger
8. The bag must be well made so that none of the grounds escape through the seams

title: Alma Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of fresh butter
- 1/2 lb. of powdered sugar
- 1/2 lb. of flour
- 1/4 lb. of currants
- 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Beat the butter to a thick cream, strew in, by degrees, the sugar, and mix both these well together.
2. Dredge the flour in gradually, add the currants, and moisten with the eggs, which should be well beaten.
3. Butter a mould that will hold the mixture exactly.
4. Tie it down with a cloth, put the pudding into boiling water, and boil for 5 hours.
5. When turned out, strew some powdered sugar over it, and serve.

title: Almond Cake
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of sweet almonds
- 1 oz. of bitter almonds
- 6 eggs
- 8 tablespoonfuls of sifted sugar
- 5 tablespoonfuls of fine flour
- the grated rind of 1 lemon
- 3 oz. of butter

## Directions

1. Blanch and pound the almonds to a paste
2. Separate the whites from the yolks of the eggs
3. Beat the latter, and add them to the almonds
4. Stir in the sugar, flour, and lemon-rind
5. Add the butter, which should be beaten to a cream
6. When all these ingredients are well mixed, put in the whites of the eggs, which should be whisked to a stiff froth
7. Butter a cake-mould, put in the mixture, and bake in a good oven from 1-1/4 to 1-3/4 hour

title: Almond Cheesecakes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of sweet almonds
- 4 bitter almonds
- 3 eggs
- 2 oz. of butter
- the rind of 1/4 lemon
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon-juice
- 3 oz. of sugar
- puff-paste

## Directions

1. Blanch and pound the almonds smoothly in a mortar, with a little rose- or spring-water
2. Stir in the eggs, which should be well beaten, and the butter, which should be warmed
3. Add the grated lemon-peel and -juice, sweeten, and stir well until the whole is thoroughly mixed
4. Line some pattypans with puff-paste
5. Put in the mixture, and bake for 20 minutes, or rather less in a quick oven

title: Almond Flowers
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff-paste
- 3 oz. of almonds per 1/2 lb. of paste
- sifted sugar
- white of an egg
- apple jelly
- strawberry jam

## Directions

1. Roll the paste out to the thickness of 1/4 inch, and, with a round fluted cutter, stamp out as many pieces as may be required
2. Work the paste up again, roll it out, and, with a smaller cutter, stamp out some pieces the size of a shilling
3. Brush the larger pieces over with the white of an egg, and place one of the smaller pieces on each
4. Blanch and cut the almonds into strips lengthwise; press them slanting into the paste closely round the rings
5. When they are all completed, sift over some pounded sugar, and bake for about 1/4 hour or 20 minutes
6. Garnish between the almonds with strips of apple jelly, and place in the centre of the ring a small quantity of strawberry jam
7. Pile them high on the dish, and serve

title: Almond Icing For Cakes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of finely-pounded loaf sugar
- 1 lb. of sweet almonds
- the whites of 4 eggs
- a little rose-water

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds, and pound them (a few at a time) in a mortar to a paste, adding a little rose-water to facilitate the operation.
2. Whisk the whites of the eggs to a strong froth.
3. Mix them with the pounded almonds, stir in the sugar, and beat altogether.
4. When the cake is sufficiently baked, lay on the almond icing, and put it into the oven to dry.
5. Before laying this preparation on the cake, great care must be taken that it is nice and smooth, which is easily accomplished by well beating the mixture.

title: Almond Paste for Second-Course Dishes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of sweet almonds
- 6 bitter almonds
- 1 lb. of very finely sifted sugar
- the whites of 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds, and dry them thoroughly
2. Put them into a mortar, and pound them well, wetting them gradually with the whites of 2 eggs
3. When well pounded, put them into a small preserving-pan, add the sugar, and place the pan on a small but clear fire (a hot-plate is better)
4. Keep stirring until the paste is dry
5. Take it out of the pan, put it between two dishes, and, when cold, make it into any shape that fancy may dictate

title: Almond Puffs
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 2 oz. of butter
- 2 oz. of pounded sugar
- 2 oz. of sweet almonds
- 4 bitter almonds

## Directions

1. Blanch and pound the almonds in a mortar to a smooth paste
2. Melt the butter, dredge in the flour, and add the sugar and pounded almonds
3. Beat the mixture well, and put it into cups or very tiny jelly-pots, which should be well buttered
4. Bake in a moderate oven for about 20 minutes, or longer should the puffs be large
5. Turn them out on a dish, the bottom of the puff upper-most, and serve

title: Almond Soup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 lbs. of lean beef or veal
- 1/2 a scrag of mutton
- 1 oz. of vermicelli
- 4 blades of mace
- 6 cloves
- 1/2 lb. of sweet almonds
- the yolks of 6 eggs
- 1 gill of thick cream
- rather more than 2 quarts of water

## Directions

1. Boil the beef, or veal, and the mutton, gently in water that will cover them, till the gravy is very strong, and the meat very tender
2. Strain off the gravy, and set it on the fire with the specified quantities of vermicelli, mace, and cloves, to 2 quarts
3. Let it boil till it has the flavour of the spices
4. Have ready the almonds, blanched and pounded very fine; the yolks of the eggs boiled hard
5. Mix the almonds, whilst pounding, with a little of the soup, lest the latter should grow oily
6. Pound them till they are a mere pulp, and keep adding to them, by degrees, a little soup until they are thoroughly mixed together
7. Let the soup be cool when mixing, and do it perfectly smooth
8. Strain it through a sieve, set it on the fire, stir frequently, and serve hot
9. Just before taking it up, add the cream

title: Almonds and Raisins
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- almonds
- raisins

## Directions

1. These are usually served on glass dishes, the fruit piled high in the centre, and the almonds blanched, and strewn over.
2. To blanch the almonds, put them into a small mug or teacup, pour over them boiling water, let them remain for 2 or 3 minutes, and the skins may then be easily removed.

title: An Easy Method of Making a Charlotte-Aux-Pommes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of flour
- 1/4 lb. of butter
- 1/4 lb. of powdered sugar
- 1/2 teaspoonful of baking-powder
- 1 egg
- milk
- 1 glass of raisin-wine
- apple marmalade
- 1/4 pint of cream
- 2 dessertspoonfuls of pounded sugar
- 2 tablespoonfuls of lemon-juice

## Directions

1. Make a cake with the flour, butter, sugar, and baking-powder; moisten with the egg and sufficient milk to make it the proper consistency, and bake it in a round tin.
2. When cold, scoop out the middle, leaving a good thickness all round the sides, to prevent them breaking.
3. Take some of the scooped-out pieces, which should be trimmed into neat slices; lay them in the cake, and pour over sufficient raisin-wine, with the addition of a little brandy, if approved, to soak them well.
4. Place a layer of apple marmalade over the soaked cake, then a layer of cake and a layer of apples.
5. Whip the cream to a froth, mixing with it the sugar and lemon-juice.
6. Pile it on the top of the charlotte, and garnish it with pieces of clear apple jelly.
7. This dish is served cold, but may be eaten hot, by omitting the cream, and merely garnishing the top with bright jelly just before it is sent to table.

title: An Easy Way of Making a Tipsy Cake
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 stale small sponge-cakes
- raisin wine
- 1/2 lb. of jam
- 1 pint of custard No. 1423

## Directions

1. Soak the sponge-cakes, which should be stale (on this account they should be cheaper), in a little raisin wine.
2. Arrange them on a deep glass dish in four layers, putting a layer of jam between each, and pour round them a pint of custard, made by recipe No. 1423, decorating the top with cut preserved fruit.

title: An Easy Way of Making Orange Marmalade
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Seville oranges
- 1-1/2 lb. of loaf sugar to every lb. of pulp
- 1/2 pint of the water the oranges were last boiled in

## Directions

1. Choose some fine Seville oranges; put them whole into a stewpan with sufficient water to cover them, and stew them until they become perfectly tender, changing the water 2 or 3 times
2. Drain them, take off the rind, remove the pips from the pulp, weigh it
3. Boil the sugar and water together for 10 minutes
4. Put in the pulp, boil for another 10 minutes
5. Add the peel cut into strips, and boil the marmalade for another 10 minutes
6. Pour it into jars; let it cool; then cover down with bladders, or tissue-paper brushed over on both sides with the white of an egg

title: An Excellent Pickle
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Equal quantities of medium-sized onions, cucumbers, and sauce-apples
- 1-1/2 teaspoonful of salt
- 3/4 teaspoonful of cayenne
- 1 wineglassful of soy
- 1 wineglassful of sherry
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Slice sufficient cucumbers, onions, and apples to fill a pint stone jar, taking care to cut the slices very thin
2. Arrange them in alternate layers, shaking in as you proceed salt and cayenne in the above proportion
3. Pour in the soy and wine, and fill up with vinegar
4. It will be fit for use the day it is made

title: An Excellent Plum-Pudding, made without Eggs
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of flour
- 6 oz. of raisins
- 6 oz. of currants
- 1/4 lb. of chopped suet
- 1/4 lb. of brown sugar
- 1/4 lb. of mashed carrot
- 1/4 lb. of mashed potatoes
- 1 tablespoonful of treacle
- 1 oz. of candied lemon-peel
- 1 oz. of candied citron

## Directions

1. Mix the flour, currants, suet, and sugar well together
2. have ready the above proportions of mashed carrot and potato, which stir into the other ingredients
3. add the treacle and lemon-peel; but put no liquid in the mixture, or it will be spoiled
4. Tie it loosely in a cloth, or, if put in a basin, do not quite fill it, as the pudding should have room to swell, and boil it for 4 hours
5. Serve with brandy-sauce

title: An Excellent Pomatum
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 lb. of lard
- 1/2 pint of olive-oil
- 1/2 pint of castor-oil
- 4 oz. of spermaceti
- bergamot, or any other scent
- elder-flower water

## Directions

1. Wash the lard well in the elder-flower water
2. Drain, and beat it to a cream
3. Mix the two oils together, and heat them sufficiently to dissolve the spermaceti, which should be beaten fine in a mortar
4. Mix all these ingredients together with the brandy and whatever kind of scent may be preferred
5. Whilst warm pour into glass bottles for use, keeping them well corked
6. To liquefy the pomatum, set the bottle in a saucepan of warm water

title: An Excellent Way of Preserving Cucumbers
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Salt and water
- 1 lb. of lump sugar
- the rind of 1 lemon
- 1 oz. of ginger
- cucumbers
- cabbage-leaf

## Directions

1. Choose the greenest cucumbers, and those that are most free from seeds
2. Put them in strong salt and water, with a cabbage-leaf to keep them down
3. Tie a paper over them, and put them in a warm place till they are yellow
4. Wash them and set them over the fire in fresh water, with a very little salt, and another cabbage-leaf over them
5. Cover very closely, but take care they do not boil
6. If they are not a fine green, change the water again, cover them as before, and make them hot
7. When they are a good colour, take them off the fire and let them cool
8. Cut them in quarters, take out the seeds and pulp, and put them into cold water
9. Let them remain for 2 days, changing the water twice each day, to draw out the salt
10. Put the sugar, with 1/4 pint of water, in a saucepan over the fire
11. Remove the scum as it rises, and add the lemon-peel and ginger with the outside scraped off
12. When the syrup is tolerably thick, take it off the fire, and when cold, wipe the cucumbers dry, and put them in
13. Boil the syrup once in 2 or 3 days for 3 weeks
14. Strengthen it if required, and let it be quite cold before the cucumbers are put in

title: An Excellent Way to Cook a Breast of Mutton
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Breast of mutton
- 2 onions
- salt and pepper to taste
- flour
- a bunch of savoury herbs
- green peas

## Directions

1. Cut the mutton into pieces about 2 inches square, and let it be tolerably lean.
2. Put it into a stewpan, with a little fat or butter, and fry it of a nice brown.
3. Dredge in a little flour, slice the onions, and put it with the herbs in the stewpan.
4. Pour in sufficient water just to cover the meat, and simmer the whole gently until the mutton is tender.
5. Take out the meat, strain, and skim off all the fat from the gravy, and put both the meat and gravy back into the stewpan.
6. Add about a quart of young green peas, and let them boil gently until done. 2 or 3 slices of bacon added and stewed with the mutton give additional flavour; and, to insure the peas being a beautiful green colour, they may be boiled in water separately, and added to the stew at the moment of serving.

title: An Excellent Wine Sauce for Puddings
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The yolks of 4 eggs
- 1 teaspoonful of flour
- 2 oz. of pounded sugar
- 2 oz. of fresh butter
- 1/4 saltspoonful of salt
- 1/2 pint of sherry or Madeira

## Directions

1. Put the butter and flour into a saucepan, and stir them over the fire until the former thickens; then add the sugar, salt, and wine, and mix these ingredients well together.
2. Separate the yolks from the whites of 4 eggs; beat up the former, and stir them briskly to the sauce; let it remain over the fire until it is on the point of simmering; but do not allow it to boil, or it will instantly curdle.
3. Serve separately, and not poured over the pudding.

title: An Indian Dish of Fowl
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast fowl
- 3 or 4 sliced onions
- 1 tablespoonful of curry-powder
- salt to taste
- butter

## Directions

1. Divide the fowl into joints
2. Slice and fry the onions in a little butter, taking care not to burn them
3. Sprinkle over the fowl a little curry-powder and salt
4. Fry these nicely, pile them high in the centre of the dish, cover with the onion, and serve with a cut lemon on a plate
5. Care must be taken that the onions are not greasy: they should be quite dry, but not burnt

title: An Unrivalled Plum-Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 lb. of muscatel raisins
- 1-3/4 lb. of currants
- 1 lb. of sultana raisins
- 2 lbs. of the finest moist sugar
- 2 lbs. of bread crumbs
- 16 eggs
- 2 lbs. of finely-chopped suet
- 6 oz. of mixed candied peel
- the rind of 2 lemons
- 1 oz. of ground nutmeg
- 1 oz. of ground cinnamon
- 1/2 oz. of pounded bitter almonds
- 1/4 pint of brandy

## Directions

1. Stone and cut up the raisins, but do not chop them; wash and dry the currants, and cut the candied peel into thin slices.
2. Mix all the dry ingredients well together, and moisten with the eggs, which should be well beaten and strained, to the pudding.
3. Stir in the brandy, and, when all is thoroughly mixed, well butter and flour a stout new pudding-cloth.
4. Put in the pudding, tie it down very tightly and closely, boil from 6 to 8 hours, and serve with brandy-sauce.
5. A few sweet almonds, blanched and cut in strips, and stuck on the pudding, ornament it prettily.
6. This quantity may be divided and boiled in buttered moulds. For small families this is the most desirable way, as the above will be found to make a pudding of rather large dimensions.

title: Anchovy Butter
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of butter
- 6 anchovies
- 1 small bunch of parsley

## Directions

1. Wash, bone, and pound the anchovies well in a mortar
2. Scald the parsley, chop it, and rub through a sieve
3. Pound all the ingredients together
4. Mix well, and make the butter into pats immediately
5. Garnish with parsley

title: Anchovy Butter or Paste
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 dozen anchovies
- 1/2 lb. of fresh butter

## Directions

1. Wash the anchovies thoroughly
2. Bone and dry them, and pound them in a mortar to a paste
3. Mix the butter gradually with them, and rub the whole through a sieve
4. Put it by in small pots for use, and carefully exclude the air with a bladder, as it soon changes the colour of anchovies, besides spoiling them

title: Anchovy Sauce for Fish
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 anchovies
- 1 oz. of butter
- 1/2 pint of melted butter
- cayenne to taste
- lemon-juice (optional)

## Directions

1. Bone the anchovies, and pound them in a mortar to a paste, with 1 oz. of butter.
2. Make the melted butter hot, stir in the pounded anchovies and cayenne; simmer for 3 or 4 minutes; and if liked, add a squeeze of lemon-juice.
3. Alternatively, stir in 1-1/2 tablespoonfuls of anchovy essence to 1/2 pint of melted butter, and add seasoning to taste.
4. Boil the whole up for 1 minute, and serve hot.

title: Anchovy Toast
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 slices of bread
- clarified butter
- anchovy paste (No. 227)
- made mustard or a few grains of cayenne

## Directions

1. Toast 2 or 3 slices of bread, or, if wanted very savoury, fry them in clarified butter
2. Spread on them the paste, No. 227
3. Made mustard, or a few grains of cayenne, may be added to the paste before laying it on the toast

title: Anchovy Toast
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- toast (2 or 3 slices of bread)
- clarified butter
- anchovy paste
- made mustard or cayenne

## Directions

1. Toast 2 or 3 slices of bread, or if wanted very savoury, fry them in clarified butter
2. Spread the paste on them
3. May add made mustard or a few grains of cayenne to the paste before laying it on the toast

title: Another Good Short Crust
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 8 oz. of butter
- yolks of 2 eggs
- 2 oz. of sifted sugar
- about 1/4 pint of milk

## Directions

1. Rub the butter into the flour
2. Add the sugar, and mix the whole as lightly as possible to a smooth paste, with the yolks of eggs well beaten, and the milk

title: Another Recipe for Pomatum
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 oz. of olive-oil
- 1 oz. of spermaceti
- 3 pennyworth of essential oil of almonds
- 3 pennyworth of essence of lemon

## Directions

1. Mix these ingredients together, and store away in jars for use

title: Another Way to Dress Sweetbreads
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweetbreads
- egg and bread crumbs
- 1/2 pint of gravy No. 442
- 1/2 glass of sherry
- water-cresses

## Directions

1. Soak the sweetbreads in water for an hour, and throw them into boiling water to render them firm.
2. Let them stew gently for about 1/4 hour, take them out and put them into a cloth to drain all the water from them.
3. Brush them over with egg, sprinkle them with bread crumbs, and either brown them in the oven or before the fire.
4. Have ready the above quantity of gravy, to which add 1/2 glass of sherry; dish the sweetbreads, pour the gravy under them, and garnish with water-cresses.

title: Apple Cheesecakes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of apple pulp
- 1/4 lb. of sifted sugar
- 1/4 lb. of butter
- 4 eggs
- the rind and juice of 1 lemon
- puff-paste

## Directions

1. Pare, core, and boil sufficient apples to make 1/2 lb. when cooked
2. Add to these the sugar, the butter, which should be melted
3. Add the eggs, leaving out 2 of the whites, and take grated rind and juice of 1 lemon
4. Stir the mixture well
5. Line some patty-pans with puff-paste
6. Put in the mixture
7. Bake about 20 minutes

title: Apple Fritters
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of flour
- 1/2 oz. of butter
- 1/2 saltspoonful of salt
- 2 eggs
- milk
- apples
- hot lard or clarified beef-dripping
- pounded sugar

## Directions

1. Break the eggs and separate the whites from the yolks; beat them separately
2. Put the flour into a basin and stir in the melted butter
3. Add the salt and moisten with sufficient warm milk to make a batter that will drop from the spoon
4. Stir well and rub down any lumps
5. Add the whisked whites of eggs and beat up the batter for a few minutes
6. Peel and cut the apples into rather thick whole slices without dividing them
7. Stamp out the middle of each slice where the core is with a cutter
8. Throw the slices into the batter
9. Take out the pieces of apple one by one and fry them in hot lard until nice brown, turning when required
10. Lay them on blotting-paper before the fire to absorb the greasy moisture
11. Dish on a white d'oyley piled one above the other
12. Strew over them some pounded sugar and serve very hot

title: Apple Ginger
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of any kind of hard apples
- 2 lbs. of loaf sugar
- 1-1/2 pint of water
- 1 oz. of tincture of ginger

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar and water until they form a rich syrup, adding the ginger when it boils up.
2. Pare, core, and cut the apples into pieces.
3. Dip them in cold water to preserve the colour, and boil them in the syrup until transparent; but be careful not to let them break.
4. Put the pieces of apple into jars, pour over the syrup, and carefully exclude the air, by well covering them.
5. Keep in a dry place.

title: Apple Jam
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- apples, pared, cored, and sliced
- 3/4 lb. of preserving-sugar per lb. of fruit
- grated rind of 1 lemon
- juice of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Peel the apples, core and slice them very thin, and be particular that they are all the same sort.
2. Put them into a jar, stand this in a saucepan of boiling water, and let the apples stew until quite tender.
3. Weigh the fruit, to ascertain the proportion of sugar that may be required.
4. Put the apples into a preserving-pan, crush the sugar to small lumps, and add it, with the grated lemon-rind and juice, to the apples.
5. Simmer these over the fire for 1/2 hour, reckoning from the time the jam begins to simmer properly; remove the scum as it rises.
6. When the jam is done, put it into pots for use.
7. Place a piece of oiled paper over the jam, and to exclude the air, cover the pots with tissue-paper dipped in the white of an egg, and stretched over the top.

title: Apple Jelly I
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 lbs. of apples
- 3 pints of water
- 2 lbs. of loaf sugar per quart of juice
- juice of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Pare, core, and cut the apples into slices, and put them into a jar, with water in the above proportion.
2. Place them in a cool oven, with the jar well covered, and when the juice is thoroughly drawn and the apples are quite soft, strain them through a jelly-bag.
3. To every quart of juice allow 2 lbs. of loaf sugar, which should be crushed to small lumps, and put into a preserving-pan with the juice.
4. Boil these together for rather more than 1/2 hour, remove the scum as it rises.
5. Add the lemon-juice just before it is done, and put the jelly into pots for use.

title: Apple Jelly II
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- water
- 3/4 lb. of loaf sugar per pint of syrup

## Directions

1. Pare and cut the apples into pieces, remove the cores, and put them in a preserving-pan with sufficient cold water to cover them.
2. Let them boil for an hour; then drain the syrup from them through a hair sieve or jelly-bag, and measure the juice.
3. To every pint allow 3/4 lb. of loaf sugar, and boil these together for 3/4 hour, removing every particle of scum as it rises, and keeping the jelly well stirred, that it may not burn.
4. A little lemon-rind may be boiled with the apples, and a small quantity of strained lemon-juice may be put in the jelly just before it is done, when the flavour is liked.
5. Put it into small pots for storage.

title: Apple Sauce for Geese, Pork, &c.
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 good-sized apples
- sifted sugar to taste
- a piece of butter the size of a walnut
- water

## Directions

1. Pare, core, and quarter the apples, and throw them into cold water to preserve their whiteness.
2. Put them in a saucepan, with sufficient water to moisten them, and boil till soft enough to pulp.
3. Beat them up, adding sugar to taste, and a small piece of butter.

title: Apple Snow
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 good-sized apples
- the whites of 10 eggs
- the rind of 1 lemon
- 1/2 lb. of pounded sugar

## Directions

1. Peel, core, and cut the apples into quarters, and put them into a saucepan with the lemon-peel and sufficient water to prevent them from burning,--rather less than 1/2 pint
2. When they are tender, take out the peel, beat them to a pulp, let them cool, and stir them to the whites of the eggs, which should be previously beaten to a strong froth
3. Add the sifted sugar, and continue the whisking until the mixture becomes quite stiff
4. Either heap it on a glass dish, or serve it in small glasses
5. The dish may be garnished with preserved barberries, or strips of bright-coloured jelly
6. Serve with a dish of custards or a jug of cream

title: Apple Snowballs
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 teacupfuls of rice
- apples
- moist sugar
- cloves

## Directions

1. Boil the rice in milk until three-parts done; then strain it off.
2. Pare and core the apples without dividing them.
3. Put a small quantity of sugar and a clove into each apple.
4. Put the rice round them, and tie each ball separately in a cloth.
5. Boil until the apples are tender; then take them up, remove the cloths, and serve.

title: Apple Soup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of good boiling apples
- 3/4 teaspoonful of white pepper
- 6 cloves
- cayenne or ginger to taste
- 3 quarts of medium stock

## Directions

1. Peel and quarter the apples, taking out their cores
2. Put them into the stock, stew them gently till tender
3. Rub the whole through a strainer, add the seasoning, give it one boil up, and serve

title: Apple Tart Or Pie
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff-paste No. 1205 or 1206
- apples
- 2 oz. of moist sugar per lb. of unpared apples
- 1/2 teaspoonful of finely-minced lemon-peel
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon-juice
- 2 or 3 tablespoonfuls of water

## Directions

1. Make 1/2 lb. of puff-paste by either of the above-named recipes
2. Place a border of it round the edge of a pie-dish
3. Fill it with apples pared, cored, and cut into slices
4. Sweeten with moist sugar
5. Add the lemon-peel and juice, and 2 or 3 tablespoonfuls of water
6. Cover with crust
7. Cut it evenly round close to the edge of the pie-dish
8. Bake in a hot oven from 1/2 to 3/4 hour, or rather longer, should the pie be very large
9. When it is three-parts done, take it out of the oven
10. Put the white of an egg on a plate, and with the blade of a knife, whisk it to a froth
11. Brush the pie over with this
12. Sprinkle upon it some sifted sugar, and then a few drops of water
13. Put the pie back into the oven, and finish baking
14. Be particularly careful that it does not catch or burn

title: Apple Tourte or Cake
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 or 12 apples
- sugar to taste
- the rind of 1 small lemon
- 3 eggs
- 1/4 pint of cream or milk
- 1/4 lb. of butter
- 3/4 lb. of good short crust No. 1211
- 3 oz. of sweet almonds

## Directions

1. Pare, core, and cut the apples into small pieces; put sufficient moist sugar to sweeten them into a basin.
2. Add the lemon-peel, which should be finely minced, and the cream.
3. Stir these ingredients well, whisk the eggs, and melt the butter.
4. Mix altogether, add the sliced apple, and let these be well stirred into the mixture.
5. Line a large round plate with the paste, place a narrow rim of the same round the outer edge, and lay the apples thickly in the middle.
6. Blanch the almonds, cut them into long shreds, and strew over the top of the apples.
7. Bake from 1/2 to 3/4 hour, taking care that the almonds do not get burnt.
8. When done, strew some sifted sugar over the top, and serve.

title: Apple Trifle
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 good-sized apples
- the rind of 1/2 lemon
- 6 oz. of pounded sugar
- 1/2 pint of milk
- 1/2 pint of cream
- 2 eggs
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Peel, core, and cut the apples into thin slices, and put them into a saucepan with 2 tablespoonfuls of water, the sugar, and minced lemon-rind
2. Boil all together until quite tender, and pulp the apples through a sieve
3. If they should not be quite sweet enough, add a little more sugar, and put them at the bottom of the dish to form a thick layer
4. Stir together the milk, cream, and eggs, with a little sugar, over the fire, and let the mixture thicken, but do not allow it to reach the boiling-point
5. When thick, take it off the fire
6. Let it cool a little, then pour it over the apples
7. Whip some cream with sugar, lemon-peel, &c., the same as for other trifles
8. Heap it high over the custard, and the dish is ready for table
9. It may be garnished as fancy dictates, with strips of bright apple jelly, slices of citron, &c.

title: Apples A La Portugaise
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 good boiling apples
- 1/2 pint of water
- 6 oz. of sugar
- a layer of apple marmalade
- 8 preserved cherries
- garnishing of apricot jam

## Directions

1. Peel the apples, and, with a vegetable-cutter, push out the cores
2. Boil them in the above proportion of sugar and water, without being too much done, and take care they do not break
3. Have ready a white apple marmalade
4. Cover the bottom of the dish with this, level it, and lay the apples in a sieve to drain
5. Pile them neatly on the marmalade, making them high in the centre, and place a preserved cherry in the middle of each
6. Garnish with strips of candied citron or apricot jam, and the dish is ready for table

title: Apples and Rice
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 good sized apples
- 3 oz. of butter
- the rind of 1/2 lemon minced very fine
- 6 oz. of rice
- 1-1/2 pint of milk
- sugar to taste
- 1/2 teaspoonful of grated nutmeg
- 6 tablespoonfuls of apricot jam

## Directions

1. Peel the apples, halve them, and take out the cores
2. Put them into a stewpan with the butter, and strew sufficient sifted sugar over to sweeten them nicely, and add the minced lemon-peel
3. Stew the apples very gently until tender, taking care they do not break
4. Boil the rice, with the milk, sugar, and nutmeg, until soft
5. When thoroughly done, dish it, piled high in the centre
6. Arrange the apples on it, warm the apricot jam, pour it over the whole, and serve hot

title: Apples in Red Jelly
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 good-sized apples
- 12 cloves
- pounded sugar
- 1 lemon
- 2 teacupfuls of water
- 1 tablespoonful of gelatine
- a few drops of prepared cochineal

## Directions

1. Choose rather large apples
2. Peel them and take out the cores, either with a scoop or a small silver knife, and put into each apple 2 cloves and as much sifted sugar as they will hold
3. Place them, without touching each other, in a large pie-dish
4. Add more white sugar, the juice of 1 lemon, and 2 teacupfuls of water
5. Bake in the oven, with a dish over them, until they are done
6. Look at them frequently, and, as each apple is cooked, place it in a glass dish
7. When the apples are neatly arranged in the dish without touching each other, strain the liquor in which they have been stewing, into a lined saucepan
8. Add to it the rind of the lemon, and a tablespoonful of gelatine which has been previously dissolved in cold water, and, if not sweet, a little more sugar, and 6 cloves
9. Boil till quite clear
10. Colour with a few drops of prepared cochineal, and strain the jelly through a double muslin into a jug
11. Let it cool a little
12. Pour it into the dish round the apples
13. When quite cold, garnish the tops of the apples with a bright-coloured marmalade, a jelly, or the white of an egg, beaten to a strong froth, with a little sifted sugar

title: Apricot Cream
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 to 16 ripe apricots
- 1/4 lb. of sugar
- 1-1/2 pint of milk
- the yolks of 8 eggs
- 1 oz. of isinglass

## Directions

1. Divide the apricots, take out the stones, and boil them in a syrup made with 1/4 lb. of sugar and 1/4 pint of water, until they form a thin marmalade, which rub through a sieve
2. Boil the milk with the other 1/4 lb. of sugar
3. Let it cool a little, then mix with it the yolks of eggs which have been previously well beaten
4. Put this mixture into a jug, place this jug in boiling water, and stir it one way over the fire until it thickens; but on no account let it boil
5. Strain through a sieve
6. Add the isinglass, previously boiled with a small quantity of water, and keep stirring it till nearly cold
7. Mix the cream with the apricots
8. Stir well, put it into an oiled mould, and, if convenient, set it on ice; at any rate, in a very cool place
9. It should turn out on the dish without any difficulty

title: Apricot Jam or Marmalade
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe apricots, weighed after being skinned and stoned
- Sugar, 1 lb. to every lb. of apricots

## Directions

1. Pare the apricots, which should be ripe, as thinly as possible, break them in half, and remove the stones.
2. Weigh the fruit, and to every lb. allow the same proportion of loaf sugar.
3. Pound the sugar very finely in a mortar, strew it over the apricots, which should be placed on dishes, and let them remain for 12 hours.
4. Break the stones, blanch the kernels, and put them with the sugar and fruit into a preserving-pan.
5. Let these simmer very gently until clear; take out the pieces of apricot singly as they become so, and, as fast as the scum rises, carefully remove it.
6. Put the apricots into small jars, pour over them the syrup and kernels, cover the jam with pieces of paper dipped in the purest salad-oil, and stretch over the top of the jars tissue-paper, cut about 2 inches larger and brushed over with the white of an egg: when dry, it will be perfectly hard and air-tight.

title: Apricot Tart
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 or 14 apricots
- sugar to taste
- puff-paste or short crust

## Directions

1. Break the apricots in half, take out the stones, and put them into a pie-dish.
2. In the centre of the dish place a very small cup or jar, bottom uppermost.
3. Sweeten with good moist sugar, but add no water.
4. Line the edge of the dish with paste, put on the cover, and ornament the pie in any of the usual modes.
5. Bake from 1/2 to 3/4 hour, according to size.
6. If puff-paste is used, glaze it about 10 minutes before the pie is done, and put it into the oven again to set the glaze.
7. Short crust merely requires a little sifted sugar sprinkled over it before being sent to table.

title: Arrowroot Biscuits Or Drops
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 6 eggs
- 1/2 lb. of flour
- 6 oz. of arrowroot
- 1/2 lb. of pounded loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Beat the butter to a cream.
2. Whisk the eggs to a strong froth, add them to the butter.
3. Stir in the flour a little at a time, and beat the mixture well.
4. Break down all the lumps from the arrowroot, and add that with the sugar to the other ingredients.
5. Mix all well together.
6. Drop the dough on a buttered tin, in pieces the size of a shilling.
7. Bake the biscuits about 1/4 hour in a slow oven.

title: Arrowroot Blanc-Mange
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 heaped tablespoonfuls of arrowroot
- 1-1/2 pint of milk
- 3 laurel-leaves or the rind of 1/2 lemon
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Mix to a smooth batter the arrowroot with 1/2 pint of the milk
2. Put the other pint on the fire, with laurel-leaves or lemon-peel, whichever may be preferred, and let the milk steep until it is well flavoured
3. Strain the milk, and add it, boiling, to the mixed arrowroot
4. Sweeten it with sifted sugar, and let it boil, stirring it all the time, till it thickens sufficiently to come from the saucepan
5. Grease a mould with pure salad-oil
6. Pour in the blanc-mange, and when quite set, turn it out on a dish, and pour round it a compôte of any kind of fruit, or garnish it with jam
7. A tablespoonful of brandy, stirred in just before the blanc-mange is moulded, very much improves the flavour of this sweet dish

title: Arrowroot Sauce for Puddings
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 small teaspoonfuls of arrowroot
- 4 dessert-spoonfuls of pounded sugar
- the juice of 1 lemon
- 1/4 teaspoonful of grated nutmeg
- 1/2 pint of water

## Directions

1. Mix the arrowroot smoothly with the water; put this into a stewpan; add the sugar, strained lemon-juice, and grated nutmeg.
2. Stir these ingredients over the fire until they boil, when the sauce is ready for use.

title: Artichoke (Jerusalem) Soup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 slices of lean bacon or ham
- 1/2 a head of celery
- 1 turnip
- 1 onion
- 3 oz. of butter
- 4 lbs. of artichokes
- 1 pint of boiling milk, or 1/2 pint of boiling cream
- salt and cayenne to taste
- 2 lumps of sugar
- 2-1/2 quarts of white stock

## Directions

1. Put the bacon and vegetables, which should be cut into thin slices, into the stewpan with the butter
2. Braise these for 1/4 of an hour, keeping them well stirred
3. Wash and pare the artichokes, and after cutting them into thin slices, add them, with a pint of stock, to the other ingredients
4. When these have gently stewed down to a smooth pulp, put in the remainder of the stock
5. Stir it well, adding the seasoning, and when it has simmered for five minutes, pass it through a strainer
6. Pour it back into the stewpan, let it again simmer five minutes, taking care to skim it well, and stir it to the boiling milk or cream
7. Serve with small sippets of bread fried in butter

title: Artichokes à l'Italienne
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 6 artichokes
- salt and butter
- about 1/2 pint of good gravy
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Trim and cut the artichokes into quarters, and boil them until tender in water mixed with a little salt and butter.
2. When done, drain them well, and lay them all round the dish, with the leaves outside.
3. Have ready some good gravy, highly flavoured with mushrooms; reduce it until quite thick, and pour it round the artichokes, and serve.

title: Asparagus Peas
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 heads of asparagus
- 2 oz. of butter
- a small bunch of parsley
- 2 or 3 green onions
- flour
- 1 lump of sugar
- the yolks of 2 eggs
- 4 tablespoonfuls of cream
- salt

## Directions

1. Carefully scrape the asparagus
2. Cut it into pieces of an equal size, avoiding that which is in the least hard or tough
3. Throw them into cold water
4. Boil the asparagus in salt and water until three-parts done
5. Take it out, drain, and place it on a cloth to dry the moisture away from it
6. Put it into a stewpan with the butter, parsley, and onions
7. Shake over a brisk fire for 10 minutes
8. Dredge in a little flour
9. Add the sugar
10. Moisten with boiling water
11. When boiled a short time and reduced, take out the parsley and onions
12. Thicken with the yolks of 2 eggs beaten with the cream
13. Add a seasoning of salt
14. When the whole is on the point of simmering, serve
15. Make the sauce sufficiently thick to adhere to the vegetable

title: Asparagus Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of asparagus peas
- 4 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 1 tablespoonful of very finely minced ham
- 1 oz. of butter
- pepper and salt to taste
- milk

## Directions

1. Cut up the nice green tender parts of asparagus, about the size of peas
2. Put them into a basin with the eggs, which should be well beaten, and the flour, ham, butter, pepper, and salt
3. Mix all these ingredients well together
4. Moisten with sufficient milk to make the pudding of the consistency of thick batter
5. Put it into a pint buttered mould
6. Tie it down tightly with a floured cloth
7. Place it in boiling water
8. Let it boil for 2 hours
9. Turn it out of the mould on to a hot dish
10. Pour plain melted butter round, but not over, the pudding

title: Asparagus Sauce
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bunch of green asparagus
- salt
- 1 oz. of fresh butter
- 1 small bunch of parsley
- 3 or 4 green onions
- 1 large lump of sugar
- 4 tablespoonfuls of sauce tournée

## Directions

1. Break the asparagus in the tender part, wash well, and put them into boiling salt and water to render them green.
2. When they are tender, take them out, and put them into cold water; drain them on a cloth till all moisture is absorbed from them.
3. Put the butter in a stewpan, with the parsley and onions; lay in the asparagus, and fry the whole over a sharp fire for 5 minutes.
4. Add salt, the sugar and sauce tournée, and simmer for another 5 minutes.
5. Rub all through a tammy, and if not a very good colour, use a little spinach green.

title: Asparagus Soup I
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 lbs. of lean beef
- 3 slices of bacon
- 1/2 pint of pale ale
- a few leaves of white beet
- spinach
- 1 cabbage lettuce
- a little mint, sorrel, and marjoram
- a pint of asparagus-tops cut small
- the crust of 1 French roll
- seasoning to taste
- 2 quarts of water

## Directions

1. Put the beef, cut in pieces and rolled in flour, into a stewpan, with the bacon at the bottom
2. Cover it close, and set it on a slow fire, stirring it now and then till the gravy is drawn
3. Put in the water and ale, and season to taste with pepper and salt, and let it stew gently for 2 hours
4. Strain the liquor, and take off the fat, and add the white beet, spinach, cabbage lettuce, and mint, sorrel, and sweet marjoram, pounded
5. Let these boil up in the liquor, then put in the asparagus-tops cut small, and allow them to boil till all is tender
6. Serve hot, with the French roll in the dish

title: Asparagus Soup II
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 pint of split peas
- a teacupful of gravy
- 4 young onions
- 1 lettuce cut small
- 1/2 a head of celery
- 1/2 a pint of asparagus cut small
- 1/2 a pint of cream
- 3 quarts of water
- spinach juice for colouring

## Directions

1. Boil the peas, and rub them through a sieve
2. Add the gravy, and then stew by themselves the celery, onions, lettuce, and asparagus, with the water
3. Stew altogether, and add the colouring and cream, and serve

title: Aspic, or Ornamental Savoury Jelly
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 lbs. of knuckle of veal
- 1 cow-heel
- 3 or 4 slices of ham
- any poultry trimmings
- 2 carrots
- 1 onion
- 1 faggot of savoury herbs
- 1 glass of sherry
- 3 quarts of water
- salt
- whole white pepper
- 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Lay the ham on the bottom of a stewpan, cut up the veal and cow-heel into small pieces, and lay them on the ham; add the poultry trimmings, vegetables, herbs, sherry, and water, and let the whole simmer very gently for 4 hours, carefully taking away all scum that may rise to the surface.
2. Strain through a fine sieve, and pour into an earthen pan to get cold.
3. Have ready a clean stewpan, put in the jelly, and be particular to leave the sediment behind, or it will not be clear.
4. Add the whites of 3 eggs, with salt and pepper, to clarify; keep stirring over the fire, till the whole becomes very white; then draw it to the side, and let it stand till clear.
5. When this is the case, strain it through a cloth or jelly-bag, and use it for moulding poultry, etc. Tarragon vinegar may be added to give an additional flavour.

title: Aunt Nelly's Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of flour
- 1/2 lb. of treacle
- 1/2 lb. of suet
- the rind and juice of 1 lemon
- a few strips of candied lemon-peel
- 3 tablespoonfuls of cream
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Chop the suet finely
2. Mix with it the flour, treacle, lemon-peel minced, and candied lemon-peel
3. Add the cream, lemon-juice, and 2 well-beaten eggs
4. Beat the pudding well, put it into a buttered basin, tie it down with a cloth, and boil from 3-1/2 to 4 hours

title: Bachelor's Omelet
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 eggs
- 2 oz. of butter
- 1 teaspoonful of flour
- 1/2 teacupful of milk
- a pinch of salt
- a few grains of cayenne
- chopped herbs
- a few shreds of onion

## Directions

1. Make a thin cream of the flour and milk
2. Beat up the eggs, mix all together, and add a pinch of salt and a few grains of cayenne
3. Melt the butter in a small frying-pan, and, when very hot, pour in the batter
4. Let the pan remain for a few minutes over a clear fire
5. Sprinkle upon the omelet some chopped herbs and a few shreds of onion
6. Double the omelet dexterously, and shake it out of the pan on to a hot dish

title: Baked Almond Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of almonds
- 4 bitter almonds
- 1 glass of sherry
- 4 eggs
- the rind and juice of 1/2 lemon
- 3 oz. of butter
- 1 pint of cream
- 2 tablespoonfuls of sugar
- puff-paste for lining pie-dish

## Directions

1. Blanch and pound the almonds to a smooth paste with the water
2. Mix these with the butter, which should be melted
3. Beat up the eggs, grate the lemon-rind, and strain the juice
4. Add these, with the cream, sugar, and wine, to the other ingredients, and stir them well together
5. When well mixed, put it into a pie-dish lined with puff-paste, and bake for 1/2 hour

title: Baked Apple Custard
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dozen large apples
- moist sugar to taste
- 1 small teacupful of cold water
- the grated rind of one lemon
- 1 pint of milk
- 4 eggs
- 2 oz. of loaf sugar
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Peel, cut, and core the apples
2. Put them into a lined saucepan with the cold water
3. As they heat, bruise them to a pulp
4. Sweeten with moist sugar and add the grated lemon-rind
5. When cold, put the fruit at the bottom of a pie-dish
6. Pour over it a custard made with the above proportion of milk, eggs, and sugar
7. Grate a little nutmeg over the top
8. Place the dish in a moderate oven and bake from 25 to 35 minutes

title: Baked Apple Dumplings
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 apples
- 3/4 lb. of suet-crust
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Pare and take out the cores of the apples without dividing them
2. Make 1/2 lb. of suet-crust by recipe No. 1215
3. Roll the apples in the crust, previously sweetening them with moist sugar, and taking care to join the paste nicely
4. When they are formed into round balls, put them on a tin, and bake them for about 1/2 hour, or longer should the apples be very large
5. Arrange them pyramidically on a dish, and sift over them some pounded white sugar

title: Baked Apple Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 moderate-sized apples
- 2 tablespoonfuls of finely-chopped suet
- 3 eggs
- 3 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 1 pint of milk
- a little grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Mix the flour to a smooth batter with the milk
2. Add the eggs, which should be well whisked
3. Put this batter into a well-buttered pie-dish
4. Wipe the apples clean, but do not pare them
5. Cut them in halves, and take out the cores
6. Lay them in the batter, rind uppermost
7. Shake the suet on the top, over which also grate a little nutmeg
8. Bake in a moderate oven for an hour
9. Cover, when served, with sifted loaf sugar

title: Baked Apricot Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 large apricots
- 3/4 pint of bread crumbs
- 1 pint of milk
- 3 oz. of pounded sugar
- the yolks of 4 eggs
- 1 glass of sherry

## Directions

1. Make the milk boiling hot, and pour it on to the bread crumbs.
2. When half cold, add the sugar, the well-whisked yolks of the eggs, and the sherry.
3. Divide the apricots in half, scald them until they are soft, and break them up with a spoon.
4. Add a few of the kernels, which should be well pounded in a mortar.
5. Mix the fruit and other ingredients together, put a border of paste round the dish, fill with the mixture, and bake the pudding from 1/2 to 3/4 hour.

title: Baked Batter Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/4 pint of milk
- 4 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 2 oz. of butter
- 4 eggs
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Mix the flour with a small quantity of cold milk
2. make the remainder hot, and pour it on to the flour, keeping the mixture well stirred
3. add the butter, eggs, and salt
4. beat the whole well, and put the pudding into a buttered pie-dish
5. bake for 3/4 hour, and serve with sweet sauce, wine sauce, or stewed fruit

title: Baked Batter Pudding with Dried or Fresh Fruit
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/4 pint of milk
- 4 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 3 eggs
- 2 oz. of finely-shredded suet
- 1/4 lb. of currants
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Mix the milk, flour, and eggs to a smooth batter
2. add a little salt, the suet, and the currants, which should be well washed, picked, and dried
3. put the mixture into a buttered pie-dish, and bake in a moderate oven for 1-1/4 hour
4. When fresh fruits are in season, the pudding may be made with damsons, plums, red currants, gooseberries, or apples
5. when made with these, the pudding must be thickly sprinkled over with sifted sugar

title: Baked Beef (Cold Meat Cookery) I
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- About 2 lbs. of cold roast beef
- 2 small onions
- 1 large carrot or two small ones
- 1 turnip
- a small bunch of savoury herbs
- salt and pepper to taste
- 4 tablespoonfuls of gravy
- 3 tablespoonfuls of ale
- crust or mashed potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut the beef in slices, allowing a small amount of fat to each slice
2. place a layer of this in the bottom of a pie-dish, with a portion of the onions, carrots, and turnips, which must be sliced
3. mince the herbs, strew them over the meat, and season with pepper and salt
4. Then put another layer of meat, vegetables, and seasoning; and proceed in this manner until all the ingredients are used
5. Pour in the gravy and ale (water may be substituted for the former, but it is not so nice)
6. cover with a crust or mashed potatoes, and bake for 1/2 hour, or rather longer

title: Baked Beef (Cold Meat Cookery) II
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Slices of cold roast beef
- salt and pepper to taste
- 1 sliced onion
- 1 teaspoonful of minced savoury herbs
- 5 or 6 tablespoonfuls of gravy or sauce of any kind
- mashed potatoes

## Directions

1. Butter the sides of a deep dish, and spread mashed potatoes over the bottom of it
2. on this place layers of beef in thin slices (this may be minced if there is not sufficient beef to cut into slices), well seasoned with pepper and salt, and a very little onion and herbs, which should be previously fried of a nice brown
3. then put another layer of mashed potatoes, and beef, and other ingredients, as before
4. pour in the gravy or sauce, cover the whole with another layer of potatoes, and bake for 1/2 hour
5. This may be served in the dish, or turned out

title: Baked Beef Tea
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of fleshy beef
- 1-1/2 pint of water
- 1/4 saltspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Cut the beef into small square pieces, after trimming off all the fat, and put it into a baking-jar, with the above proportion of water and salt
2. Cover the jar well, place it in a warm, but not hot oven, and bake for 3 or 4 hours
3. When the oven is very fierce in the daytime, it is a good plan to put the jar in at night, and let it remain till the next morning, when the tea will be done
4. Strain it, and put by in a cool place until wanted
5. It may also be flavoured with an onion, a clove, and a few sweet herbs, &c., when the stomach is sufficiently strong to take those

title: Baked Beef-Steak Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 oz. of flour
- 2 eggs
- not quite 1 pint of milk
- salt to taste
- 1-1/2 lb. of rump-steaks
- 1 kidney
- pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Cut the steaks into nice square pieces, with a small quantity of fat, and the kidney divide into small pieces.
2. Make a batter of flour, eggs, and milk in the above proportion; lay a little of it at the bottom of a pie-dish.
3. Put in the steaks and kidney, which should be well seasoned with pepper and salt, and pour over the remainder of the batter.
4. Bake for 1-1/2 hour in a brisk but not fierce oven.

title: Baked Bread Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of grated bread
- 1 pint of milk
- 4 eggs
- 4 oz. of butter
- 4 oz. of moist sugar
- 2 oz. of candied peel
- 6 bitter almonds
- 1 tablespoonful of brandy

## Directions

1. Put the milk into a stewpan, with the bitter almonds
2. let it infuse for 1/4 hour
3. bring it to the boiling point
4. strain it on to the bread crumbs, and let these remain till cold
5. then add the eggs, which should be well whisked, the butter, sugar, and brandy, and beat the pudding well until all the ingredients are thoroughly mixed
6. line the bottom of a pie-dish with the candied peel sliced thin, put in the mixture, and bake for nearly 3/4 hour

title: Baked Bread-and-Butter Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 thin slices of bread and butter
- 1-1/2 pint of milk
- 4 eggs
- sugar to taste
- 1/4 lb. of currants
- flavouring of vanilla, grated lemon-peel or nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cut 9 slices of bread and butter not very thick, and put them into a pie-dish, with currants between each layer and on the top
2. Sweeten and flavour the milk, either by infusing a little lemon-peel in it, or by adding a few drops of essence of vanilla
3. Well whisk the eggs, and stir these to the milk
4. Strain this over the bread and butter, and bake in a moderate oven for 1 hour, or rather longer
5. This pudding may be very much enriched by adding cream, candied peel, or more eggs than stated above
6. It should not be turned out, but sent to table in the pie-dish, and is better for being made about 2 hours before it is baked

title: Baked Carp
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 carp
- forcemeat
- bread crumbs
- 1 oz. butter
- 1/2 pint of stock No. 105
- 1/2 pint of port wine
- 6 anchovies
- 2 onions sliced
- 1 bay-leaf
- a faggot of sweet herbs
- flour to thicken
- the juice of 1 lemon
- cayenne and salt to taste
- 1/2 teaspoonful of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Stuff the carp with a delicate forcemeat, after thoroughly cleansing it, and sew it up to prevent the stuffing from falling out.
2. Rub it over with an egg, and sprinkle it with bread crumbs, lay it in a deep earthen dish, and drop the butter, oiled, over the bread crumbs.
3. Add the stock, onions, bay-leaf, herbs, wine, and anchovies, and bake for 1 hour.
4. Put 1 oz. of butter into a stewpan, melt it, and dredge in sufficient flour to dry it up; put in the strained liquor from the carp, stir frequently, and when it has boiled, add the lemon-juice and seasoning.
5. Serve the carp on a dish garnished with parsley and cut lemon, and the sauce in a boat.

title: Baked Custard Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 pint of milk
- the rind of 1/4 lemon
- 1/4 lb. of moist sugar
- 4 eggs
- nutmeg
- paste

## Directions

1. Put the milk into a saucepan with the sugar and lemon-rind, and let this infuse for about 1/4 hour, or until the milk is well flavoured
2. Whisk the eggs, yolks and whites
3. Pour the milk to them, stirring all the while
4. Have ready a pie-dish, lined at the edge with paste ready baked
5. Strain the custard into the dish, grate a little nutmeg over the top, and bake in a very slow oven for about 1/2 hour, or rather longer

title: Baked Damsons for Winter Use
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Damsons
- 6 oz. of pounded sugar per lb. of fruit
- melted mutton suet

## Directions
