2. Stone the cherries, and boil them in a preserving-pan until nearly all the juice is dried up; then add the sugar, which should be

title: Cherry Jam
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of fruit, weighed before stoning
- 1/2 lb. of sugar per lb. of fruit
- 1 pint of red-currant juice per 6 lbs. of fruit
- 1 lb. of sugar per pint of juice

## Directions

1. Weigh the fruit before stoning, and allow half the weight of sugar
2. Stone the cherries, and boil them in a preserving-pan until nearly all the juice is dried up
3. Add the sugar, which should be crushed to powder, and the currant-juice, allowing 1 pint to every 6 lbs. of cherries (original weight), and 1 lb. of sugar to every pint of juice
4. Boil all together until it jellies, which will be in from 20 minutes to 1/2 hour
5. Skim the jam well, keep it well stirred
6. A few minutes before it is done, crack some of the stones, and add the kernels

title: Cherry Sauce for Sweet Puddings
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of cherries
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 1 oz. of butter
- 1/2 pint of water
- 1 wineglassful of port wine
- a little grated lemon-rind
- 4 pounded cloves
- 2 tablespoonfuls of lemon-juice
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Stone the cherries, and pound the kernels in a mortar to a smooth paste; put the butter and flour into a saucepan; stir them over the fire until of a pale brown; then add the cherries, the pounded kernels, the wine, and the water.
2. Simmer these gently for 1/4 hour, or until the cherries are quite cooked, and rub the whole through a hair sieve; add the remaining ingredients, let the sauce boil for another 5 minutes, and serve.

title: Cherry Tart
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 lb. of cherries
- 2 small tablespoonfuls of moist sugar
- 1/2 lb. of short crust

## Directions

1. Pick the stalks from the cherries, put them, with the sugar, into a deep pie-dish just capable of holding them, with a small cup placed upside down in the midst of them
2. Make a short crust with 1/2 lb. of flour
3. Lay a border round the edge of the dish; put on the cover, and ornament the edges
4. Bake in a brisk oven from 1/2 hour to 40 minutes
5. Strew finely-sifted sugar over, and serve hot or cold

title: Chestnut (Spanish) Soup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 lb. of Spanish chestnuts
- 1/4 pint of cream
- salt to taste
- cayenne to taste
- mace to taste
- 1 quart of stock No. 105

## Directions

1. Take the outer rind from the chestnuts, and put them into a large pan of warm water.
2. As soon as this becomes too hot for the fingers to remain in it, take out the chestnuts, peel them quickly, and immerse them in cold water, and wipe and weigh them.
3. Now cover them with good stock, and stew them gently for rather more than 3/4 of an hour, or until they break when touched with a fork; then drain, pound, and rub them through a fine sieve reversed.
4. Add sufficient stock, mace, cayenne, and salt, and stir it often until it boils, and put in the cream.

title: Chestnut Sauce for Fowls or Turkey
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of chestnuts
- 1/2 pint of white stock
- 2 strips of lemon-peel
- Cayenne to taste
- 1/4 pint of cream or milk

## Directions

1. Peel off the outside skin of the chestnuts, and put them into boiling water for a few minutes.
2. Take off the thin inside peel, and put them into a saucepan, with the white stock and lemon-peel, and let them simmer for 1-1/2 hour, or until the chestnuts are quite tender.
3. Rub the whole through a hair-sieve with a wooden spoon.
4. Add seasoning and the cream.
5. Let it just simmer, but not boil, and keep stirring all the time.
6. Serve very hot and quickly.

title: Chicken Broth
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 fowl, or the inferior joints of a whole one
- 1 quart of water
- 1 blade of mace
- 1/2 onion
- a small bunch of sweet herbs
- salt to taste
- 10 peppercorns

## Directions

1. Put the fowl into a saucepan, with all the ingredients, and simmer gently for 1-1/2 hour, carefully skimming the broth well
2. When done, strain, and put by in a cool place until wanted
3. Then take all the fat off the top, warm up as much as may be required, and serve

title: Chicken Cutlets (an Entrée)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 chickens
- salt to taste
- white pepper to taste
- cayenne to taste
- 2 blades of pounded mace
- egg
- bread crumbs
- clarified butter
- 1 strip of lemon-rind
- 2 carrots
- 1 onion
- 2 tablespoonfuls of mushroom ketchup
- butter and flour for thickening
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Remove the breast and leg bones of the chickens; cut the meat into neat pieces after having skinned it, and season the cutlets with pepper, salt, pounded mace, and cayenne.
2. Put the bones, trimmings, &c., into a stewpan with 1 pint of water, adding carrots, onions, and lemon-peel in the above proportion; stew gently for 1-1/2 hour, and strain the gravy.
3. Thicken it with butter and flour, add the ketchup and 1 egg well beaten; stir it over the fire, and bring it to the simmering-point, but do not allow it to boil.
4. In the mean time, egg and bread-crumb the cutlets, and give them a few drops of clarified butter; fry them a delicate brown, occasionally turning them.
5. Arrange them pyramidically on the dish, and pour over them the sauce.

title: Chicken or Fowl Patties
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- remains of cold roast chicken or fowl
- 2 oz. of ham per 1/4 lb. of meat
- 3 tablespoonfuls of cream
- 2 tablespoonfuls of veal gravy
- 1/2 teaspoonful of minced lemon-peel
- cayenne, salt, and pepper to taste
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon-juice
- 1 oz. of butter rolled in flour
- puff paste
- yolk of an egg

## Directions

1. Mince very small the white meat from a cold roast fowl, after removing all the skin
2. Weigh it, and to every 1/4 lb. of meat allow the proportion of minced ham
3. Put these into a stewpan with the remaining ingredients
4. Stir over the fire for 10 minutes or 1/4 hour, taking care that the mixture does not burn
5. Roll out some puff paste about 1/4 inch in thickness
6. Line the patty-pans with this
7. Put upon each a small piece of bread, and cover with another layer of paste
8. Brush over with the yolk of an egg
9. Bake in a brisk oven for about 1/4 hour
10. When done, cut a round piece out of the top, and with a small spoon, take out the bread
11. Be particular in not breaking the outside border of the crust
12. Fill the patties with the mixture

title: Chicken or Fowl Pie
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 small fowls or 1 large one
- white pepper and salt to taste
- 1/2 teaspoonful of grated nutmeg
- 1/2 teaspoonful of pounded mace
- forcemeat No. 417
- a few slices of ham
- 3 hard-boiled eggs
- 1/2 pint of water
- puff crust
- yolk of an egg
- onion
- bunch of savoury herbs
- blade of mace

## Directions

1. Skin and cut up the fowls into joints
2. Put the neck, leg, and backbones in a stewpan with a little water, an onion, a bunch of savoury herbs, and a blade of mace
3. Let these stew for about an hour
4. When done, strain off the liquor for gravy
5. Put a layer of fowl at the bottom of a pie-dish, then a layer of ham, then one of forcemeat and hard-boiled eggs cut in rings
6. Between the layers put a seasoning of pounded mace, nutmeg, pepper, and salt
7. Proceed in this manner until the dish is full
8. Pour in about 1/2 pint of water
9. Border the edge of the dish with puff crust
10. Put on the cover and ornament the top
11. Glaze it by brushing over it the yolk of an egg
12. Bake from 1-1/4 to 1-1/2 hour, should the pie be very large
13. When done, pour in at the top the gravy made from the bones
14. If to be eaten cold and wished particularly nice, the joints of the fowls should be boned and placed in the dish with alternate layers of forcemeat
15. Sausage-meat may be substituted for the forcemeat
16. When the chickens are boned and mixed with sausage-meat, the pie will take about 2 hours to bake
17. Cover with a piece of paper when about half-done to prevent the paste from being dried up or scorched

title: Chicken or Fowl Salad
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast or boiled chicken
- 2 lettuces
- a little endive
- 1 cucumber
- a few slices of boiled beetroot
- salad-dressing No. 506
- hard-boiled eggs for garnish

## Directions

1. Trim neatly the remains of the chicken
2. Wash, dry, and slice the lettuces
3. Place the lettuce in the middle of a dish
4. Put the pieces of fowl on the top
5. Pour the salad-dressing over them
6. Garnish the edge of the salad with hard-boiled eggs cut in rings, sliced cucumber, and boiled beetroot cut in slices
7. Alternatively, rub the yolks through a hair sieve, chop the whites very finely, and arrange on the salad in small bunches, yellow and white alternately
8. Do not make long before it is wanted for table

title: Chili Vinegar
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 fresh red English chilies
- 1 pint of vinegar

## Directions

1. Pound or cut the chilies in half
2. Infuse them in the vinegar for a fortnight, when it will be fit for use

title: China Chilo
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 lb. of leg, loin, or neck of mutton
- 2 onions
- 2 lettuces
- 1 pint of green peas
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 1 teaspoonful of pepper
- 1/4 pint of water
- 1/4 lb. of clarified butter
- a little cayenne (when liked)

## Directions

1. Mince the above quantity of undressed leg, loin, or neck of mutton, adding a little of the fat, also minced
2. Put it into a stewpan with the remaining ingredients
3. Shred the lettuce and onion rather fine
4. Closely cover the stewpan after the ingredients have been well stirred
5. Simmer gently for rather more than 2 hours
6. Serve in a dish with a border of rice round, the same as for curry

title: Chocolate Cream
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 oz. of grated chocolate
- 1/4 lb. of sugar
- 1-1/2 pint of cream
- 1/2 oz. of clarified isinglass
- the yolks of 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs well; put them into a basin with the grated chocolate, the sugar, and 1 pint of the cream
2. Stir these ingredients well together, pour them into a jug, and set this jug in a saucepan of boiling water
3. Stir it one way until the mixture thickens, but do not allow it to boil, or it will curdle
4. Strain the cream through a sieve into a basin
5. Stir in the isinglass and the other 1/2 pint of cream, which should be well whipped
6. Mix all well together, and pour it into a mould which has been previously oiled with the purest salad-oil, and, if at hand, set it in ice until wanted for table

title: Chocolate Souffle
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 3 teaspoonfuls of pounded sugar
- 1 teaspoonful of flour
- 3 oz. of the best chocolate

## Directions

1. Break the eggs, separating the whites from the yolks, and put them into different basins
2. Add to the yolks the sugar, flour, and chocolate, which should be very finely grated, and stir these ingredients for 5 minutes
3. Well whisk the whites of the eggs in the other basin, until they are stiff
4. When firm, mix lightly with the yolks, till the whole forms a smooth and light substance
5. Butter a round cake-tin, put in the mixture, and bake in a moderate oven from 15 to 20 minutes
6. Pin a white napkin round the tin, strew sifted sugar over the top of the soufflé, and send it immediately to table

title: Christmas Cake
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 teacupfuls of flour
- 1 teacupful of melted butter
- 1 teacupful of cream
- 1 teacupful of treacle
- 1 teacupful of moist sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1/2 oz. of powdered ginger
- 1/2 lb. of raisins
- 1 teaspoonful of carbonate of soda
- 1 tablespoonful of vinegar

## Directions

1. Make the butter sufficiently warm to melt it, but do not allow it to oil
2. Put the flour into a basin
3. Add to it the sugar, ginger, and raisins, which should be stoned and cut into small pieces
4. When these dry ingredients are thoroughly mixed, stir in the butter, cream, treacle, and well-whisked eggs, and beat the mixture for a few minutes
5. Dissolve the soda in the vinegar, add it to the dough, and be particular that these latter ingredients are well incorporated with the others
6. Put the cake into a buttered mould or tin, place it in a moderate oven immediately, and bake it from 1-3/4 to 2-1/4 hours

title: Christmas Plum-Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 lb. of raisins
- 1/2 lb. of currants
- 1/2 lb. of mixed peel
- 3/4 lb. of bread crumbs
- 3/4 lb. of suet
- 8 eggs
- 1 wineglassful of brandy

## Directions

1. Stone and cut the raisins in halves, but do not chop them; wash, pick, and dry the currants, and mince the suet finely.
2. Cut the candied peel into thin slices, and grate down the bread into fine crumbs.
3. When all these dry ingredients are prepared, mix them well together; then moisten the mixture with the eggs, which should be well beaten, and the brandy.
4. Stir well, that everything may be very thoroughly blended, and press the pudding into a buttered mould.
5. Tie it down tightly with a floured cloth, and boil for 5 or 6 hours.
6. It may be boiled in a cloth without a mould, and will require the same time allowed for cooking.
7. When the pudding is taken out of the pot, hang it up immediately, and put a plate or saucer underneath to catch the water that may drain from it.
8. The day it is to be eaten, plunge it into boiling water, and keep it boiling for at least 2 hours.
9. Turn it out of the mould, and serve with brandy-sauce.
10. On Christmas-day a sprig of holly is usually placed in the middle of the pudding, and about a wineglassful of brandy poured round it, which, at the moment of serving, is lighted, and the pudding thus brought to table encircled in flame.

title: Christopher North's Sauce for Meat or Game
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 glass of port wine
- 2 tablespoonfuls of Harvey's sauce
- 1 dessertspoonful of mushroom ketchup
- 1 dessertspoonful of pounded white sugar
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon-juice
- 1/4 teaspoonful of cayenne pepper
- 1/4 teaspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Mix all the ingredients thoroughly together
2. Heat the sauce gradually, by placing the vessel in which it is made in a saucepan of boiling water
3. Do not allow it to boil
4. Serve directly it is ready
5. If bottled immediately, it will keep good for a fortnight

title: Claret-Cup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bottle of claret
- 1 bottle of soda-water
- 1/2 lb. of pounded ice
- 4 tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar
- 1/4 teaspoonful of grated nutmeg
- 1 liqueur-glass of Maraschino
- a sprig of green borage

## Directions

1. Put all the ingredients into a silver cup, regulating the proportion of ice by the state of the weather
2. If very warm, use a larger quantity of ice
3. Hand the cup round with a clean napkin

title: Claret-Cup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bottle of claret
- 1 bottle of soda-water
- about 1/2 lb. of pounded ice
- 4 tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar
- 1/4 teaspoonful of grated nutmeg
- 1 liqueur-glass of Maraschino
- a sprig of green borage

## Directions

1. Put all the ingredients into a silver cup, regulating the proportion of ice by the state of the weather: if very warm, a larger quantity would be necessary.
2. Hand the cup round with a clean napkin passed through one of the handles, that the edge of the cup may be wiped after each guest has partaken of the contents thereof.

title: Clear Apple Jelly
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 dozen apples
- 1-1/2 pint of spring-water
- to every pint of juice 1/2 lb. of loaf sugar
- 1/2 oz. of isinglass
- the rind of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Pare, core, and cut the apples into quarters
2. Boil them with the lemon-peel until tender
3. Strain off the apples and run the juice through a jelly-bag
4. Boil the isinglass in 1/2 pint of water
5. Put the strained juice, sugar, and boiled isinglass into a lined saucepan or preserving-pan
6. Boil all together for about 1/4 hour
7. Put the jelly into moulds
8. Serve as a supper-dish with custard or whipped cream round it, optionally adding lemon-juice to improve the flavour

title: Cock-A-Leekie
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- A capon or large fowl (sometimes an old cock, from which the recipe takes its name, is used), which should be trussed as for boiling
- 2 or 3 bunches of fine leeks
- 5 quarts of stock No. 105
- pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Well wash the leeks (and, if old, scald them in boiling water for a few minutes), taking off the roots and part of the heads, and cut them into lengths of about an inch.
2. Put the fowl into the stock, with, at first, one half of the leeks, and allow it to simmer gently.
3. In half an hour add the remaining leeks, and then it may simmer for 3 or 4 hours longer.
4. It should be carefully skimmed, and can be seasoned to taste.
5. In serving, take out the fowl, and carve it neatly, placing the pieces in a tureen, and pouring over them the soup, which should be very thick of leeks.

title: Cocoa
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 teaspoonfuls of prepared cocoa
- 1 breakfast-cup
- boiling milk
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Put the cocoa into a breakfast-cup
2. Pour over it sufficient cold milk to make it into a smooth paste
3. Add equal quantities of boiling milk and boiling water
4. Stir all well together
5. Take care not to allow the milk to get burnt

title: Cocoa-nut Biscuits Or Cakes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 oz. of sifted sugar
- 3 eggs
- 6 oz. of grated cocoa-nut

## Directions

1. Whisk the eggs until they are very light.
2. Add the sugar gradually.
3. Stir in the cocoa-nut.
4. Roll a tablespoonful of the paste at a time in your hands in the form of a pyramid.
5. Place the pyramids on paper, put the paper on tins.
6. Bake the biscuits in rather a cool oven until they are just coloured a light brown.

title: Cocoa-Nut Soup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 oz. of grated cocoa-nut
- 6 oz. of rice flour
- 1/2 a teaspoonful of mace
- cayenne to taste
- salt to taste
- 1/4 of a pint of boiling cream
- 3 quarts of medium stock No. 105

## Directions

1. Take the dark rind from the cocoa-nut, and grate it down small on a clean grater; weigh it, and allow, for each quart of stock, 2 oz. of the cocoa-nut.
2. Simmer it gently for 1 hour in the stock, which should then be strained closely from it, and thickened for table.

title: Cod A L'Italienne
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 slices of crimped cod
- 1 shalot
- 1 slice of ham minced very fine
- 1/2 pint of white stock
- 1/2 teacupful of cream
- salt to taste
- a few drops of garlic vinegar
- a little lemon-juice
- 1/2 teaspoonful of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Chop the shalots, mince the ham very fine, pour on the stock, and simmer for 15 minutes
2. If the colour should not be good, add cream in the above proportion, and strain it through a fine sieve
3. season it, and put in the vinegar, lemon-juice, and sugar
4. Now boil the cod, take out the middle bone, and skin it
5. put it on the dish without breaking, and pour the sauce over it

title: Cod A La Bechamel
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Any remains of cold cod
- 4 tablespoonfuls of béchamel
- 2 oz butter
- pepper and salt to taste
- fried bread
- a few bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Flake the cod carefully, leaving out all skin and bone
2. put the béchamel in a stewpan with the butter, and stir it over the fire till the latter is melted
3. add seasoning, put in the fish, and mix it well with the sauce
4. Make a border of fried bread round the dish, lay in the fish, sprinkle over with bread crumbs, and baste with butter
5. Brown either before the fire or with a salamander, and garnish with toasted bread cut in fanciful shapes

title: Cod A La Creme
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large slice of cod
- 1 oz. of butter
- 1 chopped shalot
- a little minced parsley
- 1/4 teacupful of white stock
- 1/4 pint of milk or cream
- flour to thicken
- cayenne and lemon-juice to taste
- 1/4 teaspoonful of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Boil the cod, and while hot, break it into flakes
2. put the butter, shalot, parsley, and stock into a stewpan, and let them boil for 5 minutes
3. Stir in sufficient flour to thicken, and pour to it the milk or cream
4. Simmer for 10 minutes, add the cayenne and sugar, and, when liked, a little lemon-juice
5. Put the fish in the sauce to warm gradually, but do not let it boil
6. Serve in a dish garnished with croûtons

title: Cod A La Maitre D'Hotel
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 slices of cod
- 1/4 lb. of butter
- a little chopped shalot and parsley
- pepper to taste
- 1/4 teaspoonful of grated nutmeg
- the juice of 1/4 lemon

## Directions

1. Boil the cod, and either leave it whole, or, what is still better, flake it from the bone, and take off the skin
2. Put it into a stewpan with the butter, parsley, shalot, pepper, and nutmeg
3. Melt the butter gradually, and be very careful that it does not become like oil
4. When all is well mixed and thoroughly hot, add the lemon-juice, and serve

title: Cod Pie (Economical)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Any remains of cold cod
- 12 oysters
- Sufficient melted butter to moisten it
- Mashed potatoes enough to fill up the dish

## Directions

1. Flake the fish from the bone, and carefully take away all the skin.
2. Lay it in a pie-dish, pour over the melted butter and oysters (or oyster sauce, if there is any left), and cover with mashed potatoes.
3. Bake for 1/2 an hour, and send to table of a nice brown colour.

title: Cod Pie II
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 slices of cod
- Pepper and salt to taste
- 1/2 a teaspoonful of grated nutmeg
- 1 large blade of pounded mace
- 2 oz. of butter
- 1/2 pint of stock No. 107
- Paste crust
- 1 tablespoonful of stock
- 1/4 pint of cream or milk
- Thickening of flour or butter
- Lemon-peel chopped very fine to taste
- 12 oysters

## Directions

1. Lay the cod in salt for 4 hours, then wash it and place it in a dish; season, and add the butter and stock; cover with the crust, and bake for 1 hour, or rather more.
2. Make the sauce, by mixing the tablespoonful of stock, cream or milk, thickening of flour or butter, lemon-peel, and oysters; give it one boil, and pour it into the pie by a hole made at the top of the crust, which can easily be covered by a small piece of pastry cut and baked in any fanciful shape--such as a leaf, or otherwise.

title: Cod Sounds, En Poule
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 chopped oysters
- 3 chopped anchovies
- 1/4 lb. of bread crumbs
- 1 oz. of butter
- 2 eggs
- Salt, pepper, nutmeg, and mace to taste
- 4 cod sounds
- Milk and water
- Lard
- Flour

## Directions

1. Make the forcemeat by mixing the chopped oysters, anchovies, bread crumbs, butter, eggs, and seasoning well together.
2. Wash the sounds, and boil them in milk and water for 1/2 an hour; take them out and let them cool.
3. Cover each with a layer of forcemeat, roll them up in a nice form, and skewer them.
4. Rub over with lard, dredge with flour, and cook them gently before the fire in a Dutch oven.

title: Cod's Head and Shoulders
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- cod's head and shoulders
- sufficient water to cover the fish
- 5 oz. of salt to each gallon of water
- cut lemon
- horseradish
- roe and liver
- oyster sauce
- plain melted butter

## Directions

1. Cleanse the fish thoroughly
2. Rub a little salt over the thick part and inside of the fish 1 or 2 hours before dressing it
3. Lay it in the fish-kettle with sufficient cold water to cover it
4. Be very particular not to pour the water on the fish as it is liable to break it
5. Keep it just simmering
6. If the water should boil away, add a little by pouring it in at the side of the kettle, not on the fish
7. Add salt in the proportion of 5 oz. to each gallon and bring it gradually to a boil
8. Skim very carefully
9. Draw it to the side of the fire and let it gently simmer till done
10. Take it out and drain it
11. Serve on a hot napkin and garnish with cut lemon, horseradish, the roe and liver
12. Serve oyster sauce and plain melted butter with this

title: Coffee Essence
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- ground coffee
- chicory
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Roast the coffee and put it into a percolator or filter with the chicory
2. Pour slowly over it the boiling water
3. When it has all filtered through, warm the coffee sufficiently to bring it to the simmering-point, but do not allow it to boil
4. Filter it a second time
5. Put it into a clean and dry bottle and cork it well
6. Two tablespoonfuls of this essence are quite sufficient for a breakfast-cupful of hot milk
7. When the essence is bottled, pour another 3 tea-cupfuls of boiling water slowly on the grounds
8. When filtered through, this will be a very weak coffee
9. The next time essence is to be prepared, make this weak coffee boiling and pour it on the ground coffee instead of plain water
10. Always cork the bottle well that contains this preparation until the day it is wanted for making fresh essence

title: Cold Mutton Pie
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- the remains of a cold leg, loin, or neck of mutton
- pepper and salt to taste
- 2 blades of pounded mace
- 1 dessertspoonful of chopped parsley
- 1 teaspoonful of minced savoury herbs
- a little minced onion or shalot
- 3 or 4 potatoes
- 1 teacupful of gravy
- crust

## Directions

1. Cold mutton may be made into very good pies if well seasoned and mixed with a few herbs; if the leg is used, cut it into very thin slices; if the loin or neck, into thin cutlets.
2. Place some at the bottom of the dish; season well with pepper, salt, mace, parsley, and herbs.
3. Then put a layer of potatoes sliced, then more mutton, and so on till the dish is full.
4. Add the gravy, cover with a crust, and bake for 1 hour.

title: Cold Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 1 pint of milk
- sugar to taste
- a little grated lemon-rind
- 2 oz. of raisins
- 4 tablespoonfuls of marmalade
- a few slices of sponge cake

## Directions

1. Sweeten the milk with lump sugar, add a little grated lemon-rind
2. Stir to this the eggs, which should be well whisked
3. Line a buttered mould with the raisins, stoned and cut in half
4. Spread the slices of cake with the marmalade, and place them in the mould
5. Pour in the custard
6. Tie the pudding down with paper and a cloth
7. Boil gently for 1 hour: when cold, turn it out, and serve

title: Collared Beef
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 lbs. of the thin end of the flank of beef
- 2 oz. of coarse sugar
- 6 oz. of salt
- 1 oz. of saltpetre
- 1 large handful of parsley minced
- 1 dessertspoonful of minced sage
- a bunch of savoury herbs
- 1/2 teaspoonful of pounded allspice
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Choose fine tender beef, but not too fat; lay it in a dish.
2. Rub in the sugar, salt, and saltpetre, and let it remain in the pickle for a week or ten days, turning and rubbing it every day.
3. Then bone it, remove all the gristle and the coarse skin of the inside part, and sprinkle it thickly with parsley, herbs, spice, and seasoning in the above proportion, taking care that the former are finely minced, and the latter well pounded.
4. Roll the meat up in a cloth as tightly as possible, in the same shape as shown in the engraving.
5. Bind it firmly with broad tape, and boil it gently for 6 hours.
6. Immediately on taking it out of the pot, put it under a good weight, without undoing it, and let it remain until cold.

title: Collared Calf's Head
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- A calf's head
- 4 tablespoonfuls of minced parsley
- 4 blades of pounded mace
- 1/2 teaspoonful of grated nutmeg
- white pepper to taste
- a few thick slices of ham
- the yolks of 6 eggs boiled hard

## Directions

1. Scald the head for a few minutes; take it out of the water, and with a blunt knife scrape off all the hair.
2. Clean it nicely, divide the head and remove the brains.
3. Boil it tender enough to take out the bones, which will be in about 2 hours.
4. When the head is boned, flatten it on the table, sprinkle over it a thick layer of parsley, then a layer of ham, and then the yolks of the eggs cut into thin rings and put a seasoning of pounded mace, nutmeg, and white pepper between each layer.
5. Roll the head up in a cloth, and tie it up as tightly as possible.
6. Boil it for 4 hours.
7. When it is taken out of the pot, place a heavy weight on the top.
8. Let it remain till cold; then remove the cloth and binding, and it will be ready to serve.

title: Collared Eel
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large eel
- pepper and salt to taste
- 2 blades of mace
- 2 cloves
- a little allspice very finely pounded
- 6 leaves of sage
- a small bunch of herbs minced very small
- water
- salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Bone the eel and skin it
2. split it, and sprinkle it over with the ingredients, taking care that the spices are very finely pounded, and the herbs chopped very small
3. Roll it up and bind with a broad piece of tape
4. boil it in water, mixed with a little salt and vinegar, till tender
5. It may either be served whole or cut in slices
6. when cold, the eel should be kept in the liquor it was boiled in, but with a little more vinegar put to it

title: Collared Pig's Face
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pig's face
- salt
- 1 gallon of spring water
- 1 lb. of common salt
- 1/2 handful of chopped juniper-berries
- 6 bruised cloves
- 2 bay-leaves
- a few sprigs of thyme
- a few sprigs of basil
- a few sprigs of sage
- 1/4 oz. of saltpetre
- 1/2 lb. of ham
- 1/2 lb. bacon
- 1 teaspoonful of mixed spices
- pepper to taste
- 1/4 lb. of lard
- 1 tablespoonful of minced parsley
- 6 young onions

## Directions

1. Singe the head carefully, bone it without breaking the skin, and rub it well with salt.
2. Make the brine by boiling the ingredients (spring water, common salt, juniper-berries, cloves, bay-leaves, thyme, basil, sage, and saltpetre) for 1/4 hour, and letting it stand to cool.
3. When cold, pour the brine over the head, and let it steep in this for 10 days, turning and rubbing it often.
4. Then wipe, drain, and dry it.
5. For the forcemeat, pound the ham and bacon very finely, and mix with the remaining ingredients (mixed spices, pepper, lard, minced parsley, and young onions), taking care that the whole is thoroughly incorporated.
6. Spread the forcemeat equally over the head, roll it tightly in a cloth, and bind it securely with broad tape.
7. Put it into a saucepan with a few meat trimmings, and cover it with stock; let it simmer gently for 4 hours, and be particular that it does not stop boiling the whole time.
8. When quite tender, take it up, put it between 2 dishes with a heavy weight on the top, and when cold, remove the cloth and tape.
9. Send to table on a napkin, or garnished with a piece of deep white paper with a ruche at the top.

title: Collared Salmon
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of salmon, say 3 lbs.
- salt
- pounded mace
- pepper
- water and vinegar
- 3 bay-leaves
- whole pepper
- melted butter or anchovy sauce

## Directions

1. Split the fish; scale, bone, and wash it thoroughly clean
2. Wipe it, and rub in the seasoning inside and out
3. Roll it up, and bind firmly
4. Lay it in a kettle, cover it with vinegar and water (1/3 vinegar, in proportion to the water)
5. Add the bay-leaves and a good seasoning of salt and whole pepper, and simmer till done
6. Do not remove the lid
7. Serve with melted butter or anchovy sauce
8. For preserving the collared fish, boil up the liquor in which it was cooked, and add a little more vinegar
9. Pour over when cold

title: College Puddings
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of bread crumbs
- 6 oz. of finely-chopped suet
- 1/4 lb. of currants
- a few thin slices of candied peel
- 3 oz. of sugar
- 1/4 nutmeg
- 3 eggs
- 4 tablespoonfuls of brandy
- butter or lard

## Directions

1. Put the bread crumbs into a basin; add the suet, currants, candied peel, sugar, and nutmeg, grated
2. Stir these ingredients until they are thoroughly mixed
3. Beat up the eggs, moisten the pudding with these, and put in the brandy
4. Beat well for a few minutes, then form the mixture into round balls or egg-shaped pieces
5. Fry these in hot butter or lard, letting them stew in it until thoroughly done, and turn them two or three times, till of a fine light brown
6. Drain them on a piece of blotting-paper before the fire
7. Dish, and serve with wine sauce

title: Common Cake, Suitable For Sending To Children At School
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of flour
- 4 oz. of butter or clarified dripping
- 1/2 oz. of caraway seeds
- 1/4 oz. of allspice
- 1/2 lb. of pounded sugar
- 1 lb. of currants
- 1 pint of milk
- 3 tablespoonfuls of fresh yeast

## Directions

1. Rub the butter lightly into the flour
2. Add all the dry ingredients, and mix these well together
3. Make the milk warm, but not hot
4. Stir in the yeast, and with this liquid make the whole into a light dough
5. Knead it well, and line the cake-tins with strips of buttered paper
6. The paper should be about 6 inches higher than the top of the tin
7. Put in the dough
8. Stand it in a warm place to rise for more than an hour
9. Bake the cakes in a well-heated oven
10. If this quantity be divided in two, they will take from 1-1/2 to 2 hours' baking

title: Common Crust for Raised Pies
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. flour
- 1/2 pint of water
- 1-1/2 oz. of butter
- 1-1/2 oz. of lard
- 1/2 saltspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Put into a saucepan the water; when it boils, add the butter and lard
2. When these are melted, make a hole in the middle of the flour
3. Pour in the water gradually
4. Beat it well with a wooden spoon, and be particular in not making the paste too soft
5. When it is well mixed, knead it with the hands until quite stiff, dredging a little flour over the paste and board, to prevent them from sticking
6. When it is well kneaded, place it before the fire, with a cloth covered over it, for a few minutes; it will then be more easily worked into shape

title: Common Paste for Family Pies
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/4 lb. flour
- 1/2 lb. butter
- rather more than 1/2 pint water

## Directions

1. Rub the butter lightly into the flour
2. Mix it to a smooth paste with the water
3. Roll it out 2 or 3 times, and it will be ready for use

title: Common Plum Cake
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lbs. of flour
- 6 oz. of butter or good dripping
- 6 oz. of moist sugar
- 6 oz. of currants
- 4 oz. of pounded allspice
- 2 tablespoonfuls of fresh yeast
- 1 pint of new milk

## Directions

1. Rub the butter into the flour; add the sugar, currants, and allspice; warm the milk, stir to it the yeast, and mix the whole into a dough; knead it well, and put it into 6 buttered tins; place them near the fire for nearly an hour for the dough to rise, then bake the cakes in a good oven from 1 to 11/4 hour.
2. To ascertain when they are done, plunge a clean knife into the middle, and if on withdrawal it comes out clean, the cakes are done.

title: Common Seed-Cake
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 quartern of dough
- 1/4 lb. of good dripping
- 6 oz. of moist sugar
- 1/2 oz. of caraway seeds
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. If the dough is sent in from the baker's, put it in a basin covered with a cloth, and set it in a warm place to rise
2. With a wooden spoon beat the dripping to a liquid
3. Add it, with the other ingredients, to the dough, and beat it until everything is very thoroughly mixed
4. Put it into a buttered tin
5. Bake the cake for rather more than 2 hours

title: Common Short Crust
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb flour
- 2 oz sifted sugar
- 3 oz butter
- 1/2 pint boiling milk

## Directions

1. Crumble the butter into the flour as finely as possible
2. Add the sugar
3. Work the whole up to a smooth paste with the boiling milk
4. Roll it out thin
5. Bake in a moderate oven

title: Compote Of Apples
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 ripe apples
- 1 lemon
- 1/2 lb. of lump sugar
- 1/2 pint of water
- green angelica or candied citron

## Directions

1. Select the apples of a moderate size, peel them, cut them in halves, remove the cores, and rub each piece over with a little lemon
2. Put the sugar and water together into a lined saucepan, and let them boil until forming a thickish syrup
3. Lay in the apples with the rind of the lemon cut thin, and the juice of the same
4. Let the apples simmer till tender
5. Take them out very carefully, drain them on a sieve, and reduce the syrup by boiling it quickly for a few minutes
6. When both are cold, arrange the apples neatly on a glass dish, pour over the syrup, and garnish with strips of green angelica or candied citron

title: Compote of Apricots
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of syrup No. 1512
- 12 green apricots

## Directions

1. Make the syrup by recipe No. 1512, and when it is ready, put in the apricots whilst the syrup is boiling.
2. Simmer them very gently until tender, taking care not to let them break.
3. Take them out carefully, arrange them on a glass dish, let the syrup cool a little, pour it over the apricots, and, when cold, serve.

title: Compote of Damsons
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of damsons
- 1 pint of syrup No. 1512

## Directions

1. Procure sound ripe damsons; pick the stalks from them, and put them into boiling syrup, made by recipe No. 1512
2. Simmer them gently until the fruit is tender, but not sufficiently soft to break
3. Take them up, boil the syrup for 5 minutes
4. Pour it over the damsons, and serve in a glass dish

title: Compote of Gooseberries
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- syrup made by recipe No. 1512
- nearly a quart of gooseberries
- boiling water
- cold water
- tablespoonful of vinegar

## Directions

1. Top and tail the gooseberries, which should not be very ripe
2. Pour over them some boiling water; then take them out, and plunge them into cold water, with which has been mixed a tablespoonful of vinegar
3. Make a pint of syrup by recipe No. 1512
4. When it boils, drain the gooseberries and put them in
5. Simmer them gently until the fruit is nicely pulped and tender, without being broken
6. Dish the gooseberries on a glass dish, boil the syrup for 2 or 3 minutes
7. Pour over the gooseberries, and serve cold

title: Compote Of Green Figs
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of syrup No. 1512
- 1-1/2 pint of green figs
- The rind of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Make a syrup by recipe No. 1512, boiling with it the lemon-rind, and carefully remove all the scum as it rises
2. Put in the figs, and simmer them very slowly until tender
3. Dish them on a glass dish
4. Reduce the syrup by boiling it quickly for 5 minutes
5. Take out the lemon-peel, pour the syrup over the figs
6. When cold, the compote will be ready for table
7. A little port wine, or lemon-juice, added just before the figs are done, will be found an improvement

title: Compote of Greengages
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- syrup made by recipe No. 1512 (1 pint)
- greengages (1 quart)

## Directions

1. Make a syrup by recipe No. 1512, skim it well
2. Put in the greengages when the syrup is boiling, having previously removed the stalks and stones from the fruit
3. Boil gently for 1/4 hour, or until the fruit is tender; but take care not to let it break
4. Take the greengages carefully out, place them on a glass dish
5. Boil the syrup for another 5 minutes, let it cool a little
6. Pour over the fruit, and when cold, it will be ready for use

title: Compote of Oranges
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of syrup No. 1512
- 6 oranges

## Directions

1. Peel the oranges, remove as much of the white pith as possible, and divide them into small pieces without breaking the thin skin with which they are surrounded
2. Make the syrup by recipe No. 1512, adding the rind of the orange cut into thin narrow strips
3. When the syrup has been well skimmed, and is quite clear, put in the pieces of orange, and simmer them for 5 minutes
4. Take them out carefully with a spoon without breaking them, and arrange them on a glass dish
5. Reduce the syrup by boiling it quickly until thick; let it cool a little, pour it over the oranges

title: Compote of Peaches
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of syrup No. 1512
- about 15 small peaches

## Directions

1. Divide the peaches, take out the stones, and pare the fruit
2. Make a syrup by recipe No. 1512, put in the peaches, and stew them gently for about 10 minutes
3. Take them out without breaking, arrange them on a glass dish, boil the syrup for 2 or 3 minutes, let it cool, pour it over the fruit, and, when cold, it will be ready for table

title: Cow-Heel Stock For Jellies
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cow-heels
- 3 quarts of water
- 1/2 oz. of isinglass to each quart

## Directions

1. Procure 2 heels that have only been scalded, and not boiled
2. Split them in two, and remove the fat between the claws
3. Wash them well in warm water, and put them into a saucepan with the above proportion of cold water
4. Bring it gradually to boil, remove all the scum as it rises, and simmer the heels gently from 7 to 8 hours, or until the liquor is reduced one-half
5. Strain it into a basin, measuring the quantity, and put it in a cool place
6. Clarify it in the same manner as calf's-feet stock, using about 1/2 oz. of isinglass to each quart
7. This stock should be made the day before it is required for use

title: Cowslip Wine
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lbs. of lump sugar per gallon of water
- rind of 2 lemons
- juice of 1 lemon
- rind of 1 Seville orange
- juice of 1 Seville orange
- 1 gallon of cowslip pips
- 1 bottle of brandy to every 4-1/2 gallons of wine
- 3 tablespoonfuls of good fresh brewers' yeast to every 9 gallons of wine

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar and water together for 1/2 hour
2. Carefully remove all the scum as it rises
3. Pour this boiling liquor on the orange and lemon-rinds and the strained juice
4. When milk-warm, add the cowslip pips or flowers, picked from the stalks and seeds
5. Add the yeast
6. Let it ferment 3 or 4 days
7. Put all together in a cask with the brandy
8. Let it remain for 2 months
9. Bottle it off for use

title: Crab Sauce for Fish
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 crab
- salt to taste
- pounded mace to taste
- cayenne to taste
- 1/2 pint of melted butter made with milk

## Directions

1. Choose a nice fresh crab
2. Pick all the meat away from the shell
3. Cut it into small square pieces
4. Make 1/2 pint of melted butter by recipe No. 380
5. Put in the fish and seasoning
6. Let it gradually warm through
7. Simmer for 2 minutes
8. It should not boil

title: Crayfish
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- crayfish
- salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Throw crayfish into boiling water, to which has been added a good seasoning of salt and a little vinegar.
2. When done, which will be in 1/4 hour, take them out and drain them.
3. Let them cool, arrange them on a napkin, and garnish with plenty of double parsley.

title: Crayfish Soup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 crayfish
- 1/4 lb. of butter
- 6 anchovies
- the crumb of 1 French roll
- a little lobster-spawn
- seasoning to taste
- 2 quarts of medium stock, No. 105, or fish stock, No. 192

## Directions

1. Shell the crayfish, and put the fish between two plates until they are wanted.
2. Pound the shells in a mortar, with the butter and anchovies; when well beaten, add a pint of stock, and simmer for 3/4 of an hour.
3. Strain it through a hair sieve, put the remainder of the stock to it, with the crumb of the rolls; give it one boil, and rub it through a tammy, with the lobster-spawn.
4. Put in the fish, but do not let the soup boil, after it has been rubbed through the tammy.
5. If necessary, add seasoning.

title: Cream a la Valois
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 sponge-cakes
- jam
- 3/4 pint of cream
- sugar to taste
- the juice of 1/2 lemon
- 1/4 glass of sherry
- 1-1/4 oz. of isinglass

## Directions

1. Cut the sponge-cakes into thin slices; place two together, with preserve between them, and pour over them a small quantity of sherry mixed with a little brandy.
2. Sweeten and flavour the cream with the lemon-juice and sherry.
3. Add the isinglass, which should be dissolved in a little water, and beat up the cream well.
4. Place a little in an oiled mould; arrange the pieces of cake in the cream; then fill the mould with the remainder.
5. Let it cool, and turn it out on a dish.

title: Cream Sauce for Fish or White Dishes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/3 pint of cream
- 2 oz. of butter
- 1 teaspoonful of flour
- salt to taste
- cayenne to taste
- a small quantity of pounded mace or lemon-juice

## Directions

1. Put the butter in a very clean saucepan
2. Dredge in the flour
3. Keep shaking round till the butter is melted
4. Add the seasoning and cream
5. Stir the whole till it boils
6. Let it just simmer for 5 minutes
7. Add either pounded mace or lemon-juice to taste to give it a flavour

title: Creamed Apple Tart
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff-crust No. 1205 or 1206
- apples
- 2 oz. of moist sugar per lb. of pared and cored apples
- 1/2 teaspoonful of minced lemon-peel per lb. of apples
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon-juice per lb. of apples
- 1/2 pint of boiled custard
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Make an apple tart by the preceding recipe, with the exception of omitting the icing.
2. When the tart is baked, cut out the middle of the lid or crust, leaving a border all round the dish.
3. Fill up with a nicely-made boiled custard.
4. Grate a little nutmeg over the top, and the pie is ready for table.
5. This tart is usually eaten cold.

title: Crimped Salmon
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Salmon
- salt and water
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Cut salmon into slices 2 or 3 inches thick
2. Lay these in cold salt and water for 1 hour
3. Have ready some boiling water, salted, as in recipe No. 301, and well skimmed
4. Put in the fish, and simmer gently for 1/4 hour, or rather more
5. Garnish the same as boiled salmon, and serve with the same sauces

title: Crisp Biscuits
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- the yolk of 1 egg
- milk

## Directions

1. Mix the flour and the yolk of the egg with sufficient milk to make the whole into a very stiff paste.
2. Beat it well, and knead it until it is perfectly smooth.
3. Roll the paste out very thin.
4. With a round cutter shape it into small biscuits.
5. Bake them a nice brown in a slow oven from 12 to 18 minutes.

title: Croquettes of Fowl
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 shalots
- 1 oz. of butter
- 1 teaspoonful of flour
- white sauce
- pepper
- salt
- pounded mace
- 1/2 teaspoonful of pounded sugar
- remains of cold roast fowls
- yolks of 2 eggs
- egg
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Mince the fowl, carefully removing all skin and bone
2. Fry the shalots in the butter
3. Add the minced fowl, dredge in the flour, put in the pepper, salt, mace, and pounded sugar
4. Add sufficient white sauce to moisten it
5. Stir to it the yolks of 2 well-beaten eggs, and set it by to cool
6. Make the mixture up into balls, egg and bread-crumb them, and fry a nice brown
7. Serve on a border of mashed potatoes, with gravy or sauce in the centre

title: Croquettes of Turkey
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- remains of cold turkey
- ham or bacon
- shalots
- butter
- flour
- yolks of eggs
- egg and bread crumbs
- parsley-stems
- rolled bacon

## Directions

1. Mince the meat finely with ham or bacon
2. Make a gravy of the bones and trimmings, well seasoning it
3. Mince the shalots, put them into a stewpan with the butter
4. Add the flour and mix well
5. Put in the mince and about 1/2 pint of the gravy made from the bones
6. When just boiled, add the yolks of 2 eggs
7. Put the mixture out to cool and then shape it in a wineglass
8. Cover the croquettes with egg and bread crumbs
9. Fry them a delicate brown
10. Put small pieces of parsley-stems for stalks
11. Serve with rolled bacon cut very thin

title: Crumpets
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- mixture more like batter than dough
- iron rings
- good butter

## Directions

1. Make the mixture in the same manner as muffins, but let it be more like batter than dough
2. Let it rise for about 1/2 hour
3. Pour it into iron rings, which should be ready on a hot-plate
4. Bake them, and when one side appears done, turn them quickly on the other
5. Have ready a very bright clear fire
6. Put the crumpet on a toasting-fork, and hold it before the fire, not too close, until it is nicely brown on one side, but do not allow it to blacken
7. Turn it, and brown the other side
8. Spread it with good butter
9. Cut it in half
10. When all are done, pile them on a hot dish, and send them quickly to table

title: Cucumber Sauce
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 cucumbers
- 2 oz. of butter
- 6 tablespoonfuls of brown gravy

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumbers
2. Quarter them and take out the seeds
3. Cut them into small pieces
4. Put them in a cloth and rub them well, to take out the water which hangs about them
5. Put the butter in a saucepan
6. Add the cucumbers
7. Shake them over a sharp fire until they are of a good colour
8. Pour over it the gravy
9. Mix this with the cucumbers
10. Simmer gently for 10 minutes
11. It will then be ready to serve

title: Cucumber Sauce, White
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 cucumbers
- 1/2 pint of white stock, No. 107
- cayenne to taste
- salt to taste
- the yolks of 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Cut the cucumbers into small pieces, after peeling them and taking out the seeds
2. Put them in a stewpan with the white stock and seasoning
3. Simmer gently till the cucumbers are tender, which will be in about 1/4 hour
4. Add the yolks of the eggs well beaten
5. Stir them to the sauce
6. Do not allow it to boil
7. Serve very hot

title: Cucumber Soup (French Recipe)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large cucumber
- a piece of butter the size of a walnut
- a little chervil and sorrel cut in large pieces
- salt and pepper to taste
- the yolks of 2 eggs
- 1 gill of cream
- 1 quart of medium stock No. 105

## Directions

1. Pare the cucumber, quarter it, and take out the seeds
2. Cut it in thin slices, put these on a plate with a little salt, to draw the water from them
3. Drain, and put them in your stewpan, with the butter
4. When they are warmed through, without being browned, pour the stock on them
5. Add the sorrel, chervil, and seasoning, and boil for 40 minutes
6. Mix the well-beaten yolks of the eggs with the cream, which add at the moment of serving

title: Cucumber Vinegar
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 large cucumbers, or 12 smaller ones
- 1 quart of vinegar
- 2 onions
- 2 shalots
- 1 tablespoonful of salt
- 2 tablespoonfuls of pepper
- 1/4 teaspoonful of cayenne

## Directions

1. Pare and slice the cucumbers, put them in a stone jar or wide-mouthed bottle, with the vinegar
2. Slice the onions and shalots, and add them, with all the other ingredients, to the cucumbers
3. Let it stand 4 or 5 days
4. Boil it all up
5. When cold, strain the liquor through a piece of muslin
6. Store it away in small bottles well sealed

title: Cucumbers a la Poulette
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 cucumbers
- salt and vinegar
- 2 oz. of butter
- flour
- 1/2 pint of broth
- 1 teaspoonful of minced parsley
- a lump of sugar
- the yolks of 2 eggs
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Pare and cut the cucumbers into slices of an equal thickness, and let them remain in a pickle of salt and vinegar for 1/2 hour
2. Drain them in a cloth, and put them into a stewpan with the butter
3. Fry them over a brisk fire, but do not brown them, and then dredge over them a little flour
4. Add the broth, skim off all the fat, which will rise to the surface, and boil gently until the gravy is somewhat reduced; but the cucumber should not be broken
5. Stir in the yolks of the eggs, add the parsley, sugar, and a seasoning of pepper and salt
6. Bring the whole to the point of boiling, and serve

title: Curds and Whey
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- A very small piece of rennet
- 1/2 gallon of milk

## Directions

1. Procure from the butcher's a small piece of rennet, which is the stomach of the calf, taken as soon as it is killed, scoured, and well rubbed with salt, and stretched on sticks to dry.
2. Pour some boiling water on the rennet, and let it remain for 6 hours; then use the liquor to turn the milk.
3. The milk should be warm and fresh from the cow: if allowed to cool, it must be heated till it is of a degree quite equal to new milk; but do not let it be too hot.
4. About a tablespoonful or rather more, would be sufficient to turn the above proportion of milk into curds and whey; and whilst the milk is turning, let it be kept in rather a warm place.
5. Time: From 2 to 3 hours to turn the milk.

title: Currant Dumplings
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 6 oz. of suet
- 1/2 lb. of currants
- rather more than 1/2 pint of water

## Directions

1. Chop the suet finely, mix it with the flour, and add the currants, which should be nicely washed, picked, and dried
2. Mix the whole to a limp paste with the water
3. Divide it into 7 or 8 dumplings; tie them in cloths, and boil for 1-1/4 hour
4. Alternatively, they may be boiled without a cloth: make them into round balls, and drop into boiling water, and move them about at first, to prevent them from sticking to the bottom of the saucepan
5. Serve with a cut lemon, cold butter, and sifted sugar

title: Currant Dumplings
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- suet
- flour
- currants
- water or milk
- lemon
- cold butter
- sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Chop the suet finely, mix it with the flour, and add the currants, which should be nicely washed, picked, and dried
2. Mix the whole to a limp paste with the water (if wanted very nice, use milk)
3. Divide it into 7 or 8 dumplings; tie them in cloths, and boil for 1-1/4 hour
4. Alternatively, they may be boiled without a cloth: make them into round balls, and drop into boiling water, and move about at first, to prevent them from sticking to the bottom of the saucepan
5. Serve with a cut lemon, cold butter, and sifted sugar

title: Currant Fritters
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of milk
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 4 eggs
- 3 tablespoonfuls of boiled rice
- 3 tablespoonfuls of currants
- sugar to taste
- a very little grated nutmeg
- hot lard or clarified dripping

## Directions

1. Put the milk into a basin with the flour, which should previously be rubbed to a smooth batter with a little cold milk
2. Stir these ingredients together
3. Add the well-whisked eggs, the rice, currants, sugar, and nutmeg
4. Beat the mixture for a few minutes, and, if not sufficiently thick, add a little more boiled rice
5. Drop it, in small quantities, into a pan of boiling lard or clarified dripping
6. Fry the fritters a nice brown, and, when done, drain them on a piece of blotting-paper, before the fire
7. Pile them on a white d'oyley, strew over sifted sugar, and serve them very hot
8. Send a cut lemon to table with them

title: Curried Beef (Cold Meat Cookery)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- A few slices of tolerably lean cold roast or boiled beef
- 3 oz. of butter
- 2 onions
- 1 wineglassful of beer
- 1 dessertspoonful of curry powder

## Directions

1. Cut up the beef into pieces about 1 inch square.
2. Put the butter into a stewpan with the onions sliced, and fry them of a lightly-brown colour.
3. Add all the other ingredients, and stir gently over a brisk fire for about 10 minutes.
4. Should this be thought too dry, more beer, or a spoonful or two of gravy or water, may be added; but a good curry should not be very thin.
5. Place it in a deep dish, with an edging of dry boiled rice, in the same manner as for other curries.

title: Curried Cod
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 slices of large cod, or the remains of any cold fish
- 3 oz. of butter
- 1 onion sliced
- A teacupful of white stock
- Thickening of butter and flour
- 1 small teaspoonful of curry-powder
- 1/4 pint of cream
- Salt and cayenne to taste

## Directions

1. Flake the fish, and fry it of a nice brown colour with the butter and onions; put this in a stewpan, add the stock and thickening, and simmer for 10 minutes.
2. Stir the curry-powder into the cream; put it, with the seasoning, to the other ingredients; give one boil, and serve.

title: Curried Fowl
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 fowl
- 2 oz. of butter
- 3 onions sliced
- 1 pint of white veal gravy
- 1 tablespoonful of curry-powder
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 1 apple
- 4 tablespoonfuls of cream
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon-juice

## Directions

1. Put the butter into a stewpan, with the onions sliced, the fowl cut into small joints, and the apple peeled, cored, and minced
2. Fry of a pale brown, add the stock, and stew gently for 20 minutes
3. Rub down the curry-powder and flour with a little of the gravy, quite smoothly, and stir this to the other ingredients
4. Simmer for rather more than 1/2 hour
5. Just before serving, add the above proportion of hot cream and lemon-juice
6. Serve with boiled rice, which may either be heaped lightly on a dish by itself, or put round the curry as a border

title: Curried Fowl or Chicken
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast fowls
- 2 large onions
- 1 apple
- 2 oz. of butter
- 1 dessertspoonful of curry-powder
- 1 teaspoonful of flour
- 1/2 pint of gravy
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon-juice

## Directions

1. Slice the onions, peel, core, and chop the apple, and cut the fowl into neat joints
2. Fry these in the butter of a nice brown
3. Add the curry-powder, flour, and gravy, and stew for about 20 minutes
4. Put in the lemon-juice, and serve with boiled rice, either placed in a ridge round the dish or separately
5. Two or three shallots or a little garlic may be added, if approved

title: Curried Mutton
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of any joint of cold mutton
- 2 onions
- 1/4 lb. of butter
- 1 dessertspoonful of curry powder
- 1 dessertspoonful of flour
- salt to taste
- 1/4 pint of stock or water

## Directions

1. Slice the onions in thin rings
2. Put them into a stewpan with the butter
3. Fry of a light brown
4. Stir in the curry powder, flour, and salt
5. Mix all well together
6. Cut the meat into nice thin slices (or mince if insufficient)
7. Add it to the other ingredients
8. When well browned, add the stock or gravy
9. Stew gently for about 1/2 hour
10. Serve in a dish with a border of boiled rice, the same as for other curries

title: Curried Rabbit
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 rabbit
- 2 oz. of butter
- 3 onions
- 1 pint of stock
- 1 tablespoonful of curry powder
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 1 teaspoonful of mushroom powder
- juice of 1/2 lemon
- 1/2 lb. of rice

## Directions

1. Empty, skin, and wash the rabbit thoroughly, and cut it neatly into joints.
2. Put it into a stewpan with the butter and sliced onions, and let them acquire a nice brown colour, but do not allow them to blacken.
3. Pour in the stock, which should be boiling; mix the curry powder and flour smoothly with a little water, add it to the stock, with the mushroom powder, and simmer gently for rather more than 1/2 hour.
4. Squeeze in the lemon-juice, and serve in the centre of a dish, with an edging of boiled rice all round.
5. Where economy is studied, water may be substituted for the stock; in this case, the meat and onions must be very nicely browned.
6. A little sour apple and rasped cocoa-nut stewed with the curry will be found a great improvement.

title: Curried Salmon
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Any remains of boiled salmon
- 3/4 pint of strong or medium stock
- 1 onion
- 1 tablespoonful of curry-powder
- 1 teaspoonful of Harvey's sauce
- 1 teaspoonful of anchovy sauce
- 1 oz. of butter
- the juice of 1/2 lemon
- cayenne and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Cut up the onions into small pieces, and fry them of a pale brown in the butter
2. Add all the ingredients but the salmon, and simmer gently till the onion is tender, occasionally stirring the contents
3. Cut the salmon into small square pieces, carefully take away all skin and bone
4. Lay it in the stewpan, and let it gradually heat through; but do not allow it to boil long

title: Curried Veal
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast veal
- 4 onions
- 2 apples sliced
- 1 tablespoonful of curry-powder
- 1 dessertspoonful of flour
- 1/2 pint of broth or water
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon-juice
- butter
- boiled rice
- pickles, capsicums, and gherkins

## Directions

1. Slice the onions and apples, and fry them in a little butter; then take them out, cut the meat into neat cutlets, and fry these of a pale brown.
2. Add the curry-powder and flour, put in the onion, apples, and a little broth or water, and stew gently till quite tender; add the lemon-juice, and serve with an edging of boiled rice.
3. The curry may be ornamented with pickles, capsicums, and gherkins arranged prettily on the top.

title: Custard Sauce for Sweet Puddings or Tarts
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of milk
- 2 eggs
- 3 oz. of pounded sugar
- 1 tablespoonful of brandy

## Directions

1. Put the milk in a very clean saucepan, and let it boil
2. Beat the eggs, stir to them the milk and pounded sugar, and put the mixture into a jug
3. Place the jug in a saucepan of boiling water
4. Keep stirring well until it thickens, but do not allow it to boil, or it will curdle
5. Serve the sauce in a tureen, stir in the brandy, and grate a little nutmeg over the top

title: Cutlets of Cold Mutton
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold loin or neck of mutton
- 1 egg
- bread crumbs
- brown gravy (No. 436) or tomato sauce (No. 529)

## Directions

1. Cut the remains of cold loin or neck of mutton into cutlets
2. Trim them and take away a portion of the fat, should there be too much
3. Dip them in beaten egg
4. Sprinkle with bread crumbs
5. Fry them a nice brown in hot dripping
6. Arrange them on a dish
7. Pour round them either a good gravy or hot tomato sauce

title: Dampfnudeln, or German Puddings
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 1/4 lb. of butter
- 5 eggs
- 2 small tablespoonfuls of yeast
- 2 tablespoonfuls of finely-pounded sugar
- milk
- a very little salt

## Directions

1. Put the flour into a basin, make a hole in the centre, into which put the yeast, and rather more than 1/4 pint of warm milk; make this into a batter with the middle of the flour, and let the sponge rise in a warm temperature
2. When sufficiently risen, mix the eggs, butter, sugar, and salt with a little more warm milk, and knead the whole well together with the hands, beating the dough until it is perfectly smooth, and it drops from the fingers
3. Then cover the basin with a cloth, put it in a warm place, and when the dough has nicely risen, knead it into small balls
4. butter the bottom of a deep sauté-pan, strew over some pounded sugar, and let the dampfnudeln be laid in, but do not let them touch one another; then pour over sufficient milk to cover them, put on the lid, and let them rise to twice their original size by the side of the fire
5. Now place them in the oven for a few minutes, to acquire a nice brown colour, and serve them on a napkin, with custard sauce flavoured with vanilla, or a compôte of any fruit that may be preferred

title: Damson Cheese
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Damsons
- 1/2 lb. of loaf sugar per lb. of fruit pulp

## Directions

1. Pick the stalks from the damsons, and put them into a preserving-pan
2. Simmer them over the fire until they are soft, occasionally stirring them
3. Beat them through a coarse sieve, and put the pulp and juice into the preserving-pan, with sugar in the above proportion, having previously carefully weighed them
4. Stir the sugar well in, and simmer the damsons slowly for 2 hours
5. Skim well; then boil the preserve quickly for 1/2 hour, or until it looks firm and hard in the spoon
6. Put it quickly into shallow pots, or very tiny earthenware moulds, and when cold, cover it with oiled papers, and the jars with tissue-paper brushed over on both sides with the white of an egg

title: Damson Jam
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Damsons
- 3/4 lb. of loaf sugar per lb. of fruit

## Directions

1. Have the fruit gathered in dry weather; pick it over, and reject any that is at all blemished
2. Stone the damsons, weigh them, and to every lb. allow 3/4 lb. of loaf sugar
3. Put the fruit and sugar into a preserving-pan; keep stirring them gently until the sugar is dissolved, and carefully remove the scum as it rises
4. Boil the jam for about an hour, reckoning from the time it commences to simmer all over alike
5. It must be well stirred all the time, or it will be liable to burn and stick to the pan
6. When the jam looks firm, and the juice appears to set, it is done
7. Take it off the fire, put into pots, cover it down, when quite cold, with oiled and egged papers, and store it away in a dry place

title: Damson Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 pint of damsons
- 1/4 lb. of moist sugar
- 3/4 lb. of suet or butter crust
- 3/4 lb. of flour

## Directions

1. Make a suet crust with 3/4 lb. of flour by recipe No. 1215
2. Line a buttered pudding-basin with a portion of it
3. Fill the basin with the damsons
4. Sweeten them, and put on the lid
5. Pinch the edges of the crust together, that the juice does not escape
6. Tie over a floured cloth
7. Put the pudding into boiling water
8. Boil from 2-1/2 to 3 hours

title: Damson Tart
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/4 pint of damsons
- 1/4 lb. of moist sugar
- 1/2 lb. of short or puff crust
- white of an egg
- sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Put the damsons, with the sugar between them, into a deep pie-dish
2. In the midst of which, place a small cup or jar turned upside down
3. Pile the fruit high in the middle
4. Line the edges of the dish with short or puff crust, whichever may be preferred
5. Put on the cover, ornament the edges
6. Bake from 1/2 to 3/4 hour in a good oven
7. If puff-crust is used, about 10 minutes before the pie is done, take it out of the oven, brush it over with the white of an egg beaten to a froth with the blade of a knife
8. Strew some sifted sugar over, and a few drops of water, and put the tart back to finish baking
9. With short crust, a little plain sifted sugar, sprinkled over, is all that will be required

title: Darioles A La Vanille
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of milk
- 1/2 pint of cream
- 2 oz. of flour
- 3 oz. of pounded sugar
- 6 eggs
- 2 oz. of butter
- puff-paste
- essence of vanilla

## Directions

1. Mix the flour to a smooth batter, with the milk
2. Stir in the cream, sugar, the eggs, which should be well whisked, and the butter, which should be beaten to a cream
3. Put in some essence of vanilla, drop by drop, until the mixture is well flavoured
4. Line some dariole-moulds with puff-paste, three-parts fill them with the batter, and bake in a good oven from 25 to 35 minutes
5. Turn them out of the moulds on a dish, without breaking them
6. Strew over sifted sugar, and serve

title: Delhi Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large apples
- a little grated nutmeg
- 1 teaspoonful of minced lemon-peel
- 2 large tablespoonfuls of sugar
- 6 oz. of currants
- 3/4 lb. of suet crust

## Directions

1. Pare, core, and cut the apples into slices
2. Put them into a saucepan, with the nutmeg, lemon-peel, and sugar
3. Stir them over the fire until soft
4. Have ready the above proportion of crust, roll it out thin
5. Spread the apples over the paste
6. Sprinkle over the currants
7. Roll the pudding up, closing the ends properly
8. Tie it in a floured cloth
9. Boil for 2 hours

title: Dessert Biscuits
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 1/2 lb. of sifted sugar
- the yolks of 6 eggs
- flavouring to taste

## Directions

1. Put the butter into a basin; warm it, but do not allow it to oil.
2. With the hand beat it to a cream.
3. Add the flour by degrees, then the sugar and flavouring.
4. Moisten the whole with the yolks of the eggs, which should previously be well beaten.
5. When all the ingredients are thoroughly incorporated, drop the mixture from a spoon on to a buttered paper, leaving a distance between each cake, as they spread as soon as they begin to get warm.
6. Bake in rather a slow oven from 12 to 18 minutes, and do not let the biscuits acquire too much colour.

title: Devonshire Junket
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint new milk
- 2 dessertspoonfuls brandy
- 1 dessertspoonful sugar
- 1-1/2 dessertspoonful prepared rennet
- thick cream
- pounded cinnamon or grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Make the milk blood-warm
2. Put it into a deep dish with the brandy, sugar, and rennet
3. Stir it altogether, and cover it over until it is set
4. Spread some thick or clotted cream over the top
5. Grate some nutmeg, and strew some sugar over
6. The dish will be ready to serve

title: Devonshire Junket
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- To every pint of new milk allow 2 dessertspoonfuls of brandy
- 1 dessertspoonful of sugar
- 1-1/2 dessertspoonful of prepared rennet
- thick cream
- pounded cinnamon or grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Make the milk blood-warm; put it into a deep dish with the brandy, sugar, and rennet; stir it altogether, and cover it over until it is set.
2. Then spread some thick or clotted cream over the top, grate some nutmeg, and strew some sugar over, and the dish will be ready to serve.
3. Time: About 2 hours to set the milk.

title: Dish of Apples
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- evergreen leaves

## Directions

1. The apples should be nicely wiped with a dry cloth, and arranged on a dish, piled high in the centre, with evergreen leaves between each layer.
2. The inferior apples should form the bottom layer, with the bright-coloured large ones at the top.
3. The leaves of the laurel, bay, holly, or any shrub green in winter, are suitable for garnishing dessert dishes.

title: Dish of Mixed Fruit
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- pine
- moss
- apples
- pears
- plums
- peaches
- grapes

## Directions

1. Place a tumbler in the centre of the dish, and in this tumbler, the pine, crown uppermost.
2. Round the tumbler put a thick layer of moss, and over this, apples, pears, plums, peaches, and such fruit as is simultaneously in season.
3. By putting a layer of moss underneath, so much fruit is not required, besides giving a better shape to the dish.
4. Place grapes on the top of the fruit, a portion of some of the bunches hanging over the sides of the dish in a négligé kind of manner, which takes off the formal look of the dish.
5. In arranging the plums, apples, &c., let the colours contrast well.

title: Dish of Mixed Summer Fruit
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries
- raspberries
- currants
- strawberries
- leaves

## Directions

1. Pile cherries, raspberries, currants, and strawberries in different layers, with plenty of leaves between each layer; so that each fruit is well separated.
2. The fruit should be arranged with a due regard to colour, so that they contrast nicely one with the other.
3. Arrange a layer of white cherries at the bottom, then one of red raspberries; over that a layer of white currants, and at the top some fine scarlet strawberries.

title: Dormers
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of cold mutton
- 2 oz. of beef suet
- pepper and salt to taste
- 3 oz. of boiled rice
- 1 egg
- bread crumbs
- made gravy

## Directions

1. Chop the meat, suet, and rice finely
2. Mix well together
3. Add a high seasoning of pepper and salt
4. Roll into sausages
5. Cover them with egg and bread crumbs
6. Fry in hot dripping of a nice brown
7. Serve in a dish with made gravy poured round them and a little in a tureen

title: Dried Haddock II
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large thick haddock
- 2 bay-leaves
- savoury herbs
- parsley
- butter
- pepper
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Cut up the haddock into square pieces
2. make a basin hot by means of hot water, which pour out
3. lay in the fish, with the bay-leaves and herbs
4. cover with boiling water
5. put a plate over to keep in the steam
6. let it remain for 10 minutes
7. take out the slices
8. put them in a hot dish
9. rub over with butter and pepper
10. serve

title: Dried Sprats
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- dried sprats
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Put dried sprats into a basin, and pour boiling water over them.
2. They may then be skinned and served.

title: Dripping Crust for Kitchen Puddings, Pies, &c.
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb flour
- 6 oz clarified beef dripping
- 1/2 pint water

## Directions

1. Clarify the dripping by recipes No. 621 or 622
2. Weigh it, and to every lb of flour allow the above proportion of dripping
3. With a knife, work the flour into a smooth paste with the water
4. Roll it out 3 times, each time placing on the crust 2 oz of the dripping, broken into small pieces

title: Dutch Flummery
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 oz. of isinglass
- the rind and juice of 1 lemon
- 1 pint of water
- 4 eggs
- 1 pint of sherry, Madeira, or raisin-wine
- sifted sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Put the water, isinglass, and lemon-rind into a lined saucepan, and simmer gently until the isinglass is dissolved
2. Strain this into a basin
3. Stir in the eggs, which should be well beaten, the lemon-juice, which should be strained, and the wine
4. Sweeten to taste with pounded sugar
5. Mix all well together
6. Pour it into a jug, set this jug in a saucepan of boiling water over the fire, and keep stirring it one way until it thickens, but take care that it does not boil
7. Strain it into a mould that has been oiled or laid in water for a short time, and put it in a cool place to set
8. A tablespoonful of brandy stirred in just before it is poured into the mould improves the flavour of this dish

title: Dutch Sauce for Fish
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 teaspoonful of flour
- 2 oz. of butter
- 4 tablespoonfuls of vinegar
- the yolks of 2 eggs
- the juice of 1/2 lemon
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Put all the ingredients, except the lemon-juice, into a stew-pan
2. Set it over the fire, and keep continually stirring
3. When it is sufficiently thick, take it off, as it should not boil
4. If it happens to curdle, strain the sauce through a tammy
5. Add the lemon-juice, and serve

title: Economical Cake
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 1/4 lb. of sugar
- 1/4 lb. of butter or lard
- 1/2 lb. of currants
- 1 teaspoonful of carbonate of soda
- the whites of 4 eggs
- 1/2 pint of milk

## Directions

1. Beat the butter to a cream
2. Well whisk the whites of the eggs
3. Stir all the ingredients together but the soda
4. Add the soda when all is well mixed and the cake is ready to be put into the oven
5. After the mixture has been well beaten, stir in the soda
6. Put the cake into a buttered mould
7. Bake it in a moderate oven for 1-1/2 hour

title: Economical Lemon Cream
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of milk
- 8 bitter almonds
- 2 oz. of gelatine
- 2 large lemons
- 3/4 lb. of lump sugar
- the yolks of 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Put the milk into a lined saucepan with the almonds, which should be well pounded in a mortar, the gelatine, lemon-rind, and lump sugar, and boil these ingredients for about 5 minutes.
2. Beat up the yolks of the eggs, strain the milk into a jug, add the eggs, and pour the mixture backwards and forwards a few times, until nearly cold.
3. Stir briskly to it the lemon-juice, which should be strained, and keep stirring until the cream is almost cold.
4. Put it into an oiled mould, and let it remain until perfectly set.
5. The lemon-juice must not be added to the cream when it is warm, and should be well stirred after it is put in.

title: Economical Stock
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The liquor in which a joint of meat has been boiled, say 4 quarts
- trimmings of fresh meat or poultry
- shank-bones
- roast-beef bones
- any pieces the larder may furnish
- vegetables, spices, and the same seasoning as in the foregoing recipe

## Directions

1. Let all the ingredients simmer gently for 6 hours, taking care to skim carefully at first
2. Strain it off, and put by for use

title: Eel Broth
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of eels
- a small bunch of sweet herbs, including parsley
- 1/2 onion
- 10 peppercorns
- 3 pints of water
- 2 cloves
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. After having cleaned and skinned the eel, cut it into small pieces, and put it into a stewpan, with the other ingredients
2. Simmer gently until the liquid is reduced nearly half, carefully removing the scum as it rises
3. Strain it through a hair sieve; put it by in a cool place, and, when wanted, take off all the fat from the top, warm up as needed

title: Eel Pie
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of eels
- a little chopped parsley
- 1 shalot
- grated nutmeg
- pepper and salt to taste
- the juice of 1/2 a lemon
- small quantity of forcemeat
- 1/4 pint of béchamel
- puff paste

## Directions

1. Skin and wash the eels, cut them into pieces 2 inches long, and line the bottom of the pie-dish with forcemeat
2. Put in the eels, and sprinkle them with the parsley, shalots, nutmeg, seasoning, and lemon-juice, and cover with puff-paste
3. Bake for 1 hour, or rather more
4. make the béchamel hot, and pour it into the pie

title: Eel Soup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lbs. of eels
- 1 onion
- 2 oz. of butter
- 3 blades of mace
- 1 bunch of sweet herbs
- 1/4 oz. of peppercorns
- salt to taste
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 1/4 pint of cream
- 2 quarts of water

## Directions

1. Wash the eels, cut them into thin slices, and put them in the stewpan with the butter; let them simmer for a few minutes, then pour the water to them, and add the onion, cut in thin slices, the herbs, mace, and seasoning.
2. Simmer till the eels are tender, but do not break the fish.
3. Take them out carefully, mix the flour smoothly to a batter with the cream, bring it to a boil, pour over the eels, and serve.

title: Eels a la Tartare
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of eels
- 1 carrot
- 1 onion
- a little flour
