- 1 glass of sherry
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg to taste
- bread crumbs
- 1 egg
- 2 tablespoonfuls of vinegar
- butter

## Directions

1. Rub the butter on the bottom of the stewpan
2. Cut up the carrot and onion, and stir them over the fire for 5 minutes
3. Dredge in a little flour, add the wine and seasoning, and boil for 1/2 an hour
4. Skin and wash the eels, cut them into pieces, put them to the other ingredients, and simmer till tender
5. When they are done, take them out, let them get cold, cover them with egg and bread crumbs, and fry them of a nice brown
6. Put them on a dish, pour sauce piquante over, and serve them hot

title: Eels en Matelote
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 or 6 young onions
- a few mushrooms, when obtainable
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg to taste
- 1 laurel-leaf
- 1/2 pint of port wine
- 1/2 pint of medium stock
- butter
- flour to thicken
- 2 lbs. of eels

## Directions

1. Rub the stewpan with butter, dredge in a little flour, add the onions cut very small, slightly brown them, and put in all the other ingredients
2. Wash, and cut up the eels into pieces 3 inches long; put them in the stewpan, and simmer for 1/2 hour
3. Make round the dish, a border of croutons, or pieces of toasted bread; arrange the eels in a pyramid in the centre, and pour over the sauce
4. Serve very hot

title: Effervescing Gooseberry Wine
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- To every gallon of water allow 6 lbs. of green gooseberries
- 3 lbs. of lump sugar

## Directions

1. Select unripe gooseberries that have nearly attained their full growth, before they show any tendency to ripen
2. Reject any bruised or decayed berries, and those that are very small
3. Remove the blossom and stalk ends
4. Well bruise the fruit in a tub or pan, in such quantities as to insure each berry being broken without crushing the seeds
5. Pour the warm water on the fruit, squeeze and stir it with the hand until all the pulp is removed from the skin and seeds
6. Cover the whole closely for 24 hours
7. Strain it through a coarse bag, and press it with as much force as can be conveniently applied, to extract the whole of the juice and liquor the fruit may contain
8. To every 40 or 50 lbs. of fruit one gallon more of hot water may be passed through the marc, or husks, in order to obtain any soluble matter that may remain, and be again pressed
9. Put the juice into a tub or pan of sufficient size to contain all of it, and add the sugar to it
10. Well stir until the sugar is dissolved, and place the pan in a warm situation
11. Keep it closely covered, and let it ferment for a day or two
12. Draw off into clean casks, placed a little on one side for the scum that arises to be thrown out
13. Keep the casks filled with the remaining must that should be reserved for that purpose
14. When the active fermentation has ceased, plug the casks upright, fill again if necessary, put in the bungs loosely
15. After a few days, when the fermentation is a little more languid, drive in the bungs tight, and make a spile-hole to give vent if necessary
16. About November or December, on a clear fine day, rack the wine from its lees into clean casks, which may be rinsed with brandy
17. After a month, examine to see if it is sufficiently clear for bottling; if not, fine with isinglass, which may be dissolved in some of the wine: 1 oz. will be sufficient for 9 gallons
18. In March or April, or when the gooseberry bushes begin to blossom, bottle the wine, in order to insure its being effervescing

title: Egg Balls For Soups and Made Dishes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 eggs
- a little flour
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Boil 6 eggs for 20 minutes, strip off the shells, take the yolks and pound them in a mortar.
2. Beat the yolks of the other 2 eggs; add them, with a little flour and salt, to those pounded; mix all well together, and roll into balls.
3. Boil them before they are put into the soup or other dish they may be intended for.

title: Egg Sauce For Salt Fish
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 1/2 pint of melted butter
- a very little lemon-juice when liked

## Directions

1. Boil the eggs until quite hard, which will be in about 20 minutes, and put them into cold water for 1/2 hour.
2. Strip off the shells, chop the eggs into small pieces, not, however, too fine.
3. Make the melted butter very smoothly, and, when boiling, stir in the eggs, and serve very hot.
4. Lemon-juice may be added at pleasure.

title: Egg Soup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- A tablespoonful of flour
- 4 eggs
- 2 small blades of finely-pounded mace
- 2 quarts of stock No. 105

## Directions

1. Beat up the flour smoothly in a teaspoonful of cold stock, and put in the eggs
2. Throw them into boiling stock, stirring all the time
3. Simmer for 1/4 of an hour
4. Season and serve with a French roll in the tureen, or fried sippets of bread

title: Egg Tea or Coffee
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 new-laid egg
- 1 large breakfast-cupful of tea or coffee

## Directions

1. Beat up the whole of the egg in a basin
2. Put it into a cup (or a portion of it, if the cup be small)
3. Pour over it the tea or coffee very hot, added very gradually
4. Stir all the time to prevent the egg from curdling

title: Egg Wine
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 tablespoonful and 1/2 glass of cold water
- 1 glass of sherry
- sugar and grated nutmeg to taste

## Directions

1. Beat the egg, mixing with it a tablespoonful of cold water
2. make the wine-and-water hot, but not boiling
3. pour it on the egg, stirring all the time
4. Add sufficient lump sugar to sweeten the mixture, and a little grated nutmeg
5. put all into a very clean saucepan, set it on a gentle fire, and stir the contents one way until they thicken, but do not allow them to boil
6. Serve in a glass with sippets of toasted bread or plain crisp biscuits

title: Eggs a la Maitre d'Hotel
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of fresh butter
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 1/2 pint of milk
- pepper and salt to taste
- 1 tablespoonful of minced parsley
- the juice of 1/2 lemon
- 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Put the flour and half the butter into a stewpan; stir them over the fire until the mixture thickens
2. pour in the milk, which should be boiling
3. add a seasoning of pepper and salt, and simmer the whole for 5 minutes
4. Put the remainder of the butter into the sauce, and add the minced parsley
5. boil the eggs hard, strip off the shells, cut the eggs into quarters, and put them on a dish
6. Bring the sauce to the boiling-point, add the lemon-juice, pour over the eggs, and serve

title: Eggs A La Tripe
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 eggs
- 3/4 pint of Béchamel sauce No. 368
- dessertspoonful of finely-minced parsley
- leaves of puff-paste or fried croûtons

## Directions

1. Boil the eggs hard
2. Put them into cold water and peel them
3. Take out the yolks whole and shred the whites
4. Make 3/4 pint of Béchamel sauce by recipe No. 368
5. Add the parsley to the sauce
6. When the sauce is quite hot, put the yolks of the eggs into the middle of the dish and the shred whites round them
7. Pour over the sauce
8. Garnish with leaves of puff-paste or fried croûtons

title: Elder Wine
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 gallons of water
- 1 peck of elderberries
- 3 lbs. of sugar per gallon of juice
- 1/2 oz. of ground ginger
- 6 cloves
- 1 lb. of good Turkey raisins
- 1/2 pint of brandy per gallon of wine
- 3 or 4 tablespoonfuls of fresh brewer's yeast to every 9 gallons of wine

## Directions

1. Pour the water, quite boiling, on the elderberries, which should be picked from the stalks
2. Let these stand covered for 24 hours
3. Strain the whole through a sieve or bag, breaking the fruit to express all the juice from it
4. Measure the liquor, and to every gallon allow the above proportion of sugar
5. Boil the juice and sugar with the ginger, cloves, and raisins for 1 hour
6. Skim the liquor the whole time
7. Let it stand until milk-warm
8. Put it into a clean dry cask with the yeast
9. Let it ferment for about a fortnight
10. Add the brandy
11. Bung up the cask and let it stand some months before it is bottled

title: Empress Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of rice
- 2 oz. of butter
- 3 eggs
- jam
- sufficient milk

## Directions

1. Boil the rice in the milk until very soft
2. Add the butter
3. Boil it for a few minutes after the latter ingredient is put in
4. Set it by to cool
5. Well beat the eggs
6. Stir these in
7. Line a dish with puff-paste
8. Put over this a layer of rice, then a thin layer of any kind of jam, then another layer of rice, and proceed in this manner until the dish is full
9. Bake in a moderate oven for 3/4 hour

title: Endive a la Francaise
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 heads of endive
- 1 pint of broth
- 3 oz. of fresh butter
- salt, pepper, and grated nutmeg to taste

## Directions

1. Wash and boil the endive as in the preceding recipe; chop it rather fine, and put into a stewpan with the broth.
2. Boil over a brisk fire until the sauce is all reduced.
3. Put in the butter, pepper, salt, and grated nutmeg (the latter must be very sparingly used).
4. Mix all well together, bring it to the boiling point, and serve very hot.

title: Epicurean Sauce For Steaks, Chops, Gravies, Or Fish
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pint of walnut ketchup
- 1/4 pint of mushroom ketchup
- 2 tablespoonfuls of Indian soy
- 2 tablespoonfuls of port wine
- 1/4 oz. of white pepper
- 2 oz. of shalots
- 1/4 oz. of cayenne
- 1/4 oz. of cloves
- 3/4 pint of vinegar

## Directions

1. Put the whole of the ingredients into a bottle, and let it remain for a fortnight in a warm place, occasionally shaking up the contents.
2. Strain, and bottle off for use.
3. This sauce will be found an agreeable addition to gravies, hashes, stews, &c.

title: Espagnole, Or Brown Spanish Sauce
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 slices of lean ham
- 1 lb. of veal
- 1-1/2 pint of white stock
- 2 or 3 sprigs of parsley
- 1/2 a bay-leaf
- 2 or 3 sprigs of savoury herbs
- 6 green onions
- 3 shalots
- 2 cloves
- 1 blade of mace
- 2 glasses of sherry or Madeira
- thickening of butter and flour

## Directions

1. Cut up the ham and veal into small square pieces, and put them into a stewpan.
2. Moisten these with 1/2 pint of the stock, and simmer till the bottom of the stewpan is covered with a nicely-coloured glaze, when put in a few more spoonfuls to detach it.
3. Add the remainder of the stock, with the spices, herbs, shalots, and onions, and simmer very gently for 1 hour.
4. Strain and skim off every particle of fat, and when required for use, thicken with butter and flour, or with a little roux.
5. Add the wine, and, if necessary, a seasoning of cayenne; when it will be ready to serve.

title: Everton Toffee
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of powdered loaf sugar
- 1 teacupful of water
- 1/4 lb. of butter
- 6 drops of essence of lemon

## Directions

1. Put the water and sugar into a brass pan, and beat the butter to a cream.
2. When the sugar is dissolved, add the butter, and keep stirring the mixture over the fire until it sets, when a little is poured on to a buttered dish.
3. Just before the toffee is done, add the essence of lemon.
4. Butter a dish or tin, pour on it the mixture, and when cool, it will easily separate from the dish.

title: Excellent Rolls
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 1 oz. of butter
- 1/4 pint of milk
- 1 large teaspoonful of yeast
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Warm the butter in the milk, add to it the yeast and salt, and mix these ingredients well together.
2. Put the flour into a pan, stir in the above ingredients, and let the dough rise, covered in a warm place.
3. Knead it well, make it into rolls, let them rise again for a few minutes, and bake in a quick oven.
4. Richer rolls may be made by adding 1 or 2 eggs and a larger proportion of butter, and their appearance improved by brushing the tops over with yolk of egg or a little milk.

title: Exeter Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 oz. of bread crumbs
- 4 oz. of sago
- 7 oz. of finely-chopped suet
- 6 oz. of moist sugar
- the rind of 1/2 lemon
- 1/4 pint of rum
- 7 eggs
- 4 tablespoonfuls of cream
- 4 small sponge cakes
- 2 oz. of ratafias
- 1/2 lb. of jam
- 3 tablespoonfuls of black-currant jelly
- 2 glasses of sherry
- butter

## Directions

1. Put the bread crumbs into a basin with the sago, suet, sugar, minced lemon-peel, rum, and 4 eggs
2. Stir these ingredients well together
3. Add 3 more eggs and the cream
4. Let the mixture be well beaten
5. Butter a mould
6. Strew in a few bread crumbs
7. Cover the bottom with a layer of ratafias
8. Put in a layer of the mixture
9. Then a layer of sliced sponge cake spread thickly with any kind of jam
10. Then add some ratafias, then some of the mixture and sponge cake, and so on until the mould is full
11. Take care that a layer of the mixture is on the top of the pudding
12. Bake in a good oven from 3/4 to 1 hour
13. Put 3 tablespoonfuls of black-currant jelly into a stewpan
14. Add 2 glasses of sherry
15. When warm, turn the pudding out of the mould
16. Pour the sauce over it, and serve hot

title: Fairy Butter
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of 2 hard-boiled eggs
- 1 tablespoonful orange-flower water
- 2 tablespoonfuls pounded sugar
- 1/4 lb. good fresh butter

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs smoothly in a mortar, with the orange-flower water and the sugar, until the whole is reduced to a fine paste
2. Add the butter
3. Force all through an old but clean cloth by wringing the cloth and squeezing the butter very hard
4. The butter will then drop on the plate in large and small pieces, according to the holes in the cloth

title: Fanchonnettes, or Custard Tartlets
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 3/4 pint of milk
- 2 oz. of butter
- 2 oz. of pounded sugar
- 3 dessertspoonfuls of flour
- flavouring to taste
- whites of 2 eggs
- 2 oz. of pounded sugar
- good puff-paste

## Directions

1. Well beat the eggs; stir to them the milk, the butter, which should be beaten to a cream, the sugar, and flour; mix these ingredients well together
2. Put them into a very clean saucepan, and bring them to the simmering point, but do not allow them to boil
3. Flavour with essence of vanilla, bitter almonds, lemon, grated chocolate, or any flavouring ingredient that may be preferred
4. Line some round tartlet-pans with good puff-paste; fill them with the custard, and bake in a moderate oven for about 20 minutes
5. Take them out of the pans; let them cool, and in the mean time whisk the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth
6. Stir into this the pounded sugar, and spread smoothly over the tartlets a little of this mixture
7. Put them in the oven again to set the icing, but be particular that they do not scorch
8. When the icing looks crisp, they are done
9. Arrange them, piled high in the centre, on a white napkin, and garnish the dish, and in between the tartlets, with strips of bright jelly, or very firmly-made preserve

title: Fennel Sauce For Mackerel
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of melted butter
- rather more than 1 tablespoonful of chopped fennel

## Directions

1. Make the melted butter very smoothly, by recipe No. 376
2. chop the fennel rather small, carefully cleansing it from any grit or dirt
3. put it to the butter when this is on the point of boiling
4. Simmer for a minute or two, and serve in a tureen

title: Fig Pudding I
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of figs
- 1 lb. of suet
- 1/2 lb. of flour
- 1/2 lb. of bread crumbs
- 2 eggs
- milk

## Directions

1. Cut the figs into small pieces, grate the bread finely, and chop the suet very small; mix these well together, add the flour, the eggs, which should be well beaten, and sufficient milk to form the whole into a stiff paste
2. butter a mould or basin, press the pudding into it very closely, tie it down with a cloth, and boil for 3 hours, or rather longer
3. turn it out of the mould, and serve with melted butter, wine-sauce, or cream

title: Fig Pudding II (Staffordshire Recipe)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of figs
- 6 oz. of suet
- 3/4 lb. of flour
- milk

## Directions

1. Chop the suet finely, mix with it the flour, and make these into a smooth paste with milk
2. roll it out to the thickness of about 1/2 inch, cut the figs in small pieces, and strew them over the paste
3. roll it up, make the ends secure, tie the pudding in a cloth, and boil it from 1-1/2 to 2 hours

title: Fillet of Veal au Bechamel (Cold Meat Cookery)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- A small fillet of veal
- 1 pint of Béchamel sauce No. 367
- a few bread crumbs
- clarified butter
- forcemeat

## Directions

1. Use a fillet of veal that has been roasted the preceding day
2. Cut the middle out rather deep, leaving a good margin round, from which to cut nice slices
3. If there should be any cracks in the veal, fill them up with forcemeat
4. Mince finely the meat that was taken out, mixing with it a little of the forcemeat to flavour, and stir to it sufficient Béchamel to make it of a proper consistency
5. Warm the veal in the oven for about an hour, taking care to baste it well, that it may not be dry
6. Put the mince in the place where the meat was taken out, sprinkle a few bread crumbs over it, and drop a little clarified butter on the bread crumbs
7. Put it into the oven for 1/4 hour to brown, and pour Béchamel round the sides of the dish

title: Filleted Soles A L'Italienne
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 soles
- salt, pepper, and grated nutmeg to taste
- egg and bread crumbs
- butter
- the juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Skin, and carefully wash the soles, separate the meat from the bone, and divide each fillet in two pieces.
2. Brush them over with white of egg, sprinkle with bread crumbs and seasoning, and put them in a baking-dish.
3. Place small pieces of butter over the whole, and bake for 1/2 hour.
4. When they are nearly done, squeeze the juice of a lemon over them, and serve on a dish, with Italian sauce poured over.

title: Fillets of Mackerel
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large mackerel
- 1 oz. butter
- 1 small bunch of chopped herbs
- 3 tablespoonfuls of medium stock
- 3 tablespoonfuls of béchamel
- salt, cayenne, and lemon-juice to taste

## Directions

1. Clean the fish, and fillet it; scald the herbs, chop them fine, and put them with the butter and stock into a stewpan.
2. Lay in the mackerel, and simmer very gently for 10 minutes; take them out, and put them on a hot dish.
3. Dredge in a little flour, add the other ingredients, give one boil, and pour it over the mackerel.

title: Fillets of Turbot a L'Italienne
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Remains of cold turbot
- Italian sauce

## Directions

1. Clear the fish carefully from the bone, and take away all skin, which gives an unpleasant flavour to the sauce
2. Make the sauce hot, lay in the fish to warm through, but do not let it boil
3. Garnish with croutons

title: Fish and Oyster Pie
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Any remains of cold fish, such as cod or haddock
- 2 dozen oysters
- pepper and salt to taste
- bread crumbs sufficient for the quantity of fish
- 1/2 teaspoonful of grated nutmeg
- 1 teaspoonful of finely-chopped parsley
- made melted butter or a little thin white sauce
- oyster-liquor

## Directions

1. Clear the fish from the bones, and put a layer of it in a pie-dish, which sprinkle with pepper and salt
2. Then a layer of bread crumbs, oysters, nutmeg, and chopped parsley
3. Repeat this till the dish is quite full
4. You may form a covering either of bread crumbs, which should be browned, or puff-paste, which should be cut into long strips, and laid in cross-bars over the fish, with a line of the paste first laid round the edge
5. Before putting on the top, pour in some made melted butter, or a little thin white sauce, and the oyster-liquor, and bake

title: Fish Cake
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of any cold fish
- 1 onion
- 1 faggot of sweet herbs
- salt and pepper to taste
- 1 pint of water
- equal quantities of bread crumbs and cold potatoes
- 1/2 teaspoonful of parsley
- 1 egg
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Pick the meat from the bones of the fish, which latter put, with the head and fins, into a stewpan with the water
2. Add pepper and salt, the onion and herbs, and stew slowly for gravy about 2 hours
3. Chop the fish fine, and mix it well with bread crumbs and cold potatoes, adding the parsley and seasoning
4. Make the whole into a cake with the white of an egg, brush it over with egg, cover with bread crumbs, and fry of a light brown
5. Strain the gravy, pour it over, and stew gently for 1/4 hour, stirring it carefully once or twice
6. Serve hot, and garnish with slices of lemon and parsley

title: Fish Pie, With Tench and Eels
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tench
- 2 eels
- 2 onions
- a faggot of herbs
- 4 blades of mace
- 3 anchovies
- 1 pint of water
- pepper and salt to taste
- 1 teaspoonful of chopped parsley
- the yolks of 6 hard-boiled eggs
- puff paste

## Directions

1. Clean and bone the tench, skin and bone the eels, and cut them into pieces 2 inches long, and leave the sides of the tench whole.
2. Put the bones into a stewpan with the onions, herbs, mace, anchovies, water, and seasoning, and let them simmer gently for 1 hour.
3. Strain it off, put it to cool, and skim off all the fat.
4. Lay the tench and eels in a pie-dish, and between each layer put seasoning, chopped parsley, and hard-boiled eggs.
5. Pour in part of the strained liquor, cover in with puff paste, and bake for 1/2 hour or rather more.
6. When done, heat the remainder of the liquor, which pour into the pie.

title: Fish Sauce
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 oz. of cayenne
- 2 tablespoonfuls of walnut ketchup
- 2 tablespoonfuls of soy
- a few shreds of garlic and shalot
- 1 quart of vinegar

## Directions

1. Put all the ingredients into a large bottle, and shake well every day for a fortnight
2. Keep it in small bottles well sealed, and in a few days it will be fit for use

title: Fish Scallop I
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Remains of cold fish of any sort
- 1/2 pint of cream
- 1/2 tablespoonful of anchovy sauce
- 1/2 teaspoonful of made mustard
- 1/2 teaspoonful of walnut ketchup
- pepper and salt to taste
- bread crumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Put all the ingredients into a stewpan, carefully picking the fish from the bones.
2. Set it on the fire, let it remain till nearly hot, occasionally stir the contents, but do not allow it to boil.
3. When done, put the fish into a deep dish or scallop shell, with a good quantity of bread crumbs.
4. Place small pieces of butter on the top, set in a Dutch oven before the fire to brown, or use a salamander.

title: Fish Scallop II
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Any cold fish
- 1 egg
- milk
- 1 large blade of pounded mace
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 1 teaspoonful of anchovy sauce
- pepper and salt to taste
- bread crumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Pick the fish carefully from the bones, and moisten with milk and the egg.
2. Add the other ingredients, and place in a deep dish or scallop shells.
3. Cover with bread crumbs, butter the top, and brown before the fire.
4. When quite hot, serve.

title: Fish Stock
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of beef or veal
- any kind of white fish trimmings, of fish which are to be dressed for table
- 2 onions
- the rind of 1/2 a lemon
- a bunch of sweet herbs
- 2 carrots
- 2 quarts of water

## Directions

1. Cut up the fish, and put it, with the other ingredients, into the water.
2. Simmer for 2 hours; skim the liquor carefully, and strain it.
3. When a richer stock is wanted, fry the vegetables and fish before adding the water.

title: Flanc of Apples, or Apples in a Raised Crust
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 lb. of short crust No. 1211 or 1212
- 9 moderate-sized apples
- the rind and juice of 1/2 lemon
- 1/2 lb. of white sugar
- 3/4 pint of water
- a few strips of candied citron
- flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Make a short crust by either of the above recipes
2. Roll it out to the thickness of 1/2 inch and butter an oval mould
3. Line it with the crust and press it carefully all round the sides to obtain the form of the mould, but be particular not to break the paste
4. Pinch the part that just rises above the mould with the paste-pincers
5. Fill the case with flour and bake it for about 3/4 hour
6. Take it out of the oven and remove the flour
7. Put the case back in the oven for another 1/4 hour and do not allow it to get scorched
8. Peel and take out the cores of the apples with a small knife or a cutter without dividing the apples
9. Put them into a small lined saucepan with sugar, water, lemon juice and rind in the above proportion
10. Let them simmer very gently until tender
11. Take out the apples and let them cool
12. Arrange them in the flanc or case
13. Boil down the syrup until reduced to a thick jelly
14. Pour it over the apples and garnish them with a few slices of candied citron

title: Flanc of Apricots, or Compote of Apricots in a Raised Crust
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 lb. of short crust No. 1212
- from 9 to 12 good-sized apricots
- 3/4 pint of water
- 1/2 lb. of sugar

## Directions

1. Make a short crust by recipe No. 1212, and line a mould with it as directed in recipe No. 1391
2. Boil the sugar and water together for 10 minutes
3. Halve the apricots, take out the stones, and simmer them in the syrup until tender
4. Watch them carefully, and take them up the moment they are done, for fear they break
5. Arrange them neatly in the flanc or case
6. Boil the syrup until reduced to a jelly
7. Pour it over the fruit, and serve either hot or cold

title: Fluted Rolls
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff-paste
- white of an egg
- sifted sugar
- jelly or preserve

## Directions

1. Make some good puff-paste; roll it out to the thickness of 1/4 inch, and, with a round fluted paste-cutter, stamp out as many round pieces as may be required
2. Brush over the upper side with the white of an egg
3. Roll up the pieces, pressing the paste lightly together where it joins
4. Place the rolls on a baking-sheet, and bake for about 1/4 hour
5. A few minutes before they are done, brush them over with the white of an egg; strew over sifted sugar, put them back in the oven
6. When the icing is firm and of a pale brown colour, they are done
7. Place a strip of jelly or preserve across each roll, dish them high on a napkin, and serve cold

title: Folkestone Pudding-Pies
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of milk
- 3 oz. of ground rice
- 3 oz. of butter
- 1/4 lb. of sugar
- flavouring of lemon-peel or bay-leaf
- 6 eggs
- puff-paste
- currants

## Directions

1. Infuse 2 laurel or bay leaves, or the rind of 1/2 lemon, in the milk, and when it is well flavoured, strain it, and add the rice
2. boil these for 1/4 hour, stirring all the time
3. then take them off the fire, stir in the butter, sugar, and eggs, and let these latter be well beaten before they are added to the other ingredients
4. when nearly cold, line some patty-pans with puff-paste, fill with the custard, strew over each a few currants, and bake from 20 to 25 minutes in a moderate oven

title: For a Summer Draught
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lemon
- 1 tumbler-ful of cold water
- pounded sugar to taste
- 4 small teaspoonful of carbonate of soda

## Directions

1. Squeeze the juice from the lemon and strain it
2. Add it to the water, with sufficient pounded sugar to sweeten the whole nicely
3. When well mixed, put in the soda
4. Stir well
5. Drink while the mixture is in an effervescing state

title: For Curing Hams (Mons. Ude's Recipe)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of moist sugar
- 1 lb. of common salt
- 2 oz. of saltpetre
- 1 quart of good vinegar

## Directions

1. As soon as the pig is cold enough to be cut up, take the 2 hams and rub them well with common salt, and leave them in a large pan for 3 days.
2. When the salt has drawn out all the blood, drain the hams, and throw the brine away.
3. Mix sugar, salt, and saltpetre together in the above proportion, rub the hams well with these, and put them into a vessel large enough to hold them, always keeping the salt over them.
4. Let them remain for 3 days, then pour over them a quart of good vinegar.
5. Turn them in the brine every day for a month, then drain them well, and rub them with bran.
6. Have them smoked over a wood fire, and be particular that the hams are hung as high up as possible from the fire; otherwise the fat will melt, and they will become dry and hard.

title: Forcemeat Balls For Fish Soups
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 middling-sized lobster
- 1/2 an anchovy
- 1 head of boiled celery
- the yolk of a hard-boiled egg
- salt, cayenne, and mace to taste
- 4 tablespoonfuls of bread crumbs
- 2 oz. of butter
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Pick the meat from the shell of the lobster, and pound it, with the soft parts, in a mortar
2. add the celery, the yolk of the hard-boiled egg, seasoning, and bread crumbs
3. Continue pounding till the whole is nicely amalgamated
4. Warm the butter till it is in a liquid state
5. well whisk the eggs, and work these up with the pounded lobster-meat
6. Make into balls of about an inch in diameter, and fry of a nice pale brown

title: Forcemeat for Baked Pike
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 oz. of bread crumbs
- 1 teaspoonful of minced savoury herbs
- 8 oysters
- 2 anchovies
- 2 oz. of suet
- salt
- pepper
- pounded mace to taste
- 6 tablespoonfuls of cream or milk
- yolks of 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Beard and mince the oysters
2. Prepare and mix the other ingredients by recipe No. 416, and blend the whole thoroughly together
3. Moisten with the cream and eggs
4. Put all into a stewpan, and stir it over the fire till it thickens
5. Put it into the fish, which should have previously been cut open, and sew it up

title: Forcemeat For Cold Savoury Pies
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of veal
- 1 lb. of fat bacon
- salt, cayenne, pepper, and pounded mace to taste
- a very little nutmeg
- chopped lemon-peel
- 1/2 teaspoonful of chopped parsley
- 1/2 teaspoonful of minced savoury herbs
- 1 or 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Chop the veal and bacon together, and put them in a mortar with the other ingredients mentioned above
2. Pound well, and bind with 1 or 2 eggs which have been previously beaten and strained
3. Work the whole well together, and the forcemeat will be ready for use
4. If the pie is not to be eaten immediately, omit the herbs and parsley, as these would prevent it from keeping
5. Mushrooms or truffles may be added

title: Forcemeat For Pike, Carp, Haddock, And Various Kinds Of Fish
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 oz. of fresh butter
- 1 oz. of suet
- 1 oz. of fat bacon
- 1 small teaspoonful of minced savoury herbs, including parsley
- a little onion, when liked, shredded very fine
- salt, nutmeg, and cayenne to taste
- 4 oz. of bread crumbs
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Mix all the ingredients well together, carefully mincing them very finely
2. beat up the egg, moisten with it, and work the whole very smoothly together
3. Oysters or anchovies may be added to this forcemeat, and will be found a great improvement

title: Forcemeat For Veal, Turkeys, Fowls, Hare, &c.
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 oz. of ham or lean bacon
- 1/4 lb. of suet
- the rind of half a lemon
- 1 teaspoonful of minced parsley
- 1 teaspoonful of minced sweet herbs
- salt, cayenne, and pounded mace to taste
- 6 oz. of bread crumbs
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Shred the ham or bacon, chop the suet, lemon-peel, and herbs, taking particular care that all be very finely minced
2. add a seasoning to taste, of salt, cayenne, and mace, and blend all thoroughly together with the bread crumbs, before wetting
3. Now beat and strain the eggs, work these up with the other ingredients, and the forcemeat will be ready for use
4. When it is made into balls, fry of a nice brown, in boiling lard, or put them on a tin and bake for 1/2 hour in a moderate oven
5. For very delicate forcemeat, it is advisable to pound the ingredients together before binding with the egg; but for ordinary cooking, mincing very finely answers the purpose
6. In forcemeat for HARE, the liver of the animal is sometimes added. Boil for 5 minutes, mince it very small, and mix it with the other ingredients. If it should be in an unsound state, it must be on no account made use of.

title: Fowl and Rice Croquettes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of rice
- 1 quart of stock or broth
- 3 oz. of butter
- minced fowl
- egg
- bread crumbs
- fried parsley

## Directions

1. Put the rice into the proportion of cold stock or broth, and let it boil very gently for 1/2 hour
2. Add the butter, and simmer it till quite dry and soft
3. When cold, make it into balls, hollow out the inside, and fill with minced fowl made by recipe No. 956
4. Cover over with rice, dip the balls into egg, sprinkle them with bread crumbs, and fry a nice brown
5. Dish them, and garnish with fried parsley
6. Oysters, white sauce, or a little cream, may be stirred into the rice before it cools

title: Fowl and Rice Croquettes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of rice
- 1 quart of stock or broth
- 3 oz. of butter
- minced fowl
- egg
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Put the rice into the above proportion of cold stock or broth, and let it boil very gently for 1/2 hour
2. then add the butter, and simmer it till quite dry and soft
3. When cold, make it into balls, hollow out the inside, and fill with minced fowl made by recipe No. 956
4. The mince should be rather thick
5. Cover over with rice, dip the balls into egg, sprinkle them with bread crumbs, and fry a nice brown
6. Dish them, and garnish with fried parsley

title: Fowl Pillau
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of rice
- 2 oz. of butter
- a fowl
- 2 quarts of stock or good broth
- 40 cardamum-seeds
- 1/2 oz. of coriander-seed
- 1/4 oz. of cloves
- 1/4 oz. of allspice
- 1/4 oz. of mace
- 1/4 oz. of cinnamon
- 1/2 oz. of peppercorns
- 4 onions
- 6 thin slices of bacon
- 2 hard-boiled eggs
- flour

## Directions

1. Well wash 1 lb. of the best Patna rice, put it into a frying-pan with the butter, which keep moving over a slow fire until the rice is lightly browned
2. Truss the fowl as for boiling, put it into a stewpan with the stock or broth
3. Pound the spices and seeds thoroughly in a mortar, tie them in a piece of muslin, and put them in with the fowl
4. Let it boil slowly until it is nearly done; then add the rice, which should stew until quite tender and almost dry
5. Cut the onions into slices, sprinkle them with flour, and fry, without breaking them, of a nice brown colour
6. Have ready the slices of bacon curled and grilled, and the eggs boiled hard
7. Lay the fowl in the form of a pyramid upon a dish, smother with the rice, garnish with the bacon, fried onions, and the hard-boiled eggs cut into quarters, and serve very hot
8. Before taking the rice out, remove the spices

title: Fowl Sauté With Peas
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast fowl
- 2 oz. of butter
- pepper, salt, and pounded mace to taste
- 1 dessertspoonful of flour
- 1/2 pint of weak stock
- 1 pint of green peas
- 1 teaspoonful of pounded sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the fowl into nice pieces
2. Put the butter into a stew-pan
3. Sautez or fry the fowl a nice brown colour, previously sprinkling it with pepper, salt, and pounded mace
4. Dredge in the flour, shake the ingredients well round, then add the stock and peas, and stew till the latter are tender, which will be in about 20 minutes
5. Put in the pounded sugar, and serve, placing the chicken round, and the peas in the middle of the dish
6. When liked, mushrooms may be substituted for the peas

title: Fowl Scollops
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast or boiled fowl
- 1/2 pint of Béchamel or white sauce

## Directions

1. Strip off the skin from the fowl
2. Cut the meat into thin slices, and warm them in about 1/2 pint, or rather more, of Béchamel or white sauce
3. When quite hot, serve, and garnish the dish with rolled ham or bacon toasted

title: Fowl à la Mayonnaise
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- A cold roast fowl
- Mayonnaise sauce No. 468
- 4 or 5 young lettuces
- 4 hard-boiled eggs
- a few water-cresses
- endive

## Directions

1. Cut the fowl into neat joints, lay them in a deep dish, piling them high in the centre
2. Sauce the fowl with Mayonnaise made by recipe No. 468
3. Garnish the dish with young lettuces cut in halves, water-cresses, endive, and hard-boiled eggs; these may be sliced in rings, or laid on the dish whole, cutting off at the bottom a piece of the white, to make the egg stand
4. The sauce should not be poured over the fowls until the moment of serving

title: French Chicken Cutlets (Cold Meat Cookery)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast or boiled fowl
- fried bread
- clarified butter
- the yolk of 1 egg
- bread crumbs
- 1/2 teaspoonful of finely-minced lemon-peel
- salt to taste
- cayenne to taste
- mace to taste
- 1 oz. of butter
- 2 minced shalots
- a few slices of carrot
- a small bunch of savoury herbs, including parsley
- 1 blade of pounded mace
- 6 peppercorns
- 1/4 pint of gravy

## Directions

1. Cut the fowls into as many nice cutlets as possible; take a corresponding number of sippets about the same size, all cut one shape; fry them a pale brown, put them before the fire.
2. Dip the cutlets into clarified butter mixed with the yolk of an egg, cover with bread crumbs seasoned with lemon-peel, mace, salt, and cayenne.
3. Fry them for about 5 minutes, put each piece on one of the sippets, pile them high in the dish.
4. Put the butter into a stewpan, add the shalots, carrot, herbs, mace, and peppercorns; fry for 10 minutes or rather longer.
5. Pour in 1/2 pint of good gravy, made of the chicken bones, stew gently for 20 minutes, strain it, and serve with the cutlets.

title: French Mode of Cooking French Beans
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of French beans
- 3 oz. of fresh butter
- pepper and salt to taste
- juice of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Cut and boil the beans by the preceding recipe
2. When tender, put them into a stewpan and shake over the fire to dry away the moisture from the beans
3. When quite dry and hot, add the butter, pepper, salt, and lemon-juice
4. Keep moving the stewpan, without using a spoon, as that would break the beans
5. When the butter is melted and all is thoroughly hot, serve
6. If the butter should not mix well, add a tablespoonful of gravy and serve very quickly

title: French Mode of Dressing Spinach
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pailfuls of spinach
- 2 tablespoonfuls of salt
- 2 oz. of butter
- 1 teaspoonful of flour
- 8 tablespoonfuls of good gravy
- when liked, a very little grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Pick, wash, and boil the spinach, as in recipe No. 1155, and when quite tender, drain and squeeze it perfectly dry from the water that hangs about it
2. Chop it very fine, put the butter into a stewpan, and lay the spinach over that
3. stir it over a gentle fire, and dredge in the flour
4. Add the gravy, and let it boil quickly for a few minutes, that it may not discolour
5. When the flavour of nutmeg is liked, grate some to the spinach, and when thoroughly hot, and the gravy has dried away a little, serve
6. Garnish the dish with sippets of toasted bread

title: French Onion Sauce, or Soubise
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of Béchamel
- 1 bay-leaf
- pounded mace
- cayenne
- 6 onions
- a small piece of ham

## Directions

1. Peel the onions and cut them in halves
2. Put them in a stewpan, with just sufficient water to cover them, and add the bay-leaf, ham, cayenne, and mace
3. Be careful to keep the lid closely shut, and simmer them until tender
4. Take them out and drain thoroughly
5. Rub them through a tammy or sieve with a wooden spoon, and put them to 1/2 pint of Béchamel
6. Keep stirring over the fire until it boils, when serve
7. If it should require any more seasoning, add it to taste

title: French Pancakes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 2 oz. of butter
- 2 oz. of sifted sugar
- 2 oz. of flour
- 1/2 pint of new milk

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs thoroughly, and put them into a basin with the butter, which should be beaten to a cream
2. Stir in the sugar and flour
3. When these ingredients are well mixed, add the milk
4. Keep stirring and beating the mixture for a few minutes
5. Put it on buttered plates, and bake in a quick oven for 20 minutes
6. Serve with a cut lemon and sifted sugar, or pile the pancakes high on a dish, with a layer of preserve or marmalade between each

title: French Puff-Paste, or Feuilletage
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 1 lb. of butter
- 1/2 saltspoonful of salt
- yolks of 2 eggs
- rather more than 1/4 pint of water

## Directions

1. Weigh the flour and ascertain that it is perfectly dry, and sift it
2. Squeeze all the water from the butter, and wring it in a clean cloth till there is no moisture remaining
3. Put the flour on the paste-board, work lightly into it 2 oz. of the butter, and then make a hole in the centre
4. Into this well put the yolks of 2 eggs, the salt, and about 1/4 pint of water
5. Knead up the paste quickly and lightly, and when quite smooth, roll it out square to the thickness of about 1/2 inch
6. Roll the butter into a ball, and place this ball of butter on the paste
7. Fold the paste over the butter all round, and secure it by wrapping it well all over
8. Flatten the paste by rolling it lightly with the rolling-pin until it is quite thin, but not thin enough to allow the butter to break through, keeping the board and paste dredged lightly with flour
9. Fold the paste in three, and roll out again, and should the weather be very warm, put it in a cold place on the ground to cool between the several turns
10. Roll out the paste again twice, put it by to cool, then roll it out twice more, which will make 6 turnings in all
11. Fold the paste in two, and it will be ready for use

title: French Rice Pudding, or Gateau de Riz
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of rice per 1 quart of milk
- the rind of 1 lemon
- 1/2 teaspoonful of salt
- sugar to taste
- 4 oz. of butter
- 6 eggs
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Put the milk into a stewpan with the lemon-rind, and let it infuse for 1/2 hour, or until the former is well flavoured
2. Take out the peel; have ready the rice washed, picked, and drained
3. Put it into the milk, and let it gradually swell over a very slow fire
4. Stir in the butter, salt, and sugar
5. When properly sweetened, add the yolks of the eggs, and then the whites, both of which should be well beaten, and added separately to the rice
6. Butter a mould, strew in some fine bread crumbs, and let them be spread equally over it
7. Carefully pour in the rice, and bake the pudding in a slow oven for 1 hour
8. Turn it out of the mould, and garnish the dish with preserved cherries, or any bright-coloured jelly or jam

title: Fricandeau of Veal
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of the fat side of a leg of veal (about 3 lbs.)
- lardoons
- 2 carrots
- 2 large onions
- a faggot of savoury herbs
- 2 blades of pounded mace
- 6 whole allspice
- 2 bay-leaves
- pepper to taste
- a few slices of fat bacon
- 1 pint of stock No. 107

## Directions

1. The veal for a fricandeau should be of the best quality, or it will not be good.
2. Take off the skin, flatten the veal on the table, then at one stroke of the knife, cut off as much as is required, for a fricandeau with an uneven surface never looks well.
3. Trim it, and with a sharp knife make two or three slits in the middle, that it may taste more of the seasoning.
4. Lard it thickly with fat bacon, as lean gives a red colour to the fricandeau.
5. Slice the vegetables, and put these, with the herbs and spices, in the middle of a stewpan, with a few slices of bacon at the top: these should form a sort of mound in the centre for the veal to rest upon.
6. Lay the fricandeau over the bacon, sprinkle over it a little salt, and pour in just sufficient stock to cover the bacon, &c., without touching the veal.
7. Let it gradually come to a boil; then put it over a slow and equal fire, and let it simmer very gently for about 2-1/2 hours, or longer should it be very large.
8. Baste it frequently with the liquor, and a short time before serving, put it into a brisk oven, to make the bacon firm, which otherwise would break when it was glazed.
9. Dish the fricandeau, keep it hot, skim off the fat from the liquor, and reduce it quickly to a glaze, with which glaze the fricandeau, and serve with a purée of whatever vegetable happens to be in season--spinach, sorrel, asparagus, cucumbers, peas, &c.

title: Fricandeau of Veal (More Economical)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The best end of a neck of veal (about 2-1/2 lbs.)
- lardoons
- 2 carrots
- 2 onions
- a faggot of savoury herbs
- 2 blades of mace
- 2 bay-leaves
- a little whole white pepper
- a few slices of fat bacon
- a little water

## Directions

1. Cut away the lean part of the best end of a neck of veal with a sharp knife, scooping it from the bones.
2. Put the bones in with a little water, which will serve to moisten the fricandeau; they should stew about 1-1/2 hour.
3. Lard the veal, proceed in the same way as in the preceding recipe, and be careful that the gravy does not touch the fricandeau.
4. Stew very gently for 3 hours; glaze, and serve it on sorrel, spinach, or with a little gravy in the dish.

title: Fricasseed Calf's Feet
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- A set of calf's feet
- for the batter allow for each egg 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 1 tablespoonful of bread crumbs
- hot lard or clarified dripping
- pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. If the feet are purchased uncleaned, dip them into warm water repeatedly, and scrape off the hair, first one foot and then the other, until the skin looks perfectly clean.
2. After washing and soaking in cold water, boil them in just sufficient water to cover them, until the bones come easily away.
3. Pick them out, and strain the liquor into a clean vessel, put the meat into a pie-dish until the next day.
4. Cut the meat down in slices about 1/2 inch thick, lay on them a stiff batter made of egg, flour, and bread crumbs in the above proportion; season with pepper and salt.
5. Plunge them into a pan of boiling lard and fry the slices a nice brown.
6. Dry them before the fire for a minute or two, dish them on a napkin, and garnish with tufts of parsley.
7. Serve with melted butter, mustard, and vinegar.

title: Fricasseed Calf's Head
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of a boiled calf's head
- 1-1/2 pint of the liquor in which the head was boiled
- 1 blade of pounded mace
- 1 onion minced
- a bunch of savoury herbs
- salt and white pepper to taste
- butter and flour for thickening
- the yolks of 2 eggs
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon-juice
- forcemeat balls
- broiled bacon

## Directions

1. Remove all the bones from the head, and cut the meat into nice square pieces.
2. Put 1-1/2 pint of the liquor it was boiled in into a saucepan, with mace, onion, herbs, and seasoning in the above proportion; let this simmer gently for 3/4 hour, then strain it and put in the meat.
3. When quite hot through, thicken the gravy with a little butter rolled in flour.
4. Just before dishing the fricassee, put in the beaten yolks of eggs and lemon-juice; but be particular, after these two latter ingredients are added, that the sauce does not boil, or it will curdle.
5. Garnish with forcemeat balls and curled slices of broiled bacon.
6. To insure the sauce being smooth, it is a good plan to dish the meat first, and then to add the eggs to the gravy: when these are set, the sauce may be poured over the meat.

title: Fricasseed Fowl
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast fowl
- 1 strip of lemon-peel
- 1 blade of pounded mace
- 1 bunch of savoury herbs
- 1 onion
- pepper and salt to taste
- 1 pint of water
- 1 teaspoonful of flour
- 1/4 pint of cream
- the yolks of 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Carve the fowls into nice joints; make gravy of the trimmings and legs, by stewing them with the lemon-peel, mace, herbs, onion, seasoning, and water, until reduced to 1/2 pint
2. Strain, and put in the fowl
3. Warm it through, and thicken with a teaspoonful of flour
4. Stir the yolks of the eggs into the cream
5. Add these to the sauce, let it get thoroughly hot, but do not allow it to boil, or it will curdle

title: Fricasseed Fowl or Chicken (an Entree)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 small fowls or 1 large one
- 3 oz. of butter
- a bunch of parsley and green onions
- 1 clove
- 2 blades of mace
- 1 shalot
- 1 bay-leaf
- salt and white pepper to taste
- 1/4 pint of cream
- yolks of 3 eggs
- flour
- button-mushrooms

## Directions

1. Choose a couple of fat plump chickens, and, after drawing, singeing, and washing them, skin, and carve them into joints
2. Blanch these in boiling water for 2 or 3 minutes
3. Take them out, and immerse them in cold water to render them white
4. Put the trimmings, with the necks and legs, into a stewpan
5. Add the parsley, onions, clove, mace, shalot, bay-leaf, and a seasoning of pepper and salt
6. Pour to these the water that the chickens were blanched in, and simmer gently for rather more than 1 hour
7. Have ready another stewpan; put in the joints of fowl, with the above proportion of butter
8. Dredge them with flour, let them get hot, but do not brown them much
9. Moisten the fricassee with the gravy made from the trimmings
10. Stew very gently for 1/2 hour
11. Lift the fowl into another stewpan, skim the sauce, reduce it quickly over the fire, by letting it boil fast, and strain it over them
12. Add the cream, and a seasoning of pounded mace and cayenne
13. Let it boil up, and when ready to serve, stir to it the well-beaten yolks of 3 eggs
14. The sauce should be made hot, but must not boil, or it will instantly curdle
15. A few button-mushrooms stewed with the fowl are by many persons considered an improvement

title: Fricasseed Soles
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 middling-sized soles
- 1 small sole
- 1/2 teaspoonful of chopped lemon-peel
- 1 teaspoonful of chopped parsley
- a little grated bread
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg to taste
- 1 egg
- 2 oz. butter
- 1/2 pint of good gravy
- 2 tablespoonfuls of port wine
- cayenne
- lemon-juice to taste

## Directions

1. Fry the soles of a nice brown, as directed in recipe No. 327, and drain them well from fat.
2. Take all the meat from the small sole, chop it fine, and mix with it the lemon-peel, parsley, bread, and seasoning.
3. Work altogether, with the yolk of an egg and the butter.
4. Make this into small balls, and fry them.
5. Thicken the gravy with a dessert-spoonful of flour, add the port wine, cayenne, and lemon-juice.
6. Lay in the 2 soles and balls; let them simmer gently for 6 minutes.
7. Serve hot, and garnish with cut lemon.

title: Fricasseed Turkey
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- remains of cold roast or boiled turkey
- strip of lemon-peel
- bunch of savoury herbs
- onion
- pepper and salt
- water
- cream
- yolk of an egg
- sippets of toasted bread

## Directions

1. Cut some nice slices from the remains of a cold turkey
2. Put the bones and trimmings into a stewpan with the lemon-peel, herbs, onion, pepper, salt, and water
3. Stew for an hour
4. Strain the gravy and lay in the pieces of turkey
5. When warm through, add the cream and the yolk of an egg
6. Stir it well round
7. When getting thick, take out the pieces, lay them on a hot dish, and pour the sauce over
8. Garnish the fricassée with sippets of toasted bread
9. Celery or cucumbers, cut into small pieces, may be put into the sauce; if the former, it must be boiled first

title: Fried Anchovies
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoonful of oil
- 1/2 a glass of white wine
- sufficient flour to thicken
- 12 anchovies

## Directions

1. Mix the oil and wine together, with sufficient flour to make them into a thickish paste
2. Cleanse the anchovies, wipe them, dip them in the paste, and fry of a nice brown colour

title: Fried Artichokes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 or 6 artichokes
- salt and water
- 1/4 lb. of flour
- a little salt
- the yolk of 1 egg
- milk
- lemon-juice
- hot lard or dripping
- crisped parsley
- plain melted butter

## Directions

1. Trim and boil the artichokes by recipe No. 1080, and rub them over with lemon-juice, to keep them white.
2. When they are quite tender, take them up, remove the chokes, and divide the bottoms.
3. Dip each piece into batter, fry them in hot lard or dripping, and garnish the dish with crisped parsley.
4. Serve with plain melted butter.

title: Fried Bread Crumbs
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- lard or clarified dripping

## Directions

1. Cut the bread into thin slices
2. Place them in a cool oven overnight
3. When thoroughly dry and crisp, roll them down into fine crumbs
4. Put some lard or clarified dripping into a frying-pan
5. Bring it to the boiling-point
6. Throw in the crumbs, and fry them very quickly
7. Lift them out with a slice as soon as done
8. Drain them before the fire from all greasy moisture
9. When quite crisp, they are ready for use

title: Fried Bread for Borders
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- lard or clarified dripping
- beaten white of an egg
- flour

## Directions

1. Cut slices of bread in any fanciful shape
2. Fry them in clear boiling lard or clarified dripping until quite crisp
3. Dip one side of the sippet into the beaten white of an egg mixed with a little flour
4. Place it on the edge of the dish
5. Continue in this manner till the border is completed
6. Arrange the sippets alternately, a pale and a dark one

title: Fried Cucumbers
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 cucumbers
- pepper and salt to taste
- flour
- oil or butter

## Directions

1. Pare the cucumbers and cut them into slices of an equal thickness, commencing to slice from the thick, and not the stalk end of the cucumber
2. Wipe the slices dry with a cloth, dredge them with flour, and put them into a pan of boiling oil or butter
3. Keep turning them about until brown
4. Lift them out of the pan, let them drain, and serve, piled lightly in a dish

title: Fried Eels
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of eels
- 1 egg
- a few bread crumbs
- flour
- hot lard
- fried parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. Wash the eels, cut them into pieces 3 inches long, trim and wipe them very dry
2. dredge with flour, rub them over with egg, and cover with bread crumbs
3. fry of a nice brown in hot lard
4. If the eels are small, curl them round, instead of cutting them up
5. Garnish with fried parsley

title: Fried Eggs
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 1/4 lb. of lard, butter or clarified dripping

## Directions

1. Place a delicately-clean frying-pan over a gentle fire.
2. Put in the fat, and allow it to come to the boiling-point.
3. Break the eggs into cups, slip them into the boiling fat, and let them remain until the whites are delicately set.
4. Whilst they are frying, ladle a little of the fat over them.
5. Take them up with a slice, drain them for a minute from their greasy moisture, trim them neatly.
6. Serve on slices of fried bacon or ham, or place the eggs in the middle of the dish, with the bacon put round as a garnish.

title: Fried Filleted Soles
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- large soles
- egg
- bread crumbs
- fried parsley
- cut lemon

## Directions

1. Skin and wash the fish, and raise the meat carefully from the bones, and divide it into nice handsome pieces.
2. Roll the fillets, after dividing each one in two pieces, and either bind them round with twine, or run a small skewer through them.
3. Brush over with egg, and cover with bread crumbs.
4. Fry them as directed in the foregoing recipe, and garnish with fried parsley and cut lemon.

title: Fried Flounders
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Flounders
- egg
- bread crumbs
- boiling lard
- crisped parsley

## Directions

1. Cleanse the fish, and, two hours before they are wanted, rub them inside and out with salt, to render them firm
2. Wash and wipe them very dry, dip them into egg, and sprinkle over with bread crumbs
3. Fry them in boiling lard, dish on a hot napkin, and garnish with crisped parsley

title: Fried Flounders
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Flounders
- egg
- bread crumbs
- boiling lard
- salt
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cleanse the fish, and, two hours before they are wanted, rub them inside and out with salt, to render them firm
2. wash and wipe them very dry
3. dip them into egg
4. sprinkle over with bread crumbs
5. fry them in boiling lard
6. dish on a hot napkin
7. garnish with crisped parsley

title: Fried Fowls I
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast fowls
- vinegar
- salt and cayenne to taste
- 3 or 4 minced shalots
- 1/2 lb. of flour
- 1/2 pint of hot water
- 2 oz. of butter
- the whites of 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Cut the fowl into nice joints
2. Steep them for an hour in a little vinegar, with salt, cayenne, and minced shalots
3. Make the batter by mixing the flour and water smoothly together
4. Melt in it the butter, and add the whites of egg beaten to a froth
5. Take out the pieces of fowl, dip them in the batter, and fry in boiling lard, a nice brown
6. Pile them high in the dish, and garnish with fried parsley or rolled bacon
7. When approved, a sauce or gravy may be served with them

title: Fried Fowls II
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast fowl
- vinegar
- salt and cayenne to taste
- 4 minced shalots
- yolk of egg
- bread crumbs
- 1 blade of pounded mace per teacupful of bread crumbs
- 5 teaspoonful of minced lemon-peel per teacupful of bread crumbs
- 1 saltspoonful of salt per teacupful of bread crumbs
- a few grains of cayenne per teacupful of bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Steep the pieces of fowl as in the preceding recipe
2. Dip them into the yolk of an egg or clarified butter
3. Sprinkle over bread crumbs with which have been mixed salt, mace, cayenne, and lemon-peel in the above proportion
4. Fry a light brown, and serve with or without gravy, as may be preferred

title: Fried Ham and Eggs
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Ham
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Cut the ham into slices, and take care that they are of the same thickness in every part.
2. Cut off the rind, and if the ham should be particularly hard and salt, soak it for about 10 minutes in hot water, and then dry it in a cloth.
3. Put it into a cold frying-pan, set it over the fire, and turn the slices 3 or 4 times whilst they are cooking.
4. When done, place them on a dish, which should be kept hot in front of the fire during the time the eggs are being poached.
5. Poach the eggs, slip them on to the slices of ham, and serve quickly.

title: Fried Kidneys
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Kidneys
- butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the kidneys open without quite dividing them and remove the skin
2. Put a small piece of butter in the frying-pan
3. When the butter is melted, lay in the kidneys the flat side downwards
4. Fry them for 7 or 8 minutes, turning them when they are half-done
5. Serve on a piece of dry toast
6. Season with pepper and salt, and put a small piece of butter in each kidney
7. Pour the gravy from the pan over them, and serve very hot

title: Fried Ox-Feet, or Cow-Heel
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Ox-feet
- the yolk of 1 egg
- bread crumbs
- parsley
- salt and cayenne to taste
- boiling butter

## Directions

1. Wash, scald, and thoroughly clean the feet, and cut them into pieces about 2 inches long
2. Have ready some fine bread crumbs mixed with a little minced parsley, cayenne, and salt
3. Dip the pieces of heel into the yolk of egg, sprinkle them with the bread crumbs, and fry them until of a nice brown in boiling butter

title: Fried Oysters
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 dozen oysters
- 2 oz. butter
- 1 tablespoonful of ketchup
- a little chopped lemon-peel
- 1/2 teaspoonful of chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Boil the oysters for 1 minute in their own liquor, and drain them; fry them with the butter, ketchup, lemon-peel, and parsley; lay them on a dish, and garnish with fried potatoes, toasted sippets, and parsley.

title: Fried Parsley, for Garnishing
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Parsley
- hot lard or clarified dripping

## Directions

1. Gather some young parsley; wash, pick, and dry it thoroughly in a cloth
2. Put it into the wire basket and hold it in boiling lard or dripping for a minute or two
3. Directly it is done, lift out the basket, and let it stand before the fire, that the parsley may become thoroughly crisp
4. Should the kitchen not be furnished with the wire basket, throw the parsley into the frying-pan, and when crisp, lift it out with a slice, dry it before the fire, and when thoroughly crisp, it will be ready for use

title: Fried Patties
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Cold roast veal
- a few slices of cold ham
- 1 egg boiled hard
- pounded mace
- pepper and salt to taste
- gravy
- cream
- 1 teaspoonful of minced lemon-peel
- good puff-paste

## Directions

1. Mince a little cold veal and ham, allowing one-third ham to two-thirds veal
2. Add an egg boiled hard and chopped, and a seasoning of pounded mace, salt, pepper, and lemon-peel
3. Moisten with a little gravy and cream
4. Make a good puff-paste; roll rather thin, and cut it into round or square pieces
5. Put the mince between two of them, pinch the edges to keep in the gravy, and fry a light brown
6. They may be also baked in patty-pans: in that case, they should be brushed over with the yolk of an egg before they are put in the oven
7. To make a variety, oysters may be substituted for the ham

title: Fried Perch
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- egg
- bread crumbs
- hot lard
- plain melted butter or anchovy sauce

## Directions

1. Scale and clean the fish, brush it over with egg, and cover with bread crumbs.
2. Have ready some boiling lard.
3. Put the fish in, and fry a nice brown.
4. Serve with plain melted butter or anchovy sauce.

title: Fried Plaice
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Hot lard, or clarified dripping
- egg and bread crumbs
- a little flour

## Directions

1. Wash and wipe the fish thoroughly dry, and let them remain in a cloth until it is time to dress them.
2. Brush them over with egg, and cover with bread crumbs mixed with a little flour.
3. Fry of a nice brown in hot dripping or lard, and garnish with fried parsley and cut lemon.
4. Send them to table with shrimp-sauce and plain melted butter.

title: Fried Potatoes (French Fashion)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Hot butter or clarified dripping
- Salt

## Directions

1. Peel and cut the potatoes into thin slices, as nearly the same size as possible.
2. Make some butter or dripping quite hot in a frying-pan.
3. Put in the potatoes, and fry them on both sides of a nice brown.
4. When they are crisp and done, take them up, place them on a cloth before the fire to drain the grease from them.
5. Serve very hot, after sprinkling them with salt.
6. The remains of cold potatoes may also be sliced and fried by the above recipe, but the slices must be cut a little thicker.

title: Fried Rabbit
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 rabbit
- flour
- dripping
- 1 oz. of butter
- 1 teaspoonful of minced shalot
- 2 tablespoonfuls of mushroom ketchup

## Directions

1. Cut the rabbit into neat joints, and flour them well; make the dripping boiling in a fryingpan, put in the rabbit, and fry it a nice brown.
2. Have ready a very hot dish, put in the butter, shalot, and ketchup; arrange the rabbit pyramidically on this, and serve as quickly as possible.
3. The rabbit may be brushed over with egg, and sprinkled with bread crumbs, and fried as above.
4. When cooked in this manner, make a gravy in the pan by recipe No. 866, and pour it round, but not over, the pieces of rabbit.

title: Fried Rashers of Bacon and Poached Eggs
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Bacon
- eggs

## Directions

1. Cut the bacon into thin slices, trim away the rusty parts, and cut off the rind.
2. Put it into a cold frying-pan, that is to say, do not place the pan on the fire before the bacon is in it.
3. Turn it 2 or 3 times, and dish it on a very hot dish.
4. Poach the eggs and slip them on to the bacon, without breaking the yolks, and serve quickly.

title: Fried Rump-Steak
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- steaks
- butter or clarified dripping
- pepper
- salt
- ketchup

## Directions

1. Put some butter or clarified dripping into a frying-pan; let it get quite hot, then lay in the steaks.
2. Turn them frequently until done, which will be in about 8 minutes, or rather more, should the steaks be very thick.
3. Serve on a very hot dish, in which put a small piece of butter and a tablespoonful of ketchup, and season with pepper and salt.
4. They should be sent to table quickly, as when cold, the steaks are entirely spoiled.
5. Optional gravy: As soon as the steaks are done, dish them, pour a little boiling water into the frying-pan, add a seasoning of pepper and salt, a small piece of butter, and a tablespoonful of Harvey's sauce or mushroom ketchup. Hold the pan over the fire for a minute or two, just let the gravy simmer, then pour on the steak, and serve.

title: Fried Salt Beef
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of cold salt beef
- pepper
- 1/4 lb. of butter
- mashed potatoes
- pickled gherkins
- liquor from mixed pickle

## Directions

1. Cut any part of cold salt beef into thin slices.
2. Fry them gently in butter, and season with a little pepper.
3. Have ready some very hot mashed potatoes, lay the slices of beef on them.
4. Garnish with 3 or 4 pickled gherkins.
5. Alternatively, cold salt beef may be warmed in a little liquor from mixed pickle, drained, and served as above.

title: Fried Sausages
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Sausages
- a small piece of butter

## Directions

1. Prick the sausages with a fork (this prevents them from bursting), and put them into a frying-pan with a small piece of butter.
2. Keep moving the pan about, and turn the sausages 3 or 4 times.
3. In from 10 to 12 minutes they will be sufficiently cooked, unless they are very large, when a little more time should be allowed for them.
4. Dish them with or without a piece of toast under them, and serve very hot.
5. Alternatively, in some counties, sausages are boiled and served on toast. They should be plunged into boiling water, and simmered for about 10 or 12 minutes.

title: Fried Sippets of Bread (for Garnishing Many Dishes)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- lard or clarified dripping

## Directions

1. Cut the bread into thin slices
2. Stamp them out in whatever shape you like—rings, crosses, diamonds, &c.
3. Fry them in clear boiling lard or clarified dripping in the same manner as bread crumbs
4. Drain them until thoroughly crisp before the fire
5. Fry some of a pale colour, and others of a darker hue for variety

title: Fried Soles
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 middling-sized soles
- hot lard or clarified dripping
- egg
- bread crumbs
- flour
- cut lemon
- fried parsley
- shrimp sauce
- plain melted butter

## Directions

1. Skin and carefully wash the soles, and cut off the fins, wipe them very dry, and let them remain in the cloth until it is time to dress them.
2. Have ready some fine bread crumbs and beaten egg.
3. Dredge the soles with a little flour, brush them over with egg, and cover with bread crumbs.
4. Put them in a deep pan, with plenty of clarified dripping or lard heated, so that it may neither scorch the fish nor make them sodden.
5. When they are sufficiently cooked on one side, turn them carefully, and brown them on the other.
6. They may be considered ready when a thick smoke rises.
7. Lift them out carefully, and lay them before the fire on a reversed sieve and soft paper, to absorb the fat.
8. Dish them on a hot napkin, garnish with cut lemon and fried parsley, and send them to table with shrimp sauce and plain melted butter.

title: Fried Sweetbreads a la Maitre d'Hotel
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 sweetbreads
- Egg and bread crumbs
- 1/4 lb. of butter
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Rather more than 1/2 pint of Maître d'hôtel sauce No. 466

## Directions

1. Soak the sweetbreads in warm water for an hour
2. Boil them for 10 minutes
3. Cut them in slices
4. Egg and bread crumb them
5. Season with pepper and salt
6. Put them into a frying-pan with the above proportion of butter
7. Keep turning them until done, which will be in about 10 minutes
8. Dish them
9. Pour over them a Maître d'hôtel sauce, made by recipe No. 466
10. The dish may be garnished with slices of cut lemon

title: Fried Vegetable Marrow
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 medium-sized vegetable marrows
- egg and bread crumbs
- hot lard
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Peel, and boil the marrows until tender in salt and water
2. Drain them and cut them in quarters, and take out the seeds
3. When thoroughly drained, brush the marrows over with egg, and sprinkle with bread crumbs
4. Have ready some hot lard, fry the marrow in this, and, when of a nice brown, dish
5. Sprinkle over a little salt and pepper, and serve

title: Fried Whiting
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg
- Bread crumbs
- A little flour
- Hot lard or clarified dripping
- Fried parsley
- Shrimp sauce
- Plain melted butter

## Directions

1. Take off the skin, clean, and thoroughly wipe the fish free from all moisture
2. Fasten the tail in the mouth by means of a small skewer
3. Brush the fish over with egg, dredge with a little flour, and cover with bread crumbs
4. Fry them in hot lard or clarified dripping of a nice colour, and serve them on a napkin, garnished with fried parsley
5. Send them to table with shrimp sauce and plain melted butter

title: Fruit Turnovers (Suitable for Pic-Nics)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff-paste
- any kind of fruit
- sugar to taste
- white of an egg
- sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Make some puff-paste; roll it out to the thickness of about 1/4 inch, and cut it out in pieces of a circular form
2. pile the fruit on half of the paste, sprinkle over some sugar, wet the edges and turn the paste over
3. Press the edges together, ornament them, and brush the turnovers over with the white of an egg; sprinkle over sifted sugar, and bake on tins, in a brisk oven, for about 20 minutes

title: Game Soup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- bones or remains of game
- 2 carrots
- 2 small onions
- 1 head of celery
- 1 turnip
- 1/4 lb. of pearl barley
- yolks of 3 eggs boiled hard
- 1/4 pint of cream
- salt to taste
- 2 quarts of stock

## Directions

1. Place the bones or remains of game in the stewpan, with the vegetables sliced
2. pour over the stock, and simmer for 2 hours
3. skim off all the fat, and strain it
4. Wash the barley, and boil it in 2 or 3 waters before putting it to the soup
5. finish simmering in the soup, and when the barley is done, take out half, and pound the other half with the yolks of the eggs
6. rub it through a clean tammy, add the cream, and salt if necessary
7. give one boil, and serve very hot, putting in the barley that was taken out first

title: Geneva Wafers
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 3 oz. of butter
- 3 oz. of flour
- 3 oz. of pounded sugar

## Directions

1. Well whisk the eggs; put them into a basin, and stir to them the butter, which should be beaten to a cream
2. Add the flour and sifted sugar gradually, and then mix all well together
3. Butter a baking-sheet, and drop on it a teaspoonful of the mixture at a time, leaving a space between each
4. Bake in a cool oven; watch the pieces of paste, and, when half done, roll them up like wafers, and put in a small wedge of bread or piece of wood, to keep them in shape
5. Return them to the oven until crisp
6. Before serving, remove the bread, put a spoonful of preserve in the widest end, and fill up with whipped cream

title: Genevese Sauce for Salmon, Trout, &c.
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small carrot
- a small faggot of sweet herbs, including parsley
- 1 onion
- 5 or 6 mushrooms (when obtainable)
- 1 bay-leaf
- 6 cloves
- 1 blade of mace
- 2 oz. of butter
- 1 glass of sherry
- 1-1/2 pint of white stock, No. 107
- thickening of butter and flour
- juice of half a lemon

## Directions

1. Cut up the onion and carrot into small rings
2. Put them into a stewpan with the herbs, mushrooms, bay-leaf, cloves, and mace
3. Add the butter
4. Simmer the whole very gently over a slow fire until the onion is quite tender
5. Pour in the stock and sherry
6. Stew slowly for 1 hour
7. Strain it off into a clean saucepan
8. Make a thickening of butter and flour and put it to the sauce
9. Stir it over the fire until perfectly smooth and mellow
10. Add the lemon-juice
11. Give one boil and it will be ready for table

title: German Method of Keeping Cucumbers for Winter Use
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Cucumbers
- salt

## Directions

1. Pare and slice the cucumbers (as for the table)
2. Sprinkle well with salt, and let them remain for 24 hours
3. Strain off the liquor
4. Pack in jars, a thick layer of cucumbers and salt alternately
5. Tie down closely
6. When wanted for use, take out the quantity required
7. Wash them well in fresh water, and dress as usual with pepper, vinegar, and oil

title: German Mode of Cooking Turnips
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 large turnips
- 3 oz. of butter
- pepper and salt to taste
- rather more than 1/2 pint of weak stock or broth
- 1 tablespoonful of flour

## Directions

1. Make the butter hot in a stewpan, lay in the turnips, after having pared and cut them into dice, and season them with pepper and salt
2. Toss them over the fire for a few minutes, then add the broth
3. Simmer the whole gently till the turnips are tender
4. Brown the above proportion of flour with a little butter
5. Add this to the turnips, let them simmer another 5 minutes, and serve

title: German Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 teaspoonfuls of flour
- 1 teaspoonful of arrowroot
- 1 pint of milk
- 2 oz. of butter
- sugar to taste
- the rind of 1/2 lemon
- 4 eggs
- 3 tablespoonfuls of brandy

## Directions

1. Boil the milk with the lemon-rind until well flavoured; then strain it, and mix with it the flour, arrowroot, butter, and sugar
2. Boil these ingredients for a few minutes, keeping them well stirred
3. then take them off the fire and mix with them the eggs, yolks and whites, beaten separately and added separately
4. Boil some sugar to candy; line a mould with this, put in the brandy, then the mixture; tie down with a cloth, and boil for rather more than 1 hour

title: Giblet Pie
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- A set of duck or goose giblets
- 1 lb. of rump-steak
- 1 onion
- 1/2 teaspoonful of whole black pepper
- a bunch of savoury herbs
- plain crust
- pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Clean, and put the giblets into a stewpan with an onion, whole pepper, and a bunch of savoury herbs.
2. Add rather more than a pint of water, and simmer gently for about 1-1/2 hour.
3. Take them out, let them cool, and cut them into pieces.
4. Line the bottom of a pie-dish with a few pieces of rump-steak.
5. Add a layer of giblets and a few more pieces of steak.
6. Season with pepper and salt, and pour in the gravy (which should be strained), that the giblets were stewed in.
7. Cover with a plain crust, and bake for rather more than 1-1/2 hour in a brisk oven.
8. Cover a piece of paper over the pie, to prevent the crust taking too much colour.

title: Giblet Soup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 sets of goose or duck giblets
- 2 lbs. of shin of beef
- a few bones
- 1 ox-tail
- 2 mutton-shanks
- 2 large onions
- 2 carrots
- 1 large faggot of herbs
- salt and pepper to taste
- 1/4 pint of cream
- 1 oz. of butter
- 1 dessert-spoonful of flour
- 3 quarts of water

## Directions

1. Scald the giblets, cut the gizzards in 8 pieces, and put them in a stewpan with the beef, bones, ox-tail, mutton-shanks, onions, herbs, pepper, and salt
2. Add the 3 quarts of water, and simmer till the giblets are tender, taking care to skim well
3. When the giblets are done, take them out, put them in your tureen, strain the soup through a sieve
4. Add the cream and butter, mixed with a dessert-spoonful of flour, boil it up a few minutes, and pour it over the giblets

title: Ginger Apples
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 oz. of whole ginger
- 1/4 pint of whiskey
- 3 lbs. of apples
- 2 lbs. of white sugar
- the juice of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Bruise the ginger, put it into a small jar, pour over sufficient whiskey to cover it, and let it remain for 3 days
2. Cut the apples into thin slices, after paring and coring them
3. Add the sugar and the lemon-juice, which should be strained
4. Simmer all together very gently until the apples are transparent, but not broken
5. Serve cold, and garnish the dish with slices of candied lemon-peel or preserved ginger

title: Ginger Beer
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2-1/2 lbs. of loaf sugar
- 1-1/2 oz. of bruised ginger
- 1 oz. of cream of tartar
- the rind and juice of 2 lemons
- 3 gallons of boiling water
- 2 large tablespoonfuls of thick and fresh brewer's yeast

## Directions

1. Peel the lemons, squeeze the juice, strain it, and put the peel and juice into a large earthen pan, with the bruised ginger, cream of tartar, and loaf sugar.
2. Pour over these ingredients 3 gallons of boiling water; let it stand until just warm, when add the yeast, which should be thick and perfectly fresh.
3. Stir the contents of the pan well, and let them remain near the fire all night, covering the pan over with a cloth.
4. The next day skim off the yeast, and pour the liquor carefully into another vessel, leaving the sediment; then bottle immediately, and tie the corks down, and in 3 days the ginger beer will be fit for use.

title: Ginger Cream
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The yolks of 4 eggs
- 1 pint of cream
- 3 oz. of preserved ginger
- 2 dessertspoonfuls of syrup
