- sifted sugar to taste
- 1 oz. of isinglass

## Directions

1. Slice the ginger finely; put it into a basin with the syrup, the well-beaten yolks of eggs, and the cream
2. Mix these ingredients well together, and stir them over the fire for about 10 minutes, or until the mixture thickens
3. Take it off the fire, whisk till nearly cold, sweeten to taste, add the isinglass, which should be melted and strained, and serve the cream in a glass dish
4. It may be garnished with slices of preserved ginger or candied citron

title: Ginger Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of flour
- 1/4 lb. of suet
- 1/4 lb. of moist sugar
- 2 large teaspoonfuls of grated ginger

## Directions

1. Shred the suet very fine, mix it with the flour, sugar, and ginger; stir all well together
2. butter a basin, and put the mixture in dry; tie a cloth over, and boil for 3 hours

title: Ginger Wine
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 gallons of water
- 27 lbs. of loaf sugar
- 9 lemons
- 12 oz. of bruised ginger
- 3 tablespoonfuls of yeast
- 2 lbs. of raisins stoned and chopped
- 1 pint of brandy

## Directions

1. Boil together for 1 hour in a copper the water, sugar, lemon-rinds, and bruised ginger
2. Remove every particle of scum as it rises
3. Put it into a large tub or pan when sufficiently boiled
4. When nearly cold, add the yeast, which must be thick and very fresh
5. The next day, put all in a dry cask with the strained lemon-juice and chopped raisins
6. Stir the wine every day for a fortnight
7. Add the brandy
8. Stop the cask down by degrees
9. In a few weeks it will be fit to bottle

title: Golden Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of bread crumbs
- 1/4 lb. of suet
- 1/4 lb. of marmalade
- 1/4 lb. of sugar
- 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Put the bread crumbs into a basin; mix with them the suet, which should be finely minced, the marmalade, and the sugar; stir all these ingredients well together
2. beat the eggs to a froth, moisten the pudding with these, and when well mixed, put it into a mould or buttered basin
3. tie down with a floured cloth, and boil for 2 hours
4. When turned out, strew a little fine-sifted sugar over the top, and serve

title: Good and Cheap Soup Made Without Meat
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 potatoes
- 4 turnips, or 2 if very large
- 2 carrots
- 2 onions
- 2 mushrooms, if obtainable
- 1 head of celery
- 1 large slice of bread
- 1 small saltspoonful of salt
- 1/4 saltspoonful of ground black pepper
- 2 teaspoonfuls of Harvey's sauce
- 6 quarts of water

## Directions

1. Peel the vegetables, and cut them up into small pieces
2. Toast the bread rather brown, and put all into a stewpan with the water and seasoning
3. Simmer gently for 3 hours, or until all is reduced to a pulp, and pass it through a sieve in the same way as pea-soup, which it should resemble in consistence; but it should be a dark brown colour
4. Warm it up again when required; put in the Harvey's sauce, and, if necessary, add to the flavouring

title: Good Bread with Wheat, Potato, and Rice Flour
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- ten parts wheat-flour
- five parts potato-flour
- one part rice-paste
- yeast

## Directions

1. Knead together
2. Add the yeast
3. Bake as usual

title: Gooseberry Jam I
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- red hairy gooseberries
- loaf sugar (3/4 lb. per lb. of fruit)
- red-currant juice (1/2 pint per 6 lbs. of fruit)
- oiled and egged papers

## Directions

1. Select red hairy gooseberries; have them gathered in dry weather, when quite ripe, without being too soft
2. Weigh them; with a pair of scissors, cut off the tops and tails
3. Prepare 1/2 pint of red-currant juice per 6 lbs. of fruit, drawn as for jelly
4. Put the gooseberries and currant-juice into a preserving-pan
5. Let them boil tolerably quickly, keeping them well stirred
6. When they begin to break, add to them the sugar, and keep simmering until the jam becomes firm
7. Carefully skim and stir it, that it does not burn at the bottom
8. It should be boiled rather a long time, or it will not keep
9. Put it into pots (not too large); let it get perfectly cold
10. Cover the pots down with oiled and egged papers

title: Gooseberry Jam II
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- red, rough, ripe gooseberries (8 lbs.)
- red-currant juice (1 quart)
- loaf sugar (5 lbs.)
- oiled paper
- tissue-paper
- white of an egg

## Directions

1. Have the fruit gathered in dry weather, and cut off the tops and tails
2. Prepare 1 quart of red-currant juice, the same as for red-currant jelly No. 1533
3. Put it into a preserving-pan with the sugar, and keep stirring until the latter is dissolved
4. Keep it boiling for about 5 minutes; skim well
5. Put in the gooseberries, and let them boil from 1/2 to 3/4 hour
6. Turn the whole into an earthen pan, and let it remain for 2 days
7. Boil the jam up again until it looks clear
8. Put it into pots, and when cold, cover with oiled paper
9. Over the jars put tissue-paper brushed over on both sides with the white of an egg, and store away in a dry place
10. Keep the jam well stirred and well skimmed, to prevent it burning at the bottom of the pan

title: Gooseberry Jelly
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- gooseberries
- loaf sugar (3/4 lb. per pint of juice)
- oiled and egged papers

## Directions

1. Put the gooseberries, after cutting off the tops and tails, into a preserving-pan, and stir them over the fire until they are quite soft
2. Strain them through a sieve, and to every pint of juice allow 3/4 lb. of sugar
3. Boil the juice and sugar together for nearly 3/4 hour, stirring and skimming all the time
4. If the jelly appears firm when a little of it is poured on to a plate, it is done
5. Take it up and put into small pots
6. Cover the pots with oiled and egged papers, the same as for currant jelly No. 1533, and store away in a dry place

title: Gooseberry Sauce for Boiled Mackerel
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of green gooseberries
- 3 tablespoonfuls of Béchamel, No. 367 (veal gravy may be substituted)
- 2 oz. of fresh butter
- salt, pepper, and grated nutmeg to taste

## Directions

1. Boil the gooseberries in water until quite tender
2. Strain them and rub them through a sieve
3. Put into a saucepan the Béchamel or gravy with the butter and seasoning
4. Add the pulp from the gooseberries
5. Mix all well together
6. Heat gradually through
7. A little pounded sugar may be added by those who consider it an improvement

title: Gooseberry Tart
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 pint of gooseberries
- 1/2 lb. of short crust
- 1/4 lb. of moist sugar

## Directions

1. With a pair of scissors cut off the tops and tails of the gooseberries
2. Put them into a deep pie-dish
3. Pile the fruit high in the centre
4. Put in the sugar
5. Line the edge of the dish with short crust
6. Put on the cover
7. Ornament the edges of the tart
8. Bake in a good oven for about 3/4 hour
9. Before being sent to table, strew over it some fine-sifted sugar
10. Serve with a jug of cream, or a dish of boiled or baked custards

title: Gooseberry Trifle
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of gooseberries
- sugar to taste
- 1 pint of custard No. 1423
- a plateful of whipped cream

## Directions

1. Put the gooseberries into a jar, with sufficient moist sugar to sweeten them, and boil them until reduced to a pulp
2. Put this pulp at the bottom of a trifle-dish
3. Pour over it a pint of custard made by recipe No. 1423, and, when cold, cover with whipped cream
4. The cream should be whipped the day before it is wanted for table, as it will then be so much firmer and more solid
5. The dish may be garnished as fancy dictates

title: Gooseberry Vinegar
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pecks of crystal gooseberries
- 6 gallons of water
- 12 lbs. of foots sugar of the coarsest brown quality

## Directions

1. Mash the gooseberries (which should be quite ripe) in a tub with a mallet
2. Put to them the water nearly milk-warm
3. Let this stand 24 hours
4. Strain it through a sieve and put the sugar to it
5. Mix it well and tun it
6. Stir the mixture from the bottom of the cask two or three times daily for three or four days to assist the melting of the sugar
7. Paste a piece of linen cloth over the bunghole
8. Set the cask in a warm place, but not in the sun
9. The following spring draw it off into stone bottles
10. The vinegar will be fit for use twelve months after it is made

title: Gravy for Roast Meat
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Gravy
- Salt

## Directions

1. Put a common dish with a small quantity of salt in it under the meat, about a quarter of an hour before it is removed from the fire.
2. When the dish is full, take it away, baste the meat, and pour the gravy into the dish on which the joint is to be served.

title: Gravy for Venison
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Trimmings of venison
- 3 or 4 mutton shank-bones
- salt to taste
- 1 pint of water
- 2 teaspoonfuls of walnut ketchup

## Directions

1. Brown the trimmings over a nice clear fire, and put them in a stewpan with the shank-bones and water
2. Simmer gently for 2 hours
3. Strain and skim, and add the walnut ketchup and a seasoning of salt
4. Let it just boil, when it is ready to serve

title: Gravy Made Without Meat For Fowls
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The necks, feet, livers, and gizzards of the fowls
- 1 slice of toasted bread
- 1/2 onion
- 1 faggot of savoury herbs
- salt and pepper to taste
- 1/2 pint of water
- thickening of butter and flour
- 1 dessertspoonful of ketchup

## Directions

1. Wash the feet of the fowls thoroughly clean, and cut them and the neck into small pieces
2. Put these into a stewpan with the bread, onion, herbs, seasoning, livers, and gizzards
3. Pour the water over them and simmer gently for 1 hour
4. Take out the liver, pound it, and strain the liquor to it
5. Add a thickening of butter and flour, and a flavouring of mushroom ketchup
6. Boil it up and serve

title: Gravy Soup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 lbs. of shin of beef
- 1 knuckle of veal weighing 5 lbs.
- a few pieces or trimmings of veal
- 2 slices of nicely-flavoured lean ham
- 1/4 lb. of butter
- 2 onions
- 2 carrots
- 1 turnip
- nearly a head of celery
- 1 blade of mace
- 6 cloves
- a bunch of savoury herb with endive
- salt and pepper to taste
- 3 lumps of sugar
- 5 quarts of boiling soft water

## Directions

1. Slightly brown the meat and ham in the butter, but do not let them burn
2. Pour to it the water, and as the scum rises, take it off
3. When no more scum appears, add all the other ingredients, and let the soup simmer slowly by the fire for 6 hours without stirring it any more from the bottom
4. Take it off, and let it settle
5. Skim off all the fat you can, and pass it through a tammy

title: Green Dutch Sauce, or Hollandaise Verte
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 tablespoonfuls of Béchamel
- salt and cayenne to taste
- parsley-green to colour
- juice of 1/2 a lemon

## Directions

1. Put the Béchamel into a saucepan with the seasoning, and bring it to a boil.
2. Make a green colouring by pounding some parsley in a mortar, and squeezing all the juice from it.
3. Let this just simmer, when add it to the sauce.
4. A moment before serving, put in the lemon-juice, but not before; for otherwise the sauce would turn yellow, and its appearance be thus spoiled.

title: Green Peas A La Francaise
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts of green peas
- 3 oz. of fresh butter
- A bunch of parsley
- 6 green onions
- Flour
- A small lump of sugar
- Salt
- Boiling water

## Directions

1. Shell sufficient fresh-gathered peas to fill 2 quarts
2. Put them into cold water, with the butter, and stir them about until they are well covered with the butter
3. Drain them in a colander, and put them in a stewpan, with the parsley and onions
4. Dredge over them a little flour, stir the peas well, and moisten them with boiling water
5. Boil them quickly over a large fire for 20 minutes, or until there is no liquor remaining
6. Dip a small lump of sugar into some water, that it may soon melt; put it with the peas, to which add 1/2 teaspoonful of salt
7. Take a piece of butter the size of a walnut, work it together with a teaspoonful of flour; and add this to the peas, which should be boiling when it is put in
8. Keep shaking the stewpan, and, when the peas are nicely thickened, dress them high in the dish, and serve

title: Green Sauce for Green Geese or Ducklings
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pint of sorrel-juice
- 1 glass of sherry
- 1/2 pint of green gooseberries
- 1 teaspoonful of pounded sugar
- 1 oz. of fresh butter

## Directions

1. Boil the gooseberries in water until they are quite tender
2. Mash them and press them through a sieve
3. Put the pulp into a saucepan with the above ingredients
4. Simmer for 3 or 4 minutes
5. Serve very hot

title: Greengage Jam
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. fruit, weighed before being stoned
- 3/4 lb. lump sugar
- kernels from the fruit, blanched

## Directions

1. Divide the greengages, take out the stones, and put them into a preserving-pan.
2. Bring the fruit to a boil, then add the sugar, and keep stirring it over a gentle fire until it is melted.
3. Remove all the scum as it rises.
4. Just before the jam is done, boil it rapidly for 5 minutes.
5. To ascertain when it is sufficiently boiled, pour a little on a plate, and if the syrup thickens and appears firm, it is done.
6. Have ready half the kernels blanched; put them into the jam, give them one boil, and pour the preserve into pots.
7. When cold, cover down with oiled papers, and, over these, tissue-paper brushed over on both sides with the white of an egg.

title: Greengage Jam
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- greengages (weighed before being stoned)
- lump sugar (3/4 lb. per lb. of fruit)

## Directions

1. Divide the greengages, take out the stones, and put them into a preserving-pan
2. Bring the fruit to a boil, then add the sugar, and keep stirring it over a gentle fire until it is melted
3. Remove all the scum as it rises
4. Just before the jam is done, boil it rapidly for 5 minutes
5. To ascertain when it is sufficiently boiled, pour a little on a plate, and if the syrup thickens and appears firm, it is done

title: Grouse Pie
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Grouse
- cayenne, salt, and pepper to taste
- 1 lb. of rump-steak
- 1/2 pint of well-seasoned broth
- puff paste
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Line the bottom of a pie-dish with the rump-steak cut into neat pieces.
2. If the grouse are large, cut them into joints; if small, they may be laid in the pie whole.
3. Season highly with salt, cayenne, and black pepper.
4. Pour in the broth and cover with puff paste.
5. Brush the crust over with the yolk of an egg.
6. Bake from 3/4 to 1 hour.
7. If the grouse are cut into joints, stew the backbones and trimmings with an onion, a little sherry, a bunch of herbs, and a blade of mace to make gravy, and pour this in after the pie is baked.

title: Grouse Salad
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 eggs
- butter
- fresh salad
- 1 or 2 grouse
- 1 teaspoonful of minced shalot
- 1 teaspoonful of pounded sugar
- the yolk of 1 egg
- 1 teaspoonful of minced parsley
- 1/4 oz. of salt
- 4 tablespoonfuls of oil
- 2 tablespoonfuls of Chili vinegar
- 1 gill of cream

## Directions

1. Boil the eggs hard, shell them, throw them into cold water
2. Cut a thin slice off the bottom to facilitate the proper placing of them in the dish
3. Cut each one into four lengthwise
4. Make a very thin flat border of butter, about one inch from the edge of the dish the salad is to be served on
5. Fix the pieces of egg upright close to each other, the yolk outside, or the yolk and white alternately
6. Lay in the centre a fresh salad of whatever is in season
7. Having previously roasted the grouse rather underdone, cut it into eight or ten pieces
8. Put the shalots into a basin, with the sugar, the yolk of an egg, the parsley, and salt, and mix in by degrees the oil and vinegar
9. When these ingredients are well mixed, put the sauce on ice or in a cool place
10. When ready to serve, whip the cream rather thick, which lightly mix with it
11. Lay the inferior parts of the grouse on the salad, sauce over so as to cover each piece
12. Lay over the salad and the remainder of the grouse, pour the rest of the sauce over, and serve
13. The eggs may be ornamented with a little dot of radishes or beetroot on the point
14. Anchovy and gherkin, cut into small diamonds, may be placed between, or cut gherkins in slices, and a border of them laid round
15. Tarragon or chervil-leaves are also a pretty addition

title: Gudgeons
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- egg
- bread crumbs
- hot lard
- flour

## Directions

1. Do not scrape off the scales, but take out the gills and inside
2. cleanse thoroughly
3. wipe them dry
4. flour and dip them into egg
5. sprinkle over with bread crumbs
6. fry of a nice brown

title: Gurnet, or Gurnard
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 gurnet
- salt
- water
- parsley
- butter
- anchovy sauce

## Directions

1. Cleanse the fish thoroughly
2. cut off the fins
3. have ready some boiling water, with salt in the proportion of 6 oz. of salt to each gallon of water
4. put the fish in
5. simmer very gently for 1/2 hour
6. serve with parsley and butter, or anchovy sauce

title: Half-Pay Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of suet
- 1/4 lb. of currants
- 1/4 lb. of raisins
- 1/4 lb. of flour
- 1/4 lb. of bread crumbs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of treacle
- 1/2 pint of milk

## Directions

1. Chop the suet finely
2. Mix with it the currants, which should be nicely washed and dried
3. Add the raisins, which should be stoned
4. Add the flour, bread crumbs, and treacle
5. Moisten with the milk
6. Beat up the ingredients until all are thoroughly mixed
7. Put them into a buttered basin
8. Boil the pudding for 3-1/2 hours

title: Ham Omelet
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 4 oz. of butter
- 1/2 saltspoonful of pepper
- 2 tablespoonfuls of minced ham

## Directions

1. Mince the ham very finely, without any fat, and fry it for 2 minutes in a little butter
2. Make the batter for the omelet, stir in the ham, and proceed as directed in recipe No. 1456
3. Do not add any salt to the batter, as the ham is usually sufficiently salt to impart a flavour to the omelet
4. Serve very hot and quickly, without gravy

title: Hare Soup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 hare fresh-killed
- 1 lb. of lean gravy-beef
- 1 slice of ham
- 1 carrot
- 2 onions
- 1 faggot of savoury herbs
- 1/4 oz. of whole black pepper
- browned flour
- 1/4 pint of port wine
- the crumb of two French rolls
- salt and cayenne to taste
- 3 quarts of water

## Directions

1. Skin and paunch the hare, saving the liver and as much blood as possible
2. Cut it in pieces, and put it in a stewpan with all the ingredients, and simmer gently for 8 hours
3. Strain through a sieve, put the best parts of the hare in the soup, and serve

title: Haricot Beans and Minced Onions
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of white haricot beans
- 4 middling-sized onions
- 1/4 pint of good brown gravy
- pepper and salt to taste
- a little flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Peel and mince the onions not too finely, and fry them in butter of a light brown colour
2. Dredge over them a little flour, and add the gravy and a seasoning of pepper and salt
3. Have ready a pint of haricot beans well boiled and drained; put them with the onions and gravy
4. Mix all well together, and serve very hot

title: Haricot Mutton
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 lbs. of the middle or best end of the neck of mutton
- 3 carrots
- 3 turnips
- 3 onions
- pepper and salt to taste
- 1 tablespoonful of ketchup or Harvey's sauce

## Directions

1. Trim off some of the fat, cut the mutton into rather thin chops, and put them into a frying-pan with the fat trimmings.
2. Fry of a pale brown, but do not cook them enough for eating.
3. Cut the carrots and turnips into dice, and the onions into slices, and slightly fry them in the same fat that the mutton was browned in, but do not allow them to take any colour.
4. Lay the mutton at the bottom of a stewpan, then the vegetables, and pour over them just sufficient boiling water to cover the whole.
5. Give one boil, skim well, and then set the pan on the side of the fire to simmer gently until the meat is tender.
6. Skim off every particle of fat, add a seasoning of pepper and salt, and a little ketchup, and serve.
7. Serve in rather a deep dish.

title: Haricot Mutton (Cold Meat Cookery)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold neck or loin of mutton
- 2 oz. of butter
- 3 onions
- 1 dessertspoonful of flour
- 1/2 pint of good gravy
- pepper and salt to taste
- 2 tablespoonfuls of port wine
- 1 tablespoonful of mushroom ketchup
- 2 carrots
- 2 turnips
- 1 head of celery

## Directions

1. Cut the cold mutton into moderate-sized chops, and take off the fat.
2. Slice the onions, and fry them with the chops, in a little butter, of a nice brown colour.
3. Stir in the flour, add the gravy, and let it stew gently nearly an hour.
4. Boil the vegetables until nearly tender, slice them, and add them to the mutton about 1/4 hour before it is to be served.
5. Season with pepper and salt, add the ketchup and port wine, give one boil, and serve.

title: Haricot Mutton II
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Breast or scrag of mutton
- flour
- pepper and salt to taste
- 1 large onion
- 3 cloves
- a bunch of savoury herbs
- 1 blade of mace
- carrots and turnips
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the mutton into square pieces, and fry them a nice colour.
2. Dredge over them a little flour and a seasoning of pepper and salt.
3. Put all into a stewpan, and moisten with boiling water, adding the onion stuck with 3 cloves, the mace, and herbs.
4. Simmer gently till the meat is nearly done, skim off all the fat, and then add the carrots and turnips, which should previously be cut in dice and fried in a little sugar to colour them.
5. Let the whole simmer again for 10 minutes.
6. Take out the onion and bunch of herbs, and serve.

title: Haricots Blancs a la Maitre d'Hotel
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of white haricot beans
- 1/4 lb. of fresh butter
- 1 tablespoonful of minced parsley
- pepper and salt to taste
- the juice of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Should the beans be very dry, soak them for an hour or two in cold water, and boil them until perfectly tender, as in the preceding recipe.
2. If the water should boil away, replenish it with a little more cold, which makes the skin of the beans tender.
3. Let them be very thoroughly done; drain them well.
4. Add to them the butter, minced parsley, and a seasoning of pepper and salt.
5. Keep moving the stewpan over the fire without using a spoon, as this would break the beans.
6. When the various ingredients are well mixed with them, squeeze in the lemon-juice, and serve very hot.

title: Hashed Beef I
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- gravy saved from the meat
- 1 teaspoonful of tomato sauce
- 1 teaspoonful of Harvey's sauce
- 1 teaspoonful of good mushroom ketchup
- 1/2 glass of port wine or strong ale
- pepper
- salt
- flour
- 1 onion finely minced
- slices of cold roast beef
- sippets of toasted bread

## Directions

1. Put all the ingredients but the beef into a stewpan with whatever gravy may have been saved from the meat the day it was roasted.
2. Let these simmer gently for 10 minutes.
3. Take the stewpan off the fire; let the gravy cool, and skim off the fat.
4. Cut the beef into thin slices, dredge them with flour, and lay them in the gravy.
5. Let the whole simmer gently for 5 minutes, but not boil, or the meat will be tough and hard.
6. Serve very hot, and garnish with sippets of toasted bread.

title: Hashed Beef II
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- remains of ribs or sirloin of beef
- 2 onions
- 1 carrot
- 1 bunch of savoury herbs
- pepper
- salt
- 1/2 blade of pounded mace
- flour
- rather more than 1 pint of water
- sippets of toasted bread

## Directions

1. Take off all the meat from the bones of ribs or sirloin of beef.
2. Remove the outside brown and gristle; place the meat on one side.
3. Well stew the bones and pieces with the onions, carrot, herbs, pepper, salt, and mace for about 2 hours, till it becomes a strong gravy, and is reduced to rather more than 1/2 pint.
4. Strain this, thicken with a teaspoonful of flour, and let the gravy cool.
5. Skim off all the fat.
6. Lay in the meat, let it get hot through, but do not allow it to boil.
7. Garnish with sippets of toasted bread.

title: Hashed Calf's Head (Cold Meat Cookery)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of a cold boiled calf's head
- 1 quart of the liquor in which it was boiled
- a faggot of savoury herbs
- 1 onion
- 1 carrot
- a strip of lemon-peel
- 2 blades of pounded mace
- salt and white pepper to taste
- a very little cayenne
- rather more than 2 tablespoonfuls of sherry
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon-juice
- 1 tablespoonful of mushroom ketchup
- forcemeat balls
- butter
- flour
- bacon

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into neat slices, and put the bones and trimmings into a stewpan with the liquor that the head was boiled in.
2. Add a bunch of savoury herbs, 1 onion, 1 carrot, a strip of lemon-peel, and 2 blades of pounded mace, and let these boil for 1 hour, or until the gravy is reduced nearly half.
3. Strain it into a clean stewpan, thicken it with a little butter and flour, and add a flavouring of sherry, lemon-juice, and ketchup, in the above proportion.
4. Season with pepper, salt, and a little cayenne.
5. Put in the meat, let it gradually warm through, but not boil more than two or three minutes.
6. Garnish the dish with forcemeat balls and pieces of bacon rolled and toasted, placed alternately, and send it to table very hot.

title: Hashed Duck
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast duck
- rather more than 1 pint of weak stock or water
- 1 onion
- 1 oz. of butter
- butter and flour for thickening
- salt and cayenne to taste
- 1/2 teaspoonful of minced lemon-peel
- 1 dessertspoonful of lemon-juice
- 1/2 glass of port wine
- sippets of toasted bread for garnish

## Directions

1. Cut the duck into nice joints
2. Put the trimmings into a stewpan
3. Slice and fry the onion in a little butter
4. Add the fried onion to the trimmings
5. Pour in the weak stock or water
6. Stew gently for 1 hour
7. Strain the liquor
8. Thicken it with butter and flour
9. Season with salt and cayenne
10. Add the lemon-peel, lemon-juice, and port wine
11. Boil it up and skim well
12. Lay in the pieces of duck
13. Let them get thoroughly hot through by the side of the fire, but do not allow them to boil
14. Let them soak in the gravy for about 1/2 hour
15. Garnish with sippets of toasted bread

title: Hashed Fowl
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- remains of cold roast fowl
- 1 pint of water
- 1 onion
- 2 or three small carrots
- 1 blade of pounded mace
- pepper and salt
- 1 small bunch of savoury herbs
- butter and flour
- 1-1/2 tablespoonful of mushroom ketchup
- sippets of toasted bread

## Directions

1. Cut off the best joints from the fowl
2. Make the remainder into gravy by adding to the bones and trimmings a pint of water, an onion sliced and fried of a nice brown, the carrots, mace, seasoning, and herbs
3. Let these stew gently for 1-1/2 hour
4. Strain the liquor, and thicken with a little flour and butter
5. Lay in the fowl, thoroughly warm it through, add the ketchup
6. Garnish with sippets of toasted bread

title: Hashed Fowl, Indian Fashion
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast fowl
- 3 or 4 sliced onions
- 1 apple
- 2 oz. of butter
- pounded mace
- pepper and salt to taste
- 1 tablespoonful of curry-powder
- 2 tablespoonfuls of vinegar
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 1 teaspoonful of pounded sugar
- 1 pint of gravy

## Directions

1. Cut the onions into slices, mince the apple, and fry these in the butter
2. Add pounded mace, pepper, salt, curry-powder, vinegar, flour, and sugar in the above proportions
3. When the onion is brown, put in the gravy, which should be previously made from the bones and trimmings of the fowls, and stew for 3/4 hour
4. Add the fowl cut into nice-sized joints, let it warm through, and when quite tender, serve
5. The dish should be garnished with an edging of boiled rice

title: Hashed Game
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Remains of cold game
- 1 onion stuck with 3 cloves
- a few whole peppers
- a strip of lemon-peel
- salt to taste
- thickening of butter and flour
- 1 glass of port wine
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon-juice
- 1 tablespoonful of ketchup
- 1 pint of water or weak stock

## Directions

1. Cut the remains of cold game into joints, reserving the best pieces.
2. Put the inferior pieces and trimmings into a stewpan with the onion, pepper, lemon-peel, salt, and water or weak stock.
3. Stew these for about an hour and strain the gravy.
4. Thicken the gravy with butter and flour.
5. Add the wine, lemon-juice, and ketchup.
6. Lay in the pieces of game and let them gradually warm through by the side of the fire.
7. Do not allow it to boil, or the game will be hard.
8. When on the point of simmering, serve and garnish the dish with sippets of toasted bread.

title: Hashed Goose
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast goose
- 2 onions
- 2 oz. of butter
- 1 pint of boiling water
- 1 dessertspoonful of flour
- pepper and salt to taste
- 1 tablespoonful of port wine
- 2 tablespoonfuls of mushroom ketchup
- sippets of toasted bread

## Directions

1. Cut up the goose into pieces of the size required.
2. Put the inferior joints, trimmings, &c., into a stewpan to make the gravy.
3. Slice and fry the onions in the butter of a very pale brown.
4. Add these to the trimmings, and pour over about a pint of boiling water.
5. Stew these gently for 3/4 hour, then skim and strain the liquor.
6. Thicken it with flour, and flavour with port wine and ketchup, in the above proportion.
7. Add a seasoning of pepper and salt, and put in the pieces of goose.
8. Let these get thoroughly hot through, but do not allow them to boil, and serve with sippets of toasted bread.

title: Hashed Hare
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast hare
- 1 blade of pounded mace
- 2 or 3 allspice
- pepper and salt to taste
- 1 onion
- a bunch of savoury herbs
- 3 tablespoonfuls of port wine
- thickening of butter and flour
- 2 tablespoonfuls of mushroom ketchup

## Directions

1. Cut the cold hare into neat slices, and put the head, bones, and trimmings into a stewpan, with 3/4 pint of water.
2. Add the mace, allspice, seasoning, onion, and herbs, and stew for nearly an hour, and strain the gravy.
3. Thicken it with butter and flour, add the wine and ketchup, and lay in the pieces of hare, with any stuffing that may be left.
4. Let the whole gradually heat by the side of the fire, and, when it has simmered for about 5 minutes, serve, and garnish the dish with sippets of toasted bread.
5. Send red-currant jelly to table with it.

title: Hashed Lamb and Broiled Blade-Bone
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of a cold shoulder of lamb
- pepper and salt to taste
- 2 oz. of butter
- about 1/2 pint of stock or gravy
- 1 tablespoonful of shalot vinegar
- 3 or 4 pickled gherkins

## Directions

1. Take the blade-bone from the shoulder, and cut the meat into collops as neatly as possible
2. Season the bone with pepper and salt, pour a little oiled butter over it, and place it in the oven to warm through
3. Put the stock into a stewpan, add the ketchup and shalot vinegar, and lay in the pieces of lamb
4. Let these heat gradually through, but do not allow them to boil
5. Take the blade-bone out of the oven, and place it on a gridiron over a sharp fire to brown
6. Slice the gherkins, put them into the hash, and dish it with the blade-bone in the centre
7. It may be garnished with croutons or sippets of toasted bread

title: Hashed Mutton
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast shoulder or leg of mutton
- 6 whole peppers
- 6 whole allspice
- a faggot of savoury herbs
- 1/2 head of celery
- 1 onion
- 2 oz. of butter
- flour
- ketchup
- Harvey's sauce
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the meat in nice even slices from the bones, trimming off all superfluous fat and gristle.
2. Chop the bones and fragments of the joint, put them into a stewpan with the pepper, spice, herbs, and celery.
3. Cover with water, and simmer for 1 hour.
4. Slice and fry the onion of a nice pale-brown colour, dredge in a little flour to make it thick, and add this to the bones, &c.
5. Stew for 1/4 hour, strain the gravy, and let it cool.
6. Skim off every particle of fat, and put it, with the meat, into a stewpan.
7. Flavour with ketchup, Harvey's sauce, tomato sauce, or any flavouring that may be preferred, and let the meat gradually warm through, but not boil, or it will harden.

title: Hashed Pork
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast pork
- 2 onions
- 1 teaspoonful of flour
- 2 blades of pounded mace
- 2 cloves
- 1 tablespoonful of vinegar
- 1/2 pint of gravy
- pepper and salt to taste
- sippets of toasted bread

## Directions

1. Chop the onions and fry them of a nice brown.
2. Cut the pork into thin slices, season them with pepper and salt, and add these to the remaining ingredients.
3. Stew gently for about 1/2 hour, and serve garnished with sippets of toasted bread.

title: Hashed Turkey
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast turkey
- 1 onion
- pepper and salt to taste
- rather more than 1 pint of water
- 1 carrot
- 1 turnip
- 1 blade of mace
- a bunch of savoury herbs
- 1 tablespoonful of mushroom ketchup
- 1 tablespoonful of port wine
- thickening of butter and flour

## Directions

1. Cut the turkey into neat joints; the best pieces reserve for the hash, the inferior joints and trimmings put into a stewpan with an onion cut in slices, pepper and salt, a carrot, turnip, mace, herbs, and water in the above proportion
2. Simmer these for an hour, then strain the gravy
3. Thicken it with butter and flour, flavour with ketchup and port wine, and lay in the pieces of turkey to warm through
4. If there is any stuffing left, put that in also, as it so much improves the flavour of the gravy
5. When it boils, serve, and garnish the dish with sippets of toasted bread

title: Hashed Venison
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of roast venison
- its own or mutton gravy
- butter and flour for thickening

## Directions

1. Cut the meat from the bones in neat slices
2. If there is sufficient of its own gravy left, put the meat into this, as it is preferable to any other
3. Should there not be enough, put the bones and trimmings into a stewpan, with about a pint of mutton gravy; let them stew gently for an hour, and strain the gravy
4. Put a little flour and butter into the stewpan, keep stirring until brown, then add the strained gravy, and give it a boil up; skim and strain again, and, when a little cool, put in the slices of venison
5. Place the stewpan by the side of the fire, and, when on the point of simmering, serve: do not allow it to boil, or the meat will be hard
6. Send red-currant jelly to table with it

title: Hashed Wild Duck
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast wild duck
- 1 pint of good brown gravy
- 2 tablespoonfuls of bread crumbs
- 1 glass of claret
- salt
- cayenne
- mixed spices to taste
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon or Seville orange-juice

## Directions

1. Cut the remains of the duck into neat joints, put them into a stewpan, with all the above ingredients.
2. Let them get gradually hot by the side of the fire, and occasionally stir the contents.
3. When on the point of boiling, serve, and garnish the dish with sippets of toasted bread.

title: Herb Powder for Flavouring, when Fresh Herbs are not obtainable
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 oz. of dried lemon-thyme
- 1 oz. of dried winter savory
- 1 oz. of dried sweet marjoram and basil
- 2 oz. of dried parsley
- 1 oz. of dried lemon-peel

## Directions

1. Prepare and dry the herbs by recipe No. 445
2. Pick the leaves from the stalks, pound them, and sift them through a hair-sieve
3. Mix in the above proportions, and keep in glass bottles, carefully excluding the air
4. This provides them ready for use at a moment's notice

title: Herodotus Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of bread crumbs
- 1/2 lb. of good figs
- 6 oz. of suet
- 6 oz. of moist sugar
- 1/2 saltspoonful of salt
- 3 eggs
- nutmeg to taste

## Directions

1. Mince the suet and figs very finely
2. Add the remaining ingredients, taking care that the eggs are well whisked
3. Beat the mixture for a few minutes
4. Put it into a buttered mould
5. Tie it down with a floured cloth
6. Boil the pudding for 5 hours
7. Serve with wine sauce

title: Hessian Soup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 ox's head
- 1 pint of split peas
- 3 carrots
- 6 turnips
- 6 potatoes
- 6 onions
- 1 head of celery
- 1 bunch of savoury herbs
- pepper and salt to taste
- 2 blades of mace
- a little allspice
- 4 cloves
- the crumb of a French roll
- 6 quarts of water

## Directions

1. Clean the head, rub it with salt and water, and soak it for 5 hours in warm water
2. Simmer it in the water till tender, put it into a pan and let it cool
3. Skim off all the fat
4. Take out the head, and add the vegetables cut up small, and the peas which have been previously soaked
5. Simmer them without the meat, till they are done enough to pulp through a sieve
6. Add the seasoning, with pieces of the meat cut up
7. Give one boil, and serve

title: Hodge-Podge
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of shin of beef
- 3 quarts of water
- 1 pint of table-beer
- 2 onions
- 2 carrots
- 2 turnips
- 1 head of celery
- pepper and salt to taste
- thickening of butter and flour

## Directions

1. Put the meat, beer, and water in a stewpan; simmer for a few minutes, and skim carefully.
2. Add the vegetables and seasoning; stew gently till the meat is tender.
3. Thicken with the butter and flour, and serve with turnips and carrots, or spinach and celery.

title: Hodge-Podge
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- About 1 lb. of underdone cold mutton
- 2 lettuces
- 1 pint of green peas
- 5 or 6 green onions
- 2 oz. of butter
- pepper and salt to taste
- 1/2 teacupful of water

## Directions

1. Mince the mutton, and cut up the lettuces and onions in slices.
2. Put these in a stewpan, with all the ingredients except the peas, and let these simmer very gently for 3/4 hour, keeping them well stirred.
3. Boil the peas separately, mix these with the mutton, and serve very hot.

title: Hodge-Podge (Cold Meat Cookery)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- About 1 lb. of underdone cold mutton
- 2 lettuces
- 1 pint of green peas
- 5 or 6 green onions
- 2 oz. of butter
- pepper and salt to taste
- 1/2 teacupful of water

## Directions

1. Mince the mutton, and cut up the lettuces and onions in slices.
2. Put these in a stewpan, with all the ingredients except the peas, and let these simmer very gently for 3/4 hour, keeping them well stirred.
3. Boil the peas separately, mix these with the mutton, and serve very hot.

title: Home-Made Noyeau
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 oz. of bitter almonds
- 1 oz. of sweet almonds
- 1 lb. of loaf sugar
- the rinds of 3 lemons
- 1 quart of Irish whiskey or gin
- 1 tablespoonful of clarified honey
- 4 pint of new milk

## Directions

1. Blanch and pound the almonds, and mix with them the sugar, which should also be pounded
2. Boil the milk; let it stand till quite cold
3. Mix all the ingredients together, and let them remain for 10 days, shaking them every day
4. Filter the mixture through blotting-paper
5. Bottle off for use in small bottles, and seal the corks down

title: Honey Cake
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 breakfast-cupful of sugar
- 1 breakfast-cupful of rich sour cream
- 2 breakfast-cupfuls of flour
- 1/2 teaspoonful of carbonate of soda
- honey to taste

## Directions

1. Mix the sugar and cream together
2. Dredge in the flour, with as much honey as will flavour the mixture nicely
3. Stir it well, that all the ingredients may be thoroughly mixed
4. Add the carbonate of soda
5. Beat the cake well for another 5 minutes
6. Put it into a buttered tin
7. Bake it from 1/2 to 3/4 hour
8. Let it be eaten warm

title: Horseradish Sauce, to Serve with Roast Beef
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoonfuls of grated horseradish
- 1 teaspoonful of pounded sugar
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 1/2 teaspoonful of pepper
- 2 teaspoonfuls of made mustard
- vinegar
- 3 or 4 tablespoonfuls of cream

## Directions

1. Grate the horseradish, and mix it well with the sugar, salt, pepper, and mustard
2. Moisten it with sufficient vinegar to give it the consistency of cream
3. Serve in a tureen
4. To heat it to serve with hot roast beef, put it in a bain marie or a jar, which place in a saucepan of boiling water
5. Make it hot, but do not allow it to boil, or it will curdle

title: Horseradish Sauce, to serve with Roast Beef
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoonfuls of grated horseradish
- 1 teaspoonful of pounded sugar
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 1/2 teaspoonful of pepper
- 2 teaspoonfuls of made mustard
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Grate the horseradish, and mix it well with the sugar, salt, pepper, and mustard
2. Moisten it with sufficient vinegar to give it the consistency of cream
3. Serve in a tureen

title: Horseradish Vinegar
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of scraped horseradish
- 1 oz. of minced shalot
- 1 drachm of cayenne
- 1 quart of vinegar

## Directions

1. Put all the ingredients into a bottle, which shake well every day for a fortnight
2. When it is thoroughly steeped, strain and bottle
3. It will be fit for use immediately

title: Hot Crab
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 crab
- nutmeg
- salt and pepper to taste
- 3 oz. of butter
- 1/4 lb. of bread crumbs
- 3 tablespoonfuls of vinegar

## Directions

1. After having boiled the crab, pick the meat out from the shells, and mix with it the nutmeg and seasoning.
2. Cut up the butter in small pieces, and add the bread crumbs and vinegar.
3. Mix altogether, put the whole in the large shell, and brown before the fire or with a salamander.

title: Hot Lobster
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lobster
- 2 oz. of butter
- grated nutmeg
- salt, pepper, and pounded mace, to taste
- bread crumbs
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Pound the meat of the lobster to a smooth paste with the butter and seasoning, and add a few bread crumbs.
2. Beat the eggs, and make the whole mixture into the form of a lobster; pound the spawn, and sprinkle over it.
3. Bake 1/4 hour, and just before serving, lay over it the tail and body shell, with the small claws underneath, to resemble a lobster.

title: Hot Spice, a Delicious Adjunct to Chops, Steaks, Gravies, &c.
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 drachms of ginger
- 3 drachms of black pepper
- 3 drachms of cinnamon
- 7 cloves
- 1/2 oz. mace
- 1/4 oz. of cayenne
- 1 oz. grated nutmeg
- 1-1/2 oz. white pepper

## Directions

1. Pound the ingredients, and mix them thoroughly together, taking care that everything is well blended.
2. Put the spice in a very dry glass bottle for use.
3. The quantity of cayenne may be increased, should the above not be enough to suit the palate.

title: How To Boil A Ham To Give It An Excellent Flavour
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Vinegar and water
- 2 heads of celery
- 2 turnips
- 3 onions
- Large bunch of savoury herbs

## Directions

1. Prepare the ham as in the preceding recipe
2. Let it soak for a few hours in vinegar and water
3. Put it on in cold water
4. When it boils, add the vegetables and herbs
5. Simmer very gently until tender
6. Take it out and strip off the skin
7. Cover with bread-raspings
8. Put a paper ruche or frill round the knuckle

title: How to Mix Mustard
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Mustard
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Mix mustard with water that has been boiled and allowed to cool.
2. Put the mustard in a cup, with a small pinch of salt, and mix with it very gradually sufficient boiled water to make it drop from the spoon without being watery.
3. Stir and mix well, and rub the lumps well down with the back of a spoon, as well-mixed mustard should be perfectly free from these.

title: How to Use Cold Potatoes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Cold potatoes, 1 lb
- Flour, 2 tablespoonfuls per lb of potatoes
- Minced onions, 2 tablespoonfuls per lb of potatoes
- Butter, 1 oz per lb of potatoes
- Milk

## Directions

1. Mash the potatoes with a fork until perfectly free from lumps.
2. Stir in the flour, minced onions, and butter.
3. Add sufficient milk to moisten them well.
4. Press the potatoes into a mould, and bake in a moderate oven until nicely brown, which will be in from 20 minutes to 1/2 hour.
5. Turn them out of the mould, and serve.

title: Hunter's Beef
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 lbs. round of beef
- 3 oz. saltpetre
- 3 oz. coarse sugar
- 1 oz. cloves
- 1 grated nutmeg
- 1/2 oz. allspice
- 1 lb. salt
- 1/2 lb. bay-salt
- 1/2 pint water
- suet
- flour and water for crust

## Directions

1. Let the beef hang for 2 or 3 days, and remove the bone.
2. Pound spices, salt, &c. in the above proportion, and let them be reduced to the finest powder.
3. Put the beef into a pan, rub all the ingredients well into it, and turn and rub it every day for rather more than a fortnight.
4. When it has been sufficiently long in pickle, wash the meat, bind it up securely with tape, and put it into a pan with 1/2 pint of water at the bottom.
5. Mince some suet, cover the top of the meat with it, and over the pan put a common crust of flour and water.
6. Bake for 6 hours, and, when cold, remove the paste.
7. Save the gravy that flows from it. The beef may be glazed and garnished with meat jelly.

title: Hunter's Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of raisins
- 1 lb. of currants
- 1 lb. of suet
- 1 lb. of bread crumbs
- 3 lb. of moist sugar
- 8 eggs
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 3 lb. of mixed candied peel
- 1 glass of brandy
- 10 drops of essence of lemon
- 10 drops of essence of almonds
- 1/2 nutmeg
- 2 blades of mace
- 6 cloves

## Directions

1. Stone and shred the raisins rather small
2. Chop the suet finely
3. Rub the bread until all lumps are well broken
4. Pound the spice to powder
5. Cut the candied peel into thin shreds
6. Mix all these ingredients well together, adding the sugar
7. Beat the eggs to a strong froth
8. As they are beaten, drop into them the essence of lemon and essence of almonds
9. Stir these to the dry ingredients
10. Mix well
11. Add the brandy
12. Tie the pudding firmly in a cloth
13. Boil it for 6 hours at the least: 7 or 8 hours would be still better for it
14. Serve with boiled custard, or red-currant jelly, or brandy sauce

title: Iced Apple Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 dozen apples
- a small pot of apricot-jam
- 1/2 lb. of sugar
- 1 Seville orange
- 1/4 pint of preserved cherries
- 1/4 lb. of raisins
- 1 oz. of citron
- 2 oz. of almonds
- 1 gill of Curaçoa
- 1 gill of Maraschino
- 1 pint of cream

## Directions

1. Peel, core, and cut the apples into quarters, and simmer them over the fire until soft
2. Mix with them the apricot-jam and the sugar, on which the rind of the orange should be previously rubbed
3. Work all these ingredients through a sieve, and put them into the freezing-pot
4. Stone the raisins, and simmer them in a little syrup for a few minutes
5. Add these, with the sliced citron, the almonds cut in dice, and the cherries drained from their syrup, to the ingredients in the freezing-pot
6. Put in the Curaçoa and Maraschino, and freeze again
7. Add as much whipped cream as will be required, freeze again, and fill the mould
8. Put the lid on, and plunge the mould into the ice-pot
9. Cover it with a wet cloth and pounded ice and saltpetre, where it should remain until wanted for table
10. Turn the pudding out of the mould on to a clean and neatly-folded napkin, and serve with iced whipped cream in a sauce-tureen or glass dish

title: Iced Apples, or Apple Hedgehog
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- About 3 dozen good boiling apples
- 1/2 lb. of sugar
- 1/2 pint of water
- the rind of 1/2 lemon minced very fine
- the whites of 2 eggs
- 3 tablespoonfuls of pounded sugar
- a few sweet almonds

## Directions

1. Peel and core a dozen of the apples without dividing them
2. Stew them very gently in a lined saucepan with 1/2 lb. of sugar and 1/2 pint of water until tender, then lift them carefully on to a dish
3. Pare, core, and cut the remainder of the apples into thin slices
4. Put them into the same syrup with the lemon-peel and boil gently until reduced to a marmalade, keeping them stirred to prevent burning
5. Cover the bottom of a dish with some of the marmalade
6. Layer the stewed apples over the marmalade, placing marmalade in the insides and between each apple
7. Continue layering, forming the whole into a raised oval shape
8. Whip the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth and mix with the pounded sugar
9. Cover the apples very smoothly all over with the icing
10. Blanch and cut each almond into 4 or 5 strips
11. Place these strips at equal distances over the icing sticking up
12. Strew over a little rough pounded sugar
13. Place the dish in a very slow oven to colour the almonds and for the apples to get warm through

title: Iced Currants
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- red or white currants
- egg whites
- water
- fine pounded sugar

## Directions

1. Select very fine bunches of red or white currants, and well beat the whites of the eggs
2. Mix these with the water
3. Take the currants, a bunch at a time, and dip them in
4. Let them drain for a minute or two, and roll them in very fine pounded sugar
5. Lay them to dry on paper, when the sugar will crystallize round each currant

title: Iced Currants for Dessert
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pint of water
- whites of 2 eggs
- currants
- pounded sugar

## Directions

1. Select very fine bunches of red or white currants, and well beat the whites of the eggs.
2. Mix these with the water.
3. Take the currants, a bunch at a time, and dip them in.
4. Let them drain for a minute or two, and roll them in very fine pounded sugar.
5. Lay them to dry on paper, when the sugar will crystallize round each currant.

title: Iced Oranges
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Oranges
- pounded loaf sugar
- whites of eggs

## Directions

1. Whisk the whites of the eggs well, stir in the sugar, and beat this mixture for 1/4 hour
2. Skin the oranges, remove as much of the white pith as possible without injuring the pulp of the fruit
3. Pass a thread through the centre of each orange, dip them into the sugar, and tie them to a stick
4. Place this stick across the oven, and let the oranges remain until dry, when they will have the appearance of balls of ice
5. Care must be taken not to have the oven too fierce, or the oranges would scorch and acquire a brown colour

title: Iced Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of sweet almonds
- 2 oz. of bitter ones
- 3/4 lb. of sugar
- 8 eggs
- 1-1/2 pint of milk

## Directions

1. Blanch and dry the almonds thoroughly in a cloth
2. Pound them in a mortar until reduced to a smooth paste
3. Add to these the well-beaten eggs, the sugar, and milk
4. Stir these ingredients over the fire until they thicken, but do not allow them to boil
5. Strain and put the mixture into the freezing-pot
6. Surround it with ice
7. Freeze it as directed in recipe 1290
8. When quite frozen, fill an iced-pudding mould
9. Put on the lid
10. Keep the pudding in ice until required for table
11. Turn it out on the dish
12. Garnish it with a compôte of any fruit that may be preferred
13. Pour a little over the top of the pudding
14. This pudding may be flavoured with vanilla, Curaçoa, or Maraschino

title: Iced Rice Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 oz. of rice
- 1 quart of milk
- 1/2 lb. of sugar
- the yolks of 6 eggs
- 1 small teaspoonful of essence of vanilla

## Directions

1. Put the rice into a stewpan, with the milk and sugar, and let these simmer over a gentle fire until the rice is sufficiently soft to break up into a smooth mass
2. Should the milk dry away too much, a little more may be added
3. Stir the rice occasionally, to prevent its burning
4. Beat it to a smooth mixture
5. Add the yolks of the eggs, which should be well whisked, and the vanilla
6. Put this rice custard into the freezing-pot, and proceed as directed in recipe No. 1290
7. When wanted for table, turn the pudding out of the mould, and pour over the top, and round it, a compôte of oranges, or any other fruit that may be preferred

title: Indian Chetney Sauce
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 oz. of sharp, sour apples, pared and cored
- 8 oz. of tomatoes
- 8 oz. of salt
- 8 oz. of brown sugar
- 8 oz. of stoned raisins
- 4 oz. of cayenne
- 4 oz. of powdered ginger
- 2 oz. of garlic
- 2 oz. of shalots
- 3 quarts of vinegar
- 1 quart of lemon-juice

## Directions

1. Chop the apples in small square pieces, and add to them the other ingredients
2. Mix the whole well together, and put in a well-covered jar
3. Keep this in a warm place, and stir every day for a month, taking care to put on the lid after this operation
4. Strain, but do not squeeze it dry
5. Store it away in clean jars or bottles for use, and the liquor will serve as an excellent sauce for meat or fish

title: Indian Curry-Powder, Founded on Dr. Kitchener's Recipe
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of coriander-seed
- 1/4 lb. of turmeric
- 2 oz. of cinnamon-seed
- 1/2 oz. of cayenne
- 1 oz. of mustard
- 1 oz. of ground ginger
- 1/2 ounce of allspice
- 2 oz. of fenugreek-seed

## Directions

1. Put all the ingredients in a cool oven, where they should remain one night
2. Pound them in a mortar
3. Rub them through a sieve
4. Mix thoroughly together
5. Keep the powder in a bottle, from which the air should be completely excluded

title: Indian Fritters
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoonfuls of flour
- boiling water
- the yolks of 4 eggs
- the whites of 2
- hot lard or clarified dripping
- jam

## Directions

1. Put the flour into a basin, and pour over it sufficient boiling water to make it into a stiff paste, taking care to stir and beat it well, to prevent it getting lumpy
2. Leave it a little time to cool, and then break into it (without beating them at first) the yolks of 4 eggs and the whites of 2, and stir and beat all well together
3. Have ready some boiling lard or butter; drop a dessertspoonful of batter in at a time, and fry the fritters of a light brown
4. Serve on a dish, with a spoonful of preserve or marmalade dropped in between each fritter

title: Indian Mustard, an Excellent Relish to Bread and Butter, or any Cold Meat
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of the best mustard
- 1/4 lb. of flour
- 1/2 oz. of salt
- 4 shalots
- 4 tablespoonfuls of vinegar
- 4 tablespoonfuls of ketchup
- 1/4 bottle of anchovy sauce
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Put the mustard, flour, and salt into a basin, and make them into a stiff paste with boiling water
2. Boil the shalots with the vinegar, ketchup, and anchovy sauce, for 10 minutes
3. Pour the whole, boiling, over the mixture in the basin
4. Stir well, and reduce it to a proper thickness
5. Put it into a bottle, with a bruised shalot at the bottom, and store away for use

title: Indian Pickle (very Superior)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- vinegar
- 6 cloves of garlic per gallon of vinegar
- 12 shalots per gallon of vinegar
- 2 sticks of sliced horseradish per gallon of vinegar
- 1/4 lb. of bruised ginger per gallon of vinegar
- 2 oz. of whole black pepper per gallon of vinegar
- 1 oz. of long pepper per gallon of vinegar
- 1 oz. of allspice per gallon of vinegar
- 12 cloves per gallon of vinegar
- 1/4 oz. of cayenne per gallon of vinegar
- 2 oz. of mustard-seed per gallon of vinegar
- 1/4 lb. of mustard per gallon of vinegar
- 1 oz. of turmeric per gallon of vinegar
- a white cabbage
- cauliflowers
- radish-pods
- French beans
- gherkins
- small round pickling-onions
- nasturtiums
- capsicums
- chilies
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbage, which must be hard and white, into slices, and the cauliflowers into small branches
2. Sprinkle salt over them in a large dish, and let them remain two days
3. Dry them, and put them into a very large jar, with garlic, shalots, horseradish, ginger, pepper, allspice, and cloves, in the above proportions
4. Boil sufficient vinegar to cover them, which pour over, and, when cold, cover up to keep them free from dust
5. As the other things for the pickle ripen at different times, they may be added as they are ready: these will be radish-pods, French beans, gherkins, small onions, nasturtiums, capsicums, chilies, &c.
6. As these are procured, they must, first of all, be washed in a little cold vinegar, wiped, and then simply added to the other ingredients in the large jar, only taking care that they are covered by the vinegar
7. If more vinegar should be wanted to add to the pickle, boil it before adding it to the rest
8. When you have collected all the things you require, turn all out in a large pan, and thoroughly mix them
9. Put the mixed vegetables into smaller jars, without any of the vinegar
10. Boil the vinegar again, adding as much more as will be required to fill the different jars, and also cayenne, mustard-seed, turmeric, and mustard, which must be well mixed with a little cold vinegar, allowing the quantities named above to each gallon of vinegar
11. Pour the vinegar, boiling hot, over the pickle, and when cold, tie down with a bladder

title: Indian Trifle
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of milk
- the rind of 1/2 large lemon
- sugar to taste
- 5 heaped tablespoonfuls of rice-flour
- 1 oz. of sweet almonds
- 1/2 pint of custard

## Directions

1. Boil the milk and lemon-rind together until the former is well flavoured
2. Take out the lemon-rind and stir in the rice-flour, which should first be moistened with cold milk, and add sufficient loaf sugar to sweeten it nicely
3. Boil gently for about 5 minutes, and keep the mixture stirred
4. Take it off the fire, let it cool a little, and pour it into a glass dish
5. When cold, cut the rice out in the form of a star, or any other shape that may be preferred
6. Take out the spare rice, and fill the space with boiled custard
7. Blanch and cut the almonds into strips
8. Stick them over the trifle, and garnish it with pieces of brightly-coloured jelly, or preserved fruits, or candied citron

title: Indian-Corn-Flour Bread
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 lbs. of flour
- 2 lbs. of Indian-corn flour
- 2 tablespoonfuls of yeast
- 3 pints of warm water
- 1/4 oz. of salt

## Directions

1. Mix the two flours well together, with the salt
2. Make a hole in the centre, and stir the yeast up well with 1/2 pint of the warm water
3. Put this into the middle of the flour, and mix enough of it with the yeast to make a thin batter
4. Throw a little flour over the surface of this batter, cover the whole with a thick cloth, and set it to rise in a warm place
5. When the batter has nicely risen, work the whole to a nice smooth dough, adding the water as required
6. Knead it well, and mould the dough into loaves
7. Let them rise for nearly 1/2 hour, then put them into a well-heated oven
8. If made into 2 loaves, they will require from 1-1/2 to 2 hours baking

title: Invalid's Jelly
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 shanks of mutton
- 3 quarts of water
- a bunch of sweet herbs
- pepper and salt to taste
- 3 blades of mace
- 1 onion
- 1 lb. of lean beef
- a crust of bread toasted brown

## Directions

1. Soak the shanks in plenty of water for some hours, and scrub them well
2. put them, with the beef and other ingredients, into a saucepan with the water, and let them simmer very gently for 5 hours
3. Strain the broth, and, when cold, take off all the fat
4. It may be eaten either warmed up or cold as a jelly

title: Irish Stew (I)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lbs. of the loin or neck of mutton
- 5 lbs. of potatoes
- 5 large onions
- pepper and salt to taste
- rather more than 1 pint of water

## Directions

1. Trim off some of the fat of the above quantity of loin or neck of mutton, and cut it into chops of a moderate thickness.
2. Pare and halve the potatoes, and cut the onions into thick slices.
3. Put a layer of potatoes at the bottom of a stewpan, then a layer of mutton and onions, and season with pepper and salt; proceed in this manner until the stewpan is full, taking care to have plenty of vegetables at the top.
4. Pour in the water, and let it stew very gently for 2-1/2 hours, keeping the lid of the stewpan closely shut the whole time, and occasionally shaking it to prevent its burning.

title: Irish Stew (II)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 lbs. of the breast of mutton
- 1-1/2 pint of water
- salt and pepper to taste
- 4 lbs. of potatoes
- 4 large onions

## Directions

1. Put the mutton into a stewpan with the water and a little salt, and let it stew gently for an hour.
2. Cut the meat into small pieces, skim the fat from the gravy, and pare and slice the potatoes and onions.
3. Put all the ingredients into the stewpan in layers, first a layer of vegetables, then one of meat, and sprinkle seasoning of pepper and salt between each layer; cover closely, and let the whole stew very gently for 1 hour or rather more, shaking it frequently to prevent its burning.

title: Isinglass Or Gelatine Jelly
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 oz. of isinglass or gelatine
- 2 quarts of water

## Directions

1. Put the isinglass or gelatine into a saucepan with the above proportion of cold water
2. Bring it quickly to boil, and let it boil very fast, until the liquor is reduced one-half
3. Carefully remove the scum as it rises
4. Strain it through a jelly-bag, and it will be ready for use
5. If not required very clear, it may be merely strained through a fine sieve, instead of being run through a bag

title: Italian Cream
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of milk
- 1/4 pint of cream
- sugar to taste
- 1 oz. of isinglass
- 1 lemon
- the yolks of 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Put the cream and milk into a saucepan, with sugar to sweeten, and the lemon-rind
2. Boil until the milk is well flavoured then strain it into a basin, and add the beaten yolks of eggs
3. Put this mixture into a jug; place the jug in a saucepan of boiling water over the fire, and stir the contents until they thicken, but do not allow them to boil
4. Take the cream off the fire, stir in the lemon-juice and isinglass, which should be melted, and whip well
5. Fill a mould, place it in ice if at hand, and, when set, turn it out on a dish, and garnish as taste may dictate
6. The mixture may be whipped and drained, and then put into small glasses, when this mode of serving is preferred

title: Italian Mode of Dressing Truffles
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 truffles
- 1/4 pint of salad-oil
- pepper and salt to taste
- 1 tablespoonful of minced parsley
- a very little finely-minced garlic
- 2 blades of pounded mace
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon-juice

## Directions

1. After cleansing and brushing the truffles, cut them into thin slices, and put them in a baking-dish, on a seasoning of oil, pepper, salt, parsley, garlic, and mace in the above proportion.
2. Bake them for nearly an hour, and, just before serving, add the lemon-juice, and send them to table very hot.

title: Italian Mutton Cutlets
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- About 3 lbs. of the neck of mutton
- clarified butter
- the yolk of 1 egg
- 4 tablespoonfuls of bread crumbs
- 1 tablespoonful of minced savoury herbs
- 1 tablespoonful of minced parsley
- 1 teaspoonful of minced shalot
- 1 saltspoonful of finely-chopped lemon-peel
- pepper, salt, and pounded mace to taste
- flour
- 1/2 pint of hot broth or water
- 2 teaspoonfuls of Harvey's sauce
- 1 teaspoonful of soy
- 2 teaspoonfuls of tarragon vinegar
- 1 tablespoonful of port wine

## Directions

1. Cut the mutton into nicely-shaped cutlets, flatten them, and trim off some of the fat.
2. Dip them in clarified butter, and then into the beaten yolk of an egg.
3. Mix well together bread crumbs, herbs, parsley, shalot, lemon-peel, and seasoning in the above proportion, and cover the cutlets with these ingredients.
4. Melt some butter in a frying-pan, lay in the cutlets, and fry them a nice brown; take them out, and keep them hot before the fire.
5. Dredge some flour into the pan, and if there is not sufficient butter, add a little more; stir till it looks brown, then pour in the hot broth or water, and the remaining ingredients; give one boil, and pour round the cutlets.
6. If the gravy should not be thick enough, add a little more flour.

title: Italian Sauce (Brown)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- A few chopped mushrooms and shalots
- 1/2 pint of stock, No. 105
- 1/2 glass of Madeira
- the juice of 1/2 lemon
- 1/2 teaspoonful of pounded sugar
- 1 teaspoonful of chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Put the stock into a stewpan with the mushrooms, shalots, and Madeira, and stew gently for 1/4 hour
2. Then add the remaining ingredients, and let them just boil
3. When the sauce is done enough, put it in another stewpan, and warm it in a bain marie

title: Italian Sauce (White)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of white stock, No. 107
- 2 tablespoonfuls of chopped mushrooms
- 1 dessertspoonful of chopped shalots
- 1 slice of ham, minced very fine
- 1/4 pint of Béchamel, No. 367
- salt to taste
- a few drops of garlic vinegar
- 1/2 teaspoonful of pounded sugar
- a squeeze of lemon-juice

## Directions

1. Put the shalots and mushrooms into a stewpan with the stock and ham, and simmer very gently for 1/2 hour
2. When add the Béchamel
3. Let it just boil up, and then strain it through a tammy
4. Season with the above ingredients, and serve very hot
5. If this sauce should not have retained a nice white colour, a little cream may be added

title: Jaunemange
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 oz. of isinglass
- 1 pint of water
- 1/2 pint of white wine
- the rind and juice of 1 large lemon
- sugar to taste
- the yolks of 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Put the isinglass, water, and lemon-rind into a saucepan, and boil gently until the former is dissolved
2. Add the strained lemon-juice, the wine, and sufficient white sugar to sweeten the whole nicely
3. Boil for 2 or 3 minutes, strain the mixture into a jug, and add the yolks of the eggs, which should be well beaten
4. Place the jug in a saucepan of boiling water; keep stirring the mixture one way until it thickens, but do not allow it to boil
5. Take it off the fire, and keep stirring until nearly cold
6. Pour it into a mould, omitting the sediment at the bottom of the jug, and let it remain until quite firm

title: Jelly Moulded With Fresh Fruit, or Macedoine de Fruits
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Rather more than 1-1/2 pint of jelly
- a few nice strawberries, or red or white currants, or raspberries, or any fresh fruit that may be in season

## Directions

1. Have ready the above proportion of jelly, which must be very clear and rather sweet, the raw fruit requiring an additional quantity of sugar
2. Select ripe, nice-looking fruit; pick off the stalks, unless currants are used, when they are laid in the jelly as they come from the tree
3. Begin by putting a little jelly at the bottom of the mould, which must harden
4. Arrange the fruit round the sides of the mould, recollecting that it will be reversed when turned out
5. Pour in some more jelly to make the fruit adhere, and, when that layer is set, put another row of fruit and jelly until the mould is full
6. If convenient, put it in ice until required for table, then wring a cloth in boiling water, wrap it round the mould for a minute, and turn the jelly carefully out
7. Peaches, apricots, plums, apples are better for being boiled in a little clear syrup before they are laid in the jelly
8. Strawberries, raspberries, grapes, cherries, and currants are put in raw
9. In winter, when fresh fruits are not obtainable, a very pretty jelly may be made with preserved fruits or brandy cherries
10. It may be garnished with the same fruit as is laid in the jelly

title: Jelly of Two Colours
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 pint of calf's-feet jelly
- a few drops of prepared cochineal

## Directions

1. Make 1-1/2 pint of jelly by recipe No. 1416, or, if wished more economical, of clarified syrup and gelatine, flavouring it in any way that may be preferred.
2. Colour one-half of the jelly with a few drops of prepared cochineal, and the other half leave as pale as possible.
3. Have ready a mould well wetted in every part; pour in a small quantity of the red jelly, and let this set.
4. When quite firm, pour on it the same quantity of the pale jelly, and let this set.
5. Proceed in this manner until the mould is full, always taking care to let one jelly set before the other is poured in, or the colours would run one into the other.
6. When turned out, the jelly should have a striped appearance.
7. For variety, half the mould may be filled at once with one of the jellies, and, when firm, filled up with the other.

title: Jerusalem Artichokes with White Sauce
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 to 15 artichokes
- 12 to 15 Brussels sprouts
- 1/2 pint of white sauce, No. 538

## Directions

1. Peel and cut the artichokes in the shape of a pear
2. Cut a piece off the bottom of each, that they may stand upright in the dish
3. Boil them in salt and water until tender
4. Have ready 1/2 pint of white sauce, made by recipe No. 538
5. Dish the artichokes
6. Pour over them the sauce
7. Place between each a fine Brussels sprout: these should be boiled separately, and not with the artichokes

title: Jugged Gravy (Excellent)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of shin of beef
- 1/4 lb. of lean ham
- 1 onion or a few shalots
- 2 pints of water
- salt and whole pepper to taste
- 1 blade of mace
- a faggot of savoury herbs
- 1/2 a large carrot
- 1/2 a head of celery

## Directions

1. Cut up the beef and ham into small pieces, and slice the vegetables
2. Take a jar, capable of holding two pints of water, and arrange therein, in layers, the ham, meat, vegetables, and seasoning, alternately, filling up with the above quantity of water
3. Tie down the jar, or put a plate over the top, so that the steam may not escape
4. Place it in the oven, and let it remain there from 6 to 8 hours; should, however, the oven be very hot, less time will be required
5. When sufficiently cooked, strain the gravy, and when cold, remove the fat
6. Flavour with ketchup, wines, or any other store sauce that may be preferred

title: Jugged Hare
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 hare
- 1-1/2 lb. of gravy beef
- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 1 onion
- 1 lemon
- 6 cloves
- pepper, cayenne, and salt to taste
- 1/2 pint of port wine

## Directions

1. Skin, paunch, and wash the hare, cut it into pieces, dredge them with flour, and fry in boiling butter.
2. Have ready 1-1/2 pint of gravy, made from the above proportion of beef, and thickened with a little flour.
3. Put this into a jar; add the pieces of fried hare, an onion stuck with six cloves, a lemon peeled and cut in half, and a good seasoning of pepper, cayenne, and salt.
4. Cover the jar down tightly, put it up to the neck into a stewpan of boiling water, and let it stew until the hare is quite tender, taking care to keep the water boiling.
5. When nearly done, pour in the wine, and add a few forcemeat balls.

title: Kale Brose
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Half an ox-head or cow-heel
- a teacupful of toasted oatmeal
- salt to taste
- 2 handfuls of greens
- 3 quarts of water

## Directions

1. Make a broth of the ox-head or cow-heel, and boil it till oil floats on the top of the liquor, then boil the greens, shred, in it.
2. Put the oatmeal, with a little salt, into a basin, and mix with it quickly a teacupful of the fat broth: it should not run into one doughy mass, but form knots.
3. Stir it into the whole, give one boil, and serve very hot.

title: Kegeree
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Any cold fish
- 1 teacupful of boiled rice
- 1 oz. of butter
- 1 teaspoonful of mustard
- 2 soft-boiled eggs
- salt and cayenne to taste

## Directions

1. Pick the fish carefully from the bones, mix with the other ingredients, and serve very hot.
2. The quantities may be varied according to the amount of fish used.

title: Kidney Omelet
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 1 saltspoonful of salt
- 1/2 saltspoonful of pepper
- 2 sheep's kidneys, or 2 tablespoonfuls of minced veal kidney
- 5 oz. of butter

## Directions

1. Skin the kidneys, cut them into small dice, and toss them in a frying-pan, in 1 oz. of butter, over the fire for 2 or 3 minutes
2. Mix the ingredients for the omelet the same as in recipe No. 1456
3. When the eggs are well whisked, stir in the pieces of kidney
4. Make the butter hot in the frying-pan, and when it bubbles, pour in the omelet, and fry it over a gentle fire from 4 to 6 minutes
5. When the eggs are set, fold the edges over, so that the omelet assumes an oval form, and be careful that it is not too much done
6. To brown the top, hold the pan before the fire for a minute or two, or use a salamander until the desired colour is obtained, but never turn an omelet in the pan
7. Slip it carefully on to a very hot dish, or put a dish on the omelet, and turn the pan quickly over
8. Serve the instant it comes from the fire

title: Kirkleatham Yeast
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 oz. of hops
- 4 quarts of water
- 1/2 lb. of flour
- 1/2 pint of yeast

## Directions

1. Boil the hops and water for 20 minutes
2. Strain, and mix with the liquid 1/2 lb. of flour and not quite 1/2 pint of yeast
3. Bottle it up, and tie the corks down
4. When wanted for use, boil potatoes according to the quantity of bread to be made (about 3 lbs. are sufficient for about a peck of flour)
5. Mash them, add to them 1/2 lb. of flour, and mix about 1/2 pint of the yeast with them
6. Let this mixture stand all day, and lay the bread to rise the night before it is wanted

title: Ladies' Boot Polish
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet-oil
- vinegar
- treacle
- lamp-black 1 oz.

## Directions

1. Mix equal proportions of sweet-oil, vinegar, and treacle, with 1 oz. of lamp-black
2. When all the ingredients are thoroughly incorporated, rub the mixture on the boots with the palm of the hand
3. Put them in a cool place to dry

title: Lamb Chops
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Loin of lamb
- pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Trim off the flap from a fine loin of lamb, and cut it into chops about 3/4 inch in thickness
2. Have ready a bright clear fire; lay the chops on a gridiron, and broil them of a nice pale brown, turning them when required
3. Season them with pepper and salt
4. serve very hot and quickly, and garnish with crisped parsley, or place them on mashed potatoes
5. Asparagus, spinach, or peas are the favourite accompaniments to lamb chops

title: Lamb Cutlets and Spinach (An Entree)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 cutlets
- egg and bread crumbs
- salt and pepper to taste
- a little clarified butter

## Directions

1. Cut the cutlets from a neck of lamb, and shape them by cutting off the thick part of the chine-bone
2. Trim off most of the fat and all the skin, and scrape the top part of the bones quite clean
3. Brush the cutlets over with egg, sprinkle them with bread crumbs, and season with pepper and salt
4. Now dip them into clarified butter, sprinkle over a few more bread crumbs, and fry them over a sharp fire, turning them when required
5. Lay them before the fire to drain, and arrange them on a dish with spinach in the centre, which should be previously well boiled, drained, chopped, and seasoned

title: Lamb's Fry
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of lamb's fry
- 3 pints of water
- egg and bread crumbs
- 1 teaspoonful of chopped parsley
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Boil the fry for 1/4 hour in the above proportion of water
2. Take it out and dry it in a cloth
3. Grate some bread down finely, mix with it a teaspoonful of chopped parsley and a high seasoning of pepper and salt
4. Brush the fry lightly over with the yolk of an egg
5. Sprinkle over the bread crumbs, and fry for 5 minutes
6. Serve very hot on a napkin in a dish, and garnish with plenty of crisped parsley

title: Lamb's Sweetbreads, Larded, and Asparagus
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 sweetbreads
- 1/2 pint of veal stock
- white pepper and salt to taste
- a small bunch of green onions
- 1 blade of pounded mace
- thickening of butter and flour
- 2 eggs
- nearly 1/2 pint of cream
- 1 teaspoonful of minced parsley
- a very little grated nutmeg
- asparagus-tops

## Directions

1. Soak the sweetbreads in lukewarm water, and put them into a saucepan with sufficient boiling water to cover them, and let them simmer for 10 minutes.
2. Take them out and put them into cold water.
3. Lard them, lay them in a stewpan, add the stock, seasoning, onions, mace, and a thickening of butter and flour, and stew gently for 1/4 hour or 20 minutes.
4. Beat up the egg with the cream, to which add the minced parsley and a very little grated nutmeg.
5. Put this to the other ingredients; stir it well till quite hot, but do not let it boil after the cream is added, or it will curdle.
6. Have ready some asparagus-tops, boiled; add these to the sweetbreads, and serve.

title: Lard or Flead Crust
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. flour
- 1/2 lb. of lard or flead
- 1/2 pint of water
- 1/2 saltspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Clear the flead free from skin, and slice it into thin flakes
2. Rub it into the flour, add the salt, and work the whole into a smooth paste, with the above proportion of water
3. Fold the paste over two or three times
4. Beat it well with the rolling-pin
5. Roll it out, and it will be ready for use

title: Larded Beef with Sorrel Sauce
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- bacon
- pepper
- salt
- cloves
- mace
- allspice
- 1 pint of stock or water
- 1 glass of sherry
