- bunch of savoury herbs
- 2 shalots
- sorrel
- small piece of butter
- 4 or 6 tablespoonfuls of good gravy
- sugar
- cabbage-lettuce

## Directions

1. Cut some bacon into thin strips, and sprinkle over them a seasoning of pepper and salt, mixed with cloves, mace, and allspice, well pounded.
2. Lard the beef with these, put it into a stewpan with the stock or water, sherry, herbs, shalots, 2 cloves, and more pepper and salt.
3. Stew the meat gently until tender, then take it out and cover it closely.
4. Skim off all the fat from the gravy, and strain it.
5. Set it on the fire, and boil till it becomes a glaze.
6. Glaze the larded side of the beef with this.
7. For the sorrel sauce: Wash and pick some sorrel, and put it into a stewpan with only the water that hangs about it.
8. Keep stirring to prevent its burning, and when done, lay it in a sieve to drain.
9. Chop it, and stew it with a small piece of butter and 4 or 6 tablespoonfuls of good gravy for an hour, and rub it through a tammy.
10. If too acid, add a little sugar; and a little cabbage-lettuce boiled with the sorrel will be found an improvement.
11. Serve the beef on sorrel sauce.

title: Lark Pie (an Entree)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- a few thin slices of beef
- thin slices of bacon
- 9 larks
- flour
- 1 teacupful of bread crumbs
- 1/2 teaspoonful of minced lemon-peel
- 1 teaspoonful of minced parsley
- 1 egg
- salt and pepper to taste
- 1 teaspoonful of chopped shalot
- 1/2 pint of weak stock or water
- puff-paste

## Directions

1. Make a stuffing of bread crumbs, minced lemon-peel, parsley, and the yolk of an egg, all of which should be well mixed together.
2. Roll the larks in flour, and stuff them.
3. Line the bottom of a pie-dish with a few slices of beef and bacon.
4. Over these place the larks, and season with salt, pepper, minced parsley, and chopped shalot, in the above proportion.
5. Pour in the stock or water, cover with crust, and bake for an hour in a moderate oven.
6. During the time the pie is baking, shake it 2 or 3 times, to assist in thickening the gravy, and serve very hot.

title: Leamington Sauce
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- walnuts
- 3 quarts of vinegar per quart of walnut-juice
- 1 pint of Indian soy per quart of walnut-juice
- 1 oz. of cayenne per quart of walnut-juice
- 2 oz. of shalots per quart of walnut-juice
- 3/4 oz. of garlic per quart of walnut-juice
- 1/2 pint of port wine per quart of walnut-juice

## Directions

1. Be very particular in choosing the walnuts as soon as they appear in the market
2. Pound them in a mortar to a pulp
3. Strew some salt over them, and let them remain thus for two or three days, occasionally stirring and moving them about
4. Press out the juice
5. To each quart of walnut-liquor allow the above proportion of vinegar, soy, cayenne, shalots, garlic, and port wine
6. Pound each ingredient separately in a mortar, then mix them well together, and store away for use in small bottles
7. The corks should be well sealed

title: Leek Soup I
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- A sheep's head
- 3 quarts of water
- 12 leeks cut small
- pepper and salt to taste
- oatmeal to thicken

## Directions

1. Prepare the head, either by skinning or cleaning the skin very nicely; split it in two; take out the brains, and put it into boiling water; add the leeks and seasoning, and simmer very gently for 4 hours.
2. Mix smoothly, with cold water, as much oatmeal as will make the soup tolerably thick; pour it into the soup; continue stirring till the whole is blended and well done, and serve.

title: Lemon Biscuits
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/4 lb. of flour
- 3/4 lb. of loaf sugar
- 6 oz. of fresh butter
- 4 eggs
- 1 oz. of lemon-peel
- 2 dessertspoonfuls of lemon-juice

## Directions

1. Rub the flour into the butter
2. stir in the pounded sugar and very finely-minced lemon-peel
3. when these ingredients are thoroughly mixed, add the eggs, which should be previously well whisked, and the lemon-juice
4. Beat the mixture well for a minute or two
5. drop it from a spoon on to a buttered tin, about 2 inches apart, as the cakes will spread when they get warm
6. place the tin in the oven, and bake the cakes of a pale brown from 15 to 20 minutes

title: Lemon Blancmange
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of milk
- the yolks of 4 eggs
- 3 oz. of ground rice
- 6 oz. of pounded sugar
- 1-1/2 oz. of fresh butter
- the rind of 1 lemon
- the juice of 2 lemons
- 1/2 oz. of gelatine

## Directions

1. Make a custard with the yolks of the eggs and 1/2 pint of the milk, and, when done, put it into a basin.
2. Put half the remainder of the milk into a saucepan with the ground rice, fresh butter, lemon-rind, and 3 oz. of the sugar, and let these ingredients boil until the mixture is stiff, stirring them continually.
3. Pour it into the bowl where the custard is, mixing both well together.
4. Put the gelatine with the rest of the milk into a saucepan, and let it stand by the side of the fire to dissolve.
5. Boil for a minute or two, stir carefully into the basin, adding 3 oz. more of pounded sugar.
6. When cold, stir in the lemon-juice, which should be carefully strained.
7. Pour the mixture into a well-oiled mould, leaving out the lemon-peel, and set the mould in a pan of cold water until wanted for table.

title: Lemon Brandy
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of brandy
- the rind of two small lemons
- 2 oz. of loaf-sugar
- 1/4 pint of water

## Directions

1. Peel the lemons rather thin, taking care to have none of the white pith.
2. Put the rinds into a bottle with the brandy, and let them infuse for 24 hours, when they should be strained.
3. Boil the sugar with the water for a few minutes, skim it, and, when cold, add it to the brandy.
4. A dessertspoonful of this will be found an excellent flavouring for boiled custards.

title: Lemon Cake
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 eggs
- 3 tablespoonfuls of orange-flower water
- 3/4 lb. of pounded loaf sugar
- 1 lemon
- 3/4 lb. of flour

## Directions

1. Separate the whites from the yolks of the eggs whisk the former to a stiff froth; add the orange-flower water, the sugar, grated lemon-rind, and mix these ingredients well together.
2. Then beat the yolks of the eggs, and add them, with the lemon-juice, to the whites, &c.; dredge in the flour gradually; keep beating the mixture well; put it into a buttered mould, and bake the cake about an hour, or rather longer.

title: Lemon Cheesecakes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of loaf sugar
- 6 eggs
- the rind of 2 lemons and the juice of 3

## Directions

1. Put all the ingredients into a stewpan, carefully grating the lemon-rind and straining the juice
2. Keep stirring the mixture over the fire until the sugar is dissolved, and it begins to thicken
3. When of the consistency of honey, it is done, then put it into small jars, and keep in a dry place
4. When made into cheesecakes, add a few pounded almonds, or candied peel, or grated sweet biscuit
5. Line some patty-pans with good puff-paste, rather more than half fill them with the mixture, and bake for about 1/4 hour in a good brisk oven

title: Lemon Cream
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of cream
- the yolks of 2 eggs
- 1/4 lb. of white sugar
- 1 large lemon
- 1 oz. of isinglass

## Directions

1. Put the cream into a lined saucepan with the sugar, lemon-peel, and isinglass, and simmer these over a gentle fire for about 10 minutes, stirring them all the time.
2. Strain the cream into a jug, add the yolks of eggs, which should be well beaten, and put the jug into a saucepan of boiling water.
3. Stir the mixture one way until it thickens, but do not allow it to boil.
4. Take it off the fire, and keep stirring it until nearly cold.
5. Strain the lemon-juice into a basin, gradually pour on it the cream, and stir it well until the juice is well mixed with it.
6. Have ready a well-oiled mould, pour the cream into it, and let it remain until perfectly set.
7. When required for table, loosen the edges with a small blunt knife, put a dish on the top of the mould, turn it over quickly, and the cream should easily slip away.

title: Lemon Creams
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of cream
- 2 dozen sweet almonds
- 3 glasses of sherry
- the rind and juice of 2 lemons
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Blanch and chop the almonds, and put them into a jug with the cream.
2. In another jug put the sherry, lemon-rind, strained juice, and sufficient pounded sugar to sweeten the whole nicely.
3. Pour rapidly from one jug to the other till the mixture is well frothed.
4. Pour it into jelly-glasses, omitting the lemon-rind.
5. This may be made less rich by omitting the almonds and substituting orange or raisin wine for the sherry.

title: Lemon Creams or Custards
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 oz. of loaf sugar
- 2 pints of boiling water
- the rind of 1 lemon
- the juice of 3 lemons
- the yolks of 8 eggs

## Directions

1. Make a quart of lemonade in the following manner: Dissolve the sugar in the boiling water, having previously, with part of the sugar, rubbed off the lemon-rind, and add the strained juice.
2. After the boiling water is poured on the sugar and lemon, it should stand covered for about 1/2 hour before the eggs are added to it, that the flavour of the rind may be extracted.
3. Strain the lemonade into a saucepan, and add the yolks of the eggs, which should be well beaten.
4. Stir this one way over the fire until the mixture thickens, but do not allow it to boil.
5. Serve in custard-glasses, or on a glass dish.

title: Lemon Custard Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 pint of milk
- the rind of 1/4 lemon
- 1/4 lb. of moist sugar
- 4 eggs
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Put the milk into a saucepan with the sugar and lemon-rind, and let this infuse for about 4 hour, or until the milk is well flavoured
2. Whisk the eggs, yolks and whites
3. Pour the milk to them, stirring all the while
4. Have ready a pie-dish, lined at the edge with paste ready baked
5. Strain the custard into the dish
6. Grate a little nutmeg over the top
7. Bake in a very slow oven for about 1/2 hour, or rather longer

title: Lemon Dumplings
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of grated bread
- 1/4 lb. of chopped suet
- 1/4 lb. of moist sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1 large lemon

## Directions

1. Mix the bread, suet, and moist sugar well together, adding the lemon-peel, which should be very finely minced
2. Moisten with the eggs and strained lemon-juice
3. Stir well, and put the mixture into small buttered cups
4. Tie them down and boil for 3/4 hour
5. Turn them out on a dish, strew sifted sugar over them, and serve with wine sauce

title: Lemon Jelly
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 lemons
- 3/4 lb. of lump sugar
- 1 pint of water
- 1-1/2 oz. of isinglass
- 1/4 pint of sherry

## Directions

1. Peel 3 of the lemons, pour 1/2 pint of boiling water on the rind, and let it infuse for 1/2 hour.
2. Put the sugar, isinglass, and 1/2 pint of water into a lined saucepan, and boil these ingredients for 20 minutes.
3. Put in the strained lemon-juice, the strained infusion of the rind, and bring the whole to the point of boiling.
4. Skim well, add the wine, and run the jelly through a bag.
5. Pour it into a mould that has been wetted or soaked in water.
6. Put it in ice, if convenient, where let it remain until required for table.
7. Previously to adding the lemon-juice to the other ingredients, ascertain that it is very nicely strained, as, if this is not properly attended to, it is liable to make the jelly thick and muddy.

title: Lemon Mincemeat
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large lemons
- 6 large apples
- 1/2 lb. of suet
- 1 lb. of currants
- 1/2 lb. of sugar
- 2 oz. of candied lemon-peel
- 1 oz. of citron
- mixed spice to taste

## Directions

1. Pare the lemons, squeeze them, and boil the peel until tender enough to mash
2. Add to the mashed lemon-peel the apples, which should be pared, cored, and minced
3. Add the chopped suet, currants, sugar, sliced peel, and spice
4. Strain the lemon-juice to these ingredients, stir the mixture well, and put it in a jar with a closely-fitting lid
5. Stir occasionally, and in a week or 10 days the mincemeat will be ready for use

title: Lemon Pudding (Very Rich)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- the rind and juice of 2 large lemons
- 1/2 lb. of loaf sugar
- 1/4 pint of cream
- the yolks of 8 eggs
- 2 oz. of almonds
- 1/2 lb. of butter, melted
- puff-paste

## Directions

1. Mix the pounded sugar with the cream, and add the yolks of eggs and the butter, which should be previously warmed.
2. Blanch and pound the almonds, and put these, with the grated rind and strained juice of the lemons, to the other ingredients.
3. Stir all well together; line a dish with puff-paste, put in the mixture, and bake for 1 hour.

title: Lemon Sauce For Boiled Fowls
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small lemon
- 3/4 pint of melted butter

## Directions

1. Cut the lemon into very thin slices, and these again into very small dice
2. Have ready 3/4 pint of melted butter
3. Put in the lemon
4. Let it just simmer, but not boil, and pour it over the fowls

title: Lemon Sauce for Sweet Puddings
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The rind and juice of 1 lemon
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 1 oz. of butter
- 1 large wineglassful of sherry
- 1 wineglassful of water
- sugar to taste
- the yolks of 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Rub the rind of the lemon on to some lumps of sugar; squeeze out the juice, and strain it; put the butter and flour into a saucepan, stir them over the fire, and when of a pale brown, add the wine, water, and strained lemon-juice.
2. Crush the lumps of sugar that were rubbed on the lemon; stir these into the sauce, which should be very sweet.
3. When these ingredients are well mixed, and the sugar is melted, put in the beaten yolks of 4 eggs; keep stirring the sauce until it thickens, when serve. Do not, on any account, allow it to boil, or it will curdle, and be entirely spoiled.

title: Lemon Sponge
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 oz. of isinglass
- 1-3/4 pint of water
- 3/4 lb. of pounded sugar
- the juice of 5 lemons
- the rind of 1 lemon
- the whites of 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Dissolve the isinglass in the water, strain it into a saucepan, and add the sugar, lemon-rind, and juice.
2. Boil the whole from 10 to 15 minutes.
3. Strain it again, and let it stand till it is cold and begins to stiffen.
4. Beat the whites of the eggs, put them to it, and whisk the mixture till it is quite white.
5. Put it into a mould which has been previously wetted, and let it remain until perfectly set.
6. Turn it out, and garnish it according to taste.

title: Lemon Syrup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of loaf sugar
- 2 pints of water
- 1 oz. of citric acid
- 12 drachm of essence of lemon

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar and water together for 1/4 hour, and put it into a basin, where let it remain till cold
2. Beat the citric acid to a powder, mix the essence of lemon with it
3. Add these two ingredients to the syrup
4. Mix well, and bottle for use
5. Two tablespoonfuls of the syrup are sufficient for a tumbler of cold water

title: Lemon White Sauce, For Fowls, Fricassees, &c.
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 pint of cream
- the rind and juice of 1 lemon
- 1/2 teaspoonful of whole white pepper
- 1 sprig of lemon thyme
- 3 oz. of butter
- 1 dessertspoonful of flour
- 1 teacupful of white stock
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Put the cream into a very clean saucepan with the lemon-peel, pepper, and thyme
2. Let these infuse for 1/2 hour, when simmer gently for a few minutes, or until there is a nice flavour of lemon
3. Strain it, and add a thickening of butter and flour in the above proportions
4. Stir this well in, and put in the lemon-juice at the moment of serving
5. Mix the stock with the cream, and add a little salt
6. This sauce should not boil after the cream and stock are mixed together

title: Lemon Wine
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- To 4-1/2 gallons of water allow the pulp of 50 lemons
- the rind of 25 lemons
- 16 lbs. of loaf sugar
- 1/2 oz. of isinglass
- 1 bottle of brandy

## Directions

1. Peel and slice the lemons, but use only the rind of 25 of them, and put them into the cold water
2. Let it stand 8 or 9 days, squeezing the lemons well every day
3. Strain the water off and put it into a cask with the sugar
4. Let it work some time, and when it has ceased working, put in the isinglass
5. Stop the cask down; in about six months put in the brandy and bottle the wine off

title: Lemon-Water Ice
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of syrup, made by recipe No. 1513
- 1/3 pint of lemon-juice
- rind of 4 lemons

## Directions

1. Rub the sugar on the rinds of the lemons, and with it make the syrup by recipe No. 1513, omitting the white of egg.
2. Strain the lemon-juice, add it to the other ingredients, stir well, and put the mixture into a freezing-pot.
3. Freeze as directed for Ice Pudding, No. 1290, and, when the mixture is thoroughly and equally frozen, put it into ice-glasses.

title: Lemonade
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The rind of 2 lemons
- the juice of 3 large or 4 small ones
- 1 lb. of loaf sugar
- 1 quart of boiling water

## Directions

1. Rub some of the sugar, in lumps, on 2 of the lemons until they have imbibed all the oil from them, and put it with the remainder of the sugar into a jug; add the lemon-juice (but no pips), and pour over the whole a quart of boiling water.
2. When the sugar is dissolved, strain the lemonade through a fine sieve or piece of muslin, and, when cool, it will be ready for use.
3. The lemonade will be much improved by having the white of an egg beaten up in it; a little sherry mixed with it, also, makes this beverage much nicer.

title: Lemonade for Invalids
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lemon
- lump sugar to taste
- 1 pint of boiling water

## Directions

1. Pare off the rind of the lemon thinly
2. cut the lemon into 2 or 3 thick slices, and remove as much as possible of the white outside pith, and all the pips
3. Put the slices of lemon, the peel, and lump sugar into a jug
4. pour over the boiling water
5. cover it closely, and in 2 hours it will be fit to drink
6. It should either be strained or poured off from the sediment

title: Liaison of Eggs for Thickening Sauces
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The yolks of 3 eggs
- 8 tablespoonfuls of milk or cream

## Directions

1. Beat up the yolks of the eggs, to which add the milk, and strain the whole through a hair-sieve.
2. When the liaison is being added to the sauce it is intended to thicken, care must be exercised to keep stirring it during the whole time, or, otherwise, the eggs will curdle.
3. It should only just simmer, but not boil.

title: Light Buns
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 teaspoonful of tartaric acid
- 1/2 teaspoonful of bicarbonate of soda
- 1 lb. of flour
- 2 oz. of butter
- 2 oz. of loaf sugar
- 1/4 lb. of currants or raisins
- a few caraway seeds (when liked)
- 1/2 pint of cold new milk
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Rub the tartaric acid, soda, and flour all together through a hair sieve
2. Work the butter into the flour
3. Add the sugar, currants, and caraway seeds, when the flavour of them latter is liked
4. Mix all these ingredients well together
5. Make a hole in the middle of the flour, and pour in the milk, mixed with the egg, which should be well beaten
6. Mix quickly, and set the dough, with a fork, on baking-tins
7. Bake the buns for about 20 minutes

title: Liqueur Jelly
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of lump sugar
- 2 oz. of isinglass
- 1-1/2 pint of water
- the juice of 2 lemons
- 1/4 pint of liqueur

## Directions

1. Put the sugar, with 1 pint of the water, into a stewpan, and boil them gently by the side of the fire until there is no scum remaining, which must be carefully removed as fast as it rises.
2. Boil the isinglass with the other 1/2 pint of water, and skim it carefully in the same manner.
3. Strain the lemon-juice, and add it, with the clarified isinglass, to the syrup.
4. Put in the liqueur, and bring the whole to the boiling-point.
5. Let the saucepan remain covered by the side of the fire for a few minutes.
6. Pour the jelly through a bag, put it into a mould, and set the mould in ice until required for table.
7. Dip the mould in hot water, wipe the outside, loosen the jelly by passing a knife round the edges, and turn it out carefully on a dish.

title: Little Raised Pork Pies
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of flour
- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 1/2 lb. of mutton suet
- salt and white pepper to taste
- 4 lbs. of the neck of pork
- 1 dessertspoonful of powdered sage

## Directions

1. Well dry the flour, mince the suet, and put these with the butter into a saucepan, to be made hot, and add a little salt.
2. When melted, mix it up into a stiff paste, and put it before the fire with a cloth over it until ready to make up.
3. Chop the pork into small pieces, season it with white pepper, salt, and powdered sage.
4. Divide the paste into rather small pieces, raise it in a round or oval form, fill with the meat, and bake in a brick oven.
5. These pies will require a fiercer oven than those in the preceding recipe, as they are made so much smaller.

title: Liver and Lemon Sauce for Poultry
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The liver of a fowl
- one lemon
- salt to taste
- 1/2 pint of melted butter

## Directions

1. Wash the liver, and let it boil for a few minutes.
2. Peel the lemon very thin, remove the white part and pips, and cut it into very small dice.
3. Mince the liver and a small quantity of the lemon rind very fine.
4. Add these ingredients to 1/2 pint of smoothly-made melted butter.
5. Season with a little salt, put in the cut lemon, heat it gradually, but do not allow it to boil, lest the butter should oil.

title: Liver and Parsley Sauce for Poultry
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The liver of a fowl
- one tablespoonful of minced parsley
- 1/2 pint of melted butter

## Directions

1. Wash and score the liver, boil it for a few minutes, and mince it very fine.
2. Blanch or scald a small bunch of parsley, of which there should be sufficient when chopped to fill a tablespoon.
3. Add this, with the minced liver, to 1/2 pint of smoothly-made melted butter.
4. Let it just boil when serve.

title: Lobster a la Mode Francaise
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lobster
- 4 tablespoonfuls of white stock
- 2 tablespoonfuls of cream
- pounded mace
- cayenne to taste
- bread crumbs
- butter
- puff-paste

## Directions

1. Pick the meat from the shell, and cut it up into small square pieces
2. Put the stock, cream, and seasoning into a stewpan, add the lobster, and let it simmer gently for 6 minutes
3. Serve it in the shell, which must be nicely cleaned, and have a border of puff-paste
4. Cover it with bread crumbs, place small pieces of butter over, and brown before the fire, or with a salamander

title: Lobster Curry
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lobster
- 2 onions
- 1 oz. butter
- 1 tablespoonful of curry-powder
- 1/2 pint of medium stock
- juice of 1/2 lemon
- boiled rice

## Directions

1. Pick the meat from the shell, and cut it into nice square pieces
2. Fry the onions of a pale brown in the butter
3. Stir in the curry-powder and stock, and simmer till it thickens
4. Put in the lobster and stew the whole slowly for 1/2 hour, stirring occasionally
5. Just before sending to table, put in the lemon-juice
6. Serve boiled rice with it, the same as for other curries

title: Lobster Cutlets
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large hen lobster
- 1 oz. fresh butter
- 1/2 saltspoonful of salt
- pounded mace
- grated nutmeg
- cayenne and white pepper to taste
- egg
- bread crumbs
- lard
- béchamel

## Directions

1. Pick the meat from the shell, and pound it in a mortar with the butter
2. Gradually add the mace and seasoning, well mixing the ingredients
3. Beat all to a smooth paste, and add a little of the spawn
4. Divide the mixture into pieces of an equal size, and shape them like cutlets
5. Brush them over with egg, and sprinkle with bread crumbs, and stick a short piece of the small claw in the top of each
6. Fry them of a nice brown in boiling lard, and drain them before the fire on a sieve reversed
7. Arrange them nicely on a dish, and pour béchamel in the middle, but not over the cutlets

title: Lobster Patties
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- minced lobster
- 4 tablespoonfuls of béchamel
- 6 drops of anchovy sauce
- lemon-juice
- cayenne to taste
- puff-paste
- bread
- egg

## Directions

1. Line the patty-pans with puff-paste, and put into each a small piece of bread
2. Cover with paste, brush over with egg, and bake of a light colour
3. Take as much lobster as is required, mince the meat very fine, and add the béchamel, anchovy sauce, lemon-juice, and cayenne
4. Stir it over the fire for 6 minutes
5. Remove the lids of the patty-cases, take out the bread, fill with the mixture, and replace the covers

title: Lobster Salad
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 hen lobster
- lettuces
- endive
- small salad (whatever is in season)
- a little chopped beetroot
- 2 hard-boiled eggs
- a few slices of cucumber
- equal quantities of oil and vinegar
- 1 teaspoonful of made mustard
- the yolks of 2 eggs
- cayenne and salt to taste
- 3 teaspoonful of anchovy sauce

## Directions

1. Wash the salad, and thoroughly dry it by shaking it in a cloth.
2. Mix the oil, vinegar, mustard, egg yolks, cayenne, salt, and anchovy sauce together to form a creamy-looking sauce.
3. Cut up the lettuces and endive, pour the dressing on them, and lightly throw in the small salad.
4. Mix all well together with the pickings from the body of the lobster; pick the meat from the shell, cut it up into nice square pieces, put half in the salad, the other half reserve for garnishing.
5. Separate the yolks from the whites of 2 hard-boiled eggs; chop the whites very fine, and rub the yolks through a sieve, and afterwards the coral from the inside.
6. Arrange the salad lightly on a glass dish, and garnish, first with a row of sliced cucumber, then with the pieces of lobster, the yolks and whites of the eggs, coral, and beetroot placed alternately, and arranged in small separate bunches, so that the colours contrast nicely.

title: Lobster Sauce, to serve with Turbot, Salmon, Brill, &c.
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 middling-sized hen lobster
- 3/4 pint of melted butter
- 1 tablespoonful of anchovy sauce
- 1/2 oz. of butter
- salt and cayenne to taste
- a little pounded mace when liked
- 2 or 3 tablespoonfuls of cream

## Directions

1. Choose a hen lobster, as this is indispensable, in order to render this sauce as good as it ought to be.
2. Pick the meat from the shells, and cut it into small square pieces.
3. Put the spawn, which will be found under the tail of the lobster, into a mortar with 1/2 oz. of butter, and pound it quite smooth.
4. Rub it through a hair-sieve, and cover up till wanted.
5. Make 3/4 pint of melted butter; put in all the ingredients except the lobster-meat, and well mix the sauce before the lobster is added to it, as it should retain its square form, and not come to table shredded and ragged.
6. Put in the meat, let it get thoroughly hot, but do not allow it to boil, as the colour would immediately be spoiled.
7. It should be remembered that this sauce should always have a bright red appearance.

title: Lobster Soup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 large lobsters, or 6 small ones
- the crumb of a French roll
- 2 anchovies
- 1 onion
- 1 small bunch of sweet herbs
- 1 strip of lemon-peel
- 2 oz. of butter
- a little nutmeg
- 1 teaspoonful of flour
- 1 pint of cream
- 1 pint of milk
- forcemeat balls
- mace
- salt and pepper to taste
- bread crumbs
- 1 egg
- 2 quarts of water

## Directions

1. Pick the meat from the lobsters, and beat the fins, chine, and small claws in a mortar, previously taking away the brown fin and the bag in the head.
2. Put it in a stewpan, with the crumb of the roll, anchovies, onions, herbs, lemon-peel, and the water; simmer gently till all the goodness is extracted, and strain it off.
3. Pound the spawn in a mortar, with the butter, nutmeg, and flour, and mix with it the cream and milk.
4. Give one boil up, at the same time adding the tails cut in pieces.
5. Make the forcemeat balls with the remainder of the lobster, seasoned with mace, pepper, and salt, adding a little flour, and a few bread crumbs; moisten them with the egg, heat them in the soup, and serve.

title: Loin of Veal a la Daube
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The chump end of a loin of veal
- forcemeat No. 417
- a few slices of bacon
- a bunch of savoury herbs
- 2 blades of mace
- 1/2 teaspoonful of whole white pepper
- 1 pint of veal stock or water
- 5 or 6 green onions

## Directions

1. Cut off the chump from a loin of veal, and take out the bone; fill the cavity with forcemeat No. 417, tie it up tightly, and lay it in a stewpan with the bones and trimmings, and cover the veal with a few slices of bacon.
2. Add the herbs, mace, pepper, and onions, and stock or water; cover the pan with a closely-fitting lid, and simmer for 2 hours, shaking the stewpan occasionally.
3. Take out the bacon, herbs, and onions; reduce the gravy, if not already thick enough, to a glaze, with which glaze the meat, and serve with tomato, mushroom, or sorrel sauce.

title: Loin of Veal au Bechamel
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Loin of veal
- 1/2 teaspoonful of minced lemon-peel
- rather more than 1/2 pint of Béchamel or white sauce

## Directions

1. A loin of veal which has come from table with very little taken off, answers very well for this dish.
2. Cut off the meat from the inside, mince it, and mix with it some minced lemon-peel; put it into sufficient Béchamel to warm through.
3. In the mean time, wrap the joint in buttered paper, and place it in the oven to warm.
4. When thoroughly hot, dish the mince, place the loin above it, and pour over the remainder of the Béchamel.

title: Luncheon Cake
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of flour
- 1/2 oz. of caraway seeds
- 1/4 lb. of currants
- 6 oz. of moist sugar
- 1 oz. of candied peel
- 3 eggs
- 1/2 pint of milk
- 1 small teaspoonful of carbonate of soda

## Directions

1. Rub the butter into the flour until it is quite fine; add the caraway seeds, currants (which should be nicely washed, picked, and dried), sugar, and candied peel cut into thin slices; mix these well together, and moisten with the eggs, which should be well whisked.
2. Boil the milk, and add to it, whilst boiling, the carbonate of soda, which must be well stirred into it, and, with the milk, mix the other ingredients.
3. Butter a tin, pour the cake into it, and bake it in a moderate oven from 3/4 to 1 hour.

title: Macaroni Soup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 oz. of macaroni
- a piece of butter the size of a walnut
- salt to taste
- 2 quarts of clear stock No. 105
- grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Throw the macaroni and butter into boiling water, with a pinch of salt, and simmer for 1/2 an hour.
2. When it is tender, drain and cut it into thin rings or lengths, and drop it into the boiling stock.
3. Stew gently for 15 minutes, and serve grated Parmesan cheese with it.

title: Macaroni with Cheese Course I
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of pipe macaroni
- 1/4 lb. of butter
- 6 oz. of Parmesan or Cheshire cheese
- Pepper and salt to taste
- 1 pint of milk
- 2 pints of water
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Put the milk and water into a saucepan with sufficient salt to flavour it; place it on the fire, and, when it boils quickly, drop in the macaroni.
2. Keep the water boiling until it is quite tender; drain the macaroni, and put it into a deep dish.
3. Have ready the grated cheese, either Parmesan or Cheshire; sprinkle it amongst the macaroni and some of the butter cut into small pieces, reserving some of the cheese for the top layer.
4. Season with a little pepper, and cover the top layer of cheese with some very fine bread crumbs.
5. Warm, without oiling, the remainder of the butter, and pour it gently over the bread crumbs.
6. Place the dish before a bright fire to brown the crumbs; turn it once or twice, that it may be equally coloured, and serve very hot.

title: Macaroni with Cheese Course II
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of pipe or riband macaroni
- 1/2 pint of milk
- 1/2 pint of veal or beef gravy
- The yolks of 2 eggs
- 4 tablespoonfuls of cream
- 3 oz. of grated Parmesan or Cheshire cheese
- 1 oz. of butter

## Directions

1. Wash the macaroni, and boil it in the gravy and milk until quite tender, without being broken.
2. Drain it, and put it into rather a deep dish.
3. Beat the yolks of the eggs with the cream and 2 tablespoonfuls of the liquor the macaroni was boiled in; make this sufficiently hot to thicken, but do not allow it to boil; pour it over the macaroni.
4. Over the macaroni, sprinkle the grated cheese and the butter broken into small pieces; brown with a salamander, or before the fire, and serve.

title: Macaroni with Cheese Course III
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of pipe macaroni
- 1/2 pint of brown gravy
- 6 oz. of grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Wash the macaroni, and boil it in salt and water until quite tender; drain it, and put it into rather a deep dish.
2. Have ready a pint of good brown gravy, pour it hot over the macaroni, and send it to table with grated Parmesan served on a separate dish.
3. When the flavour is liked, a little pounded mace may be added to the water in which the macaroni is boiled; but this must always be sparingly added, as it will impart a very strong flavour.

title: Macaroons
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of sweet almonds
- 1/2 lb. of sifted loaf sugar
- the whites of 3 eggs
- wafer-paper
- orange-flower water or plain water
- almond strips
- sugar for strewing

## Directions

1. Blanch, skin, and dry the almonds, and pound them well with a little orange-flower water or plain water
2. add to them the sifted sugar and the whites of the eggs, which should be beaten to a stiff froth
3. mix all the ingredients well together
4. When the paste looks soft, drop it at equal distances from a biscuit-syringe on to sheets of wafer-paper
5. put a strip of almond on the top of each
6. strew some sugar over
7. bake the macaroons in rather a slow oven, of a light brown colour; when hard and set, they are done, and must not be allowed to get very brown

title: Maigre Maitre d'Hotel Sauce (Hot)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of melted butter
- 1 heaped tablespoonful of chopped parsley
- salt and pepper to taste
- juice of 1/2 large lemon
- 2 minced shalots

## Directions

1. Make 1/2 pint of melted butter by recipe No. 376.
2. Stir in the above ingredients, and let them just boil when it is ready to serve.

title: Maitre d'Hotel Sauce (Hot)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 slice of minced ham
- a few poultry-trimmings
- 2 shalots
- 1 clove of garlic
- 1 bay-leaf
- 3/4 pint of water
- 2 oz. of butter
- 1 dessertspoonful of flour
- 1 heaped tablespoonful of chopped parsley
- salt, pepper, and cayenne to taste
- juice of 1/2 large lemon
- 1/4 teaspoonful of pounded sugar

## Directions

1. Put at the bottom of a stewpan the minced ham, and over it the poultry-trimmings (if these are not at hand, veal should be substituted), with the shalots, garlic, and bay-leaf.
2. Pour in the water, and let the whole simmer gently for 1 hour, or until the liquor is reduced to a full 1/2 pint.
3. Strain this gravy, put it in another saucepan.
4. Make a thickening of butter and flour in the above proportions, and stir it to the gravy over a nice clear fire, until it is perfectly smooth and rather thick, care being taken that the butter does not float on the surface.
5. Skim well, add the remaining ingredients, let the sauce gradually heat, but do not allow it to boil.

title: Malt Wine
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 gallons of water
- 28 lbs. of sugar
- 6 quarts of sweet-wort
- 6 quarts of tun
- 3 lbs. of raisins
- 1/2 lb. of candy
- 1 pint of brandy

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar and water together for 10 minutes
2. Skim it well, and put the liquor into a convenient-sized pan or tub
3. Allow it to cool
4. Mix it with the sweet-wort and tun
5. Let it stand for 3 days
6. Put it into a barrel; here it will work or ferment for another three days or more
7. Bung up the cask, and keep it undisturbed for 2 or 3 months
8. After this, add the raisins (whole), the candy, and brandy
9. In 6 months' time, bottle the wine off

title: Manchester Pudding (to eat Cold)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 oz. of grated bread
- 1/2 pint of milk
- a strip of lemon-peel
- 4 eggs
- 2 oz. of butter
- sugar to taste
- puff-paste
- jam
- 3 tablespoonfuls of brandy

## Directions

1. Flavour the milk with lemon-peel, by infusing it in the milk for 1/2 hour; then strain it on to the bread crumbs, and boil it for 2 or 3 minutes; add the eggs, leaving out the whites of 2, the butter, sugar, and brandy; stir all these ingredients well together.
2. Cover a pie-dish with puff-paste, and at the bottom put a thick layer of any kind of jam; pour the above mixture, cold, on the jam, and bake the pudding for an hour.
3. Serve cold, with a little sifted sugar sprinkled over.

title: Manna Kroup Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoonfuls of manna kroup
- 12 bitter almonds
- 1 pint of milk
- sugar to taste
- 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Blanch and pound the almonds in a mortar; mix them with the manna kroup; pour over these a pint of boiling milk, and let them steep for about 1/4 hour.
2. When nearly cold, add sugar and the well-beaten eggs; mix all well together; put the pudding into a buttered dish, and bake for 1/2 hour.

title: Mansfield Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The crumb of 2 rolls
- 1 pint of milk
- sugar to taste
- 4 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of brandy
- 6 oz. of chopped suet
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 1/2 lb. of currants
- 1/2 teaspoonful of grated nutmeg
- 2 tablespoonfuls of cream

## Directions

1. Slice the roll very thin, and pour upon it a pint of boiling milk
2. let it remain covered close for 1/4 hour
3. then beat it up with a fork, and sweeten with moist sugar
4. stir in the chopped suet, flour, currants, and nutmeg
5. Mix these ingredients well together, moisten with the eggs, brandy, and cream
6. beat the mixture for 2 or 3 minutes
7. put it into a buttered dish or mould, and bake in a moderate oven for 1-1/4 hour
8. Turn it out, strew sifted sugar over, and serve

title: Marlborough Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of butter
- 1/4 lb. of powdered lump sugar
- 4 eggs
- puff-paste
- a layer of any kind of jam

## Directions

1. Beat the butter to a cream
2. stir in the powdered sugar
3. whisk the eggs, and add these to the other ingredients
4. When these are well mixed, line a dish with puff-paste
5. spread over a layer of any kind of jam that may be preferred
6. pour in the mixture, and bake the pudding for rather more than 1/2 hour

title: Marmalade and Vermicelli Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 breakfastcupful of vermicelli
- 2 tablespoonfuls of marmalade
- 1/4 lb. of raisins
- sugar to taste
- 3 eggs
- milk

## Directions

1. Pour some boiling milk on the vermicelli, and let it remain covered for 10 minutes
2. then mix with it the marmalade, stoned raisins, sugar, and beaten eggs
3. Stir all well together
4. put the mixture into a buttered mould
5. boil for 1-1/2 hour, and serve with custard sauce

title: Marrow Dumplings
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 oz. of beef marrow
- 1 oz. of butter
- 2 eggs
- 2 penny rolls
- 1 teaspoonful of minced onion
- 1 teaspoonful of minced parsley
- salt and grated nutmeg to taste

## Directions

1. Beat the marrow and butter together to a cream
2. well whisk the eggs, and add these to the other ingredients
3. When they are well stirred, put in the rolls, which should previously be well soaked in boiling milk, strained, and beaten up with a fork
4. Add the remaining ingredients, omitting the minced onion where the flavour is very much disliked
5. form the mixture into small round dumplings
6. Drop these into boiling broth, and let them simmer for about 20 minutes or 1/2 hour

title: Mashed Jerusalem Artichokes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- To each 1 gallon of water allow 1 oz. of salt
- 15 or 16 artichokes
- 1 oz. butter
- pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Boil the artichokes until tender.
2. Drain and press the water from them, and beat them up with a fork.
3. When thoroughly mashed and free from lumps, put them into a saucepan with the butter and a seasoning of white pepper and salt.
4. Keep stirring over the fire until the artichokes are quite hot, and serve.

title: Mashed Jerusalem Artichokes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 gallon of water
- 1 oz. of salt
- 15 or 16 artichokes
- 1 oz. butter
- pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Boil the artichokes as in the preceding recipe until tender
2. Drain and press the water from them
3. Beat them up with a fork
4. When thoroughly mashed and free from lumps, put them into a saucepan with the butter and a seasoning of white pepper and salt
5. Keep stirring over the fire until the artichokes are quite hot
6. Serve

title: Mashed Potatoes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- 1 oz. of butter to every lb. of mashed potatoes
- 2 tablespoonfuls of milk
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Boil the potatoes in their skins
2. When done, drain them, and let them get thoroughly dry by the side of the fire
3. Peel them, and, as they are peeled, put them into a clean saucepan
4. With a large fork beat them to a light paste
5. Add butter, milk, and salt in the above proportion, and stir all the ingredients well over the fire
6. When thoroughly hot, dish them lightly, and draw the fork backwards over the potatoes to make the surface rough, and serve

title: Mashed Turnips
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 or 12 large turnips
- 1/2 gallon of water
- 1 heaped tablespoonful of salt
- 2 oz. of butter
- cayenne or white pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Pare the turnips, quarter them, and put them into boiling water, salted in the above proportion
2. Boil them until tender
3. Drain them in a colander, and squeeze them as dry as possible by pressing them with the back of a large plate
4. When quite free from water, rub the turnips with a wooden spoon through the colander, and put them into a very clean saucepan
5. Add the butter, white pepper, or cayenne, and, if necessary, a little salt
6. Keep stirring them over the fire until the butter is well mixed with them, and the turnips are thoroughly hot
7. Dish, and serve
8. A little cream or milk added after the turnips are pressed through the colander, is an improvement to both the colour and flavour of this vegetable

title: Mashed Turnips
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 or 12 large turnips
- to each 1/2 gallon of water allow 1 heaped tablespoonful of salt
- 2 oz. of butter
- cayenne or white pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Pare the turnips, quarter them, and put them into boiling water, salted in the above proportion; boil them until tender; then drain them in a colander, and squeeze them as dry as possible by pressing them with the back of a large plate.
2. When quite free from water, rub the turnips with a wooden spoon through the colander.

title: Matelot of Tench
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of stock No. 105
- 1/2 pint of port wine
- 1 dozen button onions
- a few mushrooms
- a faggot of herbs
- 2 blades of mace
- 1 oz. of butter
- 1 teaspoonful of minced parsley
- thyme
- 1 shalot
- 2 anchovies
- 1 teacupful of stock No. 105
- flour
- 1 dozen oysters
- juice of 1/2 lemon
- the number of tench, according to size

## Directions

1. Scale and clean the tench, cut them into pieces, and lay them in a stewpan
2. Add the stock, wine, onions, mushrooms, herbs, and mace, and simmer gently for 1/2 hour
3. Put into another stewpan all the remaining ingredients but the oysters and lemon-juice, and boil slowly for 10 minutes
4. Add the strained liquor from the tench, and keep stirring it over the fire until somewhat reduced
5. Rub it through a sieve
6. Pour it over the tench with the oysters, which must be previously scalded in their own liquor
7. Squeeze in the lemon-juice, and serve
8. Garnish with croutons

title: Mayonnaise
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of 2 eggs
- 6 tablespoonfuls of salad-oil
- 4 tablespoonfuls of vinegar
- salt and white pepper to taste
- 1 tablespoonful of white stock
- 2 tablespoonfuls of cream

## Directions

1. Put the yolks of the eggs into a basin, with a seasoning of pepper and salt.
2. Have ready the above quantities of oil and vinegar, in separate vessels.
3. Add them very gradually to the eggs.
4. Continue stirring and rubbing the mixture with a wooden spoon.
5. It should be made in a very cool place, or, if ice is at hand, it should be mixed over it.
6. When the vinegar and oil are well incorporated with the eggs, add the stock and cream, stirring all the time, and it will then be ready for use.

title: Maître d'Hôtel Butter, for putting into Broiled Fish just before it is sent to Table
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of butter
- 2 dessertspoonfuls of minced parsley
- salt and pepper to taste
- the juice of 1 large lemon

## Directions

1. Work the above ingredients well together, and let them be thoroughly mixed with a wooden spoon.
2. If this is used as a sauce, it may be poured either under or over the meat or fish it is intended to be served with.

title: Meat or Sausage Rolls
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of puff-paste
- sausage-meat
- the yolk of 1 egg

## Directions

1. Make 1 lb. of puff-paste and roll it out to the thickness of about 1/2 inch, or rather less
2. Divide it into 8, 10, or 12 squares, according to the size the rolls are intended to be
3. Place some sausage-meat on one-half of each square
4. Wet the edges of the paste, and fold it over the meat
5. Slightly press the edges together, and trim them neatly with a knife
6. Brush the rolls over with the yolk of an egg
7. Bake them in a well-heated oven for about 1/2 hour, or longer should they be very large

title: Medium Puff-Paste
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. flour
- 8 oz. butter
- 4 oz. lard
- not quite 1/2 pint water

## Directions

1. Mix the flour to a smooth paste with not quite 1/2 pint of water
2. Roll it out 3 times, the first time covering the paste with butter, the second with lard, and the third with butter
3. Keep the rolling-pin and paste slightly dredged with flour, to prevent them from sticking

title: Medium Stock
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 lbs. of shin of beef, or 4 lbs. of knuckle of veal, or 2 lbs. of each
- any bones, trimmings of poultry, or fresh meat
- 1/2 a lb. of lean bacon or ham
- 2 oz. of butter
- 2 large onions
- 3 cloves
- 1 turnip
- 3 carrots
- 1/2 a leek
- 1 head of celery
- 2 oz. of salt
- 1/2 a teaspoonful of whole pepper
- 1 large blade of mace
- 1 small bunch of savoury herbs
- 4 quarts and 1/2 pint of cold water

## Directions

1. Cut up the meat and bacon or ham into pieces about 3 inches square
2. Rub the butter on the bottom of the stewpan
3. Put in 1/2 a pint of water, the meat, and all the other ingredients
4. Cover the stewpan, and place it on a sharp fire, occasionally stirring its contents
5. When the bottom of the pan becomes covered with a pale, jelly-like substance, add 4 quarts of cold water, and simmer very gently for 5 hours
6. Do not let it boil quickly
7. Skim off every particle of grease whilst it is doing, and strain it through a fine hair sieve

title: Melted Butter
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of butter
- a dessertspoonful of flour
- 1 wineglassful of water
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Cut the butter up into small pieces, put it in a saucepan, dredge over the flour, and add the water and a seasoning of salt.
2. Stir it one way constantly till the whole of the ingredients are melted and thoroughly blended.
3. Let it just boil, when it is ready to serve.
4. If the butter is to be melted with cream, use the same quantity as of water, but omit the flour; keep stirring it, but do not allow it to boil.

title: Melted Butter (More Economical)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 oz. of butter
- 1 dessertspoonful of flour
- salt to taste
- 1/2 pint of water

## Directions

1. Mix the flour and water to a smooth batter, which put into a saucepan.
2. Add the butter and a seasoning of salt, keep stirring one way till all the ingredients are melted and perfectly smooth.
3. Let the whole boil for a minute or two, and serve.

title: Melted Butter (the French Sauce Blanche)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of fresh butter
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- salt to taste
- 1/2 gill of water
- 1/2 spoonful of white vinegar
- a very little grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Mix the flour and water to a smooth batter, carefully rubbing down with the back of a spoon any lumps that may appear.
2. Put it in a saucepan with all the other ingredients, and let it thicken on the fire, but do not allow it to boil, lest it should taste of the flour.

title: Melted Butter Made with Milk
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teaspoonful of flour
- 2 oz. butter
- 1/3 pint of milk
- a few grains of salt

## Directions

1. Mix the butter and flour smoothly together on a plate, put it into a lined saucepan, and pour in the milk.
2. Keep stirring it one way over a sharp fire.
3. Let it boil quickly for a minute or two, and it is ready to serve.

title: Meringues
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of pounded sugar
- the whites of 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Whisk the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth, and, with a wooden spoon, stir in quickly the pounded sugar.
2. Have some boards thick enough to put in the oven to prevent the bottom of the meringues from acquiring too much colour.
3. Cut some strips of paper about 2 inches wide; place this paper on the board, and drop a tablespoonful at a time of the mixture on the paper, taking care to let all the meringues be the same size.
4. In dropping it from the spoon, give the mixture the form of an egg, and keep the meringues about 2 inches apart from each other on the paper.
5. Strew over them some sifted sugar, and bake in a moderate oven for 1/2 hour.
6. As soon as they begin to colour, remove them from the oven.
7. Take each slip of paper by the two ends, and turn it gently on the table, and, with a small spoon, take out the soft part of each meringue.
8. Spread some clean paper on the board, turn the meringues upside down, and put them into the oven to harden and brown on the other side.
9. When required for table, fill them with whipped cream, flavoured with liqueur.

title: Military Puddings
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of suet
- 1/2 lb. of bread crumbs
- 1/2 lb. of moist sugar
- the rind and juice of 1 large lemon

## Directions

1. Chop the suet finely, mix it with the bread crumbs and sugar
2. mince the lemon-rind and strain the juice
3. stir these into the other ingredients
4. mix well, and put the mixture into small buttered cups, and bake for rather more than 1/2 hour
5. turn them out on the dish, and serve with lemon-sauce
6. The above ingredients may be made into small balls, and boiled for about 1/2 hour; they should then be served with the same sauce as when baked

title: Milk Soup (a Nice Dish for Children)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts of milk
- 1 saltspoonful of salt
- 1 teaspoonful of powdered cinnamon
- 3 teaspoonfuls of pounded sugar
- 4 thin slices of bread
- the yolks of 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Boil the milk with the salt, cinnamon, and sugar.
2. Lay the bread in a deep dish, pour over it a little of the milk, and keep it hot over a stove, without burning.
3. Beat up the yolks of the eggs, add them to the milk, and stir it over the fire till it thickens.
4. Do not let it curdle.
5. Pour it upon the bread, and serve.

title: Mince Pies
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Good puff-paste No. 1205
- mincemeat No. 1309
- the white of an egg
- pounded sugar

## Directions

1. Make some good puff-paste by recipe No. 1205; roll it out to the thickness of about 1/4 inch, and line some good-sized pattypans with it.
2. Fill them with mincemeat, cover with the paste, and cut it off all round close to the edge of the tin.
3. Put the pies into a brisk oven, to draw the paste up, and bake for 25 minutes, or longer, should the pies be very large.
4. Brush them over with the white of an egg, beaten with the blade of a knife to a stiff froth; sprinkle over pounded sugar, and put them into the oven for a minute or two, to dry the egg.
5. Dish the pies on a white d'oyley, and serve hot.
6. They may be merely sprinkled with pounded sugar instead of being glazed, when that mode is preferred.
7. To re-warm them, put the pies on the pattypans, and let them remain in the oven for 10 minutes or 1/4 hour, and they will be almost as good as if freshly made.

title: Minced Beef (Cold Meat Cookery)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 oz. of butter
- 1 small onion
- 2 tablespoonfuls of gravy left from the meat
- 1 tablespoonful of strong ale
- 1/2 a teaspoonful of flour
- salt and pepper to taste
- a few slices of lean roast beef

## Directions

1. Put into a stewpan the butter with an onion chopped fine
2. Add the gravy, ale, and 1/2 a teaspoonful of flour to thicken
3. Season with pepper and salt, and stir these ingredients over the fire until the onion is a rich brown
4. Cut, but do not chop the meat very fine, add it to the gravy
5. Stir till quite hot, and serve
6. Garnish with sippets of toasted bread
7. Be careful in not allowing the gravy to boil after the meat is added, as it would render it hard and tough

title: Minced Beef Cakes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast beef
- 1/4 lb. of bacon or ham per pound of cold meat
- Pepper and salt to taste
- 1 small bunch of minced savoury herbs
- 1 or 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Mince the beef very finely (if underdone it will be better)
2. Add to it the bacon, which must also be chopped very small, and mix well together
3. Season and stir in the herbs
4. Bind with an egg, or 2 should 1 not be sufficient
5. Make it into small square cakes, about 1/2 inch thick
6. Fry them in hot dripping
7. Serve in a dish with good gravy poured round them

title: Minced Collops (an Entree)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of rump-steak
- salt and pepper to taste
- 2 oz. of butter
- 1 onion minced
- 1/4 pint of water
- 1 tablespoonful of Harvey's sauce, or lemon-juice, or mushroom ketchup
- 1 small bunch of savoury herbs

## Directions

1. Mince the beef and onion very small, and fry the latter in butter until of a pale brown.
2. Put all the ingredients together in a stewpan, and boil gently for about 10 minutes.
3. Garnish with sippets of toasted bread, and serve very hot.

title: Minced Fowl
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- remains of cold roast fowl
- 2 hard-boiled eggs
- salt
- cayenne
- pounded mace
- 1 onion
- 1 faggot of savoury herbs
- 6 tablespoonfuls of cream
- 1 oz. of butter
- two teaspoonfuls of flour
- 1/2 teaspoonful of finely-minced lemon-peel
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon-juice
- sippets of toasted bread

## Directions

1. Cut out from the fowl all the white meat, and mince it finely without any skin or bone
2. Put the bones, skin, and trimmings into a stewpan with an onion, a bunch of savoury herbs, a blade of mace, and nearly a pint of water
3. Let this stew for an hour, then strain the liquor
4. Chop the eggs small and mix them with the fowl
5. Add salt, cayenne, and pounded mace, put in the gravy and remaining ingredients
6. Let the whole just boil, and serve with sippets of toasted bread

title: Minced Fowl A La Bechamel
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast fowl
- 6 tablespoonfuls of Béchamel sauce
- 6 tablespoonfuls of white stock
- the white of 1 egg
- bread crumbs
- clarified butter

## Directions

1. Take the remains of roast fowls, mince the white meat very small, and put it into a stewpan with the Béchamel and stock
2. Stir it well over the fire, and just let it boil up
3. Pour the mince into a dish
4. Beat up the white of egg, spread it over, and strew on it a few grated bread crumbs
5. Pour a very little clarified butter on the whole, and brown either before the fire or with a salamander
6. Serve in a silver dish, if at hand

title: Minced Veal
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast fillet or loin of veal
- rather more than 1 pint of water
- 1 onion
- 1/2 teaspoonful of minced lemon-peel
- salt and white pepper to taste
- 1 blade of pounded mace
- 2 or 3 young carrots
- a faggot of sweet herbs
- thickening of butter and flour
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon-juice
- 3 tablespoonfuls of cream or milk

## Directions

1. Take about 1 lb. of veal, and should there be any bones, dredge them with flour, and put them into a stewpan with the brown outside, and a few meat trimmings; add rather more than a pint of water, the onion cut in slices, lemon-peel, seasoning, mace, carrots, and herbs; simmer these well for rather more than 1 hour, and strain the liquor.
2. Rub a little flour into some butter; add this to the gravy, set it on the fire, and, when it boils, skim well.
3. Mince the veal finely by cutting, and not chopping it; put it in the gravy; let it get warmed through gradually; add the lemon-juice and cream, and, when it is on the point of boiling, serve.
4. Garnish the dish with sippets of toasted bread and slices of bacon rolled and toasted.
5. Forcemeat balls may also be added.
6. If more lemon-peel is liked than is stated above, put a little very finely minced to the veal, after it is warmed in the gravy.

title: Minced Veal and Macaroni
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 lb. of minced cold roast veal
- 3 oz. of ham
- 1 tablespoonful of gravy
- pepper and salt to taste
- 3 teaspoonful of grated nutmeg
- 1/4 lb. of bread crumbs
- 1/4 lb. of macaroni
- 1 or 2 eggs to bind
- a small piece of butter

## Directions

1. Cut some nice slices from a cold fillet of veal, trim off the brown outside, and mince the meat finely with the above proportion of ham: should the meat be very dry, add a spoonful of good gravy.
2. Season highly with pepper and salt, add the grated nutmeg and bread crumbs, and mix these ingredients with 1 or 2 eggs well beaten, which should bind the mixture and make it like forcemeat.
3. In the mean time, boil the macaroni in salt and water, and drain it; butter a mould, put some of the macaroni at the bottom and sides of it, in whatever form is liked; mix the remainder with the forcemeat, fill the mould up to the top, put a plate or small dish on it, and steam for 1/2 hour.
4. Turn it out carefully, and serve with good gravy poured round, but not over, the meat.

title: Minced Veal with Béchamel Sauce
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of a fillet of veal
- 1 pint of Béchamel sauce No. 367
- 1/2 teaspoonful of minced lemon-peel
- forcemeat balls

## Directions

1. Cut--but do not chop--a few slices of cold roast veal as finely as possible, sufficient to make rather more than 1 lb., weighed after being minced.
2. Make the above proportion of Béchamel, by recipe No. 367; add the lemon-peel, put in the veal, and let the whole gradually warm through.
3. When it is at the point of simmering, dish it, and garnish with forcemeat balls and fried sippets of bread.

title: Mincemeat
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of raisins
- 3 lbs. of currants
- 1-1/2 lb. of lean beef
- 3 lbs. of beef suet
- 2 lbs. of moist sugar
- 2 oz. of citron
- 2 oz. of candied lemon-peel
- 2 oz. of candied orange-peel
- 1 small nutmeg
- 1 pottle of apples
- the rind of 2 lemons
- the juice of 1
- 1/2 pint of brandy

## Directions

1. Stone and cut the raisins once or twice across, but do not chop them
2. wash, dry, and pick the currants free from stalks and grit, and mince the beef and suet, taking care that the latter is chopped very fine
3. slice the citron and candied peel
4. grate the nutmeg, and pare, core, and mince the apples
5. mince the lemon-peel, strain the juice
6. when all the ingredients are thus prepared, mix them well together, adding the brandy when the other things are well blended
7. press the whole into a jar, carefully exclude the air, and the mincemeat will be ready for use in a fortnight

title: Mincemeat
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 large lemons
- 3 large apples
- 1 lb. of stoned raisins
- 1 lb. of currants
- 1 lb. of suet
- 2 lbs. of moist sugar
- 1 oz. of sliced candied citron
- 1 oz. of sliced candied orange-peel
- 1 oz. of sliced candied lemon-peel
- 1 teacupful of brandy
- 2 tablespoonfuls of orange marmalade

## Directions

1. Grate the rinds of the lemons; squeeze out the juice, strain it, and boil the remainder of the lemons until tender enough to pulp or chop very finely.
2. Add to this pulp the apples, which should be baked, and their skins and cores removed.
3. Put in the remaining ingredients one by one, and, as they are added, mix everything very thoroughly together.
4. Put the mincemeat into a stone jar with a closely-fitting lid, and in a fortnight it will be ready for use.

title: Miniature Bread Puddings
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of milk
- 1/2 lb. of bread crumbs
- 4 eggs
- 2 oz. of butter
- sugar to taste
- 2 tablespoonfuls of brandy
- 1 teaspoonful of finely-minced lemon-peel

## Directions

1. Make the milk boiling, pour it on to the bread crumbs, and let them soak for about 1/2 hour
2. Beat the eggs, mix these with the bread crumbs, add the remaining ingredients, and stir well until all is thoroughly mixed
3. Butter some small cups; rather more than half fill them with the mixture, and bake in a moderate oven from 20 minutes to 1/2 hour, and serve with sweet sauce
4. A few currants may be added to these puddings: about 3 oz. will be found sufficient for the above quantity

title: Miniature Rice Puddings
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of rice
- 1-1/2 pint of milk
- 2 oz. of fresh butter
- 4 eggs
- sugar to taste
- flavouring of lemon-peel, bitter almonds, or vanilla
- a few strips of candied peel

## Directions

1. Let the rice swell in 1 pint of the milk over a slow fire, putting with it a strip of lemon-peel
2. Stir to it the butter and the other 1/2 pint of milk, and let the mixture cool
3. Add the well-beaten eggs, and a few drops of essence of almonds or essence of vanilla, whichever may be preferred
4. Butter well some small cups or moulds
5. Line them with a few pieces of candied peel sliced very thin
6. Fill them three parts full, and bake for about 40 minutes
7. Turn them out of the cups on to a white d'oyley, and serve with sweet sauce

title: Miniature Round of Beef
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- From 5 to 10 lbs. of rib of beef
- sufficient brine to cover the meat
- salt
- tape

## Directions

1. Choose a fine rib, have the bone removed, rub some salt over the inside, and skewer the meat up into a nice round form, and bind it with tape
2. Put it into sufficient brine to cover it (the brine should be made by recipe No. 654), and let it remain for 6 days, turning the meat every day
3. When required to be dressed, drain from the pickle, and put the meat into very hot water
4. Let it boil rapidly for a few minutes, when draw the pot to the side of the fire, and let it simmer very gently until done
5. Remove the skewer, and replace it by a plated or silver one
6. Carrots and turnips should be served with this dish, and may be boiled with the meat

title: Mint Sauce
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 dessertspoonfuls of chopped mint
- 2 dessertspoonfuls of pounded white sugar
- 1/4 pint of vinegar

## Directions

1. Wash the mint, which should be young and fresh-gathered, free from grit.
2. Pick the leaves from the stalks, mince them very fine, and put them into a tureen.
3. Add the sugar and vinegar, and stir till the former is dissolved.

title: Mint Vinegar
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- vinegar
- mint

## Directions

1. Procure some nice fresh mint, pick the leaves from the stalks, and fill a bottle or jar with them.
2. Add vinegar to them until the bottle is full.
3. Cover closely to exclude the air, and let it infuse for a fortnight.
4. Strain the liquor, and put it into small bottles for use, of which the corks should be sealed.

title: Miroton of Beef
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- A few slices of cold roast beef
- 3 oz. of butter
- salt and pepper to taste
- 3 onions
- 1/2 pint of gravy

## Directions

1. Slice the onions and put them into a frying-pan with the cold beef and butter
2. Place it over the fire, and keep turning and stirring the ingredients to prevent them burning
3. When of a pale brown, add the gravy and seasoning
4. Let it simmer for a few minutes, and serve very hot

title: Mixed Pickle
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 gallon of vinegar
- 1/4 lb. of bruised ginger
- 1/4 lb. of mustard
- 1/4 lb. of salt
- 2 oz. of mustard-seed
- 1-1/2 oz. of turmeric
- 1 oz. of ground black pepper
- 1/4 oz. of cayenne
- cauliflowers
- onions
- celery
- sliced cucumbers
- gherkins
- French beans
- nasturtiums
- capsicums

## Directions

1. Have a large jar, with a tightly-fitting lid, in which put as much vinegar as required, reserving a little to mix the various powders to a smooth paste.
2. Put into a basin the mustard, turmeric, pepper, and cayenne; mix them with vinegar, and stir well until no lumps remain.
3. Add all the ingredients to the vinegar, and mix well.
4. Keep this liquor in a warm place, and thoroughly stir every morning for a month with a wooden spoon.
5. As vegetables come into season, have them gathered on a dry day, and, after merely wiping them with a cloth, to free them from moisture, put them into the pickle.
6. Divide the cauliflowers into small bunches.
7. Put all these into the pickle raw, and at the end of the season, when there have been added as many of the vegetables as could be procured, store it away in jars, and tie over with bladder.

title: Mock Turtle
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 a calf's head
- 1/4 lb. of butter
- 1/4 lb. of lean ham
- 2 tablespoonfuls of minced parsley
- a little minced lemon thyme
- sweet marjoram
- basil
- 2 onions
- a few chopped mushrooms
- 2 shallots
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 1/4 bottle of Madeira or sherry
- force-meat balls
- cayenne
- salt and mace to taste
- the juice of 1 lemon and 1 Seville orange
- 1 dessert-spoonful of pounded sugar
- 3 quarts of best stock

## Directions

1. Scald the head with the skin on, remove the brain, tie the head up in a cloth, and let it boil for 1 hour.
2. Take the meat from the bones, cut it into small square pieces, and throw them into cold water.
3. Put the meat into a stewpan, and cover with stock; let it boil gently for an hour, or rather more, if not quite tender, and set it on one side.
4. Melt the butter in another stewpan, and add the ham, cut small, with the herbs, parsley, onions, shallots, mushrooms, and nearly a pint of stock; let these simmer slowly for 2 hours, and then dredge in as much flour as will dry up the butter.
5. Fill up with the remainder of the stock, add the wine, let it stew gently for 10 minutes, rub it through a tammy, and put it to the calf's head.
6. Season with cayenne, and, if required, a little salt; add the juice of the orange and lemon; and when liked, 1/4 teaspoonful of pounded mace, and the sugar.
7. Put in the force-meat balls, simmer 5 minutes, and serve very hot.

title: Mock Turtle (More Economical)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- A knuckle of veal weighing 5 or 6 lbs.
- 2 cow-heels
- 2 large onions stuck with cloves
- 1 bunch of sweet herbs
- 3 blades of mace
- salt to taste
- 12 peppercorns
- 1 glass of sherry
- 24 force-meat balls
- a little lemon-juice
- 4 quarts of water

## Directions

1. Put all the ingredients, except the force-meat balls and lemon-juice, in an earthen jar, and stew for 6 hours.
2. Do not open it till cold.
3. When wanted for use, skim off all the fat, and strain carefully; place it on the fire, cut up the meat into inch-and-a-half squares, put it, with the force-meat balls and lemon-juice, into the soup, and serve.
4. It can be flavoured with a tablespoonful of anchovy, or Harvey's sauce.

title: Monday's Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold plum-pudding
- brandy
- 5 eggs
- 1 pint of milk
- nutmeg or lemon-rind for flavouring

## Directions

1. Cut the remains of a good cold plum-pudding into finger-pieces, soak them in a little brandy, and lay them cross-barred in a mould until full.
2. Make a custard with the proportion of milk and eggs, flavouring it with nutmeg or lemon-rind.
3. Fill up the mould with it; tie it down with a cloth, and boil or steam it for an hour.
4. Serve with a little of the custard poured over, to which has been added a tablespoonful of brandy.

title: More Economical Rich Baked Apple Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 large apples
- 6 oz. of moist sugar
- 1/4 lb. of butter
- 4 eggs
- 1 pint of bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Pare, core, and cut the apples, as for sauce
2. Boil them until reduced to a pulp
3. Add the butter, melted, and the eggs, which should be well whisked
4. Beat up the pudding for 2 or 3 minutes
5. Butter a pie-dish
6. Put in a layer of bread crumbs
7. Then the apple
8. Then another layer of bread crumbs
9. Flake over these a few tiny pieces of butter
10. Bake for about 1/2 hour

title: Moulded Minced Veal
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 lb. of cold roast veal
- a small slice of bacon
- 1/4 teaspoonful of minced lemon-peel
- 1/2 onion chopped fine
- salt, pepper, and pounded mace to taste
- a slice of toast soaked in milk
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Mince the meat very fine, after removing from it all skin and outside pieces, and chop the bacon; mix these well together, adding the lemon-peel, onion, seasoning, mace, and toast.
2. When all the ingredients are thoroughly incorporated, heat up an egg, with which bind the mixture.
3. Butter a shape, put in the meat, and bake for 3/4 hour; turn it out of the mould carefully, and pour round it a good brown gravy.

title: Moulded Pears
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large pears or 6 small ones
- 8 cloves
- sugar to taste
- water
- a small piece of cinnamon
- 1/4 pint of raisin wine
- a strip of lemon-peel
- the juice of 1/2 lemon
- 1/2 oz. of gelatine

## Directions

1. Peel and cut the pears into quarters; put them into a jar with 3/4 pint of water, cloves, cinnamon, and sufficient sugar to sweeten the whole nicely.
2. Cover down the top of the jar, and bake the pears in a gentle oven until perfectly tender, but do not allow them to break.
3. When done, lay the pears in a plain mould, which should be well wetted, and boil 1/2 pint of the liquor the pears were baked in with the wine, lemon-peel, strained juice, and gelatine.
4. Let these ingredients boil quickly for 5 minutes, then strain the liquid warm over the pears.
5. Put the mould in a cool place, and when the jelly is firm, turn it out on a glass dish.

title: Muffins
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of milk
- 1-1/2 oz. of German yeast
- a little salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Warm the milk, add to it the yeast, and mix these well together; put them into a pan, and stir in sufficient flour to make the whole into a dough of rather a soft consistence.
2. Cover it over with a cloth, and place it in a warm place to rise, and, when light and nicely risen, divide the dough into pieces, and round them to the proper shape with the hands.
3. Place them in a layer of flour about two inches thick on wooden trays, and let them rise again; when this is effected, they each will exhibit a semi-globular shape.
4. Place them carefully on a hot-plate or stove, and bake them until they are slightly browned, turning them when they are done on one side.
5. To toast them: divide the edge of the muffin all round, by pulling it open, to the depth of about an inch, with the fingers.
6. Put it on a toasting-fork, and hold it before a very clear fire until one side is nicely browned, but not burnt; turn, and toast it on the other.
7. Do not toast them too quickly, as, if this is done, the middle of the muffin will not be warmed through.
8. When done, divide them by pulling them open; butter them slightly on both sides, put them together again, and cut them into halves.
9. When sufficient are toasted and buttered, pile them on a very hot dish, and send them very quickly to table.

title: Mullagatawny Soup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoonfuls of curry powder
- 6 onions
- 1 clove of garlic
- 1 oz. of pounded almonds
- a little lemon-pickle, or mango-juice, to taste
- 1 fowl or rabbit
- 4 slices of lean bacon
- 2 quarts of medium stock, or, if wanted very good, best stock

## Directions

1. Slice and fry the onions of a nice colour; line the stewpan with the bacon.
2. Cut up the rabbit or fowl into small joints, and slightly brown them.
3. Put in the fried onions, the garlic, and stock, and simmer gently till the meat is tender; skim very carefully.
4. When the meat is done, rub the curry powder to a smooth batter; add it to the soup with the almonds, which must be first pounded with a little of the stock.
5. Put in seasoning and lemon-pickle or mango-juice to taste, and serve boiled rice with it.

title: Mushroom Ketchup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- full-grown mushroom-flaps
- 1/2 lb. of salt per peck of mushrooms
- 1/4 oz. of cayenne per quart of mushroom-liquor
- 1/2 oz. of allspice per quart of mushroom-liquor
- 1/2 oz. of ginger per quart of mushroom-liquor
- 2 blades of pounded mace per quart of mushroom-liquor
- brandy

## Directions

1. Choose full-grown mushroom-flaps, and take care they are perfectly fresh-gathered when the weather is tolerably dry
2. Put a layer of them in a deep pan, sprinkle salt over them, and then another layer of mushrooms, and so on alternately
3. Let them remain for a few hours, when break them up with the hand; put them in a nice cool place for 3 days, occasionally stirring and mashing them well, to extract from them as much juice as possible
4. Measure the quantity of liquor without straining, and to each quart allow the above proportion of spices
5. Put all into a stone jar, cover it up very closely, put it in a saucepan of boiling water, set it over the fire, and let it boil for 3 hours
6. Turn into a nice clean stewpan the contents of the jar, and let the whole simmer very gently for 1/2 hour
7. Pour it into a jug, where it should stand in a cool place till the next day
8. Pour it off into another jug, and strain it into very dry clean bottles, and do not squeeze the mushrooms
9. To each pint of ketchup add a few drops of brandy
10. Cork well, and either seal or rosin the cork, so as perfectly to exclude the air

title: Mushroom Powder
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 peck of large mushrooms
- 2 onions
- 12 cloves
- 1/4 oz. of pounded mace
- 2 teaspoonfuls of white pepper

## Directions

1. Peel the mushrooms, wipe them perfectly free from grit and dirt, remove the black fur, and reject all those that are at all worm-eaten.
2. Put them into a stewpan with the above ingredients, but without water.
3. Shake them over a clear fire, till all the liquor is dried up, and be careful not to let them burn.
4. Arrange them on tins, and dry them in a slow oven.
5. Pound them to a fine powder, which put into small dry bottles.
6. Cork well, seal the corks, and keep it in a dry place.
7. In using this powder, add it to the gravy just before serving, when it will merely require one boil-up.

title: Mutton Broth, Quickly Made
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 or 2 chops from a neck of mutton
- 1 pint of water
- a small bunch of sweet herbs
- 1/4 of an onion
- pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into small pieces, put it into a saucepan with the bones, but no skin or fat
2. add the other ingredients
3. cover the saucepan, and bring the water quickly to boil
4. Take the lid off, and continue the rapid boiling for 20 minutes, skimming it well during the process
5. strain the broth into a basin
6. if there should be any fat left on the surface, remove it by laying a piece of thin paper on the top: the greasy particles will adhere to the paper, and so free the preparation from them

title: Mutton Collops (Cold Meat Cookery)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- a few slices of a cold leg or loin of mutton
- salt and pepper to taste
- 1 blade of pounded mace
- 1 small bunch of savoury herbs minced very fine
- 2 or 3 shalots
- 2 or 3 oz. of butter
- 1 dessertspoonful of flour
- 1/2 pint of gravy
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon-juice

## Directions

1. Cut some very thin slices from a leg or the chump end of a loin of mutton; sprinkle them with pepper, salt, pounded mace, minced savoury herbs, and minced shalot.
2. Fry them in butter, stir in a dessertspoonful of flour, add the gravy and lemon-juice, simmer very gently about 5 or 7 minutes, and serve immediately.

title: Mutton Cutlets with Mashed Potatoes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- about 3 lbs. of the best end of the neck of mutton
- salt and pepper to taste
- mashed potatoes

## Directions

1. Procure a well-hung neck of mutton, saw off about 3 inches of the top of the bones, and cut the cutlets of a moderate thickness.
2. Shape them by chopping off the thick part of the chine-bone; beat them flat with a cutlet-chopper, and scrape quite clean, a portion of the top of the bone.
3. Broil them over a nice clear fire for about 7 or 8 minutes, and turn them frequently.
4. Have ready some smoothly-mashed white potatoes; place these in the middle of the dish; when the cutlets are done, season with pepper and salt; arrange them round the potatoes, with the thick end of the cutlets downwards, and serve very hot and quickly.

title: Mutton Pie
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of the neck or loin of mutton, weighed after being boned
- 2 kidneys
- pepper and salt to taste
- 2 teacupfuls of gravy or water
- 2 tablespoonfuls of minced parsley
- a little minced onion or shalot
- puff crust

## Directions
