- a very little grated lemon-rind
- 2 small wineglassfuls of brandy

## Directions

1. Separate the yolks from the whites of 3 eggs, and put the former into a stewpan; add the sugar, milk, and grated lemon-rind, and stir over the fire until the mixture thickens; but do not allow it to boil.
2. Put in the brandy; let the sauce stand by the side of the fire, to get quite hot; keep stirring it, and serve in a boat or tureen separately, or pour it over the pudding.

title: Spanish Onions--Pickled
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Onions
- vinegar
- salt
- cayenne

## Directions

1. Cut the onions in thin slices
2. Put a layer of them in the bottom of a jar
3. Sprinkle with salt and cayenne
4. Add another layer of onions and season as before
5. Proceed in this manner till the jar is full
6. Pour in sufficient vinegar to cover the whole
7. The pickle will be fit for use in a month

title: Spiced Beef
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 lbs. of the thick flank or rump of beef
- 1/2 lb. of coarse sugar
- 1 oz. of saltpetre
- 1/4 lb. of pounded allspice
- 1 lb. of common salt

## Directions

1. Rub the sugar well into the beef, and let it lay for 12 hours.
2. Then rub the saltpetre and allspice, both of which should be pounded, over the meat, and let it remain for another 12 hours.
3. Then rub in the salt.
4. Turn daily in the liquor for a fortnight.
5. Soak it for a few hours in water, dry with a cloth, cover with a coarse paste, put a little water at the bottom of the pan, and bake in a moderate oven for 4 hours.
6. If it is not covered with a paste, be careful to put the beef into a deep vessel, and cover with a plate, or it will be too crisp.
7. During the time the meat is in the oven it should be turned once or twice.

title: Spinach Dressed With Cream, à la Française
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pailfuls of spinach
- 2 tablespoonfuls of salt
- 2 oz. of butter
- 8 tablespoonfuls of cream
- 1 small teaspoonful of pounded sugar
- a very little grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Boil and drain the spinach as in recipe No. 1155
2. chop it finely, and put it into a stewpan with the butter
3. stir over a gentle fire, and, when the butter has dried away, add the remaining ingredients, and simmer for about 5 minutes
4. Previously to adding the cream, boil it first, in case it should curdle
5. Serve on a hot dish, and garnish either with sippets of toasted bread or leaves of puff-paste

title: Spinach Soup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- As much spinach as, when boiled, will half fill a vegetable-dish
- 2 quarts of very clear medium stock, No. 105

## Directions

1. Make the cooked spinach into balls the size of an egg, and slip them into the soup-tureen

title: Spinach Soup (French Recipe)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- As much spinach as, when boiled, will half fill a vegetable-dish
- 2 quarts of very clear medium stock, No. 105

## Directions

1. Make the cooked spinach into balls the size of an egg
2. Slip them into the soup-tureen

title: Split Pea Soup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 pint of split peas
- a teacupful of gravy
- 4 young onions
- 1 lettuce cut small
- 1/2 a head of celery
- 1/2 a pint of asparagus cut small
- 1/2 a pint of cream
- 3 quarts of water
- spinach juice for colouring

## Directions

1. Boil the peas, and rub them through a sieve
2. Add the gravy
3. Stew by themselves the celery, onions, lettuce, and asparagus, with the water
4. Stew altogether, and add the colouring and cream
5. Serve

title: Sponge Cake
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of loaf sugar
- not quite 1/4 pint of water
- 5 eggs
- 1 lemon
- 1/2 lb. of flour
- 1/4 teaspoonful of carbonate of soda

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar and water together until they form a thick syrup
2. Let it cool a little, then pour it to the eggs, which should be previously well whisked
3. After the eggs and syrup are mixed together, continue beating them for a few minutes
4. Grate the lemon-rind, mix the carbonate of soda with the flour, and stir these lightly to the other ingredients
5. Add the lemon-juice, and when the whole is thoroughly mixed, pour it into a buttered mould
6. Bake in rather a quick oven for rather more than 1 hour

title: Sponge-Cake
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The weight of 8 eggs in pounded loaf sugar
- the weight of 5 in flour
- the rind of 1 lemon
- 1 tablespoonful of brandy

## Directions

1. Put the eggs into one side of the scale, and take the weight of 8 in pounded loaf sugar, and the weight of 5 in good dry flour.
2. Separate the yolks from the whites of the eggs; beat the former, put them into a saucepan with the sugar, and let them remain over the fire until milk-warm, keeping them well stirred.
3. Then put them into a basin, add the grated lemon-rind mixed with the brandy, and stir these well together, dredging in the flour very gradually.
4. Whisk the whites of the eggs to a very stiff froth, stir them to the flour, &c., and beat the cake well for 1/4 hour.
5. Put it into a buttered mould strewn with a little fine sifted sugar, and bake the cake in a quick oven for 1-1/2 hour.
6. Care must be taken that it is put into the oven immediately, or it will not be light.

title: Sprats
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- sprats
- flour
- suet

## Directions

1. Wipe them dry; fasten them in rows by a skewer run through the eyes.
2. Dredge with flour, and broil them on a gridiron over a nice clear fire.
3. The gridiron should be rubbed with suet.
4. Serve very hot.

title: Sprats Fried in Batter
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- flour
- bread crumbs
- salt
- pepper to taste
- fried parsley

## Directions

1. Wipe the sprats, and dip them in a batter made of the eggs, flour, and bread crumbs seasoned with salt and pepper.
2. Fry of a nice brown, serve very hot, and garnish with fried parsley.

title: Stew Soup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of beef
- 5 onions
- 5 turnips
- 3/4 lb. of rice
- a large bunch of parsley
- a few sweet herbs
- pepper and salt
- 2 quarts of water

## Directions

1. Cut the beef up in small pieces, add the other ingredients, and boil gently for 2 1/2 hours

title: Stew Soup II
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of beef, mutton, or pork
- 1/2 pint of split peas
- 4 turnips
- 8 potatoes
- 2 onions
- 2 oz. of oatmeal or 3 oz. of rice
- 2 quarts of water

## Directions

1. Cut the meat in small pieces, as also the vegetables, and add them, with the peas, to the water
2. Boil gently for 3 hours
3. thicken with the oatmeal, boil for another 1/4 hour, stirring all the time, and season with pepper and salt

title: Stew Soup of Salt Meat
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of salt beef or pork
- 4 carrots
- 4 parsnips
- 4 turnips
- 4 potatoes
- 1 cabbage
- 2 oz. of oatmeal or ground rice
- seasoning of salt and pepper
- 2 quarts of water

## Directions

1. Cut up the meat small, add the water, and let it simmer for 2 3/4 hours
2. add the vegetables, cut in thin small slices
3. season, and boil for 1 hour
4. Thicken with the oatmeal, and serve

title: Stewed Apples and Custard
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 good-sized apples
- the rind of 1/2 lemon or 4 cloves
- 1/2 lb. of sugar
- 3/4 pint of water
- 1/2 pint of custard No. 1423

## Directions

1. Pare and take out the cores of the apples, without dividing them, and, if possible, leave the stalks on
2. Boil the sugar and water together for 10 minutes
3. Put in the apples with the lemon-rind or cloves, whichever flavour may be preferred, and simmer gently until they are tender, taking care not to let them break
4. Dish them neatly on a glass dish
5. Reduce the syrup by boiling it quickly for a few minutes
6. Let it cool a little
7. Pour it over the apples
8. Have ready quite 1/2 pint of custard made by recipe No. 1423
9. Pour it round, but not over, the apples when they are quite cold
10. A few almonds blanched and cut into strips, and stuck in the apples, would improve their appearance

title: Stewed Beef and Celery Sauce
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 roots of celery
- 1 pint of gravy
- 2 onions sliced
- 2 lbs. of cold roast or boiled beef

## Directions

1. Cut the celery into 2-inch pieces, put them in a stew-pan, with the gravy and onions, simmer gently until the celery is tender.
2. When the celery is tender, add the beef cut into rather thick pieces.
3. Stew gently for 10 minutes, and serve with fried potatoes.

title: Stewed Beef or Rump Steak
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- About 2 lbs. of beef or rump steak
- 3 onions
- 2 turnips
- 3 carrots
- 2 or 3 oz. of butter
- 1/2 pint of water
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 1/2 teaspoonful of pepper
- 1 tablespoonful of ketchup
- 1 tablespoonful of flour

## Directions

1. Have the steaks cut tolerably thick and rather lean; divide them into convenient-sized pieces, and fry them in the butter a nice brown on both sides.
2. Cleanse and pare the vegetables, cut the onions and carrots into thin slices, and the turnips into dice, and fry these in the same fat that the steaks were done in.
3. Put all into a saucepan, add 1/2 pint of water, or rather more should it be necessary, and simmer very gently for 2-1/2 or 3 hours.
4. When nearly done, skim well, add salt, pepper, and ketchup in the above proportions, and thicken with a tablespoonful of flour mixed with 2 of cold water.
5. Let it boil up for a minute or two after the thickening is added, and serve.

title: Stewed Beef With Oysters
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- A few thick steaks of cold ribs or sirloin of beef
- 2 oz. of butter
- 1 onion sliced
- pepper and salt to taste
- 1/2 glass of port wine
- a little flour to thicken
- 1 or 2 dozen oysters
- rather more than 1/2 pint of water

## Directions

1. Cut the steaks rather thick, from cold sirloin or ribs of beef; brown them lightly in a stewpan, with the butter and a little water.
2. Add 1/2 pint of water, the onion, pepper, and salt, and cover the stewpan closely, and let it simmer very gently for 1/2 hour.
3. Mix about a teaspoonful of flour smoothly with a little of the liquor; add the port wine and oysters, their liquor having been previously strained and put into the stewpan.
4. Stir till the oysters plump, and serve. It should not boil after the oysters are added, or they will harden.

title: Stewed Breast of Lamb
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 breast of lamb
- pepper and salt to taste
- sufficient stock, No. 105, to cover it
- 1 glass of sherry
- thickening of butter and flour

## Directions

1. Skin the lamb, cut it into pieces, and season them with pepper and salt
2. lay these in a stewpan, pour in sufficient stock or gravy to cover them, and stew very gently until tender, which will be in about 1-1/2 hour
3. Just before serving, thicken the sauce with a little butter and flour
4. add the sherry, give one boil, and pour it over the meat
5. Green peas, or stewed mushrooms, may be strewed over the meat, and will be found a very great improvement

title: Stewed Breast of Veal and Peas
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Breast of veal
- 2 oz. of butter
- a bunch of savoury herbs, including parsley
- 2 blades of pounded mace
- 2 cloves
- 5 or 6 young onions
- 1 strip of lemon-peel
- 6 allspice
- 1/4 teaspoonful of pepper
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- thickening of butter and flour
- 2 tablespoonfuls of sherry
- 2 tablespoonfuls of tomato sauce
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon-juice
- 2 tablespoonfuls of mushroom ketchup
- green peas

## Directions

1. Cut the breast in half, after removing the bone underneath, and divide the meat into convenient-sized pieces.
2. Put the butter into a frying-pan, lay in the pieces of veal, and fry until of a nice brown colour.
3. Place these in a stewpan with the herbs, mace, cloves, onions, lemon-peel, allspice, and seasoning; pour over them just sufficient boiling water to cover the meat; well close the lid, and let the whole simmer very gently for about 2 hours.
4. Strain off as much gravy as is required, thicken it with butter and flour, add the remaining ingredients, skim well, let it simmer for about 10 minutes, then pour it over the meat.
5. Have ready some green peas, boiled separately; sprinkle these over the veal, and serve.
6. It may be garnished with forcemeat balls, or rashers of bacon curled and fried.

title: Stewed Brisket of Beef
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 lbs. of a brisket of beef
- vinegar and salt
- 6 carrots
- 6 turnips
- 6 small onions
- 1 blade of pounded mace
- 2 whole allspice pounded
- thickening of butter and flour
- 2 tablespoonfuls of ketchup
- stock, or water

## Directions

1. About an hour before dressing it, rub the meat over with vinegar and salt.
2. Put it into a stewpan, with sufficient stock to cover it (when this is not at hand, water may be substituted for it), and be particular that the stewpan is not much larger than the meat.
3. Skim well, and when it has simmered very gently for 1 hour, put in the vegetables, and continue simmering till the meat is perfectly tender.
4. Draw out the bones, dish the meat, and garnish either with tufts of cauliflower or braised cabbage cut in quarters.
5. Thicken as much gravy as required, with a little butter and flour; add spices and ketchup in the above proportion, give one boil, pour some of it over the meat, and the remainder send in a tureen.

title: Stewed Carp
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 carp
- salt
- stock No. 105
- 2 onions
- 6 cloves
- 12 peppercorns
- 1 blade of mace
- 1/4 pint of port wine
- the juice of 1/2 lemon
- cayenne and salt to taste
- a faggot of savoury herbs

## Directions

1. Scale the fish, clean it nicely, and, if very large, divide it; lay it in the stewpan, after having rubbed a little salt on it, and put in sufficient stock to cover it; add the herbs, onions, and spices, and stew gently for 1 hour, or rather more, should it be very large.
2. Dish up the fish with great care, strain the liquor, and add to it the port wine, lemon-juice, and cayenne; give one boil, pour it over the fish, and serve.

title: Stewed Carrots
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 or 8 large carrots
- 1 teacupful of broth
- pepper and salt to taste
- 1/2 teacupful of cream
- butter and flour for thickening

## Directions

1. Scrape the carrots nicely; half-boil, and slice them into a stewpan
2. Add the broth, pepper and salt, and cream
3. Simmer till tender, being careful the carrots are not broken
4. A few minutes before serving, mix a little flour with about 1 oz. of butter
5. Thicken the gravy with this mixture
6. Let it just boil up, and serve

title: Stewed Celery a la Creme
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 heads of celery
- to each 1/2 gallon of water allow 1 heaped tablespoonful of salt
- 1 blade of pounded mace
- 1/3 pint of cream

## Directions

1. Wash the celery thoroughly; trim, and boil it in salt and water until tender.
2. Put the cream and pounded mace into a stewpan; shake it over the fire until the cream thickens.
3. Dish the celery, pour over the sauce, and serve.

title: Stewed Celery with White Sauce I
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 heads of celery
- 1 oz. of butter
- to each 1/2 gallon of water allow 1 heaped tablespoonful of salt
- 1/2 pint of white sauce, No. 537 or 538

## Directions

1. Have ready sufficient boiling water just to cover the celery, with salt and butter in the above proportion.
2. Wash the celery well; cut off the decayed outside leaves, trim away the green tops, and shape the root into a point.
3. Put it into the boiling water; let it boil rapidly until tender.
4. Take it out, drain well, place it upon a dish, and pour over about 1/2 pint of white sauce, made by either of the recipes No. 537 or 538.
5. It may also be plainly boiled as above, placed on toast, and melted butter poured over, the same as asparagus is dished.

title: Stewed Celery with White Sauce II
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 heads of celery
- 1/2 pint of white stock or weak broth
- 4 tablespoonfuls of cream
- thickening of butter and flour
- 1 blade of pounded mace
- a very little grated nutmeg
- pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Wash the celery, strip off the outer leaves, and cut it into lengths of about 4 inches.
2. Put these into a saucepan, with the broth, and stew till tender, which will be in from 20 to 25 minutes.
3. Then add the remaining ingredients, simmer altogether for 4 or 5 minutes, pour into a dish, and serve.
4. It may be garnished with sippets of toasted bread.

title: Stewed Cucumbers
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 large cucumbers
- flour
- butter
- rather more than 1/2 pint of good brown gravy

## Directions

1. Cut the cucumbers lengthwise the size of the dish they are intended to be served in
2. Empty them of the seeds, and put them into boiling water with a little salt, and let them simmer for 5 minutes
3. Take them out, place them in another stewpan, with the gravy, and let them boil over a brisk fire until the cucumbers are tender
4. Should these be bitter, add a lump of sugar
5. Carefully dish them, skim the sauce, pour over the cucumbers, and serve

title: Stewed Cucumbers with Onions
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 cucumbers
- 3 moderate-sized onions
- not quite 1 pint of white stock
- cayenne and salt to taste
- the yolks of 2 eggs
- a very little grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Pare and slice the cucumbers, take out the seeds, and cut the onions into thin slices
2. Put these both into a stewpan, with the stock, and let them boil for 1/4 hour or longer, should the cucumbers be very large
3. Beat up the yolks of 2 eggs
4. Stir these into the sauce
5. Add the cayenne, salt, and grated nutmeg
6. Bring it to the point of boiling, and serve
7. Do not allow the sauce to boil, or it will curdle

title: Stewed Duck and Peas
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast duck
- 2 oz. of butter
- 3 or 4 slices of lean ham or bacon
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 2 pints of thin gravy
- 1 or a small bunch of green onions
- 3 sprigs of parsley
- 3 cloves
- 1 pint of young green peas
- cayenne and salt to taste
- 1 teaspoonful of pounded sugar

## Directions

1. Put the butter into a stewpan
2. Cut up the duck into joints, lay them in with the slices of lean ham or bacon
3. Make it brown, then dredge in a tablespoonful of flour, and stir this well in before adding the gravy
4. Put in the onion, parsley, cloves, and gravy
5. When it has simmered for 1/4 hour, add a pint of young green peas, and stew gently for about 1/2 hour
6. Season with cayenne, salt, and sugar
7. Take out the duck, place it round the dish, and the peas in the middle

title: Stewed Duck and Peas (Cold Meat Cookery)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast duck
- 1/2 pint of good gravy
- cayenne and salt to taste
- 1/2 teaspoonful of minced lemon-peel
- 1 teaspoonful of pounded sugar
- 2 oz. of butter rolled in flour
- 1-1/2 pint of green peas

## Directions

1. Cut up the duck into joints, lay it in the gravy, and add a seasoning of cayenne, salt, and minced lemon-peel; let this gradually warm through, but not boil.
2. Throw the peas into boiling water slightly salted, and boil them rapidly until tender.
3. Drain them, stir in the pounded sugar, and the butter rolled in flour; shake them over the fire for two or three minutes, and serve in the centre of the dish, with the duck laid round.

title: Stewed Duck and Turnips
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast duck
- 1/2 pint of good gravy
- 4 shalots
- a few slices of carrot
- a small bunch of savoury herbs
- 1 blade of pounded mace
- 1 lb. of turnips, weighed after being peeled
- 2 oz. of butter
- pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Cut up the duck into joints, fry the shalots, carrots, and herbs, and put them, with the duck, into the gravy
2. Add the pounded mace, and stew gently for 20 minutes or 1/2 hour
3. Cut about 1 lb. of turnips, weighed after being peeled, into 1/2-inch squares
4. Put the butter into a stewpan, and stew them till quite tender, which will be in about 1/2 hour, or rather more
5. Season with pepper and salt, and serve in the centre of the dish, with the duck, &c. laid round

title: Stewed Duck and Turnips (Cold Meat Cookery)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The remains of cold roast duck
- 1/2 pint of good gravy
- 4 shalots
- a few slices of carrot
- a small bunch of savoury herbs
- 1 blade of pounded mace
- 1 lb. of turnips, weighed after being peeled
- 2 oz. of butter
- pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Cut up the duck into joints, fry the shalots, carrots, and herbs, and put them, with the duck, into the gravy; add the pounded mace, and stew gently for 20 minutes or 1/2 hour.
2. Cut about 1 lb. of turnips, weighed after being peeled, into 1/2-inch squares, put the

title: Stewed Eels
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of eels
- 1 pint of rich strong stock
- 1 onion
- 3 cloves
- a piece of lemon-peel
- 1 glass of port or Madeira
- 3 tablespoonfuls of cream
- thickening of flour
- cayenne and lemon-juice to taste

## Directions

1. Wash and skin the eels, and cut them into pieces about 3 inches long; pepper and salt them, and lay them in a stewpan.
2. Pour over the stock, add the onion stuck with cloves, the lemon-peel, and the wine.
3. Stew gently for 1/2 hour, or rather more, and lift them carefully on a dish, which keep hot.
4. Strain the gravy, stir to the cream sufficient flour to thicken; mix altogether, boil for 2 minutes, and add the seasoning.

title: Stewed Eels
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of eels
- 1 pint of rich strong stock
- 1 onion
- 3 cloves
- a piece of lemon-peel
- 1 glass of port or Madeira
- 3 tablespoonfuls of cream
- flour for thickening
- cayenne to taste
- lemon-juice to taste
- pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Wash and skin the eels, and cut them into pieces about 3 inches long
2. pepper and salt them, and lay them in a stewpan
3. pour over the stock, add the onion stuck with cloves, the lemon-peel, and the wine
4. Stew gently for 1/2 hour, or rather more, and lift them carefully on a dish, which keep hot
5. Strain the gravy, stir to the cream sufficient flour to thicken
6. mix altogether, boil for 2 minutes, and add the cayenne and lemon-juice
7. pour over the eels and serve

title: Stewed Eels II
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of middling-sized eels
- 1 pint of medium stock
- 1/4 pint of port wine
- salt to taste
- cayenne to taste
- mace to taste
- 1 teaspoonful of essence of anchovy
- the juice of 1/2 a lemon

## Directions

1. Skin, wash, and clean the eels thoroughly
2. cut them into pieces 3 inches long, and put them into strong salt and water for 1 hour
3. dry them well with a cloth, and fry them brown
4. Put the stock on with the heads and tails of the eels, and simmer for 1/2 hour
5. strain it, and add all the other ingredients
6. Put in the eels, and stew gently for 1/2 hour, then serve

title: Stewed Endive
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 heads of endive
- salt and water
- 1 pint of broth
- thickening of butter and flour
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon-juice
- a small lump of sugar

## Directions

1. Wash and free the endive thoroughly from insects, remove the green part of the leaves, and put it into boiling water, slightly salted.
2. Let it remain for 10 minutes; then take it out, drain it till there is no water remaining, and chop it very fine.
3. Put it into a stewpan with the broth; add a little salt and a lump of sugar, and boil until the endive is perfectly tender.
4. When done, which may be ascertained by squeezing a piece between the thumb and finger, add a thickening of butter and flour and the lemon-juice.
5. Let the sauce boil up, and serve.

title: Stewed Fillet of Veal
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- A small fillet of veal
- forcemeat No. 417
- thickening of butter and flour
- a few mushrooms
- white pepper to taste
- 2 tablespoonfuls of lemon-juice
- 2 blades of pounded mace
- 1/2 glass of sherry
- weak stock

## Directions

1. If the whole of the leg is purchased, take off the knuckle to stew, and also the square end, which will serve for cutlets or pies.
2. Remove the bone, and fill the space with a forcemeat No. 417.
3. Roll and skewer it up firmly; place a few skewers at the bottom of a stewpan to prevent the meat from sticking, and cover the veal with a little weak stock.
4. Let it simmer very gently until tender, as the more slowly veal is stewed, the better.
5. Strain and thicken the sauce, flavour it with lemon-juice, mace, sherry, and white pepper; give one boil, and pour it over the meat.
6. The skewers should be removed, and replaced by a silver one, and the dish garnished with slices of cut lemon.

title: Stewed French Plums
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 lb. of French plums
- 3/4 pint of syrup No. 1512
- 1 glass of port wine
- the rind and juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Stew the plums gently in water for 1 hour
2. Strain the water, and with it make the syrup
3. When it is clear, put in the plums with the port wine, lemon-juice, and rind, and simmer very gently for 1-1/2 hour
4. Arrange the plums on a glass dish, take out the lemon-rind, pour the syrup over the plums, and when cold, they will be ready for table

title: Stewed Green Peas
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of peas
- 1 Lettuce
- 1 onion
- 2 oz. of butter
- Pepper and salt to taste
- 1 egg
- 1/2 teaspoonful of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Shell the peas, and cut the onion and lettuce into slices
2. Put these into a stewpan, with the butter, pepper, and salt, but with no more water than that which hangs round the lettuce from washing
3. Stew the whole very gently for rather more than 1 hour
4. Stir to it a well-beaten egg, and about 1/2 teaspoonful of powdered sugar
5. When the peas are nicely thickened, serve but, after the egg is added, do not allow them to boil

title: Stewed Hare (A Quicker and more Economical Way)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 hare
- a bunch of sweet herbs
- 2 onions
- 6 cloves
- 6 whole allspice
- 1/2 teaspoonful of black pepper
- a strip of lemon-peel
- butter
- flour
- 2 tablespoonfuls of mushroom ketchup
- 1/4 pint of port wine
- red-currant jelly
- forcemeat balls

## Directions

1. Wash the hare nicely, cut it up into joints (not too large), and flour and brown them as in the preceding recipe.
2. Then put them into a stewpan with the herbs, onions, cloves, allspice, pepper, and lemon-peel.
3. Cover with hot water, and when it boils, carefully remove all the scum, and let it simmer gently till tender, which will be in about 1-3/4 hour, or longer, should the hare be very old.
4. Take out the pieces of hare, thicken the gravy with flour and butter, add the ketchup and port wine, let it boil for about 10 minutes, strain it through a sieve over the hare, and serve.
5. A few fried forcemeat balls should be added at the moment of serving, or instead of frying them, they may be stewed in the gravy, about 10 minutes before the hare is wanted for table.
6. Do not omit to serve red-currant jelly with it.

title: Stewed Hare in a Jar
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 hare
- 1 onion
- 1 lemon
- 6 cloves
- pepper
- cayenne
- salt
- 1/2 pint of port wine
- flour
- butter
- 1-1/2 pint of gravy made from beef
- forcemeat balls
- red-currant jelly

## Directions

1. Skin, paunch, and wash the hare, cut it into pieces, dredge them with flour, and fry in boiling butter.
2. Have ready 1-1/2 pint of gravy, made from the proportion of beef, and thickened with a little flour.
3. Put this into a jar; add the pieces of fried hare, an onion stuck with six cloves, a lemon peeled and cut in half, and a good seasoning of pepper, cayenne, and salt.
4. Cover the jar down tightly, put it up to the neck into a stewpan of boiling water, and let it stew until the hare is quite tender, taking care to keep the water boiling.
5. When nearly done, pour in the wine, and add a few forcemeat balls, made by recipe No. 417: these must be fried or baked in the oven for a few minutes before they are put to the gravy.
6. Serve with red-currant jelly.

title: Stewed Knuckle of Veal and Rice
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Knuckle of veal
- 1 onion
- 2 blades of mace
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 1/2 lb. of rice

## Directions

1. Have the knuckle cut small, or cut some cutlets from it, that it may be just large enough to be eaten the same day it is dressed, as cold boiled veal is not a particularly tempting dish.
2. Break the shank-bone, wash it clean, and put the meat into a stewpan with sufficient water to cover it.
3. Let it gradually come to a boil, put in the salt, and remove the scum as fast as it rises.
4. When it has simmered gently for about 3/4 hour, add the remaining ingredients, and stew the whole gently for 2-1/4 hours.
5. Put the meat into a deep dish, pour over it the rice, &c., and send boiled bacon, and a tureen of parsley and butter to table with it.

title: Stewed Mushrooms
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint mushroom-buttons
- 3 oz. of fresh butter
- white pepper and salt to taste
- lemon-juice
- 1 teaspoonful of flour
- cream or milk
- 1 teaspoonful of grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cut off the ends of the stalks, and pare neatly a pint of mushroom-buttons
2. Put them into a basin of water, with a little lemon-juice, as they are done
3. When all are prepared, take them from the water with the hands, to avoid the sediment, and put them into a stewpan with the fresh butter, white pepper, salt, and the juice of 1/2 lemon
4. Cover the pan closely, and let the mushrooms stew gently from 20 to 25 minutes
5. Thicken the butter with the above proportion of flour, add gradually sufficient cream, or cream and milk, to make the sauce of a proper consistency, and put in the grated nutmeg
6. If the mushrooms are not perfectly tender, stew them for 5 minutes longer, remove every particle of butter which may be floating on the top, and serve

title: Stewed Mushrooms in Gravy
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of mushroom-buttons
- 1 pint of brown gravy
- 1/4 teaspoonful of grated nutmeg
- cayenne and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Make a pint of brown gravy by recipe 436
2. Cut nearly all the stalks away from the mushrooms and peel the tops
3. Put them into a stewpan, with the gravy, and simmer them gently from 20 minutes to 1/2 hour
4. Add the nutmeg and a seasoning of cayenne and salt, and serve very hot

title: Stewed Normandy Pippins
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of Normandy pippins
- 1 quart of water
- 1/2 teaspoonful of powdered cinnamon
- 1/2 teaspoonful of ground ginger
- 1 lb. of moist sugar
- 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Well wash the pippins, and put them into 1 quart of water with the above proportion of cinnamon and ginger, and let them stand 12 hours
2. Put these all together into a stewpan, with the lemon sliced thinly, and half the moist sugar
3. Let them boil slowly until the pippins are half done
4. Add the remainder of the sugar, and simmer until they are quite tender
5. Serve on glass dishes for dessert

title: Stewed Ox-Cheek
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cheek
- salt and water
- 4 or 5 onions
- butter and flour
- 6 cloves
- 3 turnips
- 2 carrots
- 1 bay-leaf
- 1 head of celery
- 1 bunch of savoury herbs
- cayenne, black pepper and salt to taste
- 1 oz. of butter
- 2 dessertspoonfuls of flour
- 2 tablespoonfuls of Chili vinegar
- 2 tablespoonfuls of mushroom ketchup
- 2 tablespoonfuls of port wine
- 2 tablespoonfuls of Harvey's sauce

## Directions

1. Have the cheek boned, and prepare it the day before it is to be eaten, by cleaning and putting it to soak all night in salt and water
2. The next day, wipe it dry and clean, and put it into a stewpan
3. Just cover it with water, skim well when it boils, and let it gently simmer till the meat is quite tender
4. Slice and fry 3 onions in a little butter and flour, and put them into the gravy
5. Add 2 whole onions, each stuck with 3 cloves, 3 turnips quartered, 2 carrots sliced, a bay-leaf, 1 head of celery, a bunch of herbs, and seasoning to taste of cayenne, black pepper, and salt
6. Let these stew till perfectly tender
7. Take out the cheek, divide into pieces fit to help at table, skim and strain the gravy, and thicken 1-1/2 pint of it with butter and flour in the above proportions
8. Add the vinegar, ketchup, and port wine
9. Put in the pieces of cheek
10. Let the whole boil up, and serve quite hot
11. Send it to table in a ragout-dish
12. If the colour of the gravy should not be very good, add a tablespoonful of the browning, No. 108

title: Stewed Ox-Tails
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ox-tails
- 1 onion
- 3 cloves
- 1 blade of mace
- 1 teaspoonful of whole black pepper
- 1 teaspoonful of allspice
- 1/2 a teaspoonful of salt
- a small bunch of savoury herbs
- thickening of butter and flour
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon-juice
- 1 tablespoonful of mushroom ketchup

## Directions

1. Divide the tails at the joints, wash, and put them into a stewpan with sufficient water to cover them, and set them on the fire
2. When the water boils, remove the scum, and add the onion cut into rings, the spice, seasoning, and herbs
3. Cover the stewpan closely, and let the tails simmer very gently until tender, which will be in about 2-1/2 hours
4. Take them out, make a thickening of butter and flour, add it to the gravy, and let it boil for 1/4 hour
5. Strain it through a sieve into a saucepan, put back the tails, add the lemon-juice and ketchup
6. Let the whole just boil up, and serve
7. Garnish with croûtons or sippets of toasted bread

title: Stewed Oysters
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of oysters
- 1 oz. of butter
- flour
- 1/3 pint of cream
- cayenne and salt to taste
- 1 blade of pounded mace

## Directions

1. Scald the oysters in their own liquor, take them out, beard them, and strain the liquor; put the butter into a stewpan, dredge in sufficient flour to dry it up, add the oyster-liquor and mace, and stir it over a sharp fire with a wooden spoon; when it comes to a boil, add the cream, oysters, and seasoning.
2. Let all simmer for 1 or 2 minutes, but not longer, or the oysters would harden.
3. Serve on a hot dish, and garnish with croutons, or toasted sippets of bread.
4. A small piece of lemon-peel boiled with the oyster-liquor, and taken out before the cream is added, will be found an improvement.

title: Stewed Pears
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 large pears
- 5 oz. of loaf sugar
- 6 cloves
- 6 whole allspice
- 1/2 pint of water
- 1/4 pint of port wine
- a few drops of prepared cochineal

## Directions

1. Pare the pears, halve them, remove the cores, and leave the stalks on; put them into a lined saucepan with the above ingredients, and let them simmer very gently until tender, which will be in from 3 to 4 hours, according to the quality of the pears.
2. They should be watched, and, when done, carefully lifted out on to a glass dish without breaking them.
3. Boil up the syrup quickly for 2 or 3 minutes; allow it to cool a little, pour it over the pears, and let them get perfectly cold.
4. To improve the colour of the fruit, a few drops of prepared cochineal may be added, which rather enhances the beauty of this dish.
5. The fruit must not be boiled fast, but only simmered, and watched that it be not too much done.

title: Stewed Pigeons
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pigeons
- a few slices of bacon
- 3 oz. of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of minced parsley
- sufficient stock No. 104 to cover the pigeons
- thickening of butter and flour
- 1 tablespoonful of mushroom ketchup
- 1 tablespoonful of port wine

## Directions

1. Empty and clean the pigeons thoroughly, mince the livers, add to these the parsley and butter, and put it into the insides of the birds.
2. Truss them with the legs inward, and put them into a stewpan, with a few slices of bacon placed under and over them.
3. Add the stock, and stew gently for rather more than 1/2 hour.
4. Dish the pigeons, strain the gravy, thicken it with butter and flour, add the ketchup and port wine, give one boil, pour over the pigeons, and serve.

title: Stewed Plaice
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 5 plaice
- 2 onions
- 1/2 oz. ground ginger
- 1 pint of lemon-juice
- 1/4 pint water
- 6 eggs
- cayenne to taste

## Directions

1. Cut the fish into pieces about 2 inches wide, salt them, and let them remain 1/4 hour.
2. Slice and fry the onions a light brown.
3. Put them in a stewpan, on the top of which put the fish without washing, and add the ginger, lemon-juice, and water.
4. Cook slowly for 1/2 hour, and do not let the fish boil, or it will break.
5. Take it out, and when the liquor is cool, add 6 well-beaten eggs.
6. Simmer till it thickens, when pour over the fish, and serve.

title: Stewed Rabbit
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 rabbit
- 2 large onions
- 6 cloves
- 1 small teaspoonful of chopped lemon-peel
- a few forcemeat balls
- thickening of butter and flour
- 1 large tablespoonful of mushroom ketchup

## Directions

1. Cut the rabbit into small joints; put them into a stewpan, add the onions sliced, the cloves, and minced lemon-peel.
2. Pour in sufficient water to cover the meat, and, when the rabbit is nearly done, drop in a few forcemeat balls, to which has been added the liver, finely chopped.
3. Thicken the gravy with flour and butter, put in the ketchup, give one boil, and serve.

title: Stewed Rabbit, Larded
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 rabbit
- a few strips of bacon
- rather more than 1 pint of good broth or stock
- a bunch of savoury herbs
- salt and pepper to taste
- thickening of butter and flour
- 1 glass of sherry

## Directions

1. Well wash the rabbit, cut it into quarters, lard them with strips of bacon, and fry them
2. Put them into a stewpan with the broth, herbs, and a seasoning of pepper and salt
3. Simmer gently until the rabbit is tender
4. Strain the gravy, thicken it with butter and flour
5. Add the sherry, give one boil, pour it over the rabbit, and serve
6. Garnish with slices of cut lemon

title: Stewed Rabbits in Milk
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 very young rabbits, not nearly half grown
- 1-1/2 pint of milk
- 1 blade of mace
- 1 dessertspoonful of flour
- a little salt and cayenne

## Directions

1. Mix the flour very smoothly with 4 tablespoonfuls of the milk
2. When this is well mixed, add the remainder
3. Cut up the rabbits into joints
4. Put them into a stewpan, with the milk and other ingredients
5. Simmer them very gently until quite tender
6. Stir the contents from time to time, to keep the milk smooth and prevent it from burning
7. Cook for 1/2 hour

title: Stewed Red Cabbage
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 red cabbage
- a small slice of ham
- 1/2 oz. of fresh butter
- 1 pint of weak stock or broth
- 1 gill of vinegar
- salt and pepper to taste
- 1 tablespoonful of pounded sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbage into very thin slices
2. put it into a stewpan, with the ham cut in dice, the butter, 1/2 pint of stock, and the vinegar
3. cover the pan closely
4. let it stew for 1 hour
5. When it is very tender, add the remainder of the stock, a seasoning of salt and pepper, and the pounded sugar
6. mix all well together
7. stir over the fire until nearly all the liquor is dried away
8. serve

title: Stewed Rump
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- A rump of beef
- bunch of savoury herbs
- 2 onions
- 12 cloves
- pepper and salt to taste
- butter and flour for thickening
- 1 glass of port wine
- vinegar
- ketchup

## Directions

1. Cut out the bone, sprinkle the meat with a little cayenne, and bind and tie it firmly up with tape
2. Put it into a stewpan with sufficient stock to cover it, and add vinegar, ketchup, herbs, onions, cloves, and seasoning
3. Simmer very gently for 4 or 5 hours, or until the meat is perfectly tender, which may be ascertained by piercing it with a thin skewer
4. When done, remove the tape, lay it into a deep dish, which keep hot
5. Strain and skim the gravy, thicken it with butter and flour, add a glass of port wine and any flavouring to make the gravy rich and palatable
6. Let it boil up, pour over the meat, and serve

title: Stewed Rump of Beef
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 rump of beef
- sufficient stock to cover it
- 4 tablespoonfuls of vinegar
- 2 tablespoonfuls of ketchup
- 1 large bunch of savoury herbs
- 2 onions
- 12 cloves
- pepper and salt to taste
- thickening of butter and flour
- 1 glass of port wine

## Directions

1. Cut out the bone, sprinkle the meat with a little cayenne (this must be sparingly used), and bind and tie it firmly up with tape.
2. Put it into a stewpan with sufficient stock to cover it, and add vinegar, ketchup, herbs, onions, cloves, and seasoning in the above proportion, and simmer very gently for 4 or 5 hours, or until the meat is perfectly tender, which may be ascertained by piercing it with a thin skewer.
3. When done, remove the tape, lay it into a deep dish, which keep hot.
4. Strain and skim the gravy, thicken it with butter and flour, add a glass of port wine and any flavouring to make the gravy rich and palatable.

title: Stewed Shin of Beef
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- A shin of beef
- 1 head of celery
- 1 onion
- a faggot of savoury herbs
- 1/2 teaspoonful of allspice
- 1/2 teaspoonful of whole black pepper
- 4 carrots
- 12 button onions
- 2 turnips
- butter and flour for thickening
- 3 tablespoonfuls of mushroom ketchup
- 2 tablespoonfuls of port wine
- pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Have the bone sawn into 4 or 5 pieces, cover with hot water, bring it to a boil, and remove any scum that may rise to the surface
2. Put in the celery, onion, herbs, spice, and seasoning, and simmer very gently until the meat is tender
3. Peel the vegetables, cut them into any shape fancy may dictate, and boil them with the onions until tender
4. Lift out the beef, put it on a dish, which keep hot, and thicken with butter and flour as much of the liquor as will be wanted for gravy
5. Keep stirring till it boils, then strain and skim
6. Put the gravy back in the stewpan, add the seasoning, port wine, and ketchup, give one boil, and pour it over the beef
7. Garnish with the boiled carrots, turnips, and onions

title: Stewed Spanish Onions
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 or 6 Spanish onions
- 1 pint of good broth or gravy

## Directions

1. Peel the onions, taking care not to cut away too much of the tops or tails, or they would then fall to pieces
2. Put them into a stewpan capable of holding them at the bottom without piling them one on the top of another
3. Add the broth or gravy, and simmer very gently until the onions are perfectly tender
4. Dish them, pour the gravy round, and serve. Instead of using broth, Spanish onions may be stewed with a large piece of butter: they must be done very gradually over a slow fire or hot-plate, and will produce plenty of gravy

title: Stewed Sweetbreads
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 sweetbreads
- 1 pint of white stock No. 107
- Thickening of butter and flour
- 6 tablespoonfuls of cream
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon-juice
- 1 blade of pounded mace
- White pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Soak the sweetbreads in warm water for 1 hour
2. Boil them for 10 minutes
3. Take them out and put them into cold water for a few minutes
4. Lay them in a stewpan with the stock
5. Simmer them gently for rather more than 1/2 hour
6. Dish them
7. Thicken the gravy with a little butter and flour
8. Let it boil up
9. Add the remaining ingredients
10. Allow the sauce to get quite hot, but not boil
11. Pour it over the sweetbreads

title: Stewed Tendrons de Veau
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The gristles from 2 breasts of veal
- Stock No. 107
- 1 faggot of savoury herbs
- 2 blades of pounded mace
- 4 cloves
- 2 carrots
- 2 onions
- A strip of lemon-peel

## Directions

1. Detach the gristles from the bone
2. Cut them neatly out, so as not to spoil the joint for roasting or stewing
3. Put them into a stewpan with sufficient stock, No. 107, to cover them
4. Add the herbs, mace, cloves, carrots, onions, and lemon
5. Simmer these for nearly, or quite, 4 hours
6. They should be stewed until a fork will enter the meat easily
7. Take them up and drain them
8. Strain the gravy
9. Boil it down to a glaze
10. Glaze the meat with this glaze
11. Dish the tendrons in a circle
12. Place croûtons fried of a nice colour between each
13. Put mushroom sauce, or a purée of green peas or tomatoes, in the middle

title: Stewed Tomatoes I
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 tomatoes
- pepper and salt to taste
- 2 oz. of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of vinegar

## Directions

1. Slice the tomatoes into a lined saucepan
2. Season them with pepper and salt, and place small pieces of butter on them
3. Cover the lid down closely, and stew from 20 to 25 minutes, or until the tomatoes are perfectly tender
4. Add the vinegar, stir two or three times, and serve with any kind of roast meat

title: Stewed Tomatoes II
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 tomatoes
- about 1/2 pint of good gravy
- thickening of butter and flour
- cayenne and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Take out the stalks of the tomatoes; put them into a wide stewpan
2. Pour over them the above proportion of good brown gravy
3. Stew gently until they are tender, occasionally carefully turning them, that they may be equally done
4. Thicken the gravy with a little butter and flour worked together on a plate
5. Let it just boil up after the thickening is added, and serve

title: Stewed Trout
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 middling-sized trout
- 1/2 onion cut in thin slices
- a little parsley
- 2 cloves
- 1 blade of mace
- 2 bay-leaves
- a little thyme
- salt and pepper to taste
- 1 pint of medium stock No. 105
- 1 glass of port wine
- thickening of butter and flour

## Directions

1. Wash the fish very clean, and wipe it quite dry.
2. Lay it in a stewpan, with all the ingredients but the butter and flour, and simmer gently for 1/2 hour, or rather more, should not the fish be quite done.
3. Take it out, strain the gravy, add the thickening, and stir it over a sharp fire for 5 minutes.
4. Pour it over the trout, and serve.

title: Stewed Veal with Peas, Young Carrots, and New Potatoes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 lbs. of the loin or neck of veal
- 15 young carrots
- A few green onions
- 1 pint of green peas
- 12 new potatoes
- A bunch of savoury herbs
- Pepper and salt to taste
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon-juice
- 2 tablespoonfuls of tomato sauce
- 2 tablespoonfuls of mushroom ketchup
- Flour

## Directions

1. Dredge the meat with flour
2. Roast or bake it for about 3/4 hour until it acquires a nice brown colour
3. Put the meat into a stewpan with the carrots, onions, potatoes, herbs, pepper, and salt
4. Pour over it sufficient boiling water to cover it
5. Stew gently for 2 hours
6. Take out the meat and herbs
7. Put it in a deep dish
8. Skim off all the fat from the gravy
9. Flavour it with lemon-juice, tomato sauce, and mushroom ketchup in the above proportion
10. Have ready a pint of green peas boiled
11. Put these with the meat
12. Pour over it the gravy
13. Serve
14. The dish may be garnished with a few forcemeat balls

title: Stewed Venison
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- A shoulder of venison
- a few slices of mutton fat
- 2 glasses of port wine
- pepper and allspice to taste
- 1-1/2 pint of weak stock or gravy
- 1/2 teaspoonful of whole pepper
- 1/2 teaspoonful of whole allspice

## Directions

1. Hang the venison till tender
2. Take out the bone, flatten the meat with a rolling-pin, and place over it a few slices of mutton fat, which have been previously soaked for 2 or 3 hours in port wine
3. Sprinkle these with a little fine allspice and pepper, roll the meat up, and bind and tie it securely
4. Put it into a stewpan with the bone and the above proportion of weak stock or gravy, whole allspice, black pepper, and port wine
5. Cover the lid down closely, and simmer, very gently, from 3-1/2 to 4 hours
6. When quite tender, take off the tape, and dish the meat
7. Strain the gravy over it, and send it to table with red-currant jelly

title: Stone Cream of Tous Les Mois
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of preserve
- 1 pint of milk
- 2 oz. of lump sugar
- 1 heaped tablespoonful of tous les mois
- 3 drops of essence of cloves
- 3 drops of almond-flavouring

## Directions

1. Place the preserve at the bottom of a glass dish.
2. Put the milk into a lined saucepan, with the sugar, and make it boil.
3. Mix to a smooth batter the tous les mois, with a very little cold milk.
4. Stir it briskly into the boiling milk, add the flavouring, and simmer for 2 minutes.
5. When rather cool, but before turning solid, pour the cream over the jam, and ornament it with strips of red-currant jelly or preserved fruit.

title: Store Sauce, or Cherokee
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 oz. of cayenne pepper
- 5 cloves of garlic
- 2 tablespoonfuls of soy
- 1 tablespoonful of walnut ketchup
- 1 pint of vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil all the ingredients gently for about 1/2 hour
2. Strain the liquor and bottle off for use

title: Strawberries and Cream
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of picked strawberries
- 1/3 pint of cream
- 2 oz. of finely-pounded sugar

## Directions

1. Pick the stalks from the fruit, place it on a glass dish, and sprinkle over it pounded sugar
2. Slightly stir the strawberries, that they may all be equally sweetened
3. Pour the cream over the top, and serve

title: Strawberry Jam
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of strawberries
- 1/2 pint of red-currant juice
- 1-1/4 lb. of loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Strip the currants from the stalks, put them into a jar
2. Place this jar in a saucepan of boiling water, and simmer until the juice is well drawn from the fruit
3. Strain the currants, measure the juice, put it into a preserving-pan, and add the sugar
4. Select well-ripened but sound strawberries; pick them from the stalks, and when the sugar is dissolved in the currant juice, put in the fruit
5. Simmer the whole over a moderate fire, from 1/2 to 3/4 hour, carefully removing the scum as it rises
6. Stir the jam only enough to prevent it from burning at the bottom of the pan, as the fruit should be preserved as whole as possible
7. Put the jam into jars, and when cold, cover down

title: Strawberry Jelly
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Strawberries
- pounded sugar
- 1-1/4 oz. of isinglass per pint of juice
- water
- lemon-juice

## Directions

1. Pick the strawberries, put them into a pan, squeeze them well with a wooden spoon.
2. Add sufficient pounded sugar to sweeten them nicely, and let them remain for 1 hour, that the juice may be extracted.
3. Add 1/2 pint of water to every pint of juice.
4. Strain the strawberry-juice and water through a bag.
5. Measure it, and to every pint allow 1-1/4 oz. of isinglass, melted and clarified in 1/4 pint of water.
6. Mix this with the juice.
7. Add a little lemon-juice to improve the flavour if the fruit is very ripe, making sure it is well strained before adding to the other ingredients.
8. Put the jelly into a mould, and set the mould in ice.

title: Suet Crust for Pies or Puddings
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb flour
- 5 or 6 oz beef suet
- 1/2 pint water

## Directions

1. Free the suet from skin and shreds
2. Chop it extremely fine
3. Rub it well into the flour
4. Work the whole to a smooth paste with the above proportion of water
5. Roll it out, and it is ready for use

title: Suet Pudding, to Serve with Roast Meat
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 6 oz. of finely-chopped suet
- 1/2 saltspoonful of salt
- 1/2 saltspoonful of pepper
- 1/2 pint of milk or water

## Directions

1. Chop the suet very finely, after freeing it from skin, and mix it well with the flour
2. Add the salt and pepper (this latter ingredient may be omitted if the flavour is not liked)
3. Make the whole into a smooth paste with the above proportion of milk or water
4. Tie the pudding in a floured cloth, or put it into a buttered basin
5. Boil from 2-1/2 to 3 hours

title: Sugar Icing For Cakes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of loaf sugar
- the whites of 4 eggs
- 1 oz. of fine starch

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs to a strong froth, and gradually sift in the sugar, which should be reduced to the finest possible powder, and gradually add the starch, also finely powdered.
2. Beat the mixture well until the sugar is smooth.
3. With a spoon or broad knife lay the icing equally over the cakes.
4. Place the cakes in a very cool oven, and allow the icing to dry and harden, but not to colour.
5. All iced cakes should be kept in a very dry place.

title: Summer Salad
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lettuces
- 2 handfuls of mustard-and-cress
- 10 young radishes
- a few slices of cucumber
- hard-boiled eggs
- nasturtiums
- cut vegetable-flowers

## Directions

1. Let the herbs be as fresh as possible for a salad, and, if at all stale or dead-looking, let them lie in water for an hour or two, which will very much refresh them.
2. Wash and carefully pick them over, remove any decayed or wormeaten leaves, and drain them thoroughly by swinging them gently in a clean cloth.
3. With a silver knife, cut the lettuces into small pieces, and the radishes and cucumbers into thin slices.
4. Arrange all these ingredients lightly on a dish, with the mustard-and-cress, and pour under, but not over the salad, either of the sauces No. 506, 507, or 508.
5. Do not stir it up until it is to be eaten.
6. It may be garnished with hard-boiled eggs, cut in slices, sliced cucumbers, nasturtiums, cut vegetable-flowers, and many other things that taste will always suggest to make a pretty and elegant dish.

title: Sunderland Gingerbread Nuts
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-3/4 lb. treacle
- 1 lb. of moist sugar
- 1 lb. of butter
- 2-3/4 lbs. of flour
- 1-1/2 oz. of ground ginger
- 1-1/2 oz. of allspice
- 1-1/2 oz. of coriander seeds

## Directions

1. Let the allspice, coriander seeds, and ginger be freshly ground
2. Put them into a basin, with the flour and sugar
3. Mix these ingredients well together
4. Warm the treacle and butter together
5. With a spoon work it into the flour, &c., until the whole forms a nice smooth paste
6. Drop the mixture from the spoon on to a piece of buttered paper
7. Bake in rather a slow oven from 20 minutes to 1/2 hour

title: Superior Polishing Paste for Boots and Shoes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- pounded galls 1 oz.
- logwood-chips 1 oz.
- red French wine (ordinaire) 3 lbs.
- pounded gum-arabic 1/2 lb.
- lump-sugar 1/2 lb.
- green copperas 1 oz.
- brandy 3 lbs.

## Directions

1. Boil together the galls, logwood-chips, and wine till the liquid is reduced to half the quantity
2. Pour it off through a strainer
3. Dissolve the gum-arabic in the preceding decoction
4. Add the sugar and copperas
5. When all is dissolved and mixed together, stir in the brandy, mixing it smoothly

title: Sussex, or Hard Dumplings
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 1/2 pint of water
- 1/2 saltspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Mix the flour and water together to a smooth paste, previously adding a small quantity of salt.
2. Form this into small round dumplings.
3. Drop them into boiling water, and boil from 1/2 to 3/4 hour.
4. They may be served with roast or boiled meat; in the latter case they may be cooked with the meat, but should be dropped into the water when it is quite boiling.

title: Sweet Casserole of Rice
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 lb. of rice
- 3 pints of milk
- sugar to taste
- flavouring of bitter almonds
- 3 oz. of butter
- the yolks of 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Put the milk into a stewpan, with sufficient essence of bitter almonds to flavour it well
2. Add the rice, which should be washed, picked, and drained
3. Let it swell gradually in the milk over a slow fire
4. When it is tender, stir in the sugar, butter, and yolks of eggs
5. Butter a mould, press in the rice, and proceed in exactly the same manner as in recipe No. 1350
6. When the casserole is ready, fill it with a compôte of any fruit that may be preferred, or with melted apricot-jam, and serve

title: Sweet Macaroni Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2-1/2 oz. of macaroni
- 2 pints of milk
- the rind of 1/2 lemon
- 3 eggs
- sugar to taste
- grated nutmeg to taste
- 2 tablespoonfuls of brandy

## Directions

1. Put the macaroni, with a pint of the milk, into a saucepan with the lemon-peel, and let it simmer gently until the macaroni is tender; then put it into a pie-dish without the peel.
2. Mix the other pint of milk with the eggs; stir these well together, adding the sugar and brandy, and pour the mixture over the macaroni.
3. Grate a little nutmeg over the top, and bake in a moderate oven for 1/2 hour.
4. To make this pudding look nice, a paste should be laid round the edges of the dish, and, for variety, a layer of preserve or marmalade may be placed on the macaroni: in this case omit the brandy.

title: Sweet Sauce for Puddings
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of melted butter made with milk
- 4 heaped teaspoonfuls of pounded sugar
- flavouring of grated lemon-rind, or nutmeg, or cinnamon

## Directions

1. Make 1/2 pint of melted butter by recipe No. 380, omitting the salt; stir in the sugar, add a little grated lemon-rind, nutmeg, or powdered cinnamon, and serve.
2. Previously to making the melted butter, the milk can be flavoured with bitter almonds, by infusing about half a dozen of them in it for about 1/2 hour; the milk should then be strained before it is added to the other ingredients.

title: Sweet Vol-au-Vent of Plums, Apples, or Any Other Fresh Fruit
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 lb. of puff-paste No. 1208
- about 1 pint of fruit compôte

## Directions

1. Make 1/2 lb. of puff-paste by recipe No. 1208, taking care to bake it in a good brisk oven, to draw it up nicely and make it look light.
2. Have ready sufficient stewed fruit, the syrup of which must be boiled down until very thick.
3. Fill the vol-au-vent with this, and pile it high in the centre.
4. Powder a little sugar over it, and put it back in the oven to glaze, or use a salamander for the purpose.
5. The vol-au-vent is then ready to serve.

title: Swiss Cream
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of macaroons or 6 small sponge-cakes
- sherry
- 1 pint of cream
- 5 oz. of lump sugar
- 2 large tablespoonfuls of arrowroot
- the rind of 1 lemon
- the juice of 1/2 lemon
- 3 tablespoonfuls of milk

## Directions

1. Lay the macaroons or sponge-cakes in a glass dish, and pour over them as much sherry as will cover them, or sufficient to soak them well.
2. Put the cream into a lined saucepan, with the sugar and lemon-rind, and let it remain by the side of the fire until the cream is well flavoured, when take out the lemon-rind.
3. Mix the arrowroot smoothly with the cold milk.
4. Add this to the cream, and let it boil gently for about 3 minutes, keeping it well stirred.
5. Take it off the fire, stir till nearly cold, when add the lemon-juice, and pour the whole over the cakes.
6. Garnish the cream with strips of angelica, or candied citron cut thin, or bright-coloured jelly or preserve.

title: Tapioca Pudding
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 oz. of tapioca
- 1 quart of milk
- 2 oz. of butter
- 1/4 lb. of sugar
- 4 eggs
- flavouring of vanilla, grated lemon-rind, or bitter almonds

## Directions

1. Wash the tapioca, and let it stew gently in the milk by the side of the fire for 1/4 hour, occasionally stirring it
2. Let it cool a little
3. Mix with it the butter, sugar, and eggs, which should be well beaten, and flavour with either of the above ingredients, putting in about 12 drops of the essence of almonds or vanilla, whichever is preferred
4. Butter a pie-dish, and line the edges with puff-paste
5. Put in the pudding, and bake in a moderate oven for an hour
6. If the pudding is boiled, add a little more tapioca, and boil it in a buttered basin 1-1/2 hour

title: Tapioca Soup
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 oz. of tapioca
- 2 quarts of stock No. 105 or 106

## Directions

1. Put the tapioca into cold stock
2. Bring it gradually to a boil
3. Simmer gently till tender
4. Serve

title: Tartar Mustard
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- horseradish vinegar
- cayenne
- 1/2 a teacupful of mustard

## Directions

1. Have ready sufficient horseradish vinegar to mix with the above proportion of mustard.
2. Put the mustard in a cup, with a slight seasoning of cayenne.
3. Mix it perfectly smooth with the vinegar, adding this a little at a time.
4. Rub down with the back of a spoon any lumps that may appear, and do not let it be too thin.

title: Tartlets
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Trimmings of puff-paste
- any jam or marmalade that may be preferred

## Directions

1. Roll out the paste to the thickness of about 1/2 inch
2. Butter some small round patty-pans, line them with it, and cut off the superfluous paste close to the edge of the pan
3. Put a small piece of bread into each tartlet (this is to keep them in shape), and bake in a brisk oven for about 10 minutes, or rather longer
4. When they are done, and are of a nice colour, take the pieces of bread out carefully, and replace them by a spoonful of jam or marmalade
5. Dish them high on a white d'oyley, piled high in the centre, and serve

title: Tea-Cakes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of flour
- 1/2 teaspoonful of salt
- 1/4 lb. of butter or lard
- 1 egg
- a piece of German yeast the size of a walnut
- warm milk

## Directions

1. Put the flour (which should be perfectly dry) into a basin
2. Mix with it the salt, and rub in the butter or lard
3. Beat the egg well, stir to it the yeast, and add these to the flour with as much warm milk as will make the whole into a smooth paste
4. Knead it well
5. Let it rise near the fire, and when well risen, form it into cakes
6. Place them on tins, let them rise again for a few minutes before putting them into the oven
7. Bake from 1/4 to 1/2 hour in a moderate oven
8. Butter the cakes, and eat them hot as soon as baked

title: Tench Stewed With Wine
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of stock No. 105
- 1/2 pint of Madeira or sherry
- salt and pepper to taste
- 1 bay-leaf
- thickening of butter and flour

## Directions

1. Clean and crimp the tench
2. Carefully lay it in a stewpan with the stock, wine, salt and pepper, and bay-leaf
3. Let it stew gently for 1/2 hour
4. Take it out, put it on a dish, and keep hot
5. Strain the liquor, and thicken it with butter and flour kneaded together, and stew for 5 minutes
6. If not perfectly smooth, squeeze it through a tammy
7. Add a very little cayenne, and pour over the fish
8. Garnish with balls of veal forcemeat

title: Tendrons De Veau (an Entrée)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- The gristles from 2 breasts of veal
- stock No. 107
- 1 faggot of savoury herbs
- 1 blade of pounded mace
- 4 cloves
- 2 carrots
- 2 onions
- a strip of lemon-peel
- egg and bread crumbs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of chopped mushrooms
- salt and pepper to taste
- 2 tablespoonfuls of sherry
- the yolk of 1 egg
- 3 tablespoonfuls of cream

## Directions

1. After removing the gristles from a breast of veal, stew them for 4 hours, as in the preceding recipe, with stock, herbs, mace, cloves, carrots, onions, and lemon-peel.
2. When perfectly tender, lift them out and remove any bones or hard parts remaining.
3. Put them between two dishes, with a weight on the top, and when cold, cut them into slices.
4. Brush these over with egg, sprinkle with bread crumbs, and fry a pale brown.
5. Take 1/2 pint of the gravy they were boiled in, add 2 tablespoonfuls of chopped mushrooms, a seasoning of salt and pepper, the sherry, and the yolk of an egg beaten with 3 tablespoonfuls of cream.
6. Stir the sauce over the fire until it thickens; when it is on the point of boiling, dish the tendrons in a circle, and pour the sauce in the middle.

title: Tete De Veau En Tortue (an Entrée)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a calf's head, or the remains of a cold boiled one
- rather more than 1 pint of good white stock, No. 107
- 1 glass of sherry or Madeira
- cayenne and salt to taste
- about 12 mushroom-buttons
- 6 hard-boiled eggs
- 4 gherkins
- 8 quenelles or forcemeat balls, No. 422 or 423
- 12 crayfish
- 12 croûtons

## Directions

1. After boiling the head until tender, remove the bones, and cut the meat into neat pieces.
2. Put the stock into a stewpan, add the wine, and a seasoning of salt and cayenne.
3. Fry the mushrooms in butter for 2 or 3 minutes, and add these to the gravy.
4. Boil this quickly until somewhat reduced; then put in the yolks of the hard-boiled eggs whole, the whites cut in small pieces, and the gherkins chopped.
5. Have ready a few veal quenelles, made by recipe No. 422 or 423; add these, with the slices of head, to the other ingredients, and let the whole get thoroughly hot, without boiling.
6. Arrange the pieces of head as high in the centre of the dish as possible; pour over them the ragout, and garnish with the crayfish and croûtons placed alternately.
7. A little of the gravy should also be served in a tureen.

title: The Dutch Way to Salt Beef
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 lbs. of lean beef
- 1 lb. of treacle
- 1 oz. of saltpetre
- 1 lb. of common salt

## Directions

1. Rub the beef well with the treacle, and let it remain for 3 days, turning and rubbing it often.
2. Then wipe it, pound the salt and saltpetre very fine, rub these well in, and turn it every day for 10 days.
3. Roll it up tightly in a coarse cloth, and press it under a large weight.
4. Have it smoked, and turn it upside down every day.
5. Boil it, and, on taking it out of the pot, put a heavy weight on it to press it.

title: The Hidden Mountain
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- a few slices of citron
- sugar to taste
- 1/4 pint of cream
- a layer of any kind of jam

## Directions

1. Beat the whites and yolks of the eggs separately
2. Mix them and beat well again, adding a few thin slices of citron, the cream, and sufficient pounded sugar to sweeten it nicely
3. When the mixture is well beaten, put it into a buttered pan, and fry the same as a pancake; but it should be three times the thickness of an ordinary pancake
4. Cover it with jam, and garnish with slices of citron and holly-leaves
5. Serve this dish cold

title: The Invalid's Cutlet
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 nice cutlet from a loin or neck of mutton
- 2 teacupfuls of water
- 1 very small stick of celery
- pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Have the cutlet cut from a very nice loin or neck of mutton; take off all the fat; put it into a stewpan, with the other ingredients
2. Stew very gently indeed for nearly 2 hours, and skim off every particle of fat that may rise to the surface from time to time
3. The celery should be cut into thin slices before it is added to the meat, and care must be taken not to put in too much of this ingredient, or the dish will not be good
4. If the water is allowed to boil fast, the cutlet will be hard

title: Thick Apple Jelly or Marmalade, for Entremets or Dessert Dishes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Apples
- to every lb. of pulp 3/4 lb. of sugar
- 1/2 teaspoonful of minced lemon-peel

## Directions

1. Peel, core, and boil the apples with only sufficient water to prevent them from burning
2. Beat them to a pulp
3. To every lb. of pulp add the sugar in lumps
4. Dip the lumps into water and put them into a saucepan
5. Boil till the syrup is thick and can be well skimmed
6. Add this syrup to the apple pulp with the minced lemon-peel
7. Stir it over a quick fire for about 20 minutes, or until the apples cease to stick to the bottom of the pan
8. Pour the jelly into moulds which have been previously dipped in water
9. Turn out for dessert or a side-dish, with custard poured round and garnished with strips of citron or blanched almonds

title: Thick Gingerbread
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of treacle
- 1/4 lb. of butter
- 1/4 lb. of coarse brown sugar
- 1-1/2 lb. of flour
- 1 oz. of ginger
- 1/2 oz. of ground allspice
- 1 teaspoonful of carbonate of soda
- 1/4 pint of warm milk
- 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Put the flour into a basin, with the sugar, ginger, and allspice
2. Mix these together
3. Warm the butter, and add it, with the treacle, to the other ingredients
4. Stir well
5. Make the milk just warm
6. Dissolve the carbonate of soda in it
7. Mix the whole into a nice smooth dough with the eggs, which should be previously well whisked
8. Pour the mixture into a buttered tin
9. Bake it from 3/4 to 1 hour, or longer, should the gingerbread be very thick
10. Just before it is done, brush the top over with the yolk of an egg beaten up with a little milk
11. Put it back in the oven to finish baking

title: Thickened Butter
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pint of melted butter
- the yolks of 2 eggs
- a little lemon-juice

## Directions

1. Make the butter quite hot, and be careful not to colour it.
2. Well whisk the yolks of the eggs, pour them to the butter, beating them all the while.
3. Make the sauce hot over the fire, but do not let it boil.
4. Add a squeeze of lemon-juice.

title: Tipsy Cake
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 moulded sponge-or Savoy-cake
- sufficient sweet wine or sherry
- 6 tablespoonfuls of brandy
- 2 oz. of sweet almonds
- 1 pint of rich custard

## Directions

1. Procure a cake that is three or four days old,--either sponge, Savoy, or rice answering for the purpose of a tipsy cake.
2. Cut the bottom of the cake level, to make it stand firm in the dish; make a small hole in the centre, and pour in and over the cake sufficient sweet wine or sherry, mixed with the above proportion of brandy, to soak it nicely.
3. When the cake is well soaked, blanch and cut the almonds into strips, stick them all over the cake, and pour round it a good custard, made by recipe No. 1423, allowing 8 eggs instead of 5 to the pint of milk.
4. The cakes are sometimes crumbled and soaked, and a whipped cream heaped over them, the same as for trifles.

title: To Bake A Ham
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Ham
- Common crust

## Directions

1. Soak the ham in water for at least 12 hours
2. Wipe it dry and trim away any rusty places underneath
3. Cover it with a common crust, taking care that it is of sufficient thickness all over to keep the gravy in
4. Place it in a moderately-heated oven
5. Bake for nearly 4 hours
6. Take off the crust and skin
7. Cover with raspings, the same as for boiled ham
8. Garnish the knuckle with a paper frill

title: To Bake Potatoes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Cold butter

## Directions

1. Scrub potatoes well in lukewarm water
2. Put them into a moderate oven and bake them for about 2 hours, turning them three or four times whilst they are cooking
3. Serve them in a napkin immediately they are done
4. Send to table with them a piece of cold butter

title: To Bake Smelts
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 smelts
- bread crumbs
- 1/4 lb. of fresh butter
- 2 blades of pounded mace
- salt and cayenne to taste

## Directions

1. Wash, and dry the fish thoroughly in a cloth
2. Arrange them nicely in a flat baking-dish
3. Cover them with fine bread crumbs
4. Place little pieces of butter all over them
5. Season and bake for 15 minutes
6. Just before serving, add a squeeze of lemon-juice, and garnish with fried parsley and cut lemon

title: To Boil A Ham
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Ham
- Water
- Glaze or bread-raspings

## Directions

1. Choose a ham that is perfectly sweet by running a sharp knife into it close to the bone; if it has an agreeable smell, the ham is good
2. If it has been long hung and is very dry and salt, let it remain in soak for 24 hours, changing the water frequently; from 8 to 12 hours would be sufficient for a Yorkshire or Westmoreland ham
3. Wash it thoroughly clean and trim away from the under-side all the rusty and smoked parts
4. Put it into a boiling-pot with sufficient cold water to cover it
5. Bring it gradually to boil and carefully remove scum as it rises
6. Keep it simmering very gently until tender, being careful that it does not stop boiling nor boil too quickly
7. Take it out of the pot and strip off the skin
8. Sprinkle over it a few fine bread-raspings
9. Put a frill of cut paper round the knuckle
10. If to be eaten cold, let the ham remain in the water until nearly cold; do not remain in the saucepan all night
11. Garnish with parsley or cut vegetable flowers

title: To Boil Haricots Blancs, or White Haricot Beans
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of white haricot beans
- 2 quarts of soft water
- 1 oz. of butter
- 1 heaped tablespoonful of salt
- pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Put the beans into cold water, and let them soak from 2 to 4 hours, according to their age.
2. Put them into cold water, salted in the above proportion, bring them to boil, and let them simmer very slowly until tender.
3. Pour the water away from them, let them stand by the side of the fire, with the lid of the saucepan partially off, to allow the beans to dry.
4. Add 1 oz. of butter and a seasoning of pepper and salt.
5. Shake the beans about for a minute or two, and serve; do not stir them with a spoon, for fear of breaking them to pieces.

title: To Boil New Potatoes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- To each 1/2 gallon of water allow 1 heaped tablespoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Do not have the potatoes dug long before they are dressed, as they are never good when they have been out of the ground some time

title: To Boil New Potatoes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Water, 1/2 gallon to each batch
- Salt, 1 heaped tablespoonful per 1/2 gallon of water
- Butter, a piece the size of a walnut

## Directions

1. Do not have the potatoes dug long before they are dressed, as they are never good when they have been out of the ground some time.
2. Well wash them, rub off the skins with a coarse cloth, and put them into boiling water salted in the above proportion.
3. Let them boil until tender; try them with a fork, and when done, pour the water away from them.
4. Let them stand by the side of the fire with the lid of the saucepan partially uncovered, and when the potatoes are thoroughly dry, put them into a hot vegetable-dish, with a piece of butter the size of a walnut.
5. Pile the potatoes over the butter, and serve.
6. If the potatoes are too old to have the skins rubbed off, boil them in their jackets; drain, peel, and serve them as above, with a piece of butter placed in the midst of them.

title: To Boil Pickled Pork
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork
- Water

## Directions

1. Should the pork be very salt, let it remain in water about 2 hours before it is dressed
2. Put it into a saucepan with sufficient cold water to cover it
3. Let it gradually come to a boil, then gently simmer until quite tender
4. Allow ample time for it to cook, as nothing is more disagreeable than underdone pork, and when boiled fast, the meat becomes hard

title: To Boil Potatoes
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 or 12 potatoes
- To each 1/2 gallon of water allow 1 heaped tablespoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Choose potatoes of an equal size and pare them
2. Take out all the eyes and specks, and as they are peeled, throw them into cold water
3. Put them into a saucepan with sufficient cold water to cover them, with salt in the above proportion
4. Let them boil gently until tender
5. Ascertain when they are done by thrusting a fork in them, and take them up the moment they feel soft through
6. Drain away the water
7. Put the saucepan by the side of the fire with the lid partially uncovered to allow the steam to escape
8. Let the potatoes get thoroughly dry and do not allow them to get burnt
9. Send them to table quickly and very hot, with an opening in the cover of the dish

title: To Boil Potatoes In Their Jackets
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 or 12 potatoes
- To each 1/2 gallon of water allow 1 heaped tablespoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Wash the potatoes well and use a clean scrubbing-brush to remove the dirt from them
2. Choose the potatoes so that they may all be as nearly the same size as possible
3. Fill the saucepan half full with them and just cover the potatoes with cold water, salted in the above proportion
4. Bring them to boil, then draw the pan to the side of the fire and let them simmer gently until tender
5. Ascertain when they are done by probing them with a fork
6. Pour off the water and uncover the saucepan
7. Let the potatoes dry by the side of the fire, taking care not to let them burn
8. Peel them quickly and put them in a very hot vegetable-dish either with or without a napkin
9. Serve very quickly

title: To Boil Rice for Curries
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of the best Carolina rice
- 2 quarts of water
- 1-1/2 oz. of butter
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Wash the rice well in two waters
2. Make 2 quarts of water boiling, and throw the rice into it
3. Boil it until three-parts done, then drain it on a sieve
4. Butter the bottom and sides of a stewpan, put in the rice, place the lid on tightly, and set it by the side of the fire until the rice is perfectly tender, occasionally shaking the pan to prevent its sticking
5. Either dish it separately, or place it round the curry as a border

title: To Boil Spinach (English Mode)
author: Isabella Beeton
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pailfuls of spinach
- 2 heaped tablespoonfuls of salt
- 1 oz. of butter
- pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Pick the spinach carefully, and see that no stalks or weeds are left amongst it
2. wash it in several waters
3. Have ready two large pans or tubs filled with water; put the spinach into one of these, and thoroughly wash it
4. with the hands, take out the spinach, and put it into the other tub of water (by this means all the grit will be left at the bottom of the tub)
5. wash it again, and, should it not be perfectly free from dirt, repeat the process
6. Put it into a very large saucepan, with about 1/2 pint of water, just sufficient to keep the spinach from burning, and the above proportion of salt
7. Press it down frequently with a wooden spoon, that it may be done equally
8. when it has boiled for rather more than 10 minutes, or until it is perfectly tender, drain it in a colander, squeeze it quite dry, and chop it finely
